1. The student's parents should be staying in Andhra Pradesh.
1. The annual family income of parents/guardians should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-
1. The student should have cleared CPT/Graduation before 2018, registered, and paid CA-Inter (IPCC) exam fees. 
1. The student should not have benefitted from APBWC for the same exam (CA Inter / IPCC) earlier. 
1. The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS).

scheme_name: Bankable Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be from a scheduled caste.
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant's age must be between 18 to 60 years.
1. The applicant must be unemployed.
1. The applicant must be capable of undertaking business activity**.**1. The applicant must not be a defaulter of any financial institution.
1. The applicant's annual income must not exceed ₹1,50,000/- (if residing in urban area) and ₹1,20,000/- (if residing in rural area).

scheme_name: Bihar State Journalist Insurance Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a journalist/media representative. 
1. Independent journalists must hold a valid press accreditation card.
1. The applicant should have a minimum of 5 years of work experience in journalism. 
1. The media representative's age is between 21-70 years. 
1. The media representative should be residing and working in Bihar. 
1. The media representative educational qualification should be matriculation or equivalent for the insurance.
1. The media representative should cover 50% of news or comments in journalists from newspapers, magazines, news agencies, electronic media, and independent journalists with valid press accreditation.
1. The media representative's spouse and two dependent children are included in this Insurance Scheme. 
1. The media representative's son's age is up to 23 years (or 25 if studying any bonafide institutions), and the daughter will be covered until marriage.
1. A media representative affiliated with a private news channel must ensure that the channel's telecast reaches at least 20 districts in the state.

scheme_name: Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
All BSKY Smart Health Card/ National Food Security Act (NFSA)/ State Food Security Scheme (SFSS) card holders are eligible.
**For the referral of the patient to the empaneled private hospital for treatment:**

 1. The referral will be done if the particular package/ procedure cannot be provided by a Government facility in the district. 
 1. Cases will be referred if there is a long waiting time to undertake the procedure at a Government facility in the district. 
 1. Where there is an emergency case that requires immediate referral.
 1. Referral process under BSKY is decentralized up to the Sub-Divisional Hospital level. The following authorities have been designated to issue referrals to beneficiaries under BSKY.

 - **District Headquarter Hospital:** CDM & PHO/ District Medical Officer-cum Superintendent & concerned Specialist. - **Sub-divisional Hospital:** Superintendent & concerned Specialist. - **All Govt. Medical College & Hospitals:** Superintendent & HoDs of concerned Department of the Medical College & Hospitals

scheme_name: Bihar Shatabdi Leper Welfare Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Bihar.
 1. The applicant should be a leprosy patient with Visible Deformities Grade-II.

scheme_name: Bicycle/Sewing Machine Assistance Scheme (UKBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source: 1. The applicant must be a construction worker registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, or a dependent of such a worker.

scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Master’s Degree Overseas
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community. 
1. The student's parents should reside in Andhra Pradesh. 
1. The annual income of parents/guardians should not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.
1. The student should have been admitted in any master's degree course overseas (at least 12 months duration) in a reputed institution, during the years 2019-20 Or should be pursuing the final year of the PEC course overseas, in collaboration with the Indian university/institute. 
1. The student who has already availed benefit earlier will not be eligible. 
1. The student should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount from any other government scheme for the same purpose.

scheme_name: Bihar State Madarsa Strengthening Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The Madarsa should be located in Bihar.
 1. The Madarsa should be recognized by the Bihar State Madarsa Board or other Government-Recognized Madarsas.
 1. The Madarsa Management Committee should not have been subject to any disciplinary actions in the past three years.
 1. The Madarsa should possess sufficient land, which should be legally owned and free from disputes.
 1. The Madarsa should have an enrollment of more than 70 students.
 1. The Madarsa should have been recognized for at least two years.
 1. The Madarsa should adhere to all relevant state regulations and guidelines for educational institutions.

scheme_name: Bihar Startup Policy
eligibility_text_source:
1. Up to a period of ten years from the date of incorporation/ registration, if it is incorporated as a private limited company (as defined in the Companies Act, 2013) or registered as a partnership firm (registered under section 59 of the Partnership Act, 1932) or a limited liability partnership (under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008).
 1. Turnover of the entity for any of the financial years since incorporation/ registration has not exceeded one hundred crore rupees.
 1. The entity is working towards innovation, development or improvement of products or processes or services, or it is a scalable business model with a high potential for employment generation or wealth creation.
 1. Provided that an entity formed by splitting up or reconstruction of an existing business shall not be considered a ‘Start-up’.
 1. Provided Start-up must be incorporated or registered in Bihar and have an office in Bihar.
 1. Provided further applicable taxes from the operations of the company are payable in Bihar.

scheme_name: BSR Fellowship Scheme for Science Students - UGC
eligibility_text_source:
1. The candidates who are selected for registering to the Ph.D. programme of the university through a procedure outlined already in the UGC notification by a regular admission procedure in Universities with Potential for Excellence / Centres of Advance Studies and Department of Special Assistance identified by UGC are eligible to apply for fellowship under the scheme.
 1. The registration for Ph.D. may follow after the admissions. 

***Other Conditions:***
The University/Institutions/College shall admit the fellows under RGNF-SC scheme for research leading to M.Phil or Ph.D irrespective of the stipulated number of research students assigned to the Supervisor or the Department in the concerned subjects.
***Resignation:***
If the fellow wishes to leave the fellowship before the end of the tenure, the information regarding relinquishment may be informed to the identified Agency under intimation to UGC. Grant will be released upto the date of relinquishment, provided it is whether the total tenure of fellowship.
***Cancellation of Award:***
The fellowship in liable to cancellation, in case of:

 - Misconduct.
 - Unsatisfactory progress of research work.
 - Candidate is later found ineligible.
 - Candidate is already availing scholarship/fellowship from any other sources.
 - Candidate if found employed during the M.Phil/Ph.D research.
 - Any false information furnished by the applicant or any fraudulent activity by the Scholar/Fellow/Research Awardees.

***Procedure for Monitoring

scheme_name: UGC-BSR Faculty Fellowship Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Reader/Associate Professor/Professor in the Science and Technology departments of a university.
 1. The applicant should have successfully supervised:

 - 15 Ph.D. dissertations in Basic Sciences, or
 - 10 Ph.D. dissertations in Engineering and Technology.
 - At least 5 candidates must have received their degrees in the preceding 10 years.

 1. The cumulative impact factor of the published papers by the applicant should be > 50 (according to the Journal citation Reports of Thomson Reuters).
 1. The applicant must have handled, as Principal Investigator, at least 3 sponsored research projects funded by national / international agencies, in the last 10 years.
 1. The applicant must apply within one year of the date of superannuation in the University.
 1. The applicant shall not hold any administrative responsibility during tenure of the fellowship which would be from the date of superannuation.
 1. The Department / School / University shall have to provide an undertaking in the application that the applicant would be provided:

 - with the required laboratory infrastructure and administrative assistance for undertaking the fellowship work and 
 - with permission to take a minimum of Two stipendiary candidates for Ph.D. under the applicant, if selected for the UGC-BSR Faculty Fellowship Award.

scheme_name: Bal Sakha Yojana
eligibility_text_source: - All newborns up to 30 days old belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families and Scheduled Caste categories, who do not pay income tax, will be eligible for benefits.

scheme_name: Balika Samrithi Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a girl child born on or after 15-8-97.
- The annual income of the family of the applicant should not exceed ₹15,000.
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be a native of the Union Territory of Pondicherry by birth or continuous residence for not less than 3 years.

scheme_name: Bal Shakti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The child should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a child below 5 years of age.
1. The child must be severely malnourished or sick and meet at least one of the following criteria:

- Mid-Upper Arm Circumference (MUAC) less than 11.5 cm.
- Weight-for-height Z-score less than -3 SD (standard deviation).
- Bilateral pitting oedema (swelling on both feet).

scheme_name: Beej Swalamban Yojna
eligibility_text_source: - Farmers, who are interested in progressive and crop-specific farming.

scheme_name: Behind the Ear Hearing Aids by Welfare of Differently Abled Persons
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a hearing-impaired person.
1. The beneficiary should be studying 6th standard and above.
1. The beneficiary should be any one of the following:

 - Student
 - Self-Employed 
 - Employed Persons.

scheme_name: Bus Traveling Facility For Visually Impaired/Bus Concession
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Delhi.
1. The applicant should have 40% and above disability.
1. Before applying to the concerned depot, the applicant needs to get the card from Sub Divisional Magistrate (SDM).

**Note:** Facility of concession to the escort is given to those who have not less than 100% blindness.

scheme_name: Back-To-Lab Post-Doctoral Fellowship Programme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Only women candidates are eligible to apply.
2. Applicant should be a Permanent Resident of Kerala.
3. Applicant should be unemployed and should be under career-break due to family commitments or other related matters, at the time of application.
4. Applicant should not receive any emoluments from any source at the time of application.
5. Those who have availed KSCSTE Back-to-Lab Fellowship earlier for their PhD programme are not eligible to apply.
6. Age limit: 40 years - as on the date of application, relaxable for 3 years for OBC and 5 years for SC/ST.
7. Applicant should have qualified Ph.D. in Science/Engineering subjects.
8. Applicant should have scored a minimum of 60% marks for post-graduation.
9. Applicant should have chosen an institution as Research Centre subject to the following.
• The Research Centre should not be the same as the Ph.D. Research Centre.
• Tenable only in R&D institutions (National/State level) or University Departments in Kerala and should belong to the category of “Government Institutions” [however, premier National Institutions viz., IISER, IIT, IISc. etc. outside the State shall also be considered].
10. Applicant should have published at least one research paper in a SCI Journal/ secured patent.
11. Those who have already availed of one Post-Doctoral Fellowship from KSCSTE or elsewhere are not eligible to apply.

scheme_name: Back-to-Lab Research Fellowship Programme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Only women candidates are eligible to apply.
 1. Applicant should be of Kerala origin and unemployed.
 1. Age limit: 45 years
 1. Applicant should have qualified post-graduation with a minimum of 60% marks.
 1. Applicant should have a valid Ph.D registration in an institution of academic/ research excellence in Kerala including R&D institutions, University Departments, Govt. Aided colleges, in any of the streams; Science, Technology, Engineering, Agriculture or Medicine (STEAM).
 1. Applicant should have completed Ph.D coursework.
 1. Only those research scholars who are under (undergone) a career break spanning a minimum period of 6 months during the course of their Ph.D. programme due to maternity or other valid reasons are eligible to apply.
 1. Applicant should not have completed 4 years as on the date of Ph.D registration.
 1. Applicant should not be receiving any fellowship or grant from any other source for the Ph.D. programme.

scheme_name: Bank Tie-Up Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a domicile / permanent resident of the state of Punjab.
1. The applicant should be from Below Poverty Line (BPL).
1. The applicant should be from the Scheduled Caste category.
1. The annual income of the family of the applicant (from all sources) should be ₹ 67,649/- per annum (for rural areas); ₹ 88,756/- per annum (for urban areas).
1. The applicant should not already be availing the benefits of the scheme.

scheme_name: Balram Talab Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
- The applicant must possess agricultural land.
- The applicant must have installed drip or sprinkler sets in financial year 2017-18 or thereafter, and these must be currently in operational condition, to be eligible for subsidized pond construction.
- The applicant must construct the pond on their own land. Subsidy will not be provided for pond construction on leased land.

scheme_name: Financial assistance to Meritorious Scheduled Tribe Students who have Passed First Division from Board and University
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student. 
 1. The applicant should have passed the 12th standard examination from the Madhyamik Shiksha Board/Central Board of Secondary Education with a first division, securing 60% or more marks.
 1. The applicant should have secured at least 48% marks in the first year examination to be eligible for the incentive in the second year.
 1. The applicant should have secured at least 48% marks in the second year examination to be eligible for the incentive in the third year.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category.
 1. The applicant's parents/guardian/husband should not be income tax payers.
 1. The student should be a native of Rajasthan.
 1. There should be no gap in the applicant's studies after passing the board examination.
 1. The applicant should be enrolled as a regular student in general education in a government college.
 1. Students residing in government hostels are not eligible for benefits under this scheme.
 1. The applicant must possess a Jan Aadhaar Card and an Aadhaar Card.

scheme_name: Bhavantar Bharpai Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a landowner, lessee, or tenant farmer in Haryana.
 1. The farmer should cultivate one of the 21 listed crops.

**Note 1:** Registration on the [Meri Fasal, Mera Byora portal](https://fasal.haryana.gov.in/) during the sowing period is mandatory.
**Note 2:** The land and crop details must be certified by the Horticulture Department.

scheme_name: Braille Watches by Welfare of Differently Abled Persons Department
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a visually impaired working person. 
1. The applicant should be working in the unorganized sectors. 
1. The applicant should be any one of the following:

 - Employee.
 - Self-Employed.

scheme_name: Bima Yojana - Direct Benefit to Artisan
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a master craftsperson who has received Shilp Guru Awards, National Awards, Merit Certificates, or similar honours.
- The applicant should be aged 60 years or above (relaxation is available for artisans with disabilities).
- The applicant’s annual income should not exceed ₹1,00,000.
- The applicant should not be receiving similar financial assistance from other sources.
- The applicant should submit an income certificate and an affidavit annually, countersigned by a 1st Class Magistrate.

scheme_name: Biotechnology Young Entrepreneur Award
eligibility_text_source: 1. Students who are pursuing post-graduate programmes (including integrated PG degree programmes), doctoral degrees, B.E. or B.Tech. in Biotechnology or in the area of life sciences related to biotechnology (bioinformatics/Molecular biology/microbiology/biochemistry/Genetics and/or related subjects) from universities/Institutes in the State of Kerala are eligible to apply.

scheme_name: Bhagyalaxmi Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility**

1. A girl’s birth must be registered within a year after her birth date.
1. Girls shouldn’t engage in child labour.
1. Benefits from the Bhagyalakshmi scheme are available to BPL families with up to 2 female children.
1. Girls should have been immunized correctly in the health department’s program.
1. Girl children must be born after 31 March 2006 in a BPL family.
1. A girl must have completed the eighth grade to be eligible for the maturity amount
1. She cannot have been married off before turning 18 years old.

scheme_name: Banglar Yuba Sathi
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of West Bengal.
 - The applicant must be aged between 21 and 40 years as on 01.04.2026.
 - The applicant must be unemployed.
 - The applicant must have passed the Madhyamik or equivalent examination.

> **Notes:**

- **Yearly verification of live status of the beneficiary of the scheme:** Once a year, the status of the beneficiary, whether alive or not will be verified.

- **Stopping or withholding of financial assistance:** The Government may, after giving the beneficiary of the scheme an opportunity of being heard, stop or withhold financial assistance if:

1. The Financial Assistance has been found to be sanctioned on mistaken grounds.
1. The eligibility condition for the granting of financial assistance no longer exists.

scheme_name: Biju Yuva Sashaktikaran Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident/domiciliary of the state of Odisha.
1. The application must be a +2 Passout from the Council of Higher Secondary Education, Odisha.
1. The students clearing the Upshastri Exam from Shree Jagannath Sanskrit University, Puri, are also eligible.

scheme_name: Cash Award (A.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant student should be a permanent resident of Assam.
 1. The applicant’s parent should be engaged in any building & other construction work.
 1. The applicant’s parent should be registered with the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The parent of the applicant should have an active membership with the Board.
 1. To avail of the benefits of the scheme, students must have obtained a minimum of 55% or above in the HSLC, AHM, and H.S. examinations.

scheme_name: Cash Award (APB&OCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker should be a permanent resident of Arunachal Pradesh.
2. The worker must be employed in the Building and Other Construction works.
3. The worker should be registered with Labour Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).
4. The worker’s membership must be active.
5. Children for whom the award is applied must have passed the class X and XII Board or equivalent examinations.
6. Children (one son and one daughter) of the registered worker with a membership, who secure the highest marks in their District in Metric examination.

scheme_name: Calipers and Crutches - Tamil Nadu
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an orthopedically differently abled person. 
1. The applicant should be a resident of the district/area of Tamil Nadu.

scheme_name: Compensation Amount Towards Cattle Injury Caused by Forest Animals
eligibility_text_source:
1. The cattle of the owner should have been injured by a wild animal, as defined under the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972.
1. The cattle owner should have reported the incident to the nearest forest officer within 48 hours of its occurrence.
1. The cattle owner should be able to provide necessary proof or verification documents as required by the Forest Department.
1. The compensation applies only to cattle owners who are residents of Madhya Pradesh.

scheme_name: CA/ICWA CMA /CS Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Kerala.
1. The applicant must belong to Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Parsi, or Jain religious communities.
1. The applicant's annual family income should be below ₹8,00,000/-.
1. The applicant must be appearing for intermediate or final year examinations of Chartered Accounts, Cost & Management Accounts, or Company Secretaryship courses.
1. The applicant must have secured 60% marks in Plus Two/B.Com or other degrees.
1. The applicant should have an account in any nationalised bank in their own name.

> **Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:**

 - Preference will be given to applicants belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
 - In the absence of BPL applicants, applicants from minority religious communities with an annual income up to ₹8,00,000/- in the Above Poverty Line (APL) category will be considered.
 - Selection will be based on merit and income.

scheme_name: Cash Award (MBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a resident of Meghalaya.
 1. The beneficiary should be engaged in any building or other construction work.
 1. The beneficiary should be registered under the Meghalaya Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The beneficiary should have an active membership of the Board.
 1. Registered construction workers whose child or children achieve the highest marks in the SSLC examination within their district can apply to avail the benefits of the scheme.

scheme_name: Cash Award to the Meritorious Visually Impaired Students Obtaining First Three Ranks at the State Level in the SSLC and HSC Examination
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
- The percentage of the disability of the applicant should be 100% and above.
- The applicant should have studied in Tamil or English Medium.
- The applicant should have passed the qualifying SSLC & HSC Examination respectively.

scheme_name: Coaching Assistance for Pre-preparation for Competitive Exams of ALL INDIA level like IIM, CEPT, NIFT, NLU and IELTS, TOFEL, GRE for going Foreign
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student.
1. The applicant must belong to the SEBC or Economically Backward Class category.
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant must have passed Class 10 or Class 12 (SSC or HSC).
1. The applicant must be preparing for one of the eligible competitive exams (IIM, CEPT, NIFT, NLU, IELTS, TOEFL, GRE).
1. The applicant must have paid tuition/coaching fees for the exam preparation.
1. The annual family income of the applicant must not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Cultivation of Aromatic Plants under MGNREGA - Centre for Aromatic Plants
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a farmer residing in a rural area of Uttarakhand.
 1. The farmer must be interested in aromatic plant cultivation.
 1. The farmer must either legally own agricultural land or have leased land for a minimum period of 10 years.

scheme_name: Coaching Assistance to Scheduled Castes Students for Preparation of Examination Like IIM, NIFT, NLU, CEPT
eligibility_text_source:
> **Eligibility Criteria for Student:**

 1. The students should belong to Scheduled Caste Category.
 1. A student should have secured 70% or more in Class 10th and studying in Class 12th (any stream) to get admission in CEPT, NIFT, or NLU.
 1. To get admission in IIMs: Students should have secured 70% or more in Class 12th (any stream) and be in the third year of graduation (any stream) or should have secured 70% or more marks in graduation if completed graduation.
 1. There is no income limit.

> **Eligibility Criteria for Institute:**

 1. The organization should have more than three years of experience.
 1. The organization should have a GST Number/PAN card.
 1. Institutions should have biometric (fingerprint) machines for student attendance.
 1. The training institute should be registered under any one of the following Acts.

 - Mumbai Public Trust Act, 1950
 - Companies Act, 1956
 - Shop and Establishment Act, 1948 (Shops and Institutions Act, 1948)

scheme_name: Coaching Assistance Scheme for Preparation of Recruitment Exams for Scheduled Caste Students
eligibility_text_source:
> **Eligibility Criteria for Student:**

 1. The students should belong to Scheduled Castes Category.
 1. The student should have passed the graduation exam with 50% or more marks.
 1. The age limit will be a maximum of 35 years for males and a maximum of 40 years for females.
 1. The applicant or the applicant's parents should not be in government employment.
 1. The applicant will not be able to pursue full-time studies, job/business, etc. at any other place during the training.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will be available to the applicant only once.

**Note:** After receiving the coaching, the details of which examination the student himself/herself has given should be sent from time to time to the concerned Deputy Director, Office of Scheduled Caste Welfare.
> **Eligibility Criteria for Institution:**

 1. The Institution should have more than three years of experience.
 1. The Institution should have a GST Number/PAN Card.
 1. Institutions should have biometric (fingerprint) machines for student attendance.
 1. For competitive recruitment examinations, the faculty should have at least graduation and general knowledge.
 1. A training institution should be registered under any one of the following Acts.

 - Mumbai Public Trust Act, 1950
 - Companies Act, 1956
 - Shops and Establishments Act, 1948

scheme_name: Coaching Assistance Scheme For Scheduled Caste Students For Pre-Exam Preparation Like NEET, JEE, GUJCET
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility Criteria of the Student:**
1. Students should be of Scheduled Caste.
2. There is no income limit for students.
3. Students for preparation of exams like NEET, JEE, and GUJCET with 70% or more marks in Std. 10 and must be studying in Std. 12 (Science stream).
**Eligibility Criteria of the Institute:**
1. The organization should have more than three years of experience.
2. The organization should have a GST number/PAN card.
3. Institutions should have biometric (fingerprint) machines for student attendance.
4. Faculty for NEET, and JEE who should have done M.Sc. in that subject.
5. The training institute should be registered under any one of the following Acts.
• Mumbai Public Trust Act, 1950
• Companies Act, 1956
• Shop and Establishment Act, 1948 (Shops and Institutions Act, 1948)

scheme_name: Child Adoption - Tamil Nadu
eligibility_text_source:
1. The parents must have at least two years of a stable marital relationship.
1. The single prospective adoptive parents should be between 30-55 years of age. 
1. An unmarried/single male persons are not eligible to adopt a girl child.
1. The parents should have adequate financial resources to upbring the child.
1. Age Criteria:

 - **For a child up to 4 years old:**

 1. Maximum composite age of the Prospective Adoptive Parents: 90 years.
 1. For a single Prospective Adoptive Parent: Minimum age 25 years, maximum age 45 years.

 - **For a child above 4 years up to 8 years old:**

 1. Maximum composite age of the Prospective Adoptive Parents: 100 years.
 1. For a single Prospective Adoptive Parent: Minimum age 25 years, maximum age 50 years.

 - **For a child above 8 years up to 18 years old:**

 1. Maximum composite age of the Prospective Adoptive Parents: 110 years.
 1. For a single Prospective Adoptive Parent: Minimum age 25 years, maximum age 55 years.

scheme_name: Cash Award to Top Ranking Holders in Public Examinations
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be a native of the Union Territory of Puducherry by birth or by continuous residence for not less than five years.
- The applicant should be a Student.
- The applicant should be studying in a Government/Private High/Higher Secondary School or in Intermediate Level (2nd Year) in a Government Junior College in the Union Territory of Puducherry.
- The applicant should be among the top three Rank Holders from Secondary School Leaving Certificate (SSLC) Exam/ Secondary School Certificate (SSC) Exam/ Matriculation Exam/ Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) Exam/ Intermediate (2nd Year) Public Examination.

scheme_name: Construction of Artesian Wells in Scheduled Tribe Areas
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The beneficiary must be a farmer engaged in agricultural activities.
 1. Collective irrigation projects will be constructed under the scheme.
 1. The name of the proposed village must be included in the list of Scheduled Tribe-dominated villages issued by the Department of Social Welfare.
 1. The project site must be suitable for the construction of artesian wells.
 1. A sufficient irrigated area must be available for the project.
 1. The project cost must not exceed ₹4.00 lakh per hectare, and the benefit-cost ratio must be greater than 1.0.

scheme_name: Cash Award to Women Players of the State
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a woman.
 1. The applicant should be a student.
 1. The applicant should have passed the Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) examination.
 1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant should have secured first, second, or third position in individual or collective sports competitions held at the state level.
 1. The applicant should have represented Gujarat state in national-level sports competitions.
 1. The applicant’s accomplishment must have occurred within the respective financial year.

scheme_name: Coaching by Private Institutes for All India Services Civil Services Examination
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Tribe.
1. The applicant should have an annual income from all sources not exceeding ₹6,00,000.
1. The applicant should have passed graduation.
1. The applicant should have been selected based on merit in Arts/Commerce/Science/Engineering/Medical groups.
1. The applicant should not have availed the benefits of this scheme before.
1. The applicant should register their profile on the MPTAASC portal of the department.
1. The applicant’s age should be determined by UPSC as per the eligibility criteria for the Civil Services (Pre) exam of the respective year.

**Reservation**
5% of reserved seats for needy and promising low-income students.
**Priority**
The applicant should be given priority if they have passed the Civil Services Main Examination of Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission in the last three years.

scheme_name: Cash Benefit Scheme Scheme (PBAOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be a Student.
- The applicant should be a ward of a Construction Worker.
- The Construction Worker should be registered as a Construction Worker with the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The applicant should have secured 1st/2nd/3rd Rank at the State/Regional Level in the Higher Secondary course or its equivalent examination.

scheme_name: Capacity Building For Service Providers - Entrepreneurship Programme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should have passed Class 8th.
2. The applicant should be above the age of 18 years.

**Note 1:** A class will have a minimum of 10 trainees and a maximum of 30. 

**Note 2:** In case the number of eligible candidates exceeds the requirement of a batch and there is a requirement of additional batches, the Implementing Agency will concurrently or in phases run more batches depending on its institutional capacity. 

**Note 3:** In case of phasing, the scheduling of candidates between batches will be on first-cum-first-admitted basis. The Implementing Agency will maintain a register of applications which will also indicate the date of receipt of each.

scheme_name: Capacity Building For Service Providers - Skill Testing & Certification
eligibility_text_source:
> **For the Trainee (before enrolment) -** 

1. The applicant should have passed Class 8th.
1. The applicant should be above the age of 18 years.

> **For the Trainee (after enrolment) -** 

1. To ensure that training are viewed seriously, a trainee must attend 80% of the classes/periods to become eligible for a certificate/ for appearing in the end-of-the-course test.

**Note 1:** The batch size of all programs/courses requiring hands-on/practical training should ideally comprise 20 persons
**Note 2:** The selection of trainees is to be done in a transparent manner and the same trainee or person should not be selected again.
**Note 3:** The students and trainers to be enrolled must be linked to Aadhaar in the Implementing Agency’s records as well as the individual’s Banks account.

scheme_name: Mahila Samriddhi Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant must be between 18 to 55 years of age.
1. Applicable to Women from low-income families or women who belong to SC/ST/OBC categories as notified by the Central/State Government and whose family income is less than Rupees three lakhs per annum.
1. Applicable to Women looking to start their own business or engage in income-generating activities.

scheme_name: Credit Based Schemes For SC - Term Loan (TL)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an entrepreneur of the Scheduled Caste Category.
1. The applicant should submit a viable project proposal for an income-generating activity.
1. The applicant should apply through a State Channelising Agency, Regional Rural Bank, Public Sector Bank, or other channelising agency associated with NSFDC.
1. The project proposal should be appraised and found viable by the concerned channelising agency.

scheme_name: Concessional Bus Travel Facility to Women above 60 years of age- Punjab
eligibility_text_source: - All women of 60 years and above residing in the State of Punjab can avail of the benefits under the scheme.

scheme_name: Community Based Training Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to the religious minority community of the state.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Karnataka.
1. The applicant's age should be between 18 to 35 years. 
1. The annual income of the applicant's family from all sources should be less than ₹1,03,000/- in urban areas and less than ₹96,000/- in rural areas.
1. The selected applicants should compulsorily complete the training.
1. The applicant must have a minimum qualification of SSLC.
1. The applicant should not have previously taken training in any schemes of the state or central government.

scheme_name: Credit Card Scheme For Artisans And Weavers Of Handicrafts And Handloom Sector
eligibility_text_source:
1. All Artisans and Weavers who are duly registered with the Handicrafts and Handloom Department, J&K, and are involved in production and manufacturing processes (otherwise eligible for credit facilities for carrying out the proposed activities under any of the existing bank schemes) shall be eligible.
1. The selection of beneficiaries under the 'Credit Card Scheme for Artisans & Weavers in the Handicrafts and Handloom sector' shall be made by a District-level Selection Committee headed by the General Manager, District Industries Centre.

scheme_name: Crop Cluster Development Programme
eligibility_text_source:
- Only Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) registered under the Companies Act in Haryana are eligible to submit project proposals and avail assistance under the scheme.
- FPOs must have a minimum of 150 shareholders with a financial strength of at least Rs. 10.00 lakh in their bank account.
- The FPOs should have an area of at least 500 acres under horticulture crops, although this requirement may be flexible based on the project's viability.
- FPOs must have a business plan with backward and forward linkages of their produce.
- Disputed FPOs or those not meeting the eligibility criteria mentioned above will not be considered eligible.

scheme_name: Capital Contribution Scheme (Goa)
eligibility_text_source:
- Clusters/Units which are in operation for a period of at least 3 years shall be eligible except those covered under the Goa Sick Industrial Unit Revival and Rehabilitation Scheme, 2008 or any such Scheme in force during the tenure of this Scheme.
- Partnership firms and private limited concerns shall be eligible under this Scheme except those covered under the Goa Sick Industrial Unit Revival and Rehabilitation Scheme, 2008 or any Scheme in force during the tenure of this Scheme.
- Only those Units which are permanently registered with the Directorate of Industries, Trade and Commerce will be eligible.

_*Preference will be given to units in the small-scale sector, units involved in research and development, technically oriented units, and units run by women entrepreneurs/clusters._

scheme_name: Chetan Chauhan Labor Sports Promotion Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker must be working in an establishment registered under the Factories Act, 1948.
1. The monthly salary (basic salary + dearness allowance) of the worker should not exceed ₹15,000/-.
1. The worker must have worked continuously for at least six months in the establishment/factory and must be in service at the time of applying.
1. The benefit will be admissible to the worker or his two daughters only.
1. The benefit will be admissible to the worker or his two children only.
1. Only those players will be eligible who have been selected on or after March 23, 2021.
1. In case of the female worker herself being a player, she will be given the benefit.

scheme_name: Central Sector Scheme of National Fellowship for Providing Fellowship to Scheduled Caste Students to Pursue M.Phil. & PhD
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Caste.
 2. The applicant should be pursuing regular and full-time advanced studies/ research leading to an M.Phil/PhD Degree.
 3. The applicant should have qualified one of the following tests:
 a) National Eligibility Test - Junior Research Fellowship (NET - JRF) of UGC.
 b) UGC - Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (UGC - CSIR) NET - JRF Joint Test.
 4. The applicant should be pursuing the program from a University/Institution recognized by the University Grants Commission (UGC) as follows:
 a) Central/State Universities (including constituent and affiliated institutions) are included under Section 2(f) of the UGC Act,1956 and has valid accreditation from NAAC.
 b) Deemed Universities under Section 3 of the UGC Act i.e. Institutions for higher education notified by the Central Government to be deemed University, in consultation with UGC and having valid accreditation from NAAC.
 c) The institutions fully funded by the State/Central Government and empowered to award degrees.
 d) Institutions of National Importance as notified by the Ministry of Human Resource Development.
 5. A scholar is eligible to receive only one fellowship at a time. The scholar will not accept/receive any other monetary benefit/ scholarship/ fellowship from any other source.
> **Reservation/ Relaxation/ Preference/ Priority**

1. Scheduled Castes candidates who have already secured admission and qualify NET examination will be given preference. Such

scheme_name: Community Canning & Training On Fruit Preservation
eligibility_text_source:

- To train personals to start small scale cottage industries in fruits and vegetables for self employment. The duration of these training will be of 7 to 15 days. 
- To provide certificates to the trained personals so that they can start their own enterprise.

scheme_name: Cancer Day Care Centre - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The beneficiary must be a cancer patient.

scheme_name: Construction of Dudhghar/ Godown (Scheduled Caste Area)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The Society must not already own a Dudhghar or Godown.

scheme_name: Construction of Dudhghar/ Godown (Scheduled Tribe Area)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The Society must not already own a Dudhghar or Godown.

scheme_name: Children's Drawing Painting Competition
eligibility_text_source:
- The competition is open to children studying up to 12th standard.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Consolidation of Coffee
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a tribal grower.
2. The applicant's area is ineligible for subsidy if it received consolidation subsidy during the XII Plan period.
3. The applicant's land records must be in their name.
4. The applicant should provide land records or a certified possession certificate.
5. The applicant must procure seed material for gap filling or consolidation exclusively from the Coffee Board.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Expansion of Coffee
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a tribal grower.
1. The applicant's area should be suitable for coffee cultivation as per the opinion of the Coffee Board's officers.
1. The applicant should procure the seed material from the Coffee Board only.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Quality Upgradation / Certification: Drying Yards
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a tribal grower.
1. The applicant should not have availed of the benefits during the XII plan.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Quality Upgradation / Certification: Supply of Baby Pulpers
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a tribal grower.
1. The applicant is eligible for only one unit.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Quality Upgradation / Certification: Support for Eco-Certification / Organic Certification
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicants should be SHGs/collectives of tribal growers who obtain certification of their plantations for various sustainability and quality standards.
1. The applicant can access support only for certification of estates as per Organic, Fair Trade, Rainforest Alliance, UTZ Certified, Bird friendly/Shade grown coffee standards, or any other certification programs equivalent to the above programs.
1. The applicant claiming support for Eco/organic certification should have been certified as per the National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) standards, even if they are certified as per various international standards like EU Regulation, NOP (National Organic Programme of USA), JAS (Japanese Agriculture Standards), etc.
1. The applicant should obtain the Certificate only from the Certification Agencies accredited by the respective Certification Programmes during the MTF period.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Support for Group Nurseries
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be Self-Help Groups (SHGs) or collectives of tribal growers.
1. The applicant should have procured the seed material for the coffee nursery exclusively from the Coffee Board.

scheme_name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Water Augmentation
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a tribal grower.
1. The applicant should not have received a subsidy for Water Augmentation during the XII plan period.
1. The land records must be in the beneficiary's name.
1. These records should be certified by the Village Authority/State Government/Competent Authority.

**Eligible Activities**
Installation of the water storage tank, open well, and irrigation equipment (sprinkler/drip) to enhance production, productivity, and quality.
**Location Specifications**

- Water storage tank construction can be above or below ground level.
- Water augmentation structures must be on the estate or within its vicinity.

scheme_name: Cycle Distribution Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a girl student.
 1. The girl student should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
 1. The girl student should be studying in Class 11.
 1. The girl student should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category.
 1. The girl student who did not receive a cycle in Class 9 is eligible under the scheme.
 1. The girl student should travel more than 2 km to reach her school.
 1. There is no restriction on the annual family income of the student.

scheme_name: Cluster Development Scheme
eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant must be involved in artisan-based activities in handloom and handicraft sector.

- The applicant must be willing to participate in collective cluster activities.

scheme_name: Coconut Development Scheme
eligibility_text_source: - All farmers are eligible to apply for the scheme.

scheme_name: Children Education Allowance
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker should be engaged in building or construction work.

1. The worker’s child should be studying between Nursery and 10th Class. 
1. A maximum of two children per family are eligible.

scheme_name: Competitive Exam Coaching
eligibility_text_source: Should be a student of ST category. Both Male and Female students are eligible for taking benefit of this scheme.

scheme_name: "Conducting of Exhibitions" Component of the "Development of Coir" Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- Participants receive a daily allowance of ₹150 during the exhibition.
- Travel expenses, in 2nd sleeper class, are provided for the journey from production units to the exhibition venue and back.

- Free stall space is allocated to participants during the exhibition.

scheme_name: Credit Enhancement Guarantee Scheme For The Scheduled Castes
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should establish, promote, and operate an enterprise, project, or unit in the primary, manufacturing, or service sector resulting in asset creation.
1. The applicant should be a Scheduled Caste entrepreneur, company, partnership firm, society, sole proprietorship firm, or individual entrepreneur as specified under the scheme.
1. The applicant should have more than 51% ownership and management control held by Scheduled Caste entrepreneurs, promoters, partners, or members.
1. The applicant should maintain Scheduled Caste ownership and control above 51% throughout the currency of the loan.
1. The applicant should provide documentary proof of Scheduled Caste status.
1. The applicant may operate under any Central Government or State Government subsidy or grant scheme.
1. Preference is given to Scheduled Caste-promoted companies over registered partnership firms and registered societies.

scheme_name: Comprehensive Educational Loan Scheme - Sikkim
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student. 
1. The applicant's family annual income should not exceed ₹2,50,000/- for studies within India and ₹5,00,000/- for studies abroad.
1. The institution where the applicant wants to study, whether in India or abroad, must be recognized by the relevant authority in India.
1. Only one member per family shall be considered for the benefit, except in the case of Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.
1. Only members of Below Poverty Line (BPL) families will be considered for the study in Sikkim.

Note: Children of Government/Public Sector Undertaking employees are also eligible.

scheme_name: COP-5 Financial Assistance to Agricultural Credit Co-operative Banks (PACS/LAMPS) for Reconstruction and Reestablishment (General/Tribal Area)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a registered Agricultural Co-operative Credit Society, either a Primary Agricultural Credit Society (PACS) or a Large-sized Adivasi Multi-Purpose Society (LAMPS).
1. The society must have been operational and performing for the required duration:

 - PACS: At least the last three years.
 - LAMPS: At least the last two years.

1. The applicant must provide performance data for the relevant period, showing:

 - Growth in credit disbursement (short and medium-term).
 - Improvement in loan recovery rates.
 - Increase in deposit collection.

scheme_name: Compensation for Livestock Loss Caused by Wild Animals
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be the owner of the livestock injured or killed by wild animals.

scheme_name: Construction of Fish Markets
eligibility_text_source: The applicant should be a local body such as a Municipality or a Village Panchayat in the State of Goa.

scheme_name: Community Food Processing Centre
eligibility_text_source:
***Training:***

 - Any individual or farmer from Uttarakhand can receive training at the Community Food Processing Centre. A fee of ₹15/- is charged per trainee for the training.

***Food Processing:***

 - Food products can be prepared at the Community Food Processing Centre by providing the required raw materials. A fee of ₹15/- per kilogram is charged for processing.

scheme_name: Cultural Function And Production Grant Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant organizations that are voluntary organizations or NGOs, should, in order to qualify for the grant, have a properly constituted managing body with its powers, duties and responsibilities clearly defined and laid down in the form of a written constitution.
 1. The Organization should have been functioning and registered under the Societies Registration Act (XXI of 1860), Trusts Act, Companies Act, or any Central or State Act for at least three years.
 1. The applicant organizations that are voluntary organizations or NGOs must register themselves on the NGO Darpan Portal of NITI Aayog and obtain a Unique ID from the Portal. The organizations are required to intimate the Unique ID as obtained from the NGO Darpan Portal and PAN Number of the organization while applying under the Scheme.
 1. The Organization must have tied up or planned the matching resources at least to the extent of 25 % of the project cost.
 1. The Organization should have facilities, resources, personnel, and experience to take up the event/project for which a grant is required.

**Note 01:** Past experience of holding such functions, as applied for, would be given preference.

scheme_name: Craft & Gear (Unit: Boats of Appropriate Sizes Including Nets, Fish & Ice Holding Boxes, Etc.)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a fisherman.
- The applicant must possess the No Objection Certificate issued by the Directorate of Fisheries for the purchase or construction of a wooden or Fiberglass Reinforced Plastic Fishing Craft of below 26 feet, including fishing net and ice holding boxes.
- The applicant must produce the original Goods and Services Tax bill from the authorized dealer towards the purchase or construction of the new canoe, gill net and its accessories, and insulated fish and ice holding box to claim the subsidy.
- The applicant must not be a fishing trawler owner.
- The applicant must not be a defaulter of the Department.

**Reservation/Priority:** Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribes, Women, and their Co-operatives are eligible for a higher rate of assistance (60% of unit cost, limited to ₹60,000/-).
**Relaxation:** The Government is empowered to relax all or any of the clauses provided in this scheme if found deemed fit, for reasons to be recorded.

scheme_name: Cash Grant in lieu of Sewing Machine
eligibility_text_source: - The applicant should be the Widow of an [Ex-serviceman](https://sainik.py.gov.in/definition-ex-servicemen).- The applicant should be [registered](https://sainik.py.gov.in/registration-procedure) with the Department of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry.- The scope of this scheme will also apply to those ESM / living spouse/widows who are re-employed as regular / deemed to be regular in Government/Public Sector Undertakings/Private Organizations and self-employed IT assesses.

scheme_name: Grant on Passing the Written Examination of Competitive Examinations for Recruitment in Armed Forces
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should be the child of an ex-serviceman.
1. The applicant should have passed the written examination of NDA, CDS, IMA, OTA, Air Force, or Naval Academy.

scheme_name: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups
eligibility_text_source:
1. The Legal Status of the applicant should be: an entity recognised as a Startup by DPIIT as per the applicable Gazette Notifications.
2. The Credit History of the applicant should be: not in default to any lending or investing institution and not classified as a Non Performing Asset as per RBI guidelines.
3. The Certification requirement of the applicant should be: its eligibility must be certified by the concerned Member Institution for the purpose of obtaining guarantee cover.
4. The Nature of activity of the applicant should be: engaged in eligible startup business activities as per DPIIT recognition, and continuing the activity for which the credit facility is being sought.
5. Any other: The applicant must comply with all other eligibility criteria and scheme conditions prescribed under the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) from time to time.

scheme_name: Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme for Rural Entrepreneurs to set up Custom Hiring Centres (CHC) of Farm Machinery
eligibility_text_source:
- Individual farmers registered under Krishak Bandhu (New) Scheme.

Or,
Groups like SKUS/PACS/FPO/FPC/FIG/SHG duly graded /LAMPS etc. approved/recognized by any Govt. Institutions/Departments are eligible to set up CHC.

- The project cost/Total Financial Outlay (TFO) should be between minimum ₹20,00,000/-to maximum as per [SMAM Guideline](https://agrimachinery.nic.in/Files/Guidelines/smam.pdf) and SPMT recommendation.- The applicant should have sufficient land under his possession (own land/leased/hired) for setting up the CHC.

- The age of the beneficiary should be minimum 18 years at the time of application.

scheme_name: Comprehensive Handicrafts Cluster Development Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an implementing agency such as a state/central handicrafts corporation, autonomous body, cooperative, producer company, or SPV.
1. The applicant should have experience in the handicrafts sector.
1. The applicant should prepare a Detailed Project Report (DPR) based on cluster needs.

1. The applicant should ensure ownership of land free from litigation (where infrastructure is required).
1. The applicant should ensure a cluster-based approach in a specific geographical area or craft sector.
1. The applicant should ensure integration of artisans and SMEs for large-scale production.

scheme_name: Compensation for Human Death or Injury Caused by Wild Animals
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a victim of a wild animal attack, resulting in injury or death.
1. The applicant should submit proof that the injury or death was caused by a wild animal (excluding snakes, monitor lizards, and other poisonous creatures).
1. In the case of death, the applicant must be a legal heir, family member, or relative of the deceased person.

scheme_name: Chief Minister Sustainable Agriculture Irrigation Scheme - Magel Tyala Shettale (Individual Farm Pond)
eligibility_text_source:
1) Farmer should hold at least (Minimum) 0.20 ha. land and Kokan region, while it is 0.40 ha. limit for rest of Maharashtra.
2) The applicant farmers land will have to be technically suitable for digging farm pond. So that the runoff rainwater can be stored in the farm pond through natural flow and recharge will be possible. In case of farm ponds without inlet and outlet there should be a natural source from where extra runoff during rainy season can be lifted and stored in farm pond.
3) Before applying for farm pond, the applicant farmers should not have availed the subsidy for farm pond, community tank or bodi in paddy bunding or any Government Scheme.

scheme_name: C H Muhammedkoya Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to the Muslim, Latin, or Converted Christian community.

1. The applicant should be a girl student.
1. The applicant should be studying for the first year of Graduation or Higher courses in a Government/Aided Institution.

scheme_name: Construction of Hostels for OBC Boys and Girls
eligibility_text_source:
***Agencies eligible for financial assistance under the Scheme will be as follows:***

 1. State Governments and UT Administrations.
 1. Institutions or organizations set up by the Central Government as autonomous bodies under a statute (e.g. Central Universities, IIT, NIT, etc.).

***Location and Capacity of Hostels to be constructed under the Scheme:***
1. While sanctioning hostels, priority will be given to:
(i) Regions/districts not covered so far, and
(ii) Districts/towns having a large number of educational institutions.
2. As far as possible, hostels will be constructed in close vicinity of educational institutions.
***Eligibility criteria for students to be admitted to hostels under the Scheme:***
Students fulfilling the following eligibility criteria may be allotted seats in Hostels constructed under the Scheme:

 1. Students whose castes are included in the Central/State/UT list of Other Backward Classes and who do not belong to the "creamy layer".
 1. Hostels would be essentially for post matric students. However, if there are vacant seats, pre-matric students, especially of the secondary level, would also be eligible to be accommodated.
 1. Other things, being equal preference will be given to OBC students hailing from low-income families.
 1. At least 5% of the total seats should be reserved for students with disabilities.

scheme_name: Bal Swasthya Karyakram - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source: 1. The beneficiary must be a child between 0 and 5 years of age from Uttarakhand.

scheme_name: Coaching Help Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must belong to a non-reserved category.
 1. The applicant must have scored 70% or more in Standard 10th.
 1. The applicant must be currently studying in the science stream in Standard 11th or Standard 12th.
 1. The applicant’s annual family income should not exceed ₹4,50,000/-.

﻿
**Note:** The applicant can avail of the assistance only once per academic year.

scheme_name: Coaching For Higher Studies
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam.
- The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.
- The applicant must secure the requisite percentage of marks in the qualifying examination prescribed for the desired coaching, based on subject matter and on the available seats.
- **For Undergraduate students who are preparing for medical entrance examinations,** the applicant should be from the science stream with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology.- **For Undergraduate students who are preparing for engineering entrance examinations,** the applicant should be from the science stream with Physics, Chemistry, and Maths.

scheme_name: Chief Minister’s Startup/innovation Projects/new Industries
eligibility_text_source:
**Definitions**

 1. Startup/New Industries means any entity, Proprietor, Limited Company, registered partnership firm under Indian Partnership Act 1932, or Limited Liability Partnership, proposing to set up an enterprise in a micro or small scale category in the services sector relating to the focus areas.
 1. Innovation Project: Innovation is the process of introducing new or making changes with updated technology, large and small radical and incremental, to products, processes, and services that result in the introduction of some new and innovative products.
 1. Incubator: An incubator is any organization designed to accelerate the growth and success of entrepreneurial ventures i.e. startups, through an array of business support resources and services like physical space, capital, coaching and mentoring, common services, and providing networking connections.
 1. Host Institutes: Host Institutes (HIs) are reputed technology, management, and R & D institutes of the state and other institutions and organizations focused on entrepreneurial development and promotion to set up Incubators to galvanize the startup ecosystems in the State

**Eligibility**

 - All Startups/ new industries/Innovation Projects as defined in above points 1, 2, 3, and 4 of the scheme would be eligible for availing applicable incentives for components covered under this scheme. The same incentives cannot be availed on the same component under State and Central Scheme.

scheme_name: Coaching Help Scheme for JEE-GUJCET-NEET Exams
eligibility_text_source:

 1. The applicant must be pursuing coaching for JEE, GUJCET, or NEET.
 1. The coaching institute must have at least 3 years of experience.
 1. The annual family income of the applicant should be ₹4,50,000/- or less.

scheme_name: Compensation for Loss of Life and Property Caused by Wild Animals in and around Forest Areas: Assistance in case of Death/Injury/Disability due to Wild Animal Attack

 - All three species of bear

 - Crocodile / Gharial

scheme_name: Cash Incentive Award to Bonafede Meritorious Sportspersons

scheme_name: Compensation in Animal Death
