1. The applicant should be studying in Class VI to Class XII.
1. The applicant should have secured the first position in the preceding class.
1. The applicant should have obtained a first division in the preceding examination.

scheme_name: Banglashree for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises: Subsidy for Energy Efficiency (EES)
eligibility_text_source:
- The business/organization should be a Micro/ Small/ Medium Enterprise.
 - The business/organization should belong to the manufacturing sector.
 - The business/organization should have started production on or after 1st April 2019.
 - The business/organization should be in the private sector/ cooperative sector/ joint sector undertaking or should be a company/ undertaking owned and managed by the State Government or the Industrial SHGs.
 - The Date of Commencement of Commercial Production (DOCCP) should be between 1st April 2020 to 31st March 2025.

scheme_name: Endeavour To Empower-Skill Development
Emeritus Fellowship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a superannuated teacher from a recognized university, college, or institution.
1. Selection is based on the quality of research work and publications produced during the applicant's service career.
1. The applicant must submit a well-defined, time-bound research proposal.
1. The fellow may work under the scheme up to the age of 70 years or for two years from the date of award, whichever is earlier.
1. The applicant should not be holding any other position or gainful employment at the time of joining the fellowship.
1. Target Group: Highly qualified and experienced superannuated teachers of recognized universities, colleges, and institutions.

scheme_name: Exhibition Fair and Publicity
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a farmer.
 - The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.

scheme_name: Ex-gratia Scheme: Disability Compensation (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

 - The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
 - The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
 - The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

 - The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab.
 - The member should have complete or partial disability.
 - The member should not possess Health Cards issued under the Bhagat Puran Singh Sehat Bima Yojna.

scheme_name: Economic Help To Tribal Girls For Higher Education (Class 11 & 12)
eligibility_text_source:
1. Should be studying regularly in 11th and 12th in government schools.
1. The girl student should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category of Rajasthan.
1. The parent/guardian/guardian/husband of the student should not be an income tax payer.
1. The student must be a native of Rajasthan.
1. The student who is studying by living in the hostel run by the government. They will not be entitled to benefit under the said scheme.
1. Must have Vaidya Bhamashah card and Aadhaar card.

scheme_name: Employee Health Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Serving employees:

- All regular State Government employees. A State Government employee will have the meaning as defined under Fundamental Rules.
- Provincialised employees of local bodies.

2. Retired employees:

- All Service Pensioners
- Family pensioners
- Re-employed service pensioners

scheme_name: Economic Help To Sahariya Tribal Girl Students (Class 11 And 12)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The girl student should belong to the Saharia tribe of the Baran district of Rajasthan.
1. The girl child should be studying regularly in a government school.
1. The mother/father/guardian/guardian of the student should not be an income taxpayer.
1. The girl students, who are studying in residential schools/ashram hostels run by the government, they will not be entitled.
1. Vaidya Bhamashah card and Aadhaar should be there.
1. Students should be a native of Rajasthan.

scheme_name: Economic Help To Tribal Girls For Higher Education (College Level)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The girl student should belong to the tribal class of Rajasthan.
1. The girl student should be regularly studying general education in a private/government college.
1. The parent/guardian/guardian/husband of the student should not be an income taxpayer.
1. After passing the board examination, there should not be any gap in the continuous study of the student applying for the scheme.
1. The student who is studying by living in a hostel run by the government. They will not be entitled to benefit under the said scheme.
1. Must have Vaidya Bhamashah card and Aadhaar card.
1. The girl student should be a native of Rajasthan.

scheme_name: "Employment Incentive" under "Motivation of Entrepreneurs to Start Industries and Fiscal Assistance to Industries"
eligibility_text_source:
- The new units which commence production on or after 2-8-2004 would alone be eligible for this incentive for 5 years from the date of commencement of production.
- The unit should provide direct employment to at least 10 persons.
- The "employment incentive" will be granted if the unit has provided at least 60% of the employment to the people of UT of Puducherry.

scheme_name: "Employment Incentive" under "Motivation of Entrepreneurs to Start Industries and Fiscal Assistance to Industries - Various Incentives"
eligibility_text_source:
- All the Micro, Small, Medium and Large Industries are eligible.
- The Unit should have made the investment on or after 1st April 2017.
- The unit should provide at least 60% of employment to the people of the Union territory of Puducherry of the total employment strength of the industrial units. This 60% of the local employment will have to be maintained by the industrial unit during the period specified in the Affidavit.
- The unit should provide direct employment to at least 10 persons.
- The workers should be covered under Provident Fund (PF).
- The industrial unit shall not transfer or dispose of the fixed asset including plant and machinery in any manner till the completion of 5 years from the date of submitting the application/disbursement of the subsidy whichever is later.

_*The additional investments made by the new/existing industrial units after availing the first claim are also eligible for subsidy up to the ceiling limit._
> **Special Cases**

If, two or more industrial undertakings are set up by the same person as proprietor, common partners, common Board of Directors/shareholders, such units are eligible for subsidy as separate entity provided the location of the unit are different and licenses /clearances/ registrations are obtained separately for the enterprises.

scheme_name: Early Intervention Centres for Children with Autism
eligibility_text_source:
1. A beneficiary child should be between 0-6 years old.
1. A beneficiary child should be affected by autism.

scheme_name: Early Intervention Centre for Children with Cerebral Palsy in SRTC, Chennai and Tiruchirapalli Districts
eligibility_text_source:
1. The young child/infants should be affected with cerebral palsy. 
1. The age of a young child/infant should be 6 years or below.

scheme_name: Early Intervention Center for the Children with Intellectually Disabled
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary child's age should be 6 years or below. 
1. The child with intellectual disability and allied disabilities is eligible.

scheme_name: Early Intervention Centre for Infants and Young Children with Hearing Impairment
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary child should be between 0-6 years. 
1. The beneficiary child should have hearing impairment.
1. The beneficiary child should not have any other disabilities.

scheme_name: Early Intervention Centre for the Visually Impaired
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary child should be visually impaired.
1. The beneficiary child's age should be 6 years or below.

scheme_name: Establishment of Integrated Educational Complexes
eligibility_text_source:
1. Government-aided institutions in the city/town of Panaji/Margao/Mapusa located within 10 km. of the complex site and engaged in educational activities, if they so desire to shift from their present location due to the fact that their schools are presently functioning in areas that are congested and with insufficient infrastructure, may apply.
1. Existing Government educational institutions that have no or insufficient premises and are located within 10 km. of the proposed complex site may also apply.

scheme_name: Educational Loan Scheme (GBCDC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should belong to a socially and educationally backward class.
1. The total annual family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The student must have secured admission through a recognized entrance examination conducted by the Central or State Government, or through a selection process, with at least 50% marks in Class 12 or the qualifying examination.
1. The applicant should have secured admission in a recognized institution for one of the following courses:

 - MBA or its equivalent (recognized by AICTE)
 - Master of Computer Applications (MCA) or its equivalent (recognized by AICTE)
 - Graduate-level engineering courses conducted by IITs or other institutions (recognized by AICTE)
 - Medical or allied education such as Ayurvedic, Homeopathy, Unani, etc., from institutions recognized by the Medical Council of India
 - Diploma course in Hospitality Management recognized by the National Council of Hotel Management

> **Note:** Priority will be given to applicants whose family income is up to ₹1,50,000/- per annum.

scheme_name: Educational Loan Scheme (GMFDC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant should belong to a minority religious community (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Jain, Buddhist, Parsi, and Jewish) or a linguistic community (Sindhi, Marathi, Urdu) of Gujarat State.
 1. The applicant or any family member should not have received a loan from Gujarat Minorities Finance and Development Corporation Limited, Gujarat Backward Class Development Corporation, Gandhinagar, or any other central/state government corporation/institution or bank.
 1. The annual income of the applicant's family should be:

 - ₹98,000/- for rural areas.
 - ₹1,20,000/- for urban areas.
 - However, families with income exceeding ₹1,20,000/- and up to ₹8,00,000/- are also eligible, albeit with different interest rates

 5. The applicant's age should be between 16 and 32 years.

scheme_name: Education Loan for Persons with Disabilities- Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana State.
 1. The applicant should have a disability of more than 40%.
 1. The applicant's age should be between 18 and 55 years. For persons with mental retardation, the eligible age is above 14 years. An age certificate issued by a competent authority authorized by the State Government, or as mentioned in the 10th certificate or any other government-issued certificate, will be required document.
 1. The applicant should have secured admission to a professional or technical course in India or abroad.
 1. Unique Disability ID (UDID) registration under the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India, is mandatory for loan amounts of ₹15 lakhs and above.
 1. The applicant must have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP ID).

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary (ies) should be from the Scheduled Caste Community.
 1. Individuals, Partnership Firms, and Co-operative Societies can undertake income-generating activities.
 1. The annual family income of each member/applicant should not exceed Rs.3.00 lakh.
 1. Applicants must apply for financial assistance from NSFDC through the concerned State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs)/Channelizing Agencies (CAs). 
 1. Annual family income of the applicants should be within Rs.3.00 lakhs (for both rural and urban areas w.e.f. 08.03.2018) under Credit Based Schemes. 
 1. There is no income criterion under Skill Development Training Programmes.

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme- Credit Line 2 for Minority Community
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The age of the applicant should be between 16 and 32 years.
 1. The annual family income of the applicant should be between ₹98,001/- to ₹8,00,000/- (Rural Area) and ₹1,20,001 to ₹8,00,000/- (Urban Area).
 1. The applicant should belong to a minority community, i.e., Muslims, Sikhs, Christians, Buddhists, Parsis, and Jains.
 1. The applicant should have obtained admission to any professional and technical course approved by an appropriate agency such as AICTE, Medical Council of India, UGC, etc., in a duly accredited/recognized institute.
 1. The applicant must have secured a minimum of 50% marks in the qualifying exam i.e., the exam whose qualification is a pre-requisite for the course.
 1. The applicant should have a Parivaar Pehchan Patra (PPP ID).

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme - Delhi
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Delhi.
- The applicant should be from one of the following target groups - Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, Other Backward Class, Minority, Persons with Disability.
- The annual income of parents/guardian should be below ₹5,00,000/-.
- The applicant must have secured admission in a recognized/Government/Technical Institution in India or abroad.
- The College/Institute should have been offering the course for the last three years.
- The College/Institute should be recognized by the Central/State Government or by appropriate authorities such as UGC (University Grants Commission), AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education), etc.
- The duration of the course should not exceed 5 years.

**Post Sanction Eligibility for the Borrower**

- The borrower must not participate in any unlawful activity that would debar them from pursuing studies and must follow all rules and regulations laid down by the educational institution.
- The borrower must not enter into any pecuniary obligation or financial liability during the currency of the loan.
- The borrower must strive to secure suitable employment after completing the course and furnish full particulars of such employment, including income details, to DSFDC.

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme of NMDFC
eligibility_text_source:
a) The applicant must be Indian and belong to one of the six notified minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Parsi, Jain).
b) The annual family income limit is:
Up to Rs. 3.00 lakh per annum under Credit Line-1.
Up to Rs. 8.00 lakh per annum under Credit Line–2.
c) The applicant must have secured admission to a recognized institution in India or abroad through a merit-based selection or entrance test.
d) The course must be technical or professional, with a duration not exceeding 5 years.
e) The applicant must not have availed financial support for the same course under any other Government scheme.
f) The loan is admissible for tuition fees, hostel charges, books, equipment, and travel (for abroad studies).
g) The applicant must have a co-borrower (parent/guardian) with a verified income certificate.
h) Preference shall be given to female students and candidates from economically weaker sections.

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme (NSFDC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student pursuing regular full-time Professional or Technical courses in a recognized institution in India or abroad.
 1. The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Caste community.
 1. The applicant's annual family income should be less than ₹3,00,000/-, both in rural and urban areas.
 1. The education loan assistance will be provided as a one-time assistance.

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme for Backward Classes Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be between 18 to 55 years of age.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
1. The annual family income of the applicant should not exceed ₹3,00,000/- in both rural and urban areas.
1. The applicant should belong to Backward Classes.
1. The applicant should have obtained admission for any professional and technical courses approved by an appropriate agency, such as AICTE, Medical Council of India, UGC, etc., in a duly accredited/recognised institute.
1. The applicant should have a minimum of 50% marks in the qualifying exam, i.e., the exam whose qualification is a prerequisite for the course.
1. The applicant should have a Pariwar Pehchan Patra (PPP ID).

scheme_name: New Akanksha Scheme (Educational Loan) (GTKVN)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should belong to the Thakor or Koli caste/community. 
1. The family's annual income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should have secured admission in a recognized institution for:

- MBA or equivalent (approved by AICTE)
- MCA (Master of Computer Applications or equivalent)
- Undergraduate Engineering programs (approved by AICTE/IITs)
- MBBS or equivalent (approved by Medical Council of India)

**Note:** At least 50% of the total sanctioned loan amount will be reserved for applicants from families earning up to ₹1,50,000 annually.

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme: Haryana Women Development Corporation
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Haryana domicile girl.
1. The applicant can be a child of a Haryana Government employee.
1. The applicant can pursue higher education, professional/technical diploma/graduate/postgraduate, doctoral/post-doctoral level in the country and abroad.
1. There are no preconditions for income.
1. There are no preconditions of caste.
1. There are no preconditions for religion.
1. The scheme is applicable for studies in India and abroad.

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme under NSFDC- Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste Community.
 1. The applicant's age should be between 18 and 45 years.
 1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
 1. These loans are available to students pursuing regular full-time recognized professional/technical courses in India or abroad.
 1. The education loan for a particular student shall be made admissible only once either at Diploma/Graduation or Post Graduation Diploma/Post Graduation level. However, an Educational Loan shall be offered to a student who secures admission in any integrated course of a longer duration covering both levels.

**Note 1:** 50% of the beneficiaries having annual family income up to ₹1,50,000/- and balance 50% of the beneficiaries have annual family income above ₹1,50,000/- and upto ₹3,00,000/-.
**Note 2:** NSFDC`s Educational Loan shall also be admissible for students of the target group who have secured admission to technical /professional courses prior to 1.12.2009 subject to the condition that they have not availed Educational Loan from any other institution.

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme under NSKFDC- Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant's age should be between 18 and 55 years.
 1. There is no income limit.
 1. The applicant should be Safai Karamchari or his/her dependent.
 1. The loan shall be provided to students for pursuing regular full time recognized professional/technical courses in India or abroad.
 1. The Education loan for a particular student shall be made admissible only once either at Diploma/Graduation or Post Graduation Diploma/Post Graduation level. However, an Educational Loan shall be offered to a student who secures admission in any integrated course of a longer duration covering both levels.

**Note 1:** Permanent Safai Karamchari means a person engaged in, or employed for any sanitation work and includes his dependents.
**Note 2:** The applicant shall have to produce a Certificate of Safai karamchari or dependent of Safai karamchari from the head of the Government Departments (i.e. school, colleges, forest, health, education, animal husbandry having rank not less than Gazetted officer), elected members of municipal Body/corporation and pradhan of Gram panchayats, local revenue officer or the cantonment or an officer of the Railways not below the rank of gazette officer.
**Note 3:** NSFDC`s Educational Loan shall also be admissible for students of the target group who have secured admission to technical /professional courses prior to 01.12.2009 subject to the condition that they have not availed Educational Loan

scheme_name: Establishment of 50 Milch Animals Dairy Unit
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a livestock Farmer.
 - The applicant must purchase 50 dairy animals (cows/buffaloes).
 - The applicant must establish a new dairy farm.
 - The applicant must have ownership rights or inheritance rights or leasehold rights of the land.
 - The applicant must take a loan from a Reserve Bank-recognized institution/bank.

> **Note:** If the beneficiary does not own land, the dairy farm can be established on leased land for a minimum of 7 (seven) years.

scheme_name: Electronic Manufacturing Clusters (EMC) Scheme: Common Facility Centres/ Brownfield Electronics Manufacturing Clusters
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a legal entity such as an individual, company, society, industry association, financial institution, research and development (R&D) institution, or government agency.
1. The applicant should form a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) for implementation.
1. The applicant should submit a preliminary application along with a feasibility study and project details.
1. The applicant should ensure that the committed investment is at least 4 times the assistance sought.
1. The applicant should ensure that at least 75% of the investment is in electronics manufacturing units.
1. The applicant should propose the upgradation of an existing Electronics Manufacturing Cluster.
1. The applicant should ensure the presence of an existing industrial ecosystem/cluster.
1. The applicant should achieve financial closure before submitting the final application.
1. The applicant should submit a Detailed Project Report (DPR).

scheme_name: Electronic Manufacturing Clusters (EMC) Scheme: Greenfield Electronic Manufacturing Clusters
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a legal entity such as an individual, company, society, industry association, financial institution, research and development (R&D) institution, or government agency.
1. The applicant should form a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) for implementation.
1. The applicant should submit a preliminary application along with a feasibility study and project details.
1. The applicant should ensure that the committed investment is at least 4 times the assistance sought.
1. The applicant should ensure that at least 75% of the investment is in electronics manufacturing units.
1. The applicant should develop a new Electronics Manufacturing Cluster (EMC).
1. The applicant should procure land for the project.
1. The applicant should achieve financial closure before submitting the final application.
1. The applicant should submit a Detailed Project Report (DPR).

scheme_name: PMMSY: Establishment of New Freshwater Fin Fish Hatcheries-Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The freshwater finfish hatchery should have a minimum capacity of 15 million fry per year per unit or 6.00 crore spawn per year per unit.
1. The hatchery should have a minimum area of 0.50 hectares.
1. The fish hatchery should be managed by qualified technical staff.
1. The applicant will ensure the supply of seed produced from the centrally assisted hatcheries to farmers at an affordable and reasonable price.
1. The fish hatchery will include a brooder pond, nursery ponds, rearing tanks, a small laboratory, water and electric supply, and required infrastructure facilities.
1. The cost of accreditation of the hatchery must be mandatorily included in the project estimates.
1. The projects having land on long-term registered lease will also be considered for Central financial assistance under the scheme.
1. The lease period or agreement shall not be less than a minimum period of 10 years.
1. The applicant, having their own land, must furnish revenue records with a clear land title.

scheme_name: Establishment of Goat Unit (10 +1)
eligibility_text_source:
The applicant must belong to one of the following categories:

 1. Scheduled Caste (SC).

 1. General Category.

**Note:** Priority will be given to Scheduled tribe widows & abandoned women.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Establishment of large RAS / Biofloc culture system - Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Haryana and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant must belong to either the General, Scheduled Caste (SC), or Women category.
1. The applicant must be the owner or legal leaseholder (with a minimum 10-year lease) of the land intended for setting up the unit.
1. The applicant must have access to either surface water or groundwater for aquaculture purposes.
1. The applicant must hold a valid training certificate in fisheries or aquaculture.
1. The applicant must be willing to maintain and operate the unit at personal cost after the initial setup.
1. The applicant must submit a complete Detailed Project Report (DPR) or a Self-Contained Proposal (SCP) for evaluation.
1. The applicant must agree to sign an official agreement with the Fisheries Department to avail of benefits under the scheme.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Establishment of Medium RAS / Biofloc culture system - Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Haryana and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant must have access to own land or hold a registered lease for a minimum period of 10 years.
1. The applicant must ensure that the land is free from any legal and financial encumbrances.
1. The applicant must have access to a reliable water source such as a canal, tube well, river, or similar.
1. The applicant must provide a valid training certificate in fisheries or aquaculture.
1. The applicant must agree to manage and maintain the system at their own cost after setup.
1. The applicant must submit a Detailed Project Report (DPR) or a Self-Contained Proposal (SCP).
1. The applicant must sign an agreement with the Department of Fisheries to avail benefits under the scheme.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Establishment of small RAS /Biofloc culture system - Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Haryana and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant must own land or have a registered lease of at least 10 years, free from legal encumbrances.
1. The applicant must have access to a water source such as a canal, well, tube well, or similar.
1. The applicant must be trained in aquaculture or fisheries and hold a valid certification.
1. The applicant must be willing to maintain and operate the system at their own cost post-installation.
1. The applicant must submit a Detailed Project Report (DPR) or a Self-Contained Proposal (SCP).
1. The applicant must sign an agreement with the Fisheries Department to be eligible under the scheme.

scheme_name: Employees' Pension Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a member of the Employees' Provident Fund Scheme, 1952, or an exempted establishment under Section 17 of the Act.
- The applicant's pay must not exceed ₹15,000/month (unless contributing additional 1.16% above this limit).

- The applicant must have a minimum of 10 years of eligible service for pension.
- The applicant must have contributed to the Employees' Pension Fund.

> **For Superannuation Pension**

- The applicant must be 58 years or above in age.
- The applicant must have rendered at least 10 years of eligible service.

> **For Early Pension**

- The applicant must have rendered at least 10 years of eligible service.
- The applicant must opt for early pension between 50 and 58 years of age.

> **For Family Pension**

- The applicant must be the spouse or child of a deceased member who met the eligibility criteria.
- The applicant must provide proof of the relationship and the member's death.
- The deceased member must have contributed for at least one month.

> **Post-Selection Conditions**

- The applicant must submit the required documents for verification.
- The applicant must comply with EPFO guidelines for pension disbursement.

*International workers are eligible under specific social security agreements.

scheme_name: Employment Subsidy Scheme, 2017
eligibility_text_source:

eligibility_text_source:

> **Role and Responsibilities of the Delegates:**

 1. The delegates shall provide correct and true details and documents as may be required by the concerned nominating departments, the Department of Tourism, OTDC, passport authorities, and the visa granting authorities.

scheme_name: Ex-Gratia Payment for Loss of Hands, Legs, Eyes, or Vision
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be either disaster-affected individual or involved in disaster relief or preparedness-related activities.
1. The extent and cause of the disability must be certified by a doctor from a government hospital or dispensary.

scheme_name: Kisan-Vaigyanik Sangoshthi
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a youth, a farmer, a member of a self-help group, or a livestock farmer.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a nominee/legal heir of the deceased worker.
1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the Maharashtra Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance (GBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
- The deceased should have been a Worker.
- The deceased should have been between 18 to 60 years of age.
- The deceased should have completed ninety days of service as a registered worker.
- The deceased should have been registered with the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The applicant should be the nominee/dependant of the deceased.
- The nominee/dependant of the unregistered workers shall be eligible upon the production of a certificate from the builder or Inspector of the area stating that the deceased was a building worker.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance- Haryana Silicosis Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be the dependent/nominee of the deceased worker.
 1. The deceased worker should have been suffering from silicosis as confirmed by the Silicosis Diagnosis Board, Haryana.
 1. The worker should have been employed in a factory or at a construction site.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Aids And Appliances For Disabled Persons
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Maharashtra State.
1. The applicant should be a Person with Disability (Visually Handicapped, Low Vision, Hearing Impaired, Orthopedically Handicapped, etc).
1. The percentage of the disability should be 40% or above.
1. For financial assistance of 100% of the cost of the aids and appliances, the monthly income of the applicant should be less than ₹1,500/-.
1. For financial assistance of 50% of the cost of the aids and appliances, the monthly income of the applicant should be between ₹1,500/- to ₹2,000/-.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance (A.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Assam.
 1. The applicant should be a nominee/dependent of deceased worker.
 1. The deceased worker should have been engaged in any building and other construction work.
 1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The deceased worker should have had an active membership with the Board.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Allied Cultural Activities
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligible Projects:**
Grants will be given to projects for procurement of audio-video equipment, which will include: Microphone, Transmitter, Power conditioner, Audio Mixer, Live Encoder, Router, Streaming Transceiver, Broadcast quality video cameras, Hyper Cardioid Microphones, Digital Mixer, Audio Delay Device, Amplifiers, Line Array Speakers, Network Switch, LED Display Controller, Audio De-Embedder, etc.
**Eligible Organisations:**

 1. All not-for-profit organizations who are engaged for at least for past three years in presenting the spectacle for allied cultural activities to give first-hand experience of live performances on a regular basis and during festivals in open/closed areas/spaces where large numbers of tourists/visitors come regularly and during major events/festivals the number of visitors swells to lakhs and shall also fulfill the following criteria will be eligible:

 - The organization is registered as a society under the Registration of Societies Act (XXI of 1860) or similar Acts, or as a Trust or as a Not-for-Profit Company, at least for a period of three years; and
 - The applicant organization will submit a recommendation letter in the prescribed format ([Annexure-I](https://indiaculture.gov.in/sites/default/files/Schemes/Sub_component_1B_SCHEME_31_01_2019.pdf)) from the District Authority (District Magistrate/District Collector/Deputy Commissioner) in this regard Or State Governments/Union Territories/District Authorities.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for the Treatment of Blind, Disabled, Deaf, Dumb, or Mentally Challenged Ex-Servicemen and their Dependents (Wife and Children)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
 1. The applicant should either be an ex-serviceman as defined by the Government of India or a dependent family member (excluding parents) of ex-serviceman.
 1. Only an ex-serviceman holding the rank of Havildar or below, or their family members, are eligible to apply.
 1. The ex-serviceman or their dependent should have a disability of 40% or more.
 1. Both pensioner and non-pensioner are eligible. However, ex-serviceman who is already receiving a disability pension from the Army cannot apply for financial assistance for himself based on their disability. He may apply on behalf of a disabled family member with 40% or more disability.
 1. Under this scheme, the family members do not include parents. Only wife/widow, son/daughter are included.
 1. The ex-serviceman’s disability will be considered valid regardless of whether it occurred during military service or after retirement.
 1. The name of the disabled wife, son, or daughter should be recorded in the discharge book or Part II Order of the ex-serviceman.
 1. Assistance will not be provided to more than two members of the same family, including the ex-serviceman.
 1. A valid recommendation from the District Sainik Welfare Officer (DSWO) is mandatory.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Artificial Limbs to Workers and their Dependents- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The monthly salary of the worker should not exceed ₹20,000/-.
 1. The service period of the worker is fixed for one year.
 1. The application is to be submitted within one year from the date of purchase of artificial limbs.
 1. The assistance is available to workers and their dependents, who become disabled due to the loss of vital organs in an accident.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance (ANBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a legal nominee or dependent of the deceased worker.
 1. The deceased worker should have been a resident of the Union Territory of Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
 1. The deceased worker should have been engaged in any building or other construction work.
 1. The deceased worker should have been registered under the Andaman & Nicobar Islands Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The deceased worker should have had an active membership with the Board.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for ANM/GNM/Technical Courses
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam.
- The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.
- The applicant's family income must not exceed ₹5,00,000/- per annum.

- The applicant must currently be undergoing the course in a Government or Government Recognized Institute.
- The applicant must be undergoing professional, job-oriented technical Diploma/Degree courses.
- The applicant must be enrolled in a course with a minimum duration of one year.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Blind Ex-Servicemen
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bona fide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be a 100% permanently blind ex-serviceman.
 1. The applicant should not be in receipt of any kind of pension or financial assistance either from the Haryana State Government, any Department of the Government of India, or any entity controlled by the Central or State Government.

**Note 1:** The definition of Ex-Servicemen for the purpose of eligibility for financial assistance shall be effective as notified by the Ministry of Defence Government of India from time to time.
**Note 2:** The old beneficiaries whose cases for grant of financial assistance have already been sanctioned prior to the issue of these guidelines shall continue to get the financial assistance at enhanced rates and they need not apply afresh. However, payment shall be discontinued with immediate effect who fail to submit their survival verification proofs every year from the revenue authorities.
**Note 3:** Payment of financial assistance to those beneficiaries shall also be stopped immediately who fail to submit their survival verification proofs every year from the revenue authorities in the month of April. Secretary, Zila Sainik Board shall be personally responsible for any lapse in this regard.
**Note 4:** Every applicant shall have to produce a certificate issued by the concerned District Social Welfare Officer to the effect that the applicant is not getting any financial assistance Pension from the Social Justice & E

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Boat owner / Fishermen Captured by Pakistan Marine Security Agency (PMSA)-DNH&DD
states: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of the Union Territory of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.
1. The applicant must be a boat owner or fisherman captured by the Pakistan Marine Security Agency (PMSA), or a family member thereof.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Brackish Water Aquaculture Farms
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a farmer having a water spread area of 2 hectares or less.
- The farms owned by the applicant should be licensed by the Coastal Aquaculture Authority.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Coaching- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The applicant's monthly salary must not exceed ₹25,000/-.
 1. The applicant must have a minimum of one year of service.
 1. Benefits are provided to children of registered workers who are receiving coaching for entrance examinations in professional courses, as well as for those preparing for the main examination after passing the preliminary examination of UPSC and HPSC.
 1. The child/candidate should have scored at least 60% marks in the qualifying examination.
 1. The benefits of this scheme are available to three girls and two boys of the workers.
 1. The child of the worker will not be covered under this scheme if he/she is self-employed or employed.
 1. If a worker's child is receiving a scholarship from the Haryana Labour Welfare Board, he/she can also avail the benefits of this scheme.

> **Conditions prescribed for coaching institutes regarding the coaching being taken by students:**

 - The coaching institute must have been providing coaching for at least three years.
 - The institute should have provided coaching to a minimum of 300 students.
 - The institute should have been paying service tax (GST) to the government for running coaching classes or a coaching institute for at least three years.
 - The institute should have paid the Labour Welfare Fund due to the Board and must be registered un

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Cultural Activities In Performing Arts For Building Grants Including Studio Theatres
eligibility_text_source:
##### **Eligible Project: Grants will be given to projects for creating cultural spaces, which will include:**
1. Conventional Cultural Spaces for Performing Arts:

 - Performance venues like Auditoria, Open-air Theatres, and Concert Halls.
 - Rehearsal halls for Theatre/ Music/ Dance.
 - Training Centres/ Schools for Theatre/ Music/ Dance etc.

2. Flexible Spaces, i.e., Studio Theatres, etc.: Non-proscenium rehearsal-cum-performance spaces, referred to as Studio Theatre or Experimental Theatre, that are characterized by the following special features:

 - A small theatre, with all the essential equipment for the performance of live music, dance, or theatre or combination of these arts;
 - An unconventional space that cannot qualify as an auditorium in the traditional sense; therefore, the stage or performance area is not normally placed within a proscenium arch nor raised too high or separated from the audience by a clearly demarcated division.
 - A seating arrangement for spectators that is totally flexible and can be moved fluidly from one part of the space to another depending on the artistic aim of a particular performance; therefore, the seats/chairs must not be fixed in position.
 - A capacity that usually does not go over 100 to 200; therefore, such a space is often called a “little theatre” or “intimate theatre” because it allows for close-up and intimate viewing by spectators.
 - One or two adjoining green room(s)/dressing room(s) /makeup room(s) with toilet(s) for

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance (CBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The deceased worker should have been working at the establishment, registered under Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965 and his contributions to the labour welfare fund should have been regularly received by the board.
 1. Any legal heirs of the deceased worker would be eligible to avail the benefit of this scheme only if the deceased worker was a member of the Board for at least the last six months.
 1. The financial assistance will be given on the production of the death certificate issued by the competent authority.
 1. The applicant should apply for Funeral Assistance within three months from the date of death.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Coaching Classes (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be engaged in building or construction work.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered worker must have a minimum of one year’s regular membership.
 1. The worker must have an active membership with the Board.
 1. The son/daughter of the worker should be receiving coaching for admission in professional/technical courses from an institute.
 1. The worker should not have applied again for financial assistance for the coaching of his/her child. The assistance will be provided only once. 
 1. The spouse of the worker should not have received any financial assistance for coaching classes of his/her child from any government department/board/corporate institution, nor should they have applied for such assistance.
 1. Financial assistance for coaching will be given up to three children.

scheme_name: Finance Assistant to PACS for Computerization and Integration of PACS through Core Banking System with District Cooperative Banks
eligibility_text_source:
1. The benefit of this scheme will be available only to Primary Agricultural Credit Co-operative Societies (Including PACS, LAMP, and FSS).
 1. The statutory audit of the Primary Agricultural Credit Co-operative Societies (PACS) up to the last financial year should have been completed.
 1. The Primary Agricultural Credit Co-operative Societies (PACS) benefiting from this scheme should have their own or leased premises.
 1. The Secretary of the Primary Agricultural Credit Cooperative Societies (PACS) should be a salaried employee.
 1. Primary Agricultural Credit Cooperative Societies (PACS) benefiting from this scheme should have telecom data network facility.
 1. There should be computer literate staff at Primary Agricultural Credit Cooperative Societies (PACS) for smooth implementation of the scheme.
 1. PACS should be selected in the District Level Implementation and Monitoring Committee (DLIMC) at the district level.
 1. The PACS selected in the State Level Implementation and Monitoring Committee (SLIMC) of the State level in the District Level Implementation and Monitoring Committee (DLIMC) should have been approved in the SLIMC.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Cultural Organizations With National Presence
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant Organization should, in order to qualify for grant, have a properly constituted managing body or governing body or governing council with its powers, duties and responsibilities clearly defined and laid down in the form of a written constitution.
 1. It should have facilities, resources, personnel and experience to take up the project for which grant is required.
 1. The Applicant Organization should be registered in India having an all India Character with national presence and should have operational presence at national/international level.
 1. The activities of the organization should be primarily or significantly cultural.
 1. The Organization should have capacity to do at least 20 events/programmes in a year.
 1. The Organization should have adequate working strength artistes/staff/volunteers.
 1. The Organization should have spent ₹1 crore or more during 3 of the last 5 years on cultural activities.
 1. Financial assistance will be given for all or any of the items listed below:

 - Normally up to 25% of total Govt. grant may be utilized for Maintenance (Salary of staff, Office Expenses, Miscellaneous Expenses) and Construction/Repairs/Extension/Restoration/Renovation of the Building of the Institute/Organization/Culture focused on promotion of Art & Culture.
 - Normally up to 75% of the total Govt. grant should in any case be utilized for Payment of Honorarium and other miscellaneous expenses on showcasing/production of events important to the preserv

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Construction/ Purchase of Wooden/ FRP Fishing Vessel (Canoe) for Fishermen
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
- The applicant or any member of his household should not be a defaulter with the Directorate of Fisheries, Goa.
- The canoe purchased through the scheme should be duly registered with the Directorate of Fisheries under the Marine Fishing Regulation Act (MFR) Act 1980/ Merchant Shipping (MS) Act.
- The applicant shall not sell, lease, mortgage, or transfer the wooden/ FRP craft for 5 years from the date of purchase of the said wooden/FRP craft.
- The wooden/FRP craft purchased should have been operated exclusively for fishing purposes only.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Commercial Tiatr Troupes/Directors/Producers for Stage Setting
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must have been on the commercial tiatr stage for at least five years.
- The applicant must have five years experience on the commercial tiatr stage along with the application.
- The applicant must be a commercial tiatr troupe, director, or producer.

***Selection Process***

- A maximum of six commercial tiatr troupes/directors/producers will be selected annually.
- If more than six applicants apply, selection will be decided by way of lots.
- Remaining applicants will be wait listed and provided with financial assistance in the following financial year.
- In case of withdrawal by any selected applicant, the next applicant on the waiting list will be given preference for allotment.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To The Child Victims Of Violence Under TheNational Foundation For Communal Harmony
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a domicile / permanent resident of the Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be a child less than 18 years of age. In exceptional cases, it can be extended up to the age of 21 years.
1. The applicant should be an Orphan or Destitute.
1. The applicant's main bread earner should have either been killed or permanently incapacitated in communal, caste, ethnic, or terrorist violence. 
1. The applicant's family should be 'Below Poverty Line' (BPL).
1. The applicant should not be receiving any assistance on a regular basis from any other source.

**NOTE:** The one-time financial assistance provided by the State Government / Union Territory Administration for such a victim is not considered 'another source of assistance' for this purpose.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance for the Construction Workers
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration**

- The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
- The applicant should be a Building/Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have served the Board for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For Application**

- The nominees/dependents who have lost any registered member/worker of the due to death.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Development Of Buddhist/Tibetan Arts And Culture
eligibility_text_source:
1. The voluntary Institutions/Organizations and Societies should be registered as a Society under the Societies Registration Act (XXI of 1860) or similar Acts.
 1. Only those organizations which are mainly devoted to Buddhist/Tibetan studies and have been functioning at least for the last three years will qualify for applying for a grant.
 1. The organization should be of the regional or all-India character.
 1. Grants from this scheme will be given only to those organizations which are not in receipt of grants from any other source for similar purposes.
 1. Financial assistance may also be given for the construction of hostel buildings, classrooms, school buildings, and training centers.

**Note 01:** The grants will be ad-hoc and of a non-recurring nature.
**Note 02:** Organizations that are doing good work in the field and have resources for meeting matching funds will be given preference.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Disabled Children (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The construction worker should be registered under Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCWWB).
 1. The registered worker must have a minimum of one-year regular membership.
 1. The registered worker who has dependent children with 50% or more physical/mental disability is eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. The worker should have a disability certificate of 50% or more physical or mental disability of his/her dependent children issued by the District Medical Board.
 1. The dependent child's name has to be included in the ration card.
 1. The dependent child of the registered worker has not availed of the benefit of the scheme in the current financial year from any other Haryana Government Department/Board/Corporation.
 1. The dependent child of the worker should not have any source of income.
 1. The dependent child of the worker should not be married.

**Note:** The worker has to submit/upload the live certificate of the dependent child every year to continue the financial assistance under the scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Destitute Children Scheme
Financial Assistance on Death/Disablement of Beneficiary (CBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be working in the Establishment, registered under the Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965 and his contribution towards the labour welfare fund should regularly been received by the Board.
 1. In case of the death of a registered male construction worker, his dependent widow should apply for this scheme within one year from the date of death.
 1. Registered construction workers with full or 50% disability due to an accident are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. The beneficiary has not availed any financial assistance from any other Government body.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Destitute Elderly and Disabled Person
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
1. Applicant must be:

- Aged 60 years or more and does not have a son aged 21 years or above.
- OR, aged 45 years or more and has a disability of more than 75%.
- OR, listed in the BPL category (Score 0–20) in rural areas or included in the urban BPL list.

1. Annual Family Income must be:

- ₹1,20,000 or less for rural residents.
- ₹1,50,000 or less for urban residents.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Disabled Ex-Servicemen
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be a disabled ex-serviceman.
 1. The applicant should have been discharged from the Armed Forces on medical grounds, with a disability not attributable to military service.
 1. The financial assistance is provided regardless of the applicant's age.
 1. The applicant should not be receiving any type of financial assistance or pension from any source.

**Note 1:** The definition of Ex-Servicemen for the purpose of eligibility for financial assistance shall be effective as notified by the Ministry of Defence Government of India from time to time.
**Note 2:** Every applicant shall have to produce a certificate issued by the concerned District Social Welfare Officer to the effect that the applicant is not getting any financial assistance Pension from the District Social Welfare Department of Haryana.
**Note 3:** If any beneficiary receiving financial assistance under this scheme, after the sanction of financial assistance at any time is found getting old age pension, financial assistance or maintenance allowance from any other Department of Haryana Govt. Beneficiary shall be permanently debarred from getting the financial assistance under this scheme run by this department
**Note 4:** Old eligible beneficiaries whose cases for grant of financial assistance have already been sanctioned prior to issue of these guidelines shall continue to get the financial assistance at enhanced rates and they need n

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Dairy Farmers Belonging To Scheduled Tribes And Scheduled Caste For Purchase Of Basic Dairy Equipments
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of Goa for at least five preceding years.
- The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe or Scheduled Caste Community.
- The applicant must be a Dairy Farmer.
- The applicant must have at least two milch animals.
- The two milch animals must be Local Cows, Indigenous cows, Cross Bred cows, Local Buffaloes, or Improved Buffaloes.

scheme_name: Financial Assistive Devices for Persons with Disabilities
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should have a disability of at least 40%.
 1. The age of the applicant should be above 18 years. For persons with mental retardation, the age is relaxed to 14 years instead of the usual 18 years.
 1. The applicant should be employed, either working in a reputable organization or have a confirmed appointment letter.

**Note 1:** The head of the organization should certify that the proposed assistive device will improve the applicant's employability and that the device is not provided by the organization.
**Note 2:** For self-employment, the need for an assistive device will be assessed by the authority (SCA/RRB, etc.) scrutinizing the application and will be recommended accordingly.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Disabled For Self Employment
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Maharashtra State.
1. The applicant should be a Person with Disability (Visually Handicapped, Low Vision, Hearing Impaired, Orthopedically Handicapped, etc).
1. The percentage of the disability should be 40% or above.
1. The applicant should be in the 18 to 50 years age group.
1. The annual income of the applicant should be less than ₹1,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Disabled Students Pursuing (10th, 11th, 12th Equivalent Exams)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Kerala State.
 1. The differently abled students with 40% or more disability are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. Financial assistance will be provided to students falling in Above Poverty Line (APL) as well as Below Poverty Line (BPL) categories.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Dramatic Troupes participating in Kala Academy’s Annual Tiatr Competition
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a dramatic troupe.
- The applicant must be participating in the Tiatr Competitions organized by Kala Academy.
- The applicant must fulfill the conditions laid down in the scheme as per the format enclosed.
- The applicant's Tiatr script will be scrutinized by a scrutiny committee who will submit a report on whether the Tiatr is as per the format.
- The applicant must ensure that one typed copy of the Tiatr script and one typed copy each of the lyrics of the songs are submitted to Tiatr Academy of Goa along with the application form.
- The applicant must ensure that handwritten copies of scripts and lyrics will not be accepted.
- The applicant's Tiatr, in any form either written or verbal, must not contain any adverse remarks against any Government/Individual/Institution, etc., or anything against one’s caste, creed, and religion which may lead to hurting of religious sentiments of anybody.
- The applicant must compulsorily participate in workshops organized by Tiatr Academy of Goa by deputing at least one of the members of the troupe.

scheme_name: Fruit Area Expansion
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a farmer and hold a Udhyan Card.
1. The farmer must either own agricultural land or have land on lease.
1. If the farmer owns land, the maximum land allowed per beneficiary is 4 hectares and the minimum land allowed is 0.02 hectares.
1. The maximum land area allowed under the scheme is 4 hectares.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Ex-Convicts
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native and resident of Kerala State.
1. The applicant should be Ex-Convict (released from jail from poor economic conditions).
1. The applicant should belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education (GBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Worker registered with the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The worker should be between 18 to 60 years of age.
- The worker should have completed ninety days of service as a registered worker in the year.
- The ward of the worker should be pursuing education.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education- Haryana Silicosis Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant/worker should be employed in a factory or at a construction site.
 1. The worker who is suffering from silicosis as confirmed by the Silicosis Diagnosis Board, Haryana are eligible to apply under this scheme for their children.
 1. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Silicosis Board.
 1. The children of workers should be studying in a class ranging from Class I to Master’s Degree.
 1. Assistance will be available for up to two boys and up to three girls of affected workers, regardless of the order of birth of the children.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education of Children of Beneficiary
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker should be registered under the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The worker should hold a valid labour card issued by the MBOCWWB.
1. The worker should be residing in a relief camp due to the violence.
1. Only for families affected by the 03.05.2023 Tribal Solidarity March.
1. The worker should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
1. The student should have enrolled in Higher Secondary or equivalent/B.A./B.Sc/B.Com/Diploma/Post Graduate Degree/Diploma Course/Engineering or Medical Degree.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for the Education of Children of the Beneficiary (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of Odisha.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be a registered member of the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 - The member should be registered for a period of one year or more under the board.
 - The children should be studying in class VI and above.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education of Children of Registered Construction Worker
eligibility_text_source:
1. The children's parents or guardians must be registered construction workers under the Building & Other Construction Workers’ Welfare Board
1. The scholarships are awarded for all recognized Pre-Matric and Post-Matric courses in recognized institutions, except courses of 6 months or less.
1. The students studying in the same stage of education in different subjects (e.g., Class XI Arts after Class XI Science) are not eligible.
1. The student who completed one professional stream and is pursuing another (e.g., LLB after B.Ed.) is not eligible. The students will be allowed to take up only one professional stream.
1. The scholarship shall be tenable for the prescribed duration of a course. A candidate who receives the scholarship but fails to pass in a particular year shall not receive further scholarship till he/she passes the specific year of the course.
1. The student should maintain 50% of the annual attendance in the class.
1. Only two children of the same parents/guardians are eligible for scholarships.
1. The registered construction workers must have no dues payable to the Board.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Education Of Children Of Registered Worker (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker should be registered under Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCWWB).
 1. The registered worker should have a minimum of one-year regular membership.
 1. The children of registered workers studying in Class-I to Postgraduate are eligible to apply under the scheme. 
 1. Only those students who are studying in any institution/school/college of Haryana state will be eligible for this financial assistance.
 1. The students who are self-employed or employed will not be eligible for benefits under this scheme.

**Note 01:** Financial assistance will not be given again for the same class in case of failure of the student.
**Note 02:** It is mandatory to upload the certificate issued by the head of the school/institution that the student is continuing regular studies in the school/institution.
**Note 03:** The financial assistance for pursuing professional courses shall be paid directly to the Government Institutions except for the reimbursement of admission fees.
**Note 04:** The claim application of the students studying in private institutions shall be verified by any one of the officers i.e. Deputy Labour Commissioners, Assistant Labour Commissioner, Tehsildars, Naib Tehsildars DEEOs, DEOs, BEEOs, and BEOs.
**Note 05:** The financial assistance for education will be given up to three children only i.e. three female or two male children.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for encouraging production of Traditional Khell-Tiatr
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a dramatic troupe or an individual producing Khell-Tiatrs.
- The Khell-Tiatr produced by the applicant should be a new or old script.
- The Khell-Tiatr produced by the applicant must conform to the traditional format of the Khell-Tiatr.
- The Khell-Tiatr produced by the applicant must be one in which just one-story flows from beginning to end with an interval of 10 to 15 minutes.
- The Khell-Tiatr produced by the applicant must have 12 to 14 cantos which are related to the theme of the play.
- Out of these cantos, at least 7 must be of an interactive nature.
- The band for the Khell-Tiatr produced by the applicant should include at least two blowing instruments, one keyboard, one bass guitar, and a drum set.
- The applicant must not have any adverse remark made in any form, either written or verbal, against any Government/Individual/Institution, etc.
- The applicant must not have anything against one’s caste, creed, and religion which may lead to hurting of religious sentiments of anybody.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Ex-Servicemen of and above the Age of 60 Years and their Widows
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be an ex-serviceman or widow of an ex-serviceman aged 60 years and above.
 1. Only ex-servicemen of other ranks (i.e., Havildar and below) who are in penury and have an annual income from all sources below ₹2,00,000/- are eligible under the scheme.
 1. The applicant should not be in receipt of any kind of pension, financial assistance, or maintenance allowance from any department of the Government of India/State Government or from anybody/organization controlled by the Central/State Government.
 1. Persons who were discharged from service on the ground of "Service No Longer Required" under rule 13, III (V) due to misconduct & inefficiency are not eligible for financial assistance, and their widows’ are also not eligible for financial assistance as the individual does not fall in the definition of ex-serviceman.
 1. The widows of ex-servicemen as per definition, who were discharged from service with fair character due to inefficiency/misconduct and "Service No Longer Required" due to inefficiency/misconduct are also not eligible for grant of financial assistance.

Note 1: If any beneficiary, after the date of sanction of the financial assistance, is found drawing old age pension or any other financial assistance from any other Government source. He/she shall be permanently debarred for the grant of financial assistance under all the welfare schemes run by the Rajya Zila Sainik Boards in Haryana

scheme_name: Flower Area Expansion
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a farmer and hold a Udhyan Card.
1. The farmer must either own agricultural land or have land on lease.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Encouraging Worker's Children's Participation in Cultural Activities- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The applicant's monthly salary should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
 1. Benefits are provided to children of registered workers for participating in group or solo cultural competitions, and also for securing 1st, 2nd, or 3rd position in these competitions.
 1. There is no limit on the number of workers' children eligible under this scheme.
 1. There is no minimum service period requirement for the worker.

**Note:** It is mandatory to apply within one year from the date of issue of certificate for participation in cultural competitions.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Encouraging Worker's Children's Participation in Sports Activities- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The applicant's monthly salary should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
 1. Benefits are provided to children of registered workers for participating in group or individual sports competitions, and for securing 1st, 2nd, or 3rd place in these competitions.
 1. There is no limit on the number of workers' children eligible under this scheme.
 1. There is no minimum service period requirement for the worker.

**Note:** Application must be submitted within one year of the date of issuance of the certificate of participation in sports activities.

scheme_name: Funeral and Ex-Gratia (K.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
 - The applicant should be a minimum of 18 years of age.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The beneficiary should be a nominee of a deceased registered construction worker Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Cremation (BBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a dependent of the deceased construction worker.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have been registered with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have maintained regular membership with the Board.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education (HPBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
**For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker..
1. The applicant should be aged between 18 and 60 years.
1. The worker should have completed at least 90 days of building and other construction work in the last 12 months.
1. The registration of the applicant should have been renewed annually.

**Eligibility Criteria for Scheme:**

1. The parent of the child must be engaged in building or construction work under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).
1. The parent of the child should be registered with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).
1. The parent's membership with the Himachal Pradesh Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Funeral Expenses (JKBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be the nominee or legal heir of the deceased worker.
 1. The deceased worker must have been registered with the Jammu and Kashmir Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The deceased worker must have been an active member of the Board at the time of death.
 1. The scheme can be applied for only once.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education of Self/ Children of Beneficiaries
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be a registered member or his/ her children Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
 - The applicant should be studying Higher Secondary and above.

scheme_name: COP-34 Financial Assistance to Farmer for Interest Subvention (Kishan Credit Card Interest Asst.)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a farmer.
1. The farmer should have taken a short-term crop loan during the Kharif season (1st April to 30th September) from any of the following:

 - Primary Agricultural Credit Cooperative Society
 - District Central Cooperative Bank
 - Nationalized Bank
 - Gramin Bank
 - Private Bank

1. The farmer should have repaid the loan on time.

scheme_name: Financial assistance for Marriage for the Construction Worker
states: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Marriage (HPBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
***For Registration as a Building/Construction Worker:***

1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a Building or Construction Worker.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have worked in building or construction activities for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months.
1. The registration should be renewed annually.

***Eligibility Criteria for Scheme:***

1. The worker’s membership with the Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.
1. The worker should be unmarried at the time of applying for the financial assistance.
1. The worker should have at least a two month subscription.
1. The benefit is provided for the marriage of up to two children of a registered married worker.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Medical (JKBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker -** 

1. The applicant should be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
1. The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have engaged in any building or other construction work for at least 90 days during the preceding 12 months.
1. The applicant should be registration is renewed annually.

> **For Application to this Scheme -** 

1. The applicant must be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be suffering from an injury, a disability, or from a chronic disease.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Natural Death
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be the legal heir of the deceased construction worker.
1. The deceased construction worker should be registered under the Maharashtra Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board.
1. The death of the deceased worker must be due to natural death.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for funeral and other activities in case of death of a worker's - Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be fully dependent on the deceased worker.
1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
1. The deceased worker should have had an active membership under the Haryana Labour Welfare Board at the time of death.
1. The applicant should not have previously availed benefits under this scheme.
1. There is no minimum service period or ceiling required under this scheme.
1. The application should be submitted within two years from the date of the worker’s death.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Fishermen For Purchase of Fisheries Requisites (Gill Net/ River Gill Net with Accessories)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
- The applicant should or any of his households should not be defaulters with the Directorate of Fisheries.
- The applicant should possess their own registered fishing canoe.
- The original VAT bill may be submitted by the applicant from the authorized dealer to claim the subsidy.
- The applicant shall not sell, lease, or transfer the Gill Net for five years from the date of purchase of the first Gill Net.
- The fishing net shall be duly registered with the Directorate of Fisheries.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Purchase Of Stationery To SC/ST/OBC/Minorities Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant should belong to SC/ST/OBC/Minorities and in support thereof they are required to upload SC/ST/OBC/Minorities certificate issued by concerned district office Delhi.
1. Application should be resident of Delhi and in support thereof he/she is required to upload a supporting document (Domicile).
1. The applicant should be studying in Class 1 to 12 in a government/aided/recognized public school.
1. Student may not opt any centrally sponsored scholarship scheme with the state funded scheme.
1. Have an annual family income not exceeding INR 2 lakh.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Former Sports Person of UP
eligibility_text_source:
1. The sportsperson should be a domicile of Uttar Pradesh.
 1. The sports persons who are old, weak, or facing hardships and whose monthly income from all sources is not more than ₹10,000/- are eligible to apply under the scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Tagore Cultural Complexes (TCC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The organization must be registered as a society under the Registration of Societies Act (XXI of 1860) or under a similar Act, or as a Trust or Not-for-Profit Company for at least three years.
1. The organization’s charter must be primarily devoted to the preservation, propagation, and promotion of Indian arts and culture.
1. The organization must have a predominantly cultural profile and should have been actively working in fields such as dance, drama, theatre, music, fine arts, Indology, and literature for at least three years.
1. The organization must be well established and recognized for doing meaningful work in its field of activity.
1. The organization must have gained a local, regional, or national identity and reputation/standing.
1. The organization must be capable of setting up and operating the project effectively.
1. The organization must be able to arrange 40% of the project cost (for Non-North Eastern Region projects) or 10% (for North Eastern Region projects) as its matching share.
1. The organization must be capable of meeting the recurring costs of the project.
