1. The organization must have been inspected and recommended by an appropriate agency of the Central Government or the concerned State Government/UT Administration.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance for the Construction Worker
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of Sikkim.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have served the Board for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - Should be a nominee/ dependent of the registered worker of the Sikkim Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 - The nominees/ dependents should have lost any registered member/worker of the due to death.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to the Family of Temple Employees
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a family member of the deceased employee of the temple.
- The temple employee must have died while in service.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to the Fish Vendors Registered with the Directorate of Fisheries to Purchase the Accessories Required for Fish Vending
eligibility_text_source: The applicant should be a Fish Vendor registered with the Department of Fisheries, Government of Goa.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Fresh Water Aquaculture Farms
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Fish Farmer/ Individual/ Registered Self Help Groups/ Societies/ Associations, interested in freshwater fish/prawn farming in the State of Goa.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa for the last fifteen years.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Girl Child Attend Her Age of 18
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a registered construction worker in Maharashtra.
1. The worker or their spouse must have undergone contraceptive surgery after the birth of one female child.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Gad-Hanjis
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a Gad-Hanji (Fisherman) licensed by the Fisheries Department consecutively for the last 3 years.
1. The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹ 3,600/-.
1. The applicant should not have received assistance under this scheme during the last 2 years.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a nominee/legal heir of the deceased worker.
 1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered deceased worker should have held a regular membership with the Board.
 1. The scheme allows for application only once.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Hostel Facility in Professional/Technical Institutions (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be engaged in building or construction work.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered worker should have a minimum of one year of regular membership.
 1. The worker should have an active membership with the Board.
 1. The children of construction workers who stay in hostels while pursuing regular technical/professional courses are eligible for benefits under this scheme.
 1. Financial assistance for hostel facilities will be available for up to three children per worker.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance of Hearing Machine/Hearing Aid to Workers and their Dependents- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The monthly salary of the worker should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
 1. The service period of the worker is fixed for one year.
 1. The worker will get the benefit after every five years in the whole service period.
 1. Workers and their dependents are eligible to receive assistance if they lose their hearing due to an accident or any other reason.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Higher Studies
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam.
- The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.
- The applicant must be pursuing higher studies such as Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS), Master of Business Administration (MBA), Engineering, and such other technical/higher education.
- The applicant's family income must not exceed ₹5,00,000/- per annum.
- The applicant should not be a Government Service holder.
- Students studying through correspondence course are also eligible to apply.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Inter-Caste Married Couples (Except SC/ST Category)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Kerala State.
 1. The inter-caste married couples who are legally married are eligible for financial assistance.
 1. The combined family income of the couple should not exceed ₹1,00,000/-.
 1. The couples are entitled to financial assistance one-time only.
 1. The couples must apply for financial assistance after one year of marriage but not exceeding 2 years from the date of marriage.

**Note 01:** The financial assistance should be utilized for purposes like starting a business, for the purchase of land, or for the construction of a house. The Local Self Government Institutions (LSGIs) should ensure that funds are being utilized for the said purposes.
**Note 02:** While receiving financial assistance, couples will be required to sign an agreement and submit it to the LSGIs.

scheme_name: Farmers Accidental Insurance Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant or their parent must have a registered land record before the date of the accident.
 - The death or disability must be caused by an accident.
 - The deceased/disabled person must be the registered farmer, their spouse, or any legal heir (son/daughter).
 - The applicant must possess a valid driving license in case of death/injury (For vehicular accident).
 - The beneficiary must be between 5 to 70 years of age (For accidental death or permanent disability).
 - Application should be made in the office of the concerned District Agriculture Officer within 150 days.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Institutions/Societies Belonging To Scheduled Castes/Backward Classes
eligibility_text_source:
1. Only those institutions/ societies of Scheduled Castes/ Backward classes registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860, or the Indian Trust Act would be eligible for grant-in-aid.
 1. The society/ institution should have at least 100 sq yards of its own land or allotted /donated to it by the Govt./ Panchayat/ Local Body/ any individual etc.
 1. Second-time grants to the same institution will be given after only five years if all the UCs for the grants released earlier have been submitted to the District Welfare officer.

**Note 01:** The institution/ society will maintain separate accounts for the grant which shall be open for inspection by the Director, Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes Department, Haryana/concerned District Welfare Officer, or any other officer deputed by the competent authority.
**Note 02:** Utilization certificate duly signed by the Head of the institution/ society dully verified by the Chartered Accountant will be furnished to the District Welfare Officer, who after spot inspection will forward the same to the Director, Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes, Haryana with his comments for record.
**Note 03:** The guarantee will have to furnish an indemnity bond in the prescribed format (ANNEXURE – B) to the Govt. binding itself to fulfill all the conditions attached to the grant and to refund the amount of the grant already paid in the event of non-fulfillment of the aim to abide by the decision of the authority releas

scheme_name: Financial Assistance as Interest Subsidy on Home Loan
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a war widow, war bereaved family member, war disabled personnel, or attributable peacetime casualty (all ranks).
1. The applicant must have taken a home loan from a bank, government/public sector undertaking, LIC, GIC, or HUDCO.
1. The loan amount considered for subsidy must not exceed ₹1,00,000, regardless of the actual loan amount.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Industrial Parks: Assistance on Fixed Capital Investment
eligibility_text_source: - Industries Association / any enterprise registered under the Societies Act, Partnership Act, Trust Act, Companies Act or SPV constituted for setting up of Industrial Park shall be eligible to develop Industrial Park under this scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Industrial Parks: Infrastructure Facilities
eligibility_text_source: - Industries Association / any enterprise registered under the Societies Act, Partnership Act, Trust Act, Companies Act or SPV constituted for setting up of Industrial Park shall be eligible to develop Industrial Park under this scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Industrial Parks: Stamp Duty
eligibility_text_source: - Industries Association / any enterprise registered under the Societies Act, Partnership Act, Trust Act, Companies Act or SPV constituted for setting up of Industrial Park shall be eligible to develop Industrial Park under this scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Kashmiri Migrants Families Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant family should have been displaced from Jammu & Kashmir.
 - The applicant family should have migrated to Haryana from Jammu & Kashmir.
 - The applicant family should be residing in Haryana.
 - The annual income of the family, from all sources, should not exceed ₹2,00,000/-.

 - The term ‘Family’ would mean 'the family of the applicant at the time of displacement from Jammu & Kashmir'.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance (Kanyadaan) on the Occasion of Marriage of Daughter- Haryana Silicosis Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant/worker should be employed in a factory or at a construction site.
 1. The worker who is suffering from silicosis as confirmed by the Silicosis Diagnosis Board, Haryana can avail the benefits under this scheme for the marriage of their daughter.
 1. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Silicosis Board.
 1. The assistance is available for the marriage of up to three daughters of the worker only.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Launderers and Barbers
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of Puducherry. 
- The applicant must be a Launderer or Barber.
- The applicant must have a minimum of three years' experience.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Marriage (GBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Worker registered with the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The worker should have a continuous membership with the Board for 3 years.
- The scheme also applies to the female beneficiary for her own marriage.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Marriage (APB&OCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Arunachal Pradesh.
2. The applicant must be employed in the Building and Other Construction works.
3. The applicant should be registered with Labour Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).
4. The applicant’s membership must be active at the time of application for availing marriage assistance of his/her children.
5. The age of applicant’s children should be above 18 and 21 years of age for female and male respectively.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Marriage (BBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker should be engaged in building or construction work.
 1. The worker should be registered with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The worker should have maintained continuous membership with the Board for at least three years.
 1. The worker's membership should be active at the time of application.
 1. Both male and female registered workers can avail benefits for the marriage of their two adult daughters, while female workers are also eligible for assistance for their own marriage.
 1. Workers who remarry are not eligible for this scheme.

Note: This financial assistance is in addition to benefits provided under the “Antarjatiye Vivah Protsahan Anudan Yojana”.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance On Marriage Of Children (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker should be registered under Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCWWB).
2. The registered worker should have a minimum of one-year regular membership.
3. The worker should have the attested marriage card of his/her son/daughter. 
**Note 01:** The worker will write in writing that he/she will submit the certificate of marriage in the office of the concerned Assistant Director within a period of six months otherwise he/she will not be eligible for any benefit under any welfare scheme in the future.
**Note 02:** The worker shall submit an undertaking/self-declaration that he/she has not received this assistance from any other Government Department/Board/Corporation and will not do so.
**Note 03:** The financial assistance shall be payable up to the marriage of two children but extendable up to the marriage of three daughters.
**Note 04:** The marriage card should be attested by any one of these authorities i.e. State Government Gazetted Officer/Assistant Labour Commissioner/Labour Inspector/Secretary Gram Panchayat/Panchayat Adhikari/ BDPO/ DDPO/ Naib Tehsildar/ Tehsildar/Canongo/Patwari/Assistant Director, Industrial Safety & Health/SDO and Government Department or Board or Municipal Committee/ Junior Engineer of Municipal Corporation / Municipal Council and Head of Government School (Principal / Head Master / Administrators)

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Mussel Culture and Oyster Farming in Goa
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Fish Farmer/ Individual/ Registered Self Help Groups/ Societies/ Associations, interested in setting up the Mussel Culture/ Oyster Farming Unit in Goa.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa for the last fifteen years.
- The applicant should have undergone a training programme on Mussel Culture/ Oyster Farming organized by the Fisheries Department/ MPEDA/ ICAR/ any other institute.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Miscarriage for the Construction Workers
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration:**

- The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
- The applicant should be a Building/Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have served the Board for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For Application:**

- The applicant should be a registered women member Under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

Or,
The applicant should be a wife of registered male member Under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

- The applicant should have suffered a miscarriage during the delivery.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For The Marriage Of Daughters Of Poor Widows And Orphan Girls
eligibility_text_source:
1. Financial assistance can be granted for performing marriage up to two daughters only. 
1. Residence of a minimum of 5 years in Delhi before the date of application. 
1. The income of the applicant should be less than ₹ 1,00,000/-per year from all sources (including rent, interest/dividends on savings & investments, earnings from the farm, property sale proceeds, etc.) of the applicant. 
1. Applicant should have an Aadhar number 
1. The Applicant should have a single-operated Aadhaar-linked account with any bank in the NCT of Delhi only. 
1. The applicant should not be in receipt of any assistance from the discretionary fund of the Lt. Governor or the Chief Minister of Delhi for the same purpose. 
1. The girl whose marriage is to be solemnized should be major on the date of marriage i.e. above 18 years of age. 
1. The application must be submitted within a period of sixty days, before or after the date of the marriage being solemnized.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Marriage of Poor Scheduled Caste Brides
eligibility_text_source:
- The Parent/Guardian of the bride should be a citizen of India.
- The Parent/Guardian of the bride should be from a Scheduled Caste.
- The Parent/Guardian of the bride should be a native of Puducherry.
- The assistance is provided for the first marriage alone.
- The marriage should be registered.
- The assistance is available only for two daughters in a family.
- The application should be submitted 30 days prior to the date of marriage.
- The annual income of the parent/guardian should not exceed ₹2,00,000.
- The applicant should not be receiving financial assistance from other sources.
- The marriage should be performed in accordance with the law.
- The bride should have attained the age of 18 years.
- The bridegroom should have attained the age of 21 years.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance on Marriage of Sons- Haryana Silicosis Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant/worker should be employed in a factory or at a construction site.
 1. The worker who is suffering from silicosis as confirmed by the Silicosis Diagnosis Board, Haryana can avail the benefits under this scheme for the marriage of their sons.
 1. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Silicosis Board.
 1. The assistance is available for the marriage of up to two sons of the worker only.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Medical Treatment of Journalists
eligibility_text_source:
1. The petitioner should be working as a full-time working journalist.
1. The petitioner should have work experience of at least 5 years. 
1. The petitioner must apply within three months of undergoing medical treatment.
1. The annual income shall not be taken into account for obtaining medical assistance funds for journalists.
1. The treatment must be conducted in hospitals recognized by the Tamil Nadu State Hospital Organization.
1. The retired journalist receiving government pension are eligible to apply but they will receive 50% of the financial assistance.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Marriage for the Construction Workers
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration**

 - The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
 - The applicant should be a Building/Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have served the Board for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For Application**

 - Registered women member Under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

Or,
Wives of registered male members Under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance in Marriage of Women Workers or Daughters of Workers- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The monthly salary of the applicant should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
 1. The applicant must have a minimum of three years of regular membership of the Board till the Marriage date.
 1. The applicant should apply within six months after the marriage date.
 1. The benefits are provided to workers for the marriage of up to three daughters and for the self-marriage of female workers.

**Note:** The worker will give an undertaking that he will clearly explain to the Board whether he/she has availed the benefits of the Kanyadaan Scheme for the first time, second time or third time or never before.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Natural Disaster
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
2. The applicant or their family must have been affected by a natural disaster.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Normal Delivery and Surgical Delivery
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a registered women construction worker under the MBOCWW or the wife of a registered male worker.
1. The applicant should be Pregnant.
1. Benefit is available for the first two deliveries only.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Non-Pensioner World War-II Veterans and Their Widows
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant must be a non-pensioner World War-II veteran who served between 1 September 1939 and 14 August 1945, or his lawful widow.
1. The veteran must have been discharged from military service due to demobilization after the end of World War-II.
1. The applicant must not be receiving any pension from defense services or any other source.
1. In case of the veteran’s death, only the lawful widow is eligible for the benefit.
1. Upon the death of the widow, no other heir (children or relatives) becomes eligible.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Non-School Going Disabled Children Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. A person having a physical disability in the age group between 0-18 years.
2. Child is not able to attend formal education, training, etc. due to this disability.
3. Domicile of Haryana state and should be residing in Haryana State for the last three years at the time of submission of application.
4. Medical Certificate issued by the Civil Surgeon having the following disabilities:-
• Mental Retardation with I.Q. not exceeding 50 or a percentage of disability is 70% and above.
• Persons with cerebral palsy.
• Persons with Autism.
• Multiple disabilities with a total permanent disability of 70% or more disabilities as defined in clause (i) of Section 2 of the Persons with Disabilities, Protection of Rights and Full Participation) Act, 1995 (1 of 1996).
• Case of orthopedic disability with permanent disability of 100% which are further clarified as under:-
 a. Myhopathy
 b. Paraplegic
 c. Quadri Plegia
5. The applicant’s close relatives and parents are not in a financial position to support him/her and the income of his/her parents, close relatives or his/her own income from all sources is less than the income as prescribed under the Minimum Wages. He/She will have to submit proof of income verified by the competent authority.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be 60 years or above in age.
- The applicant should be a Old Age Pensioner or a Destitute Pensioner.
- The applicant should be suffering from T.B./Cancer.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Orphan Children of Ex-Servicemen
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be an orphan child of an ex-serviceman.
 1. The applicant should not be receiving any kind of financial assistance from any department of the Haryana Government.
 1. The applicant's age should be below 21 years.
 1. The applicant should be unmarried.
 1. The applicant should be unemployed.

**Note 1:** The definition of Ex-Servicemen for the purpose of the eligibility of grant of Financial Assistance to shall be effective as adopted by the Ministry of Defence Government of India from time to time.
**Note 2:** Details of orphan children of ESM along with their date of birth as issued by Unit Record Office of the Ex-servicemen will have to be submitted by the applicant while applying for Financial Assistance.
**Note 3:** The orphan children of ESM who are in receipt of ordinary family pension from Govt. of India are also eligible for financial assistance under this scheme.
**Note 4:** Old beneficiaries, (orphan children) whose cases for grant of Financial Assistance have already been sanctioned prior to issue of these guidelines shall continue to get the Financial Assistance at enhanced rates and they need not apply afresh.
**Note 5:** The payment shall be discontinued with immediate effect to the orphan children on their attaining the age of 21, or till he/she gets employment or till he/she gets married whichever is earlier.
**Note 6:** Payment of Financial Assistance to those beneficiaries sha

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to OBC Students Appearing in the Mains Examination of UPSC
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must belong to a Other Backward Class (OBC) community of Assam.

- The applicant must have recently qualified for the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) Preliminary Examination held in 2025.
- The annual income of the applicant's parents/guardians must not exceed ₹5,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Scheme for the Welfare of Schedule Caste Families in Fisheries Sector: Subsidy on Annual Auction of Notified Waters
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant should possess a valid Family ID Card (Parivar Pehchan Patra).
1. The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Caste category.
1. The applicant's age should be 18 years or above.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance on the Natural Death of Differently Abled Person
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be the legal heir of the differently abled person.
1. The differently-abled person should have died a natural death.
1. The differently abled person should have been a registered member of the "Welfare Board for the Differently-abled".

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Barrier Net and its Accessories
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
- The applicant should have purchased a Barrier Net and its Accessories.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Battery Operated Vehicles
eligibility_text_source: The applicant must be a resident of Puducherry.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Purchase/Construct New Fishing Craft less than 26 feet for Fishing in Inland Waters of Goa
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
- The Fishing Canoe should be registered with the Department of Captain of Ports, Government of Goa.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Existing Assets
eligibility_text_source: The scheme is applicable to the units which are in existence for a minimum period of 2 years with good track record.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Fuel (Petrol) to the Fishermen for Operation of Out Board Motors
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
1. The applicant's fishing canoe should be registered under the Merchant Shipping Act, of 1958 and the rules framed there under.
1. The applicant should be a member of the Registered Fisheries Co-operative Societies/ Association or Individual.
1. The applicant should not be a defaulter of the Directorate of Fisheries.
1. The applicant should not be a violator of the Goa Daman & Diu Marine Fishing Regulation Act,1980 (Act No.3 of 1981).
1. Petrol OBM (Out Board Motors) having 10HP or below should be incorporated on the Fishing Vessel/Canoe.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance/Pension to Former Renowned Sportspersons
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant should be an ex-sportsperson. 
1. The applicant must be above 50 years of age.
1. The applicant should not be currently employed.
1. The applicant’s monthly income from all private sources should not exceed ₹20,000/-.
1. The applicant should be a former medalist or participant in:

- Olympic Games, World Cup/Championship
- Asian Games, Commonwealth Games
- Afro-Asian Games, SAF Games
- Para-Olympic/Special Olympic (Mental/Physical Disabilities)
- World Marathon (Disabled), International Veteran (Masters) Championships

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Promotion Of Guru-Shishya Parampara (Repertory Grant)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The arts activities performing organizations having dramatic groups, theatre groups, music ensembles, and children's theatre are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. The group ensembles to be assisted with the scheme will be expected to have a repertoire of adequate number and quality and should have given performances on an all-India basis.
 1. Organizations will be eligible to obtain only one grant in a financial year.

**Note 01:** The Financial Assistance for Promotion of Guru-Shishya Parampara (Repertory Grant) will be reviewed annually by the Expert Committee set up for the purpose.
**Note 02:** Physical verification would be compulsory for the continuation of Financial Assistance for Promotion of Guru-Shishya Parampara (Repertory Grant) after every 4th (fourth) year.
**Note 03:** Those grantees that are getting financial assistance under the scheme, would be recommended for renewal of the scheme only when they stage at least two productions during the financial year. Out of these two, at least one production must be a new production i.e. which has not been staged earlier.
**Note 04:** Financial assistance under the scheme will be disbursed in one installment on fulfillment of the following conditions at the time of proposal for renewal of grant:

 - The Organisations granted financial assistance would mandatorily organize at least two cultural activities (function, lecture, seminar, workshop, exhibition, etc.) in any of the schools in their vicinity. A certif

scheme_name: Financial Aid For Providing Hostel Facility To Transgender Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be Transgender.
2. The applicant should be a resident of Kerala.
3. Transgender students pursuing courses in Government / Aided /Un-aided / Self-financing institutions can apply under this scheme.
**Note:** During the commencement of each academic year the students have to submit a new application for getting the benefit of this scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for the Purchase of Lifejackets and Lifebuoys for Fishermen
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
- The applicant should possess one of the following: a Canoe Fitted With OBM; Mechanised Trawlers Engaged in Trawling or Purse Seining Operations.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Physically/Mentally Disabled Children of Beneficiary (CBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be working in the Establishment, registered under the Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965 and his contribution towards the labour welfare fund should regularly been received by the Board.
 1. Registered construction workers whose child has a disability of 40% or more are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. This benefit is for a maximum of two children.
 1. The applicant shall be eligible to avail the benefit of the scheme only after 06 months of becoming a member of the Board.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Poor & Needy Widow Women
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a domicile of the state of Manipur.
1. The applicant should be a Widow.
1. The age of applicant should be between 18-40 years.
1. The applicant should belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) household.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Para/Tetra/Hemi Plegic Ex-Servicemen
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be an Ex-Serviceman with paraplegia, tetraplegia, or hemiplegia.
 1. In the case of Paraplegic, Tetraplegic, and Hemiplegic ex-servicemen of Haryana State financial assistance will be admissible to only those who are not fit to resettle in civil life on account of total incapacitation (100% disabled) and are penury.
 1. In the case of a Hemiplegic Ex-Serviceman of Haryana State financial assistance will be admissible to only those who after 6 months of the attack of the deceased, do not recover and who are also declared 100% disabled on account of Hemiplegic disease by the Medical authorities even after 6 months of the attack of the disease.
 1. The financial assistance will not be admissible to those paraplegia, tetraplegia, or hemiplegia ex-servicemen who are admitted in paraplegic homes run by the Government of India or the State Government and are covered by grants to paraplegic homes.
 1. The applicant should not be in receipt of any kind of financial assistance from any department of the Haryana Government.

**Note 1:** The financial assistance is admissible to eligible paraplegia, tetraplegia, or hemiplegia ex-servicemen in addition to any disability pension from Govt. of India, Ministry of Defence.
**Note 2:** The old beneficiaries whose cases for grant of financial assistance have already been sanctioned prior to the issue of the guidelines shall continue to get the financial assistan

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Pre-primary School In Remote Areas
eligibility_text_source:
1. There should not be a pre-primary school already existing in the locality.
1. The school should have at least ten (10) Scheduled Tribe (ST) students in a class of twenty (20) students, to avail the benefit of one post of teacher and one post of Helper.
1. Educational Qualification/Training for the Trained Teacher will be as per the eligibility requirements fixed by the Education Department for Primary Teachers/Pre-Primary Teachers. 
1. In case no trained teacher is available in that particular locality, the Government may consider the appointment of an untrained teacher. The untrained teacher shall obtain the necessary qualification within a period of 3 years.

> **Agencies/ Individuals**

1. Existing Primary Schools including Government Primary schools/ High Schools from the locality.
2. NGO with three years of experience in the line of managing pre-primary schools.
3. Trained teacher having experience in teaching pre-primary students.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Permanent Residents of Uttarakhand for Religious Pilgrimages
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must have successfully completed the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra organized by the Government of India.
1. The applicant must have undertaken the journey through the Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Poor Scheduled Caste People Suffering From Prolonged Diseases
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should have resided continuously in the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least five years.
- The applicant should be from Scheduled Caste.
- The applicant should be at least 18 years of age.
- The annual income of the applicant should not be more than ₹2,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Spectacles, Denture, Hearing Aid (CBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be working in the Establishment, registered under the Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965 and his contribution towards the labour welfare fund should regularly been received by the Board.
 1. The applicant shall be eligible to avail the benefit of the scheme only after 06 months of becoming a member of the Board.
 1. The financial assistance will be given on the prescription of the Government Medical Officer/Chief Medical Officer/Principal Medical Officer of any Government Institute in tri-city and once in a lifetime.
 1. The beneficiary should apply for the benefit of this scheme within one year, with all required documents. 
 1. The financial assistance for the purchase of spectacles, Denture, and hearing aid shall not be given to those beneficiaries who are covered under ESIC.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Private Self-Financed Technical Institutions
eligibility_text_source:
1. The institution should be a registered private self-financed engineering or polytechnic institute in Jharkhand.
1. The institution should have been operational for at least 5 years with audited financial statements.
1. The institution should have AICTE approval and affiliation with the State Board of Technical Education, Jharkhand or a recognized university.
1. The institution must comply with AICTE norms, including having qualified faculty and proper infrastructure.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for the Purchase of Sewing Machine (CBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be working in the Establishment, registered under the Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965 and his contribution towards labour welfare fund should regularly been received by the Board.
 1. Financial assistance will be given to the spouse of a registered male construction worker or female construction worker, once in a lifetime.
 1. This financial assistance will be given on presenting original bills of purchase, duly verified by the employer.
 1. The applicant shall be eligible to avail the benefit of the scheme only after 06 months of becoming a member of the Board.
 1. The applicant should apply for financial assistance for the purchase of a sewing machine within one year.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Stake Net and its Accessories
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
- The applicant should have purchased a Stake Net and its Accessories.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Purchase Singel Net / Small Rampon Net & Its Accessories
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
- The Fishermen should belong to the OBC (Kharvi) Community.
- The grandfather/grandmother of the fishermen should be resident(s) of Goa.
- The fishermen shall not possess registered fishing canoes or fishing trawlers.
- Only one household family member can avail of the benefit under this scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Persons Suffering from Rare Diseases
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a domicile and bonafide resident of Haryana.
 1. There are no age restrictions for applicants.
 1. The applicant's annual family income should be less than ₹3,00,000/-.
 1. The applicant must possess a Parivar Pehchan Patra.
 1. The applicant should be suffering from any of the Rare Diseases i.e. the medical conditions/diseases, mentioned in the National Policy for Rare Diseases, 2021, issued by MoHFW (GoI) & added from time to time; and diagnosed at any of the notified ‘Centres of Excellence’ (CoE), designated by MoHFW, GoI & added from time to time; under the said policy.

**Note:** The financial assistance will be provided in addition to any benefits the applicant receives under other social security pension schemes.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchasing School Uniforms, Books and Notebooks from Class 1st to 12th for Children of Workers- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The monthly salary of the worker should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
 1. The service period of the workers is fixed for two years.
 1. The worker's children studying in Class I to Class XII are eligible for the scheme's benefits at the time of admission to continue their studies.
 1. The scheme benefits are available to three girls and two boys per worker.
 1. The name of the child/children should be mentioned on the Ration Card/ESI Card of the worker.

**Note 01:** The last date for submission of applications in the respective sessions has been fixed as 31st December. Applications submitted after 31st December will not be considered.
**Note 02:** The benefit of the scheme will be made available to the girls as well as boys of the worker.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Professional/Technical Courses (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant/worker should be engaged in building or construction work.
 1. The applicant/worker should be registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered worker should have a minimum of one year of regular membership.
 1. The worker should have an active membership with the Board.
 1. The children of registered workers who are pursuing regular technical/professional courses are eligible for benefits under this scheme.
 1. Financial assistance for education will be available for up to three children per worker.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Preparation of Training Camp and Participation in Land, Mountain & Sea Adventure Sports Competitions
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant should be up to 45 years of age at the time of application.
 1. The applicant should have participated in or completed a training program, workshop, expedition, or competition related to land, sea, or air adventure sports.

scheme_name: Fatal Accident Relief - Telangana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be the nominee/dependant / legal heir of the deceased registered worker. 
1. The deceased must have been a registered building or construction worker with the Telangana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The construction worker should have died on the spot or due to the injuries caused by an accident that occurred either in the work place or anywhere else.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Registered Fishermen Society
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be fisheries co-operative societies/associations registered with the Fisheries Co-operative Societies.
2. The society should produce an audited statement of accounts.
3. The society should employ at least one personnel for the maintenance of records.
4. The society's books of accounts shall be produced before department authorities for inspection when called for.
5. The society should have more than 25 members.

scheme_name: ₹ 5 Lakh Insurance Cover To Farmers
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident farmer of Telangana.
1. The applicant should be aged between 18 and 60 years.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Revive, Preserve and Protect Goan Maand Culture
eligibility_text_source:
1. The Maand group should have a legacy of practising folk art forms for at least the past thirty years at such a traditional Maand place. 
1. The applicants who desire to join training under this scheme shall not be less than 10 years of age. Whereas any old age folk artist in the course of training could share their experience and guide the new generation with kind co-operation from Maand Guru. 

> **Relaxation / Preference / Priority**

Preference will be given to those Goan Maand culture which are on the verge of extinction.

scheme_name: Fishermen Accident Relief Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a legal heir of the deceased fisherman.
 - The deceased must have been an active fisherman from Gujarat.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to the Recipients of the Service Medal by the Government of Madhya Pradesh
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant must belong a soldier category.
1. The applicant must be a service medal awardee.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Renovation And Special Repair Works To Hindu Religious Institutions Situated In Economically Weaker Section Areas / Adi-Dravidar Colonies
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be the concerned temple authority.
- The applicant must represent a temple that is not coming under the purview of the Department of Hindu Religious Institutions.
- The applicant must represent a temple situated in Adi-Dravidar colonies or Economically Weaker Section Areas.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance Scheme (PBAOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be the nominee/dependent of the deceased Construction Worker.
- The Construction Worker should have been a citizen of India.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have been registered as a Construction Worker with the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have completed the age of 18 years.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should not have completed the age of 60 years during the preceding 12 months in the construction work.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have contributed regularly to the fund.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should not have defaulted in payment of contribution to the Board for a continuous period of more than one year.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance Scheme (PULWS)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a nominee of the deceased. 
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have been a resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have been an Unorganized Worker: Taxi and Auto Drivers, Workers Employed in Shops & Catering Establishments, Carpenters, Nathaswara Vidwans, Hair Dressers, Tailors, Cycle Rickshaw Drivers, Cobblers, Washermans, Goldsmith, Blacksmith, Brass Vessel Makers, Sculptors, Loading and Unloading Workers, Tree Climbers, Pottery Workers, Coir Workers, Toddy Neera Tappers, Bullock Cart Drivers, Thatchers, Cooks, Hawkers, News Paper Vendors, Workers Engaged in Bricks and Tiles Manufacturing, One Hundred, Workers Engaged in Distribution of Liquid Petroleum Gas Cylinder, Domestic Workers, Light Music Instrument Players, Automobile Workshop Workers.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have been enrolled with the Puducherry Unorganised Labourers' Welfare Society.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have been between 18 to 59 years of age.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have been taking an Annual Subscription of ₹100/- every year.
- The deceased, in his/her lifetime, should have been paying a Registration Fee of ₹25/-.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance and Grants to Sports Associations/ Clubs/ Sports Committees of Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source: 1. The recognized Sports Associations, Clubs, and Sports Committees in Uttarakhand are eligible to receive financial assistance or grants under this scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Studies (BBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker should be engaged in building or construction work.
2. The worker should be registered with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
3. The registered worker should have a minimum of one year of continuous membership.
4. The worker's membership should be active at the time of application.
5. The children of registered workers should be pursuing or have been admitted to one of the following regular technical or professional courses:
• Admission to prestigious government institutes like IIT, IIM, AIIMS, etc.
• Admission to a B.Tech or equivalent course in a government institution.
• Pursuing a Polytechnic, Nursing, or equivalent diploma course in a government institution.
• Pursuing an ITI or equivalent course in a government institution

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Students who Cleared Civil Services Prelims Conducted by UPSC/APSC
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam and must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.
- The applicant must produce a valid Caste certificate which is duly certified/issued by the District Commissioner of the concerned district or his/her designated officer.
- The applicant should have cleared the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission/Assam Public Service Commission during the current year.
- The applicant must produce his/her copy of the admit card and result of the Civil Services Preliminary Examination conducted by the Union Public Service Commission/Assam Public Service Commission.
- The applicant should not be a Government service holder.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Scheduled Castes for Funeral Rites
eligibility_text_source:
- The deceased should be from a Scheduled Caste.
- The applicant should be from a Scheduled Caste.
- The applicant should be a close relative of the deceased.
- The annual family income should be less than ₹ 2,00,000/-.
- The family members of the deceased should belong to the Union Territory of Puducherry by virtue of nativity or continuous residence of 3 years.

scheme_name: Financial Incentive to SC Meritorious Boy Students
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be currently enrolled as a student.
- The applicant must be male.
- The applicant must belong to a Scheduled Caste (SC) community of Assam.
- The applicant must have secured a minimum of 80% marks in the HSSLC examination held in 2025.
- The annual income of the applicant's parents/guardians must not exceed ₹5,00,000/-.

_*In instances where multiple applicants achieve an identical percentage in the HSSLC examination, preference will be given to those with a higher percentage in the HSLC examination._

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to SC Students Studying in Medical, Engineering and Diploma Course for Purchasing Educational Instrument
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste of Gujarat.
 1. The student should be studying in Medical, Engineering, or Diploma courses.
 1. The annual family income of the student should be up to ₹6,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Self-Employment For Trained Disabled Persons
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should have completed their Vocational Training in a Government or Government Recognized Institution.
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Maharashtra State.
1. The applicant should be a Person with Disability (Visually Handicapped, Low Vision, Hearing Impaired, Orthopedically Handicapped, etc).
1. The percentage of the disability should be 40% or above.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance Scheme for Journalists and Litterateurs - Rajasthan
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should be a journalist or litterateur.
1. The journalist or litterateur should meet one or more of the following criteria:

- Suffering from incurable diseases such as tuberculosis, cancer, leprosy, or requiring treatment for serious conditions like valve replacement, transplantation, angioplasty, bypass surgery, or kidney-related ailments.
- Unemployed due to ill health and without any other source of income.
- Aged 60 years or above and having no other source of income.
- Eligible on any other humanitarian grounds as recommended by the designated Committee.

**Note:** Financial assistance will be given to only one person at a time.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance Scheme for Dependents of Deceased Journalists and Litterateurs - Rajasthan
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a dependent of a deceased journalist or litterateur.
1. The deceased journalist or litterateur should have been a resident of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should fall under one of the following categories:

- Widow of the deceased journalist or litterateur with no other source of income.
- Unemployed son below 28 years of age.
- Unmarried daughter.
- Father or mother of the deceased, provided they have no other source of income.

**Note:** Financial assistance will be given to only one person at a time.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance Scheme (GBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a legal heir of the deceased worker.
 1. The deceased worker should have been engaged in any building or other construction work.
 1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board.
 1. Financial assistance is provided to the legal heir of the deceased worker in the event of their death during the ongoing membership.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance Scheme For Higher Competitive/ Entrance Examination To Scheduled Castes/ Backward Classes Students
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility of students:**

 1. The student should be a domicile of Haryana & must belong to a Scheduled caste/Backward Class.
 1. The annual Income of the parents/guardians Should not exceed ₹2.50 lacs.
 1. The candidate should possess all the requisite qualifications required for the examination.
 1. The candidate will not be permitted to avail of more than two chances for a particular competitive examination.

**Note 01:** The selected candidates shall have to attend all classes. In the event of any student remaining absent for more than 5 days at a time maximum of 15 days during the entire course without any valid reason, benefits of free coaching to him/her shall be discontinued and another candidate shall be taken in his/her place.
**Note 02:** Depending on the budget allocation, the total number of students should be fixed in advance and distributed among the agencies; it should not be left open-ended.
**Note 03:** This scheme will be applicable and available for only those courses which are not covered by any other Scheme for Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes run by other departments.
**Selection of reputed institutions:**

 1. The Director, Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes Department, Haryana will invite proposals from the implementing agencies directly every year.
 1. The proposals received from the implementing agencies will be considered and finalized by a committee consisting of the following: 

 - Chairman: Secretary to Govt. Haryana, Welfa

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Stage III & IV Cancer Patients
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
 1. The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Haryana as per Government instructions issued from time to time.
 1. The applicant should have Parivar Pehchan Patar (PPP) - Family ID.
 1. All Stage III & IV cancer patients will be eligible under the scheme.
 1. Cancer patients whose family income is less than ₹3,00,000/- per annum, except the amount of other Social Security Pension Schemes will be eligible. 

**Note:** The financial assistance provided under this scheme will be in addition to the benefits received by the applicant under any other social security pension scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance as Shagun in Marriage of Man Workers or Son of Workers - Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The monthly salary of the applicant should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
 1. The applicant must have a minimum of three years of regular membership of the Board till the Marriage date.
 1. The applicant should apply within six months after the marriage date.
 1. The benefits are provided to workers for the marriage of up to two sons and for the self-marriage of male workers.

**Note 01:** The workers will give this undertaking in which they will clearly explain to the board about not taking the benefit of the Shagun scheme for the first, second, or third time or never before.
**Note 02:** If a worker wants to avail the benefit of this scheme 03 days before the marriage, then the organization will certify the date of organizing the marriage of the worker's children or unmarried worker and will keep the necessary documents related to organizing the marriage of the workers in its records. The undertaking will be given that within 06 months after the date of marriage, the managers will present the marriage registration certificate before the Board, and if they do not present the said registration certificate before the Board within six months, then they will have to deposit the amount provided to the worker under the said scheme in the board.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The deceased should have been a member of a family registered under the labour cadre schemes operated by the Government of Madhya Pradesh.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Shaurya Medal Recipients by the Government of Madhya Pradesh
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant must be a Shaurya Medal awardee.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Self-Marriage of Registered Women Workers (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a woman and engaged in building or construction work.
 1. The applicants should be registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered woman worker must have a minimum one-year regular membership.
 1. The registered woman worker must have an active membership with the Board.
 1. The registered woman worker shall submit an undertaking/self-declaration that she has not received and will not receive this assistance from any other Government Department/Board/Corporation.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance Scheme for Purchase of Agricultural Land to Scheduled Caste Farmers
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to Scheduled Castes Category.
 1. The applicant should be a native of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant must be an agricultural labourer.
 1. The annual income limit will be ₹6,00,000/- for rural areas and ₹6,00,000/- for urban areas.
 1. Excluding government assistance, only the beneficiary who is able to purchase the land is eligible for the benefit.
 1. Only one member of the family is eligible for the benefit.
 1. Beneficiaries of land acquired with government assistance will be subject to non-sale for 15 years.
 1. Under this scheme, assistance will be available for purchasing agricultural land from government or private individuals.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Sportspersons to Pursue Sports Coaching Courses Conducted by SAI, NSNIS, Patiala as a Part of Skill Development Programme to Improve their Employability
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant belonging to the BPL or Non-BPL category both are eligible.
 1. Sportspersons who have represented the Districts of Odisha, Odisha State, and its Universities in Sports & Games and who are found eligible to undergo the academic courses as laid down by SAI, NSNIS under its eligibility criteria only will be eligible under the scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to SC Students Appearing in the Mains Examination of UPSC
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must belong to a Scheduled Caste (SC) community of Assam.
- The applicant must have recently qualified for the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) Preliminary Examination held in 2025.
- The annual income of the applicant's parents/guardians must not exceed ₹5,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Scheduled Tribe (Plains) Students for Coaching for Getting Admission into Medical/Engineering/IIT/etc.
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe (Plains) community.
- The applicant must be undergoing or have completed coaching (within the last one year) for Medical/Engineering/IIT entrance examinations.
- The applicant must have appeared/passed the 10+2 examination.

_*Selection is based on merit (percentage secured in the Matriculation Examination)._

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to ST Students under "Gagan Bharari Shiksha Yojana"
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Tribe (ST) in the State of Goa.
1. The student should be a regular, full-time student studying in a recognized Institution / University. 
1. The applicant should have availed or should be eligible for Centrally Sponsored Post Matric Scholarship during the current Academic Year.
1. The income of parents/guardians of the applicant should not exceed ₹ 5,00,000 per annum. 

**NOTE:** If a student has to repeat a class, he/she shall not be eligible to get a scholarship for a second time in the same class.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Setting up Crab Culture Unit
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Fish Farmer/ Individual/ Registered Self Help Group/ Societies, interested in the setting up of the Crab Farming Unit in the State of Goa.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Goa for the last fifteen years.
1. The applicant should undergo a training programme on Crab Farming organized by the Fisheries Department/ MPEDA/ ICAR/ Other Institutes.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Setting Up of Cold Storage
eligibility_text_source: The scheme is applicable to the Proprietary/Partnership Concerns, Public/Private Limited Companies, Co-operative Societies, Market Committees, Boards, and Agro Industries Corporations.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for setting up of Ornamental Fish Unit in Goa
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Fish Farmer/ Individual/ Registered Self Help Group/ Societies, interested in setting up of the Ornamental Fish Farming unit in Goa.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa for the last fifteen years.
- The applicant should have adequate water facilities for setting up the unit.
- The applicant should have own/ leased land, adequate to set up an ornamental unit.
- The applicant should have undergone a training programme on Ornamental Fish Farming organized by the Fisheries Department/ MPEDA/ ICAR.

scheme_name: 'Financial Assistance for Setting Up of Sales Emporia by Industrial Associations, Handicrafts Societies and Recognized Institutions' under 'Marketing And Publicity'
eligibility_text_source: The applicant should be an artisan/unit industrialist from Puducherry.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance Scheme for Unorganized Workers (Except Construction Workers)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be an heir of a deceased e-Shram card holder from the unorganized sector.
- The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
- The applicant must apply within 3 (three) months from the date of death of the deceased worker.
- The deceased worker must have between 16 to 70 years of age at the time of death.
- The applicant must have an annual income not exceeding ₹1,20,000/- (One Lakh Twenty Thousand Rupees), if residing in rural areas.
- The applicant must have an annual income not exceeding ₹1,50,000/- (One Lakh Fifty Thousand Rupees), if residing in urban areas.
- Only one heir per deceased worker is eligible to receive the assistance.

> **Priority Order for Heirs**

The legal heirs of the deceased shall be considered in the following order of priority:

1. Husband or Wife.
1. Children (Son/Daughter) - in absence of spouse.
1. Parents - in absence of spouse and children.
1. Grandchildren (Grandson/Granddaughter) - in absence of spouse, children and parents.
1. Unmarried, widowed or abandoned sisters - in absence of spouse, children, parents and grandchildren.
1. Other heirs declared under Indian Succession Act, 1925 - in absence of all above categories.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to 75% or Permanent Disability
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker must be engaged in building or construction work.
1. The worker should be registered under the Maharashtra Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (MBOCWW).
1. The worker should have a 75% or permanent disability.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Authors for Book Publication
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a native or permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be an eminent litterateur, writer, author, or poet.
1. The applicant’s literary work must remain unpublished solely due to financial constraints.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Traditional Artisans (Panchavrutthi Scheme)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to the Vishwakarma community.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Karnataka.
1. The applicant's age should be between 18 to 55 years.
1. The applicant must be engaged in one of the traditional Vishwakarma occupations, including Gold & Silver work, Sculpture, Blacksmith, Woodwork & Handicrafts, or Metallurgy.
1. The applicant or their family members should not have availed of any loan facility from the KVCDC in the previous years.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Treatment of Chronic Diseases (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be registered under the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered worker must have a regular membership for one year.
 1. The workers who are registered and require indoor treatment for chronic diseases such as cancer, tuberculosis (TB), AIDS, etc., are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. The application frequency is limited to once.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Try Cycle to Workers and their Dependents - Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The monthly salary of the worker should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
 1. The service period of the worker is fixed for one year.
 1. The worker will get the benefit after every five years in the whole service period.
 1. Workers or their dependents are eligible to receive benefits under this scheme if they suffer the loss of their legs due to an accident or any other reason.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to dependents in Case of Death of Registered Workers(JKBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a dependent of the deceased worker.
 1. The deceased should have been registered with the "Jammu and Kashmir Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
 1. The deceased should have held an active membership with the Board.
 1. The scheme can be applied only once.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Troupes Going to Mumbai/Poona and Other Parts of the Country & Vice Versa
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be the writer or director of the Tiatr.
2. The applicant must stage the Tiatr as per its traditional format.
3. The applicant's Tiatr must have 6 or 7 acts (podd’dde).
4. The applicant's Tiatr must include the following - 

- At least 4 ‘cantos’ and comedy linked to the story of the play.
- At least 10 ‘Kantaram’.

5. The applicant's 'Kantaram' must include the following - 

- At Least 2 Duets.
- At Least 1 Duo.
- At Least 1 Trio.
- At Least 1 Quartet/quintet.
- At Least 1 Choral Song and Solos.

6. The applicant must not have any adverse remark made in any form (written or verbal) against any Governments, Individuals, Institutions, or anything against caste, creed, and religion in the scripts, lyrics of songs, performances, speeches, etc., which may hurt the sentiments of anybody.
7. All advertisements, press notes, banners, tickets, and handbills must explicitly state that ‘This Tiatr is organized in collaboration with Tiatr Academy of Goa (TAG)’.
8. The applicant's troupe must stage a minimum of 5 shows of the same Tiatr in Mumbai/Poona (if a Goan troupe) or 5 shows in Goa (if a Mumbai/Poona troupe) to qualify for transport support.
9. The applicant's troupe can avail benefit even if only one show is staged in other places like Kolkata, Delhi, Gujarat, Nagpur, Bangalore, Udaipur, etc.
10. The applicant can avail the scheme only twice in a financial year for two different Tiatrs.

> ***Earmarking***

1. For performances at Mandd Sobhann at Mangalore, f

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Helpless Artists
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant must be an artist who has made significant contributions in the field of art and literature.
 1. The applicant must be 60 years of age or above.
 1. The applicant must have a monthly income of less than ₹600/- (₹7,200/- annually).
 1. The applicant must be physically disabled.
 1. The applicant must be in poor economic condition.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Indigent Artistes
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an indigent artist. 
1. The applicant's age should be 50 years and above. 
1. The annual income is less than ₹24,000/-. 
1. The applicant should contribute to music, dance, drama, folk, painting, and sculpture. 
1. The applicant should not be the recipient of any other assistance from any other Organizations.
1. The applicant should be a resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry either by birth or by domicile.
1. The applicant’s wife/children shall be eligible to receive assistance after the death of the indigent artist.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Indigent Tamil Scholars
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an indigent artist. 
1. The applicant's age should be 58 years and above. 
1. The applicant should contribute to Tamil language and literature. 
1. The income of the applicant should not exceed ₹1,500/- per month.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to the Teachers/Lecturers Children who taken loan from Nationalised Banks for studying Medical/Engineering Courses
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant parent should be a teacher/lecturer working in a government or aided institution with life membership of the Welfare Fund. 
1. The student must have passed each year or semester of the course on the first attempt.
1. The student should have studied from 1st to 10th standard in government or aided schools in Karnataka.1. The applicant should have availed educational loan from a nationalized bank for professional education in medicine or engineering.
1. The educational loan should be taken for courses within India.
1. Only one child per family can avail of the financial assistance.
1. Assistance is only available for final-year students in vocational medical, engineering, or postgraduate programs.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Meet the Funeral Expenses of Differently Abled Person
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be the legal heir of the differently abled person.
1. The differently abled person should be deceased.
1. The differently abled person should have been a registered member of the Welfare Board for the Differently Abled".

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Non Governmental Organisations
eligibility_text_source:
1. The school for children with intellectual disability should have functioned for a minimum period of 5 years. 
1. The school should be registered under section 51 of the Rights of Persons with Disability Act,2016.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Newlyweds under Kanyadan Yojana (Vijabhaj)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The bride and the groom must be residents of Maharashtra.
1. The groom should not be less than 21 years of age.
1. The bride should not be less than 18 years of age.
1. The grant is only admissible for the first marriage of the bride and groom, however, it is also admissible to a widow for her second marriage.
1. Either or both of the bride and groom must belong to Scheduled Castes (including Neo-Buddhists), Vimukta Castes, Nomadic Tribes (including Dhangars and Vanjaris), or Special Backward Classes.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Purchase Outboard Motor of 2HP to 5 HP for Fitting to the Fishing Canoe
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession.
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
- The applicant's Fishing Canoe should be registered with the Department of Captain of Ports, Government of Goa.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Pensioners Suffering From HIV and AIDS
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a permanent resident of Jharkhand.
 - The applicant should be suffering from HIV/AIDS.
 - The applicant should receive Antiretroviral Therapy (ART)/Antiretroviral Drugs (ARD) and its benefits from the District AIDS Control Society.
 - The applicant should have a Voter ID card and Aadhar Card (For applicants aged 18 years and above)
 - The applicant or their spouse should not be a beneficiary under any other Social Security pension scheme.
 - The applicant or their spouse should not be retired or permanently employed in Central and State Government or in public sector undertakings.
 - The applicant or their spouse should not be a recipient of any family pension or pension from Central and State Government or from public sector undertakings.
 - Applicant’s family is not an income tax payee.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to SC Students for Pursuing Higher Studies Abroad
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a domicile of Delhi or a bonafide resident of Delhi for at least 5 years.
1. The applicant should be the only child eligible under this scheme.
1. Employed applicant should submit their application through their employer with a No Objection Certificate (NOC).
1. The applicant should not have already completed the same level of education (Master’s/Ph.D.) in India or abroad.
1. Applicants for the Ph.D. Course should have secured at least 55% marks or equivalent grade in relevant Master’s Degree.
1. Applicants for the Master’s Degree Course should have secured at least 55% marks or an equivalent grade in a relevant Bachelor’s Degree.
1. The applicant should be below 30 years old as of the cutoff date.
1. The applicant’s total family income should not exceed ₹8,00,000/- per annum.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Take up Value Addition to Seafood
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be an individual interested in setting up a unit for value-added fisheries products. 
- The applicant should be a resident of Goa for the last 15 years.

> **Reservation/ Relaxation/ Preference/ Priority**

Preference will be given to self-help groups, women entrepreneurs, and youth (who have completed fishermen training courses/short-term training programs conducted by the Directorate of Fisheries).

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Youth Groups
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a Mangal Dal affiliated with the Youth Welfare and PRD Department of Uttarakhand.
1. The members of Mangal Dal must be permanent residents of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant (Mangal Dal) must not have benefited previously under the scheme.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for UPSC/APSC Mains Exam
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribes (ST)/Other Backward Classes (OBC)/More Other Backward Classes (MOBC) community of Assam.
- The applicant must have passed the Assam Public Service Commission (APSC) or Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) Preliminary Examination in the current year.

_*Fulfilling eligibility does not guarantee benefits, as the Selection Committee has the right to reject applications without assigning reasons._

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Widower and Unmarried Persons Scheme : For Unmarried
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a domicile of Haryana or have been residing in the state for at least the last 15 years.
1. The applicant should be unmarried, whether male or female.
1. The applicant should have attained the age of 45 years.
1. The applicant should belong to a family with a verified annual income of up to ₹1.80 lakh.
1. The applicant should possess a Family ID, as verification is based on data from the Haryana Parivar Pehchan Authority (HPPA).

**Other terms and Conditions:**

 1. Any benefits received under the Scheme, by suppressing true information or making wrong claim or the condition for which the pension was granted no longer exists or the beneficiary got married after the grant of benefit, would be recovered as arrears of land revenue with @12% interest per annum.
 1. Financial Assistance shall cease to be payable on the death of a beneficiary. However, the amount of assistance will be paid for full month in which death of the beneficiary occurs (any date of the month) to the nominee.
 1. Financial Assistance under this scheme shall not be liable to attachment under any court of law.
 1. If a person is detected of having more than one bank account under the PFMS, all his bank accounts will be terminated. He/she would become ineligible to receive benefits under any Social Security Scheme of the State in future.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Visually Challenged Teachers
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent teacher in a college. Temporary, contractual, or ad-hoc teachers are not covered under the scheme.
2. The scheme covers teachers with the following nature of blindness:

 - Fully Blind
 - Low Vision

A certificate from a Government Hospital is mandatory to establish the nature of visual impairment.
3. The college where the teacher is employed must be recognized under:

 - Section 2(f) of the UGC Act
 - Section 12(B) of the UGC Act

4. The eligible teacher's details must be included in the consolidated list of visually challenged teachers submitted by the college to the UGC Regional Office/NRCB along with the Twelfth Plan General Development Grant proposal.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Visually Impaired Advocates
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native and resident of Kerala State.
2. The applicant should be a visually impaired Advocate.
3. The applicant should be an advocate practicing in any one or more Courts in Kerala.
4. The combined annual income of the applicant and his/her family from all sources shall not exceed ₹1,00,000/-.
**Note:** A person who becomes a reading assistant to advocates should at least pass the S.S.L.C. examination.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Widow/Dependent in case of Death of a Worker- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be the widow or dependent of a deceased worker.
 1. The deceased worker should have been employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. There is no maximum limit set for the monthly salary of the worker.
 1. There is no fixed service period requirement for the worker.
 1. The application should be submitted within two years from the date of death of the worker.

scheme_name: Widow Daughter Marriage Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The financial assistance can be granted for performing marriage up to two daughters only. 
1. Residence of a minimum of 5 years in Delhi before the date of application. 
1. The income of the applicant should be less than Rs 1,00,000/-Per year from all sources (including rent, interest/dividends on savings & investments, earnings from the farm, property sale proceeds, etc.) of the applicant. 
1. Applicant should have an Aadhar number. 
1. The Applicant should have a single-operated Aadhaar-linked account with any bank in the National Capital Territory (NCT) of Delhi only. 
1. The applicant should not be in receipt of any assistance from the discretionary fund of the Lt. Governor or the Chief Minister of Delhi for the same purpose. 
1. The girl for whose marriage the Financial Assistance is sought, should be major on the date of marriage i.e., above 18 years of age.
1. The application must be submitted within a period of sixty days, before or after the date of marriage being solemnized. 
1. The applicant should not be receiving any pension from Central Govt./State Govt./MCD and/or NDMC or any other source for this purpose.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Women And Girl Acid Attack Victims
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
