- The applicant should be a woman/ girl.
- The applicant should have suffered disfigurement of a part of her body or should have become disabled due to an attack by acid or a similar corrosive substance.
- The acid attack should have happened on or after 2nd May 2011.
- The applicant should have been residing in Haryana for at least 3 years since the acid attack.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Widower and Unmarried Persons Scheme : For Widower
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a domicile of Haryana or have been residing in the state for at least the last 15 years.
1. The applicant should be a widower.
1. The applicant should have attained the age of 40 years.
1. The applicant's verified annual income should be up to ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should possess a Family ID, as verification is based on data from the Haryana Parivar Pehchaan Authority (HPPA).

**Other terms and Conditions:**

 1. Any benefits received under the Scheme, by suppressing true information or making wrong claim or the condition for which the pension was granted no longer exists or the beneficiary got married after the grant of benefit, would be recovered as arrears of land revenue with @12% interest per annum.
 1. Financial Assistance shall cease to be payable on the death of a beneficiary. However, the amount of assistance will be paid for full month in which death of the beneficiary occurs (any date of the month) to the nominee.
 1. Financial Assistance under this scheme shall not be liable to attachment under any court of law.
 1. If a person is detected of having more than one bank account under the PFMS, all his bank accounts will be terminated. He would become ineligible to receive benefits under any Social Security Scheme of the State in future.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Workers with a Visually, Physically or Mentally Disabled Child- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The benefit is provided to the disabled (blind, mentally retarded, mute, and deaf) children with a disability ranging from 70% to 100% who are dependents of registered workers.
 1. There is no maximum limit set for the monthly salary of the worker.
 1. There is no fixed service period requirement for the worker.
 1. The dependent child's name should be listed in the worker's ESI Card/Ration Card.
 1. The dependent child should not have any source of income and should not be married.
 1. The worker should not have already availed of the benefit of this scheme for his/her dependent child in the relevant financial year.

**Note:** The worker is required to submit the living certificates of his/her dependent beneficiary child every year, indicating that the dependent child has no source of income and is unmarried.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to War Widows of Defence Forces
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a bona fide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant must be the widow of Defence Force personnel (Army, Navy, or Air Force) who was killed in action during the declared wars of 1962, 1965, or 1971.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to WW II Veteran and Widows of WW II Veterans
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be an ex-serviceman of the Pre-Independence Era (World War II Veterans) i.e. ex-servicemen enrolled before 31st August 1945 & their widows.
 1. Only World War II Veterans of other ranks (i.e., Havildar and below) who are in penury and have an annual income from all sources below ₹2,00,000/- are eligible under the scheme.
 1. The applicant should not be in receipt of any kind of pension, financial assistance, or maintenance allowance from any department of the Government of India/State Government or from anybody/organization controlled by the Central/State Government.
 1. World War II Veterans and their widows, who have income from any job/profession/business, are not entitled to a grant of financial assistance.

**Note 1:** If any beneficiary, after the date of sanction of the financial assistance, is found drawing old age pension or any other financial assistance from any other Government source. He/she shall be permanently debarred for the grant of financial assistance under all the welfare schemes run by the Rajya Zila Sainik Boards in Haryana.
**Note 2:** Payment of financial assistance to those beneficiaries shall be stopped immediately who fail to submit their survival verification proofs once every year in the month of April from the authorities.
**Note 3:** Old ex-serviceman & widow beneficiaries, whose cases for grant of financial assistance have already been sanctioned prior to issue

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Widow or Widower of Registered Construction Worker
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be the widow or widower of a deceased construction worker.
1. The deceased worker must have been registered with the Maharashtra Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Working Women Workers or Wives of Male Workers for Maternity- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
- The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
- The monthly salary of the applicant should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
- The service period of the workers is fixed for one year.
- Application has to be made within 1 year from the date of birth of the child.
- The benefit is provided to women workers and wives of male workers for the delivery of up to two children (three girls or two boys).

scheme_name: Food Bill Assistance for Medical & Engineering Students of Nomadic Tribes & Denotified Tribes
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should be a student.
1. The applicant should belong to a Notified Tribe or De‑Notified Tribe (NTDNT) community of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should be pursuing a Medical or Engineering degree course within the state of Gujarat.
1. The applicant’s annual family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Food Bill Assistant for Medical & Engineering Students of SEBC
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student.
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant must belong to the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC).

scheme_name: Fund of Funds for Startups
eligibility_text_source:
**Under FFS support is provided to AIFs. Below are the key eligibility criteria for applying for contribution under FFS**
• Funds are registered as either Category I or II Alternative Investment Funds (AIF) with Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) or have applied for the same with SEBI.
• Funds with corpus of less than ₹1,000 crores.
• Fund`s investment strategy to include investment in startups (Startup as defined as per the Gazette Notification G.S.R.127(E) dt. 19/02/2019 issued by Government of India and as amended from time to time).
• Fund to agree to invest at least twice the amount of contribution sought from FFS into Startups. (For instance, if contribution sought is ₹100 crore under FFS, Fund should invest at least ₹ 200 crore in Startups).
• Fund Manager / team should have prior track record in Fund management or prior investment experience. All the Key members of the Fund should have CIBIL score more than 650 (Key member is defined as Director, Sponsor and Senior Personnel of the Fund).
• All the Key members should be legally eligible to be a Director or in a Managerial position under Indian law (i.e., not barred to be a Director or in a Managerial position under Indian Law).
• Startup should be up to 10 years old from the date of incorporation. 
• Must be DPIIT-recognized. 
• Should be working on innovation, development, or improvement of products, processes, or services. 
• Funding is indirect, through SEBI-registered AIFs supported by SIDBI.

scheme_name: Free Medical Card for Media Persons
eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant should be a native of the Union Territory of Puducherry by birth or by continuous residence for not less than five years.

scheme_name: Farm Machinery Bank (FMB)/Farm Machinery Hub (FMH)
eligibility_text_source:
- Individual Rural Youth Entrepreneur registered under Krishak Bandhu (New) Scheme.

Or,
Groups like SKUS/PACS/FPO/FPC/FIG/SHG duly graded /LAMPS etc approved/recognized by any Govt Institution/Departments .

scheme_name: Fish Marketing Scheme

scheme_name: Fodder Minikits under Schedule Castes Sub Plan (SC Category)

scheme_name: Fodder Minikits under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP) Scheme (ST Category)
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Free Motorized Tricycle Scheme for Persons with Disabilities
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Fodder Minikits under Integrated Fodder Development Scheme (General Category)

scheme_name: Family Pension (BBOCWWB)

scheme_name: Fruit Processing Centre

scheme_name: Fellowship for the Post-Graduate Students in Various Faculties Various Faculties of Sardarkrushinagar Dantiwada Agricultural University
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Family Planning Indemnity Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
##### ***For Beneficiary (Person who underwent Sterilization)***

- The applicant must be a person who has undergone sterilization operation under family planning program.
- The applicant must have undergone sterilization operation at an authorized health facility.
- The applicant must be able to provide documentary evidence of the sterilization procedure.

##### ***For Claimant (Family Member or Legal Representative)***

- The applicant must be a legal claimant or family member of the person who underwent sterilization (in case of death or incapacitation).
- The applicant should have legal authority to represent the beneficiary in case of death or inability to claim.

> ***Conditions***

_*The applicant must report complications, failure, or death within the specified timeframes as per scheme guidelines._
_*The applicant must submit the claim through proper medical channels with certification from authorized medical officers._

scheme_name: Farm Pond (Khet Talai) Nirman Karyakram

1. The applicant will be required to submit an affidavit that he/she has not taken benefit more than twice under the Scheme.

The reason for the suicide should be an agrarian reason, such as Failure of Bore wells, Raising of Commercial crops with a high cost of cultivation, Non-Remunerative Prices, Oral tenancy, and ineligibility to avail of Bank Loans, Private lending with higher interest rates, Adverse seasonal conditions, Heavy expenditure incurred towards Children education, ill health, and marriages.

 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
 - The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
 - The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

 - The applicant should be a spouse of a deceased registered construction worker Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab.
 - The deceased worker should have completed 1 year of membership with the Board.

scheme_name: Fee Waiver Scheme For SC/ST Students Pursuing Higher Education
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should have been born in Goa or resident of Goa for at least last fifteen years.
1. The applicant should have passed his/her qualifying examination from any of the institutions located in Goa.
1. The applicant should belong to the SC/ST Community of Goa.
1. The applicant should be enrolled for the First Year of their Degree/PG Courses.

scheme_name: Girnar Arohan-Avrohan Competition
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
 - The applicant should be physically fit and willing to participate in the stair-climbing competition.
 - The applicant should complete the required number of steps: 5,500 steps for men and 2,200 steps for women.

scheme_name: Group Accident Insurance Scheme under PMMSY: Permanent Partial Disability Gujarat
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a fisher, which includes fishermen, fish workers, fish farmers, and any other person directly involved in fishing or fisheries-related activities.
1. The applicant must be between 18 and 70 years old.
1. The insured person should not have been 71 years of age on the date of the accident.
1. The applicant must be approved by the State/Union Territory (UT) fisheries department.
1. At the time of a claim, the claimant or insured fisher must be verified and certified by an "Appropriate Authority".
1. The applicant should have registered himself in the concerned district office.

scheme_name: Group Accident Insurance Scheme under PMMSY: Medical Treatment - Gujarat
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a fisher, which includes fishermen, fish workers, fish farmers, and any other person directly involved in fishing or fisheries-related activities.
1. The applicant must be between 18 and 70 years old.
1. The insured person should not have been 71 years of age on the date of the accident.
1. The applicant must be approved by the State/Union Territory (UT) fisheries department.
1. At the time of a claim, the claimant or insured fisher must be verified and certified by an "Appropriate Authority".
1. The applicant should have registered himself in the concerned district office.

scheme_name: Gallantry Medal – PMG/GM
eligibility_text_source:
> **President’s Medal for Gallantry:**

1. The applicant should have performed a rare and conspicuous gallant act involving exceptional courage and skill.
1. The act should involve saving life or property, preventing crime, arresting criminals, apprehending a prisoner, or preventing a prisoner’s escape.
1. The risk incurred by the applicant is assessed with due regard to the obligations and duties of the officer.
1. The applicant should be a member of the Police Service, Fire Service, Correctional Service, Home Guard Service, or Civil Defence Service.

> **Medal for Gallantry:**

1. The applicant should have performed a conspicuous gallant act of merit.
1. The act should involve saving life or property, preventing crime, arresting criminals, apprehending a prisoner, or preventing a prisoner’s escape.
1. The risk incurred is assessed with due regard to the official duties of the applicant.
1. The applicant should be a member of the Police Service, Fire Service, Correctional Service, Home Guard Service, or Civil Defence Service.

scheme_name: Grant In Aid To Old Age Home
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a registered Non-Governmental Organization (NGO).
1. The applicant should only accommodate old/destitute/disabled victims.
1. In the case of old men, the age should be 60 years and above. In the case of old women, the age should be 55 years and above.
1. The old age person should be a citizen of India.
1. The old age person should be a permanent resident of the state of Maharashtra.

scheme_name: Griha Aadhar Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a married woman.
1. The applicant should be above the age of 18 years.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Goa for the last fifteen years.
1. The gross income of the husband and wife, taken together should not exceed ₹ 3,00,000/- per annum.
1. A widow with a minor child who is in receipt of benefits under the Dayanand Social Security Scheme (DSSS) rolled out by the Directorate of Social Welfare, Government of Goa shall be eligible for benefits under this scheme.

scheme_name: Grants In Aid To SC/ST Patients Suffering From Cancer And Other Malignant Diseases
eligibility_text_source:
1) The applicant must belong to an SC / ST community of Assam. 
2) Person suffering from cancer or other malignant diseases may apply.
3) No person shall be eligible to claim this financial aid more than once.

scheme_name: Grant of Annual Scholarship to the Students Belonging to UT of Puducherry Selected and Studying in the Sainik School, Amaravathi Nagar, Tamil Nadu
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be domiciled in the Union Territory of Puducherry.
- The applicant should be a recipient of the National Child Award for Exceptional Achievements and Other National / International Merit Awards
- The applicant should study in an educational institution run by any State Government/ Central Government/ Private School recognized by the respective State/Central Government.
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should belong to the UT of Puducherry either by birth or domicile i.e. continuous residence of not less than 5 years.
- The applicant should have studied from the educational institution run by any State Government/ Central Government/ Private School recognized by the respective State/Central Government.
- The applicant should be studying in the Sainik School, Amaravathi Nagar, Tamil Nadu.

scheme_name: Green Business Scheme
eligibility_text_source: Safai Karamcharis, Scavengers, and their Dependents are eligible for the scheme.

scheme_name: Green Business Scheme - Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant should be in the age group of 18 to 45 years.
1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
1. The applicant's annual family income should be up to ₹3,00,000/-.

**Note:** The income ceiling is at least 50% of the beneficiaries having an annual family income up to ₹1,50,000/-, and the balance 50% of the beneficiaries having an annual family income up to ₹3,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Gambhir Bimari Sahayata Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant must be registered workers under labor department. 
1. Beneficiary can not get the similar type of government scheme benefits like Pradhan Mantri Jan-Arogya Yojana and Chief Minister Jan-Arogya Yojana.
1. Only below family members are covered

 - Self.
 - Depended parents.
 - Spouse (Husband/Wife).
 - Daughter.
 - Son (only if below 21 years of age)

scheme_name: Gagan Bharari Shiksha Yojana for OBC (Dhangar) Students
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Student. 
- The applicant should belong to the OBC (Dhangar) community.
- The applicant should have availed (or be eligible for) the Centrally Sponsored Post Matric Scholarship during the current academic year.

scheme_name: Gagan Bharari Shiksha Yojana for SC Students
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Student. 
- The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste community
- The applicant should have availed (or be eligible for) the Centrally Sponsored Post Matric Scholarship during the current academic year.

scheme_name: Goggles by Welfare of Differently Abled Persons Department
eligibility_text_source: - The applicant should be a visually impaired person.

scheme_name: "Grant to Coir Cluster/Societies/Agency" Component of the "Development of Coir" Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a cluster/society/agency.
- The applicant should be engaged in coir product activities.
- The members of the organization should be coir artisans.
- The applicant should be registered under the Co-operative Act or any other Societies Act.

scheme_name: Phase-II Of The Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Programme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Residential consumers and Group Housing Society can apply for the installation of a solar rooftop system.
1. If you have electricity connection in your name and you pay regularly the electricity bill in your own name and also you have the permission of use the roof for solar rooftop installation from the owner, you can install the RTS.

scheme_name: Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Power Plant Scheme - Uttarakhand Renewable Energy Development Agency
eligibility_text_source: 1. All domestic electricity consumers across Uttarakhand who have applied on the MNRE national portal ([https://solarrooftop.gov.i](https://pmsuryaghar.gov.in/)n) under the "Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Power Plant Scheme" and whose Central Financial Assistance (CFA) for the installation of the solar plant has been approved or released are eligible.

scheme_name: Grid Connected Rooftop Scheme (Phase-II) - Uttarakhand Renewable Energy Development Agency
eligibility_text_source: 1. All domestic consumers, group housing societies, and residential welfare associations in Uttarakhand are eligible for the scheme.

scheme_name: "Green and Eco-Friendly Measures" under “Motivation of Entrepreneurs to Start Industries and Fiscal Assistance to Industries - State Awards to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises”
eligibility_text_source:
- The industry should be green and eco-friendly as per the DSTE standards.
- The unit should provide at least 60% of employment to the people of UT of Puducherry of the total employment strength of the industrial units.

scheme_name: GEN-NEXT Support for Innovative Startups (GENESIS)
eligibility_text_source:
> **Eligibility for Implementing Agencies (IAs):**

The applicant Implementing Agency must be a legal entity and must fall under one of the following categories:

1. The applicant should be an entity registered under Section 8 of the Companies Act; OR
1. The applicant should be a society registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860; OR
1. The applicant should be a trust registered under the Indian Trusts Act, 1882; OR
1. The applicant should be a statutory body created through an Act of the legislature.
1. The applicant may also be an Incubator, Accelerator, or Educational Institute recognised by the University Grants Commission (UGC) / All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), or formed under a Central/State Act.
1. The applicant should be an entity engaged in supporting startups as part of the startup ecosystem.

> **Eligibility for Startups:**

1. The applicant should be a beneficiary of MeitY Schemes or should be registered with MeitY Startup Hub (MSH).
1. The applicant should be a startup registered with the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT).
1. The shareholding by Indian promoters in the applicant startup should be at least 51% at the time of application to the incubator for the scheme, as per the Companies Act, 2013.
1. The applicant startup should have a full-time Chief Executive Officer (CEO).
1. The applicant startup should have market-ready products and should be seeking funds for business development and commercialis

scheme_name: Gunavatta Evam Navaachar Nishulk Paathy Pustak Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a student from Classes 1 to 8.
 1. The applicant must be studying in a government or government-aided school, or a recognized madrasa that follows the Uttarakhand state government curriculum.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Assistance to Differently Abled Person
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
- The applicant should have an annual income not exceeding ₹75,000/- per annum.
- For Orthopaedic/Deaf/Dumb/Blind, the disability of the applicant should be 40% and above.
- For the Mentally Retarded, the Intelligence Quotient (IQ) of the applicant should be below 69.

scheme_name: Grant Of Financial Assistance For The Funeral Expenses Of Old Age/ Destitute Pensioner
eligibility_text_source: Old Age and Destitute pensioners suffering from T.B./Cancer.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Assistance for Performance of Marriage of Poor Brides Living Below Poverty Line
eligibility_text_source:
- The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹75,000 per annum.
- The parents/guardian of the bride should be a citizen of India and a native of the Union Territory of Pondicherry by birth or by continuous residence of 5 years or more.
- The assistance is payable only for the first marriage of the bride.
- The marriage should be performed in accordance with the law in force on the date of marriage.
- The assistance is given for the performance of the marriage of one daughter only.
- The bride should have attained the age of 18 years, and the bridegroom 21 years on the date of marriage.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Assistance to the Poor Parents Having Only One Girl Child Who is Studying 8th to 10th Standard
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a native of Puducherry by virtue of birth or continuous residence of not less than five years.
1. The applicant should have an annual income not exceeding ₹75,000/-.
1. The applicant should have only one living girl child and no other issue.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Assistance to the Press Reporters of Puducherry
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be a native of the Union Territory of Puducherry by birth or by continuous residence for not less than five years.
- The applicant should be a Press Reporter.
- The applicant should have served in a Newspaper/Magazine.
- The Newspaper/Magazine should be duly registered with the "Registrar of Newspapers, Government of India, New Delhi".
- The Newspaper/Magazine should be registered continuously for not less than 10 years.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Assistance to Poor Scheduled Caste Pregnant & Lactating Women
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should belong to the Union Territory of Pondicherry by virtue of birth or continuous residence of not less than 3 years.
- The applicant should be from Scheduled Caste.
- The applicant should be a woman.
- The applicant should be pregnant.
- The applicant should apply from 6 months of pregnancy and within 3 months from the date of delivery.
- The annual income of the applicant and spouse should not exceed ₹ 2,00,000/-.
- The grant of financial assistance is made available only for 2 deliveries.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Assistance for Purchase of Textbooks/Notebooks to School-Going Children of Industrial Workers/Employees (GLWB)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a regular industrial worker/employee of the Goa Labour Welfare Board.
- The applicant should have at least 6 months of continuous service.
- The applicant should not be employed in supervisory or managerial roles earning ₹1,600/- and above per month.
- The applicant should have wages/salary not exceeding ₹20,000/-.
- The applicant should have children studying in classes 1st to 4th in recognized/approved schools.

> **Criteria for Selection**

- Lowest income of the parents.
- Preference will be given to the students studying in higher classes.
- Workers having not more than two children will be given priority.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Assistance to the School Going Children of Industrial Workers/Employees Studying in 1st to 4th Standard for Supply of One Set of Uniform
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be an industrial worker/employee.
- The applicant should be registered with the Goa Labour Welfare Board.
- The applicant should have a minimum of 6 months of continuous service to the Board at the time of application.
- The monthly salary of the applicant should not exceed ₹20,000/-.
- The age of the child of the applicant should be between 5 to 10 years.
- The child of the applicant should be studying in 1st to 4th standard.
- The child of the applicant should be enrolled in a recognized, aided, or private school.
- Preference is given to workers with no more than two children.

scheme_name: Construction of Sports Infrastructure Facilities (Badminton, Table Tennis, Squash, Indoor Cricket, Basketball, Volleyball, Football, Hockey, and Swimming) in Private Sector
eligibility_text_source: 1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Incentives for Hotels, Resorts, and Tourism Projects
eligibility_text_source:
- The scheme applies to star-classified hotels/resorts, heritage hotels, and other tourism projects completed and commissioned from 2005 onwards.
- The project must be in continuous commercial operation.
- The applicant must have financially invested in the property and developed the project.
- The hotel/resort must have star classification or heritage classification approved by the Ministry of Tourism, Government of India.
- The project must not have availed any other capital subsidy/incentive from the Government of Puducherry or the Government of India.
- The project must continue in the same name and style for eight years from the date of release of the incentive.

scheme_name: Grant of Fuel Subsidy to Differently Abled Persons Who Are Owning Motorised Vehicles
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
- The applicant should have an annual income not exceeding ₹75,000/- per annum.
- The vehicle should be registered in the name of the applicant.
- The distance between the residence of the applicant and the duty point should be greater than 6km.
- The disability of the applicant should be 40% and above.
- The applicant should not be should not be in receipt of transport allowance for conveyance from the Department or from any other source.

scheme_name: Government Food Science Training Centre
eligibility_text_source:
1. To seek admission in a diploma course, the candidate/student must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. For admission to the Canning and Food Processing course, the student must have passed Intermediate (Science/Agriculture) from an institute located in Uttarakhand.
 1. For admission to other courses, the student must have passed Intermediate from an institute located in Uttarakhand.

scheme_name: Grant to Films
eligibility_text_source: 1. The applicant must be a film producer.

scheme_name: Ghar Ghar Aushodhi Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant must be a native of Rajasthan state.
1. Should have Jan Aadhaar Card of Rajasthan Government.
1. Should have enough space to plant.

scheme_name: Gharaunda-Group Home for Adults Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility criteria for the PwD:**

1. PwD should be more than 18 years of age.
1. PwD should have one of the disabilities under the [National Trust Act, 1999](https://cdnbbsr.s3waas.gov.in/s3300891a62162b960cf02ce3827bb363c/uploads/2024/09/20240918793880368.pdf), which are Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities.
**Eligibility criteria for Registered Organization:**

1. Applicant should be registered with the National Trust.
1. Applicant should have a valid registration under the PwD Act 1995, at the time of enrolment.
1. The group home land should either be owned by RO or should have lease of at least 10 years with provision of renewal.
1. RO should have minimum 2 years of experience of working with Persons with Disabilities (PwD) with at least one year experience in one of the four disabilities under the National Trust Act.
1. RO should not be blacklisted by the National Trust or any other Government Organization on the date of submission of scheme enrolment form.

scheme_name: Go Green Scheme- Gujarat Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. Labourers/Industrial Workers should be employed in a factory/institute in the state of Gujarat.
 1. Labourers who have been regularly contributing to the Labour Welfare Fund will be eligible for this scheme.

**Other Instruction:**

 1. Subsidy will be given to those models which have been empanelled with FAME-2 and GEDA both.
 1. Vehicles manufactured in India under Make in India and “Aatma Nirbhar Bharat”
 1. Two-wheeler with a lithium-ion battery that can run at least 50 km on a single charge, does not require a separate charging station, recognized under the Motor and Vehicle Act.
 1. Labour has to pay the residual amount after deducting the amount of subsidy from the ex-showroom price.
 1. After sell, the amount of subsidy will be credited directly to the dealer's account.
 1. RTO Road Tax amount will be reimbursed to the dealer.

scheme_name: Go-Green Shramik Scheme (GBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The construction workers registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
 1. Registration period for construction workers should be one year or more.
 1. A registered construction worker should have purchased a lithium-ion battery high-speed vehicle in his own name.
 1. The registered construction worker should have a license to drive two-wheelers.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will be available once per registered construction worker.
 1. A registered construction worker cannot sell the vehicle for three years after purchasing it.

scheme_name: Government of Goa State Scholarship to NDA Cadets
eligibility_text_source:
1. The cadet should have been born in Goa and/or domiciled in Goa for a period of at least 15 years.
1. The income limit of the cadet’s parents/ guardians shall not exceed ₹ 15,000/- per month (₹ 1,80,000 per annum).
1. In case the cadet is SC/ST/OBC, the income limit of the cadet’s parents/ guardians shall not exceed ₹ 37,500/- per month (₹ 4,50,000 per annum).
1. The cadet should not be receiving financial assistance/scholarship/freeship from any other source.

scheme_name: Go-Green Three Wheelers Scheme (GBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The construction workers should be registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board.
 1. The registered construction workers who become disabled or incapacitated due to an accident while working at a construction site will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
 1. The registered construction disabled worker (Shramyogi) should have purchased a lithium-ion battery-operated vehicle in his own name.
 1. The registered construction disabled worker (Shramyogi) should hold a tri-cycle license to operate a tri-cycle.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will be available once per registered construction disabled worker.

scheme_name: Gomantak Govsamvardhan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a Dairy Farmer or an Individual of the State of Goa.
- The applicant must be a bonafide resident of Goa for at least the preceding 15 years.
- The applicant must own non-descript cattle or Shwet Kapila cattle.
- The applicant must have an adequate cattle shed as per the Bureau of Indian Standard.
- The scheme shall be applicable to rural areas and Class C and B Municipalities.
- The applicant must be responsible for fulfilling the Pollution Control Board norms if the number of cattle reared exceeds 25 in number.

_*If the number of applications received for rearing are more than 250 cattle per district, priority can be decided based on the grazing area of the beneficiary._
##### ***Reservation***

- Out of the 250 cattle per district, 12% shall be reserved for the Scheduled Tribes (ST).
- Out of the 250 cattle per district, 2% shall be reserved for the Scheduled Castes (SC) respectively.
- In case there is no demand in the reserved categories, the demand shall be booked under the general component.

scheme_name: Green House
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a native of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should be a Farmer.
1. The applicant must have agricultural land ownership and irrigation sources.

scheme_name: Greenhouse Construction
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a farmer and must hold a valid Udhyan Card.
1. The farmer must either own agricultural land or have land on lease.
1. This support is not provided to applicants working in a group.

scheme_name: Grant of House Construction Subsidy to Scheduled Caste People
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be a native or a permanent resident of the Union Territory of Pondicherry.
- The applicant should not possess a house.
- The applicant should be from Scheduled Caste or from Other Economically Backward Class.

> **For Scheduled Caste**

- The applicant should have a house site of an area of 450 sq.ft to build the house.
- The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹2,00,000/-.

> **For Other Economically Backward Class**

- The applicant should have a house site granted by Adi Dravidar Welfare Department.
- The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹24,000/-.

scheme_name: Government Hostel for Working Women/ Girl Students
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should either be a woman employed in government office/organizations/firm, or a girl student studying in college.
- The income of the working woman should not exceed ₹5000 per month.

scheme_name: The Grant of Honorarium to Legal Researcher Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a citizen of India.
- The applicant must be a resident of Puducherry.
- The applicant must be a Postgraduate in Law (2 years programme) from the recognized Universities in the Indian Union.
- The applicant must not be pursuing Postgraduate Degree in Law or any other Degrees or Programmes requiring their compulsory attendance elsewhere.
- The applicant must not have attained the age of 30 years as on 1st January or 1st July, preceding the last date fixed for submission of applications.
- The applicant must not have attained the age of 32 years as on 1st January, 2023, if applying under the one-time age relaxation granted by the Honorable Lieutenant Governor of Puducherry.

****The educational qualifications and other eligibility criteria may be relaxed in case of exceptionally outstanding and deserving candidates by the Government.***

scheme_name: Gharelu Hinsa Ki Pidita Ke Liye Sahayta Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
- The applicant must be women and girls only.
- The applicant must be affected by domestic violence.

scheme_name: "Grant To Handicrafts Societies" Component of the "Development of Handicrafts" Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The Handicrafts Society should be registered either as a Co-operative society or under the Societies Act.
- Members of the society should be artisans approved either by the State Government or Development Commissioner (Handicrafts).
- The Society should exclusively deal with handicraft products produced by artisans of the UT of Puducherry.

scheme_name: Grant In Aid Hostels
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For the Student***

- The applicant must be a student.
- The applicant must be from a Scheduled Tribe.
- The applicant must be studying in standard 5th to 12th.
- The applicant’s family annual income must be up to ₹1,20,000/- for rural area.
- The applicant’s family annual income must be up to ₹1,50,000/- for urban area.

> ***For the Institution***

- The applicant must be a voluntary institution.
- The applicant must be registered under one of the following acts: Public Trusts Act 1950, Societies Registration Act 1860
- The applicant organization must have a bank balance (liquidity/working capital) certificate showing an amount exceeding ₹15,000/-.
- The building must be suitable and equipped with complete facilities, including water and electricity, and available at the demanded site for the hostel.

> ***Preference/Priority***

- Preference must be given to selecting hostels only in places where there is currently no hostel facility.
- Priority should be given to backward and remote areas during hostel selection.

scheme_name: 'Grant-in-Aid to Industrial Association, CII, Industrial NGOs for Promotion of SME Clusters' under 'Marketing And Publicity'
eligibility_text_source: The applicant should be an Industrial Association/ CII/ Industrial NGO.

scheme_name: Grant-in-aid to Non-governmental Organisations
eligibility_text_source:
1. The NGO should have a sound financial position. 
1. The NGO should have facilities and resources for running the residential homes. 
1. The NGO should be non-sectarian offering services to its beneficiaries irrespective of religion, caste, colour or creed. 
1. The NGO should not be run for profit of any individual or a body of individuals. 
1. The NGO should be free from corrupt practices.

scheme_name: Grant-in-Aid Scheme to Provide Financial Assistance to the Mahila Mandals
eligibility_text_source:
- The Mahila Mandal must have valid registration with the competent authority.
- The Mahila Mandal must have been operating successfully within the state of Goa for the last 2 years.
- Each group must submit a project report specifying the activities/training and a list of prospective beneficiaries with educational details.
- There must be a minimum of 20 members for training/orientation.

_*The government may relax any provision of the scheme at its discretion._
_*No additional liability for house rent, electricity bills, or water bills will be borne by the government under the scheme._

scheme_name: Grant-in-Aid Scheme To Provide Financial Assistance To The Mahila Mandals/ Women’s Self-help Groups (SWAWLAMBHAN)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The Mahila Mandals/Women’s Self-Help Groups should have valid registration with the Department of Women & Child Development, Panaji-Goa.
1. The Mahila Mandals/Women’s Self-Help Groups shall be operating successfully for their members within the State of Goa.
1. There shall be a minimum of 20 members for training/orientation.

**NOTE:** The compulsion of 2 years of registration is deleted.

scheme_name: Grant-in-aid (TB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. Scheduled Tribes and Scheduled Castes persons permanently residing within the State of Mizoram
1. None of whose family members are Government employees and whose family income from all sources does not exceed ₹2000/- per month and who suffering from fell diseases are eligible to receive the grant.

scheme_name: Grant-in-Aid to Non-governmental Organisations Running Residential Homes for the Children, Aged, and Vulnerable Persons Under Social Defence
eligibility_text_source:
- The NGO should be running residential Homes for orphans, Destitute and street children, Aged and infirm, Physically and Mentally disabled persons.
- The NGO should be registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860 (Act XXI of 1860); or a public trust registered under any law for the time being in force; or a charitable company licensed under section 25 of the Companies Act, 1956.
- The NGO should ordinarily have existed for a period of three years continuously at the time of application. This condition may be relaxed by the Government in deserving cases where the institution could not be run without assistance from the Government and was started with the sole object of 
- supplementing the social welfare activities of the Government.
- The NGO should have been licensed by the Department of Social Welfare under the Women and Children Licensing Rules, 1956.
- The NGO should have a properly constituted managing body with its powers, duties and responsibilities clearly defined and laid down in writing. 
- The NGO should have a sound financial position.
- The NGO should have facilities and resources for running the residential homes. 
- The NGO should be non-sectarian offering services to its beneficiaries irrespective of religion, caste, colour or creed. 
- The NGO should not be run for profit of any individual or a body of individuals.

scheme_name: Grant to Jharkhand State Unaided Educational Institutions
eligibility_text_source:

***For Madrasas/Sanskrit Schools:***

1. The applicant should be a recognized and registered madrasa or Sanskrit school in Jharkhand.

***For Intermediate Colleges:***

1. The institution must comply with JAC and Government rules.

However, for NICU, Ventilatory Support and Kidney Dialysis, the patient may opt for any empanelled hospital even if the facilities are available in a Government hospital.

1. The applicant institution should identify a co-coordinator.

scheme_name: Goat Rearing Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a tribal woman beneficiary.
1. The applicant's age must be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant must be a tribal woman livestock keeper residing in Scheduled Areas, Pockets, and Clusters within tribal districts.
1. The applicant must be a Below Poverty Line (BPL) tribal woman livestock keeper with a BPL score between 0 and 20.
1. Only one person per family will receive the benefit under this scheme.
1. The scheme's benefit is ration card-based, meaning only one woman beneficiary per ration card can receive the benefit.
1. The applicant can avail the benefit of this scheme again after 10 years.

> **Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation**

The following priority order will be given for beneficiary selection:

1. Primitive Tribal Group (PVTG) livestock keeper women beneficiaries.
1. Tribal livestock keeper women beneficiaries holding Forest Rights Act (FRA) entitlement letters.
1. Other tribal livestock keeper women beneficiaries, with the following sub-priority:

- Disabled tribal women.
- Widowed or divorced tribal women.
- BPL tribal women livestock keeper beneficiaries with the lowest score among those with a 0 to 20 BPL score.

scheme_name: Grant Of Retention Scholarship to Scheduled Caste Girl Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. All the Scheduled Caste Girls Students of the Union Territory of Puducherry are eligible for the scholarship.
 1. The concession envisaged under the scheme will be granted to Scheduled Caste Girls Students who are native of the Union Territory of Puducherry or are residing in the Union Territory of Puducherry for a continuous period of not less than five years.
 1. The concession will be granted to the Scheduled Caste Girls Students studying from I Standard to V Standard either in Government or private recognized educational institutions.
 1. Annual income of the parent guardian shall not exceed ₹24000/-.

> **Other conditions for the scholarship:**

 1. Students who are granted the scholarships will be allowed to retain them till they complete that primary education.
 1. The scholarship will be cancelled for any misconduct on the part of the scholar.
 1. If a candidate is found to have obtained a scholarship by false statement, her scholarship will be cancelled and the amount of scholarship paid will be recovered at the discretion of the Sanctioning Authority.

scheme_name: "Generator Subsidy" under "Motivation of Entrepreneurs to Start Industries and Fiscal Assistance to Industries"
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a micro/ small/ medium industrial unit.
- The unit should be from the manufacturing sector or service sector.
- The unit should have made investment on generator sets.
- The unit should have made investment on or after 5th March 2009.

scheme_name: "Generator Subsidy" under "Motivation of Entrepreneurs to Start Industries and Fiscal Assistance to Industries - Various Incentives"
eligibility_text_source:
- All the Micro, Small, Medium and Large Industries are eligible.
- The Unit should have made the investment on or after 1st April 2017.
- The unit should provide at least 60% of employment to the people of the Union territory of Puducherry of the total employment strength of the industrial units. This 60% of the local employment will have to be maintained by the industrial unit during the period specified in the Affidavit.
- The generator should have been purchased on or after 1st April 2017.
- The industrial unit shall not transfer or dispose of the fixed asset including plant and machinery in any manner till the completion of 5 years from the date of submitting the application/disbursement of the subsidy whichever is later.
- The additional investments made by the new/existing industrial units after availing the first claim are also eligible for subsidy up to the ceiling limit.

_*The date of commencement as admitted by the entrepreneurs shall be reckoned after due verification by the officials of the Industries Department._
> **Special Cases**

If, two or more industrial undertakings are set up by the same person as proprietor, common partners, common Board of Directors/shareholders, such units are eligible for subsidy as separate entity provided the location of the unit are different and licenses /clearances/ registrations are obtained separately for the enterprises.

scheme_name: Generator Subsidy
eligibility_text_source: All new and existing Micro, Small, and Medium Manufacturing Enterprises.

scheme_name: Gatidhara Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of West Bengal.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in the Employment Bank of Labour Department, GOWB.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 25 years to 45 years (Relaxation 5 years for SC/ST & 3 years for OBC).
1. The family income of the applicant should be less than ₹25000/- per month.
1. More than one member of the same family is not eligible.

scheme_name: Goa State 25% Subsidy for Self Employed Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be an individual or a Group of individuals or a Partnership Firm.
- The applicant must have availed financial assistance for the purpose of self-employment under the "Chief Minister’s Rozgar Yojana (CMRY)" or "Dr. Verghese Kurien Rojgar Yojana (VKRY)" Schemes, or from Nationalized Banks, Scheduled Commercial Banks, Co-operative Banks, and registered Credit Societies.
- The income of the applicant along with spouse must not exceed ₹3,00,000/- per annum.
- The applicant must be minimum 7th grade passed.
- The applicant must be below 42 years of age.
- In case of a group of individuals (except registered Self Help Groups), all the individuals must be below 42 years of age.
- In the case of groups of individuals, all persons in the group must satisfy the residency, age, and education criteria.
- The activity carried out must not be covered under the Red Category.
- The activity carried out must not be related to tobacco and liquor.
- The applicant must not be receiving assistance under the "Dr. Verghese Kurien Rojgar Yojana Scheme" for the acquisition of Yellow/Black–Motorcycles, Yellow/Black–Auto Rickshaws, Yellow/Black–Taxis, Tourist Taxis, or Buses.
- The applicant must not be an existing beneficiary of the "Chief Minister’s Rojgar Yojana Scheme" financed for the acquisition of Yellow/Black–Motorcycles, Yellow/Black–Auto Rickshaws, Yellow/Black–Taxis, or Tourist Taxis.
- The applicant must not be a beneficiary under any other subsidy linked schemes of the

scheme_name: Gayathri Scheme for Academic Excellence
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community.
1. The parents of the student should reside in Andhra Pradesh. 
1. The student must be a regular (Full Time) student of the course for the academic year 2018-19. 
1. The student should be the topper of the entire course (at the end of SSC/ Intermediate or equivalent /Graduation /Professional Edn. Course) in the School/ College/ Institute for the academic year 2018-19.
1. The toppers of SSC/ Intermediate or equivalent and Graduation should have completed the course in Andhra Pradesh. However, toppers of Professional Education Course may have completed the course from anywhere in India but the parents should be residing in Andhra Pradesh.
1. The student should not have availed himself/herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose.

scheme_name: Goa State Best Library and Best Librarian Award
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Library**

1. The library should be in existence for a minimum period of 15 years. 
1. The library should have a minimum of 100 registered members for the last 5 years. 
1. The library should have in possession a minimum of 2500 books, periodicals etc., in readable condition. 
1. The library should have a proper infrastructure with retrieval facilities. 
1. The library should be spacious, having a minimum area of 40 sq. mts. and with proper ventilation. 
1. The library should have conducted regular programmes etc. for the members/public to enhance 
1. their reading habits and knowledge. 
1. The library should have maintained all the library records properly and up to date. 
1. The award shall be conferred to only one well-stacked library from the State of Goa.

> **For Librarian**

1. The Librarian should be born in the State of Goa or should be a resident of the State of Goa for a minimum period of 15 years. 
1. The Librarian should have a minimum of 10 years of continuous service as a Librarian/Section-In-Charge of a reputed library from the State of Goa.
1. The Librarian should have taken up initiatives for library development/inculcating reading culture in his/her area of work.
1. The award shall not be conferred posthumously. However, in the event of death of the individual after recommendation of his/her name for the award by the Award Committee, the award shall be conferred posthumously. Further, any illustrious service of any individual in the field of library

scheme_name: Goa State Cultural Award
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The applicant should have completed 60 years of age.
1. The applicant should have given extraordinary contributions to the field of art & culture at large and in his/her respective field of art in particular.
1. The applicant should have contributed in his/her respective field of art in Goa for a minimum of 10 years out of his/her entire career as a practising artist.
1. No award will be presented posthumously. However, during the selection process if the death of any artist occurs after the recommendation of his name then Task Force The committee may consider his name for the award, in such circumstances award will be presented posthumously.

scheme_name: Goa State Cultural Award for Institution
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Goan institution (or institution working for Goan artists) working in the field of Art and culture. 
1. The institution should have completed 15 years.
1. The institution should have given extraordinary contributions to the field of Art and culture.
1. The institution should have a properly constituted managing body with its powers and duties clearly defined in its constitution.
1. The institution should have a good reputation in the local community.
1. The cultural institutions that are financed by the Central Government Corporations, and Municipalities are also eligible.

> **Reservation / Preference / Priority**

Preference will be given to the institutions involved in Cultural Education i.e. imparting Training in Music, Dance, Drama, Fine Arts etc.

scheme_name: Grant Scheme for Cultivation of Aromatic Plants - Centre for Aromatic Plants
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
- The applicant must be a farmer.
- The farmer must either legally own agricultural land or have leased land for a minimum period of 10 years.
- The nine selected species eligible for the grant must have been cultivated by the farmer at their own expense.

scheme_name: Guruji Student Credit Card (GSCC) Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student. 
1. The student should be an Indian national.
1. The student should have completed class 10th to pursue a diploma and class 10th and 12th to pursue undergraduate or higher courses from a recognized school in Jharkhand. 
1. The student should have secured admission in institutions like IITs, IIMs, IIESTs, ISIs, NLUs, AIIMSs, NITs, XLRI, IISCs, BITS, SPA, NID, IIFTs, ICFAI Business School etc within and outside of the state (within India) which are ranked up to 200 in the overall list or ranked up to 100 in their respective individual category of NIRF Rankings or have been accredited grade either “A" or above by NAAC.
1. The student should not be aged more than 40 (forty) years at the time of application for loan.
1. The student should not be a prior beneficiary of the scheme.
1. The student should not have availed education loan from any State Co-operative Banks, Central Co-operative Banks, District Central Co-operative Banks, or any Public or Private sector Banks for the course in which he/she is applying for a loan under the Scheme.

scheme_name: Goa (Subsidy to Clay Idol Makers) Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a traditional clay idol maker or artisan from the State of Goa.
- The applicant must be registered with GHRSSIDC as a clay idol manufacturer.
- The applicant must personally manufacture idols within Goa; imported or traded idols are not eligible.
- The applicant must have a workshop or production unit within Goa for manufacturing idols such as Ganesh, Durga, Saraswati, or Mother Mary.
- The subsidy is applicable only for idols used for religious purposes (e.g., Ganesh Chaturthi, Christmas) and not for decorative items.
- The applicant must maintain records of idols produced and sold, including size, price, buyer details, and sale date.
- The applicant must comply with physical inspection by GHRSSIDC officials.

scheme_name: Grant of Stipend to Differently Abled Trainees
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should have undertaken a full-time vocational training course in any recognized vocational training centre, workshop, ITI, or any other institution sanctioned by the Central/State Government, local body, or voluntary organization.
- The applicant's age should be 5 years and above.
- The applicant should be differently abled.

scheme_name: Grant of Stipend to the Differently Abled Trainees of the Vocational Training Institutes
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
- The applicant should be differently abled.
- The applicant should have undertaken a full time vocational training course in any vocational training centre/ workshop/ ITI/ any other centre run by the Central / State Government/ Local body or any voluntary organization or institution recognized by Central / State Government.

scheme_name: Green Skill Development Programme (GSDP)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a student.
 - The applicant should have completed at least 10th standard.
 - The applicant should be a farmer/ youth or individual seeking self-employment.
 - The applicant should be below 55 years of age at the time of application.
 - The applicant should have an interest in environmental and ecological training topics such as wildlife, biodiversity, or natural resource interpretation.

scheme_name: Goa State Export Market Development Scheme, 2008
eligibility_text_source:
- Only those units set up in Goa and permanently registered or acknowledged with Entrepreneurs Memorandum II by the Directorate of Industries, Trade and Commerce were eligible.
- The unit had to be in operation for a minimum period of three years.
- The unit had to have an import/export code issued by the Reserve Bank of India/Government of India or by any competent authority of the Government of India.
- The total turnover of the unit for the last three preceding financial years should not have exceeded ₹10,00,00,000/-.
- One unit could avail this facility only once under this Scheme in its lifetime.
- If the original unit had already taken benefits under this Scheme, then the sold/transferred unit was not eligible to derive any benefits under this Scheme.

scheme_name: Goa State Employment Subsidy Scheme for the Industries, 2008
eligibility_text_source:
- Only manufacturing units were eligible.
- New and existing Micro and Small Enterprises registered with the Directorate of Industries, Trade and Commerce.
- Medium and large units approved by the High-Powered Coordination Committee after 1st April 2008.
- Units had to employ 80% local manpower (contract/temporary/daily wage employees not considered).
- Units had to fall under "Green", "Orange", or specified "Orange" categories.

_*Proprietor, partner, promoters, directors, or their relatives (spouse, father, mother, son/daughter, grandparent, son-in-law, daughter-in-law, brother, sister, first cousin) were not considered employees for subsidy claims._
> **Preference/Priority**

Women-owned proprietary concerns (100% ownership) and partnership firms (51% ownership by women, with at least one additional partner other than husband, father, brother, or son).

scheme_name: Gujarat State Electric Vehicle Policy
eligibility_text_source:
##### ***Electric Vehicle Purchase Subsidy***

- The applicant must purchase electric vehicles that have taken subsidy under Government of India's Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Hybrid and Electric Vehicles in India Scheme dated 8th March 2019 and any amendments thereafter.
- The applicant must ensure the vehicle's ex-factory price falls within the prescribed threshold limits for each segment.
- The applicant must not have claimed subsidy under any other state government scheme run by different departments.
- The applicant must authenticate documents for vehicle purchase to receive Direct Benefit Transfer.

##### ***Charging Infrastructure Subsidy***

- The applicant must set up commercial public electric vehicle charging stations for 2-wheelers, 3-wheelers, and 4-wheelers.
- The applicant must not have availed subsidies under any other promotional schemes under Government of India policies or schemes.
- The applicant must ensure charging stations meet guidelines and standards of Ministry of Power Circular dated 1st October 2019 and any amendments thereafter.
- The applicant must be among the first 250 commercial public electric vehicle charging station developers.

##### ***Manufacturing Incentives***

- The applicant must be involved in manufacturing electric vehicles and their components.
- The applicant must comply with provisions of Gujarat Industrial Policy-2020 and subsequent applicable policies.

scheme_name: Garuda Scheme for Funeral Expense
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a close relative of the deceased (Spouse/Son/Daughter/Parent/Brother/Grandson). 
1. The applicant should provide a self-declaration that he/she is the legal heir of the deceased.
1. The applicant should belong to the Brahmin community and reside in Andhra Pradesh.
1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹75,000/-.
1. The applicant should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose.

scheme_name: Goa State Financial Incentives to the Industries for Certification and Patenting Scheme, 2008
eligibility_text_source:

- Units had to be under the category of Micro & Small/Medium and large-scale industry or service industry falling under the Green/Orange Category list.
- Units permanently registered or acknowledged with Entrepreneurs Memorandum II by the Director of Industries, Trade and Commerce were eligible.
- Units not registered or acknowledged with EM II but cleared by the High Powered Coordination Committee or any Committee or Authority formed to grant such clearances for investment in the State were also eligible.

1. Hospitals or any other Medical Institutions registered with or recognized by the Directorate of Health Services.
1. Educational Institutions recognized by the Directorate of Education/Directorate of Higher Education/Directorate of Technical Education or any other educational institution affiliated to Goa University/Goa Board/Goa Board of Technical Education or any other institution covered under the Goa Education Development Corporation or Human Resource Development Fou

scheme_name: Grant for the Marriage of Daughter of Destitute Widow
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a destitute widow.
1. The widow must be receiving widow pension.
1. The family’s total monthly income from all sources must not exceed ₹4,000/-, or the family must belong to the BPL category.
1. At the time of marriage, the bride’s age must be between 18 and 45 years.
1. At the time of marriage, the groom’s age must be between 21 and 45 years.
1. The grant is applicable for the marriage of up to two daughters.

**Note:** The Social Welfare Department verifies whether the widow is receiving widow pension or not.

scheme_name: Grant for Marriage of Daughters of Scheduled Caste Individuals
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
2. The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
3. The family’s total monthly income from all sources must not exceed ₹4,000/-, or the family must belong to the BPL category.
4. At the time of marriage, the bride’s age must be between 18 and 45 years.
5. At the time of marriage, the groom’s age must be between 21 and 45 years.
6. The grant is applicable for the marriage of up to two daughters.

scheme_name: Group Supply Of Goats And Sheep
eligibility_text_source:
1. Beneficiary should belong to Scheduled Tribe.
2. Beneficiary should be below poverty line.

scheme_name: Gargi Sanskrit Balika Chhaatravrtti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a girl student of any category.
1. The girl student must be studying from class 6 to class 12 in a Sanskrit school of Uttarakhand.
1. A total of 175 enrolled girl students will be eligible.

scheme_name: Girl Students Health & Hygiene Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a girl student.
1. The beneficiary must be studying in classes VI to XII.
1. The beneficiary must be enrolled in a government or government-aided school.
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Arunachal Pradesh.

scheme_name: Government Service Home - Tamil Nadu
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a female.
1. The applicant's annual income should not exceed ₹ 72,000/- (not applicable for differently abled).
1. The age of the applicant should be between 14 to 45 years. 
1. The applicant should qualify as one of the following:

- Widow.
- Destitute Woman.
- Deserted Woman.
- Adolescent Girl.
- Differently Abled Woman.
- Woman from Socially and Economically Backward Sections. 

**NOTE: The women can stay with their male child up to 5th standard and girl child up to 12th standard in the Service Home.**

scheme_name: Grant of Subsidy For Setting up of Ice-Plant /Cold Storage/Fish Processing Unit
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Private Sector/Fishermen/Fisherwomen Cooperative Societies/Fishermen /Entrepreneurs.
- The applicant should be between 18 to 50 years old.
- The applicant should not have availed any benefit of loan/subsidy from any other source for the intended purpose.

scheme_name: Grant of Stipend to Junior Advocates Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a citizen of India.
- The applicant must be a native by birth or resident of the Union territory of Puducherry for a continuous period of five years.
- The applicant must have pursued a Law degree in any Government Law College.
- The applicant must be a Law Graduate.
- The applicant must have enrolled in the Bar Council of Tamil Nadu and Puducherry.
- The applicant must have passed the All India Bar Examination conducted by the Bar Council of India.
- The applicant's family income must not exceed ₹2,00,000/- per annum.
- The applicant must not be above 30 years of age.
- The applicant must comply with the rules of the [scheme guidelines](https://law.py.gov.in/docs/Stipend.pdf) during the period of financial assistance.
##### ***Preference, Priority, Reservation, Relaxation, Earmarking, etc.***

- **Reservation:** The Rule of reservation notified by the UT of Puducherry for Government employment shall be followed.- **Earmarking:** One-third of the beneficiaries shall be women.- **Selection Criteria (Merit):** Selection will be based on the marks obtained in the qualifying examination (Bachelor's Degree in Law).- **Selection Criteria (Merit):** Selection will be based on the marks secured in the 12th Standard Board examination.- **Selection Criteria (Merit):** Negative marks will be applied for additional attempts in the qualifying examination.
