eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be engaged in any building or construction work.
 1. The applicant should be registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered worker must have a minimum of one year of regular membership.
 1. The worker must have an active membership with the Board.
 1. A worker who is admitted to a government or government-approved private hospital for treatment for 4 to 30 days due to an accident or illness is eligible for assistance.
 1. The assistance shall be for a period not exceeding one month in a year.

scheme_name: Meghalaya Agriculture Warehouse Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
**For Individuals:**

1. The applicant should be between 18 and 58 years of age.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
1. The applicant should have ownership of the land where the warehouse is proposed or leased land for at least the next 10 years.
1. The applicant should not be a credit defaulter of any bank.

**For Registered Entities:**

1. The applicant should be based in the village where the program is being availed.
1. The applicant should have an active bank account and submit the bank statement of the last six months.
1. The applicant should have been in operation for at least two years.
1. The applicant should possess land, either self-owned or leased, duly verified by the competent authority.
1. The applicant should not be a credit defaulter of any bank.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Apprenticeship Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant must be at least 14 years old (18 years for hazardous trades).
 1. The applicant must meet the minimum educational qualification specific to the trade.
 1. The applicant must be a minimum 5th pass-out.
 1. The applicant must satisfy the physical fitness standards prescribed for the trade.
 1. The applicant must not have been engaged in any similar apprenticeship program previously.

scheme_name: Mahila Adhikarita Yojana (Gujarat Safai Kamdar Development Corporation)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a woman sweeper or a dependent of a woman sweeper.
- The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Awas Yojna (HPBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
**For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker..
1. The applicant should be aged between 18 and 60 years.
1. The worker should have completed at least 90 days of building and other construction work in the last 12 months.
1. The registration of the applicant should have been renewed annually.

**Eligibility Criteria for Scheme:**

1. The applicant should be engaged in building or construction work under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).
1. The applicant should be registered with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).
1. The applicant’s membership with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.

scheme_name: Mahila Adhikarita Yojana under NSKFDC- Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be a female.
 1. The applicant's age should be between 18 and 55 years.
 1. There is no income limit.
 1. The applicant should be either a woman Safai Karamchari or a dependent daughter of Safai Karamchari.

**Note 1:** Permanent Safai Karamchari means a person engaged in, or employed for any sanitation work and includes his/her dependents.
**Note 2:** The applicant shall have to produce a Certificate of Safai Karamchari or dependent of Safai Karamchari from the Head of the Government Departments (i.e. School, Colleges, Forest, Health, Education, Animal Husbandry having rank not less than Gazetted Officer), elected members of Municipal Body/ Corporation and Pradhan of Gram Panchayats, Local Revenue Officer or an Officer of Local Municipal Office, or Cantonment Executive Officer of the Cantonment or an officer of the Railways not below the rank of gazette officer.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit (A.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Assam.
 1. The applicant should be a woman worker.
 1. The applicant should be engaged in any building & other construction work.
 1. The construction worker should be registered with the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The applicant should have an active membership with the Board.
 1. The benefit under this scheme shall be given for the birth of two children only.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit (ANBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a resident of the Union territory of Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
 1. The beneficiary should be engaged in any building & other construction work.
 1. The beneficiary should be registered under the A&N Islands Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The beneficiary should have an active membership of the Board.
 1. The women employees, who are registered beneficiaries for a period of one year, shall be eligible for maternity benefits during the period of maternity.
 1. The male employees, who are registered beneficiaries for a period of one year, and whose wives are 19 years or older and not registered beneficiaries, shall be eligible for maternity benefits.

scheme_name: Merit Based Award and Recognition Of High Performance In The Board Exams Of SSC And HSSC In The State Of Goa
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Tribe (ST) in the State of Goa.
1. The applicant should have secured 50% or above marks in the SSC/ HSSC Board exams.
1. (For Merit Based Award) The annual income of the family of the applicant should be less than ₹ 5,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantree Baal Aasheervaad Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
**For After Care**

1. Children residing in childcare institutions continuously for 05 years including the year of release date.
1. In the case of an orphan, abandoned child, an exemption will be given in the eligibility related to the period required for residence in the childcare institution.
1. If the child is not getting the benefit of adoption, foster care, but the child is rehabilitated in the child care institution and the period of adoption and foster care will also be counted in the eligibility period.
1. Financial assistance, internship, vocational training, and education will be given for the prescribed time period or till the age of 24 years, whichever is earlier.

**For Sponsorship:**

- Children should be below 18 years of age.
- The child's parents have died. 
- The child should be living with their relative or guardian 
- Relative/guardian should not come under eligibility under Mukhyamantri covid 19 Bal Seva Yojana,

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bijli Bill Bakaya Maaf Samadhan Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
The applicant must be a registered worker under the "Mukhyamantri Mazdoor Kalyan (Sambal) Yojana".
The applicant must be a construction worker or a Below Poverty Line (BPL) consumer.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bhed/Bakri (Sheep/Goat) Palak Uthan Yojana for Scheduled Castes
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Scheduled Caste
1. The applicant should be of 18 to 55 years of age.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana 
1. The applicant should be a Parivar Pehchaan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant should not be employed even in the Private Sector/ Govt. /Semi Govt. Organizations.
1. There would be no mandatory educational qualifications or training requirements.
1. Any group/firm/organisation would not be permitted to apply under the Scheme, as the Scheme being individual beneficiary-oriented.
1. The applicant would have to submit a Self-Declaration in the format annexed as [Annexure-I (Page-9)](https://pashudhanharyana.gov.in/sites/default/files/documents/Scheme%202024-25/BhedBakri%20(SheepGoat)%20Palak%20Utthan%20Yojanafor%20the%20year%202024-25%20(N).pdf) on the portal for being unemployed and for undertaking to abide by other conditions as required under the Scheme.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bhagyalaxmi Bond Scheme (GBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The construction workers registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
 1. The construction workers are eligible to avail the benefits of the scheme in the event of the birth of a girl child.
 1. The benefit of the scheme is given upto one girl child.
 1. The application form should be made within 12 months from birth.

scheme_name: Miscarriage Benefit
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be a registered female member or wife of a male member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
 - The applicant should have gone through a miscarriage.

scheme_name: Maharishi Balmiki Chatravriti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Himachal Pradesh.
2. The applicant should be a girl student.
3. The applicant should belong to the Valmiki Family engaged in Unclean Occupation.
4. The applicant should have been admitted to a matriculation course.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Balika Cycle Yojna
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of Bihar.
 - The applicant must be a girl student.
 - The applicant must be enrolled in a government or government-recognized secondary school.
 - The applicant should have successfully completed Class VIII and be transitioning to Class IX or higher.
 - The scheme applies only to girls from economically weaker sections or as specified by the government.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit/Delivery Assistance (K.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
 - The applicant should be a minimum of 18 years of age.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be a registered woman construction worker Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka.
 - The applicant should be pregnant.
 - Assistance is only for the first two (2) deliveries.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit For Female Workers- JKBOCWWB
eligibility_text_source:
**For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

1. The applicant should be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
1. The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have engaged in any building or other construction work for at least 90 days during the preceding 12 months.
1. The applicant should be registration is renewed annually.

**For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

1. The applicant should be a registered female member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be pregnant.
1. The worker's membership with the Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit for Women Beneficiaries
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a registered women construction worker under the Nagaland Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board or the wife of a registered male worker. 
1. The applicant should be Pregnant.
1. Benefit is available for the first two live births.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit (HPBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
**For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker..
1. The applicant should be aged between 18 and 60 years.
1. The worker should have completed at least 90 days of building and other construction work in the last 12 months.
1. The registration of the applicant should be renewed annually.

**For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

1. The beneficiary should be a woman.
1. The woman should be registered as a worker with the "Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
1. The woman should be pregnant.
1. The worker's membership with the Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.
1. The worker should have at least two months of subscription with the Board.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bal Hridaya Upchar Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
- The applicant must be a member of an Economically Weaker Family or a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
- The applicant must ensure their name is recorded in the family roster and they hold a ration card.
- The intended beneficiary (child) must be aged 0 to 15 years.
- The intended beneficiary (child) must be suffering from one of the identified heart diseases.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bayan Jyoti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- He/ She must be a bonafide weaver/ full-time ancillary worker. 
- He/ She is a permanent resident/ domicile of Odisha.
- The family income from all sources does not exceed ₹3, 00,000/- per annum.
- At least 50% income of the family should be from weaving activities.
- He or she should purchase the lighting unit from a GST-registered dealer with a valid TIN.
- Where more than one member of a family is found eligible, only one of them will be entitled to benefit under this scheme

scheme_name: Mizoram Bana Kaih (Handholding) Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be an individual, group, organization, entrepreneur, or innovator.
 - The applicant should have high potential for wealth and employment generation.
 - The applicant should be selected as a “Progress Partner” by the Apex Body.
 - The applicant should be engaged in livelihood, business, startup, or development activities.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bhavishyalakshi Kaushalya Vikas Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The trainees must be a domicile of Gujarat.
- The trainees must have passed at least 10th Standard / 12th Standard / Industrial Training Institute / Degree-Diploma.
- Trainees must be registered on the portal with Aadhaar number submission by Training Provider.
- Training must be conducted only through trainers as per course guidelines and syllabus.
- On Job Training must be made mandatory as per course requirements, with On Job Training/soft skills to be covered for others.
- The Training Centers must carry branding of the MBKVY scheme.

_*Monitoring will be portal-based and payments will be made based on portal data only._
_*For National Skill Qualification Framework courses, assessment and certification will be done through concerned Sector Skill Councils/Gujarat Council for Vocational Training/Kaushalya The Skill University._ 
_*For new courses based on industry demand, assessment will be conducted as per standards set by Gujarat Council for Vocational Training or Kaushalya The Skill University, with certification provided through Gujarat Council for Vocational Training and Kaushalya The Skill University._

scheme_name: Maa-Badi / Maa-Badi Day-Care Centre Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a child aged 6 to 12 years.
1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe, Kathodi, or Sahariya community.
1. The applicant should reside in Udaipur, Dungarpur, Banswara, Pratapgarh, Sirohi, or Rajsamand districts.
1. The applicant should not currently be enrolled in any formal school.
1. The applicant should reside in areas where the nearest school is at least 1.5–2 km away.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit (MBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Meghalaya.
 1. The applicant should be a woman engaged in building or other construction works.
 1. The applicant should be registered under the Meghalaya Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The applicant should have an active membership of the Board.
 1. Women workers are eligible to avail of maternity benefits during their maternity period.
 1. This benefit shall not be allowed for more than twice.

scheme_name: Marriage Benefit (MBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a resident of Meghalaya.
 1. The beneficiary should be engaged in a building or other construction work.
 1. The beneficiary should be registered under the Meghalaya Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The workers having continuous membership for 3 years shall be eligible to get financial assistance under the scheme.
 1. The registered workers can apply for financial assistance for their own marriage. A registered worker can avail of this assistance once only.
 1. The registered workers can apply for financial assistance for the marriage of all their adult children, subject to the production of proper documentation such as Marriage Certificates, etc.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of Odisha.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be female.
 - The applicant should be a registered member of the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 - The applicant should be registered for a period of one year or more under the board.
 - The applicant should be pregnant.

scheme_name: Mahila Bakri Palan
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a woman.
1. The applicant must be from one of the following categories:

- Abandoned
- Widow
- Destitute
- Single woman
- Disaster-affected woman

scheme_name: Madhu Babu Pension Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant is a permanent resident/domicile of Odisha since 20 years.
 - The applicant should not been convicted of any criminal offence involving moral turpitude.

 - The applicant should qualify for one of the following - 

 1. The age of the applicant is 60 years and above.
 1. The applicant is a widow.
 1. The applicant is a leprosy patient with visible signs of deformity.
 1. The applicant is a person of 5 years of age or, above and unable to do normal work due to his/her deformity or disability being blind or, orthopedically handicapped, mentally retarded, with cerebral palsy.
 1. The applicant is the widow of an AIDS patient.
 1. The applicant is an AIDS patient identified by the State / District AIDS Control Society (irrespective of income as under Rule 6 (b).

 - The applicant is an unmarried woman belonging to BPL category.
 - The applicant has family income from all sources not exceeding ₹ 24,000/- per annum.
 - The applicant is not in receipt of any other pension from the union Government or the state government or any organization aided by either Government.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Balika Protsahan Yojana (Intermediate Pass)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of Bihar.
- The applicant must be an unmarried girl.
- She must have passed the intermediate examination from a recognized school, madrasa, or institution in Bihar.
- The examination must have been conducted in 2021 or later.
- The applicant must have a valid bank account.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Balika Protsahan Yojana (Matriculation)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of the respective state.
 - The applicant must be a girl student.
 - She must have secured a first division in her matriculation examination.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bal Poshan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. Children aged 3 to 6 years are eligible to receive nutritious food under this scheme.

scheme_name: Mhaji Bus Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a private bus operator holding a valid stage carriage permit issued by the competent authority in Goa.
- The applicant must be the registered owner of the bus/mini bus in the State of Goa.
- The bus to be operated under this scheme must not be older than 15 years from the date of its initial registration.
- The applicant must agree to comply with all operational, branding, safety, and service norms specified under this scheme.
- The bus must operate for the full month.
- If the bus is off the road, prior intimation must be given to the competent authority.

***Relaxation***

- For the initial period of 3 years, buses up to 20 years should be eligible to participate in this scheme.
- The bus can be kept for repairs and maintenance for two days only, which should be Saturdays, Sundays, or holidays.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit Scheme (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The women worker should be registered under Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCWWB).
 1. The registered women worker should have a minimum of one-year regular membership.
 1. The registered women worker is not eligible for maternity benefits in case the husband is availing paternity benefits from any department/board/corporation. 

**Note 01:** The benefits of the scheme will be given on the birth of child up to two children but expandable up to the birth of three daughters irrespective of the order of their birth.
**Note 02:** It is mandatory to submit the application form along with all the documents to the competent authority within one year of delivery.
**Note 03:** After the birth of the children, it is necessary to attach the birth certificate along with the application.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit Scheme - Telangana
eligibility_text_source:
> **For construction workers themselves (Women only)**

1. The construction worker should be registered under the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. 
1. The construction worker should be a woman.
1. The construction worker should have been registered for at least 12 months before the date of delivery.
1. The construction work can get this assistance only twice.

> **For wife/daughters of construction workers**

1. The construction workers should be registered under the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The construction worker can receive the scheme benefits twice for up to two daughters.
1. The construction worker should have been registered for at least 12 months before the date of delivery.
1. If both parents are registered workers, only one parent is eligible for the scheme amount.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit Scheme (APB&OCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Arunachal Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a woman.
1. The woman worker must be employed in the Building and Other Construction works.
1. The woman worker should be registered with Labour Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).
1. The applicant's membership must be active at the time of application.

**Note:** The woman worker is not to be entitled to the benefits in case of second marriage.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit Scheme (APBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a registered women construction worker under the APBOCWWB, wives of registered male workers, and their daughters.
1. The applicant should be Pregnant.
1. Benefit is available for the first two live births.
1. The worker should have completed one year of registration with the board before applying.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefits Scheme (BBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be female.
- The applicant should be registered with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (BBOCWWB).
- The applicant should have completed at least one year of membership with the Board.
- The membership must be active at the time of application.
- The benefits will be provided for the first two childbirths.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

 - The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
 - The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
 - The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

 - The beneficiary should have been a registered member of the Board for at least 6 months prior to the birth of the child.
 - The beneficiary should have regularly depositing his contribution.
 - The scheme applies upto two living children.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Balika (Snatak) Protsahan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Bihar.
 1. The applicant must have graduated or obtained an equivalent degree (including Alim or Shastri) after April 25, 2018, from a government-recognized or affiliated institution within Bihar.
 1. The scheme applies to graduates from general, technical, or professional courses.
 1. The applicant must have an account in any nationalized bank, recognized private bank or India Postal Payment Bank located in Bihar.

scheme_name: Merit Based Scholarship Scheme for Dhangar Students
eligibility_text_source:
**For Rank Holders Category**

- The applicant must belong to the Dhangar community.
- The applicant must be among the top 5 rank holders in their respective examination (SSC or HSSC).
- The applicant has no income ceiling restrictions.

**For Merit-based Awards**

- The applicant must belong to the Dhangar community.
- The applicant's annual family income should be less than ₹2,00,000/-.
- The applicant must achieve the minimum qualifying percentage as per the benefit slabs.

scheme_name: Maternity Financial Assistance (UKBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a woman construction worker registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. Financial assistance is admissible only for the birth of up to two children.

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit to the Female Beneficiary
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a female construction worker. 
1. The worker should be registered under the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The applicant should have gone through a miscarriage or medical termination.

scheme_name: Mai Bhago Vidya Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Girl students should be residents of Punjab state.
1. Girl students should be studying in standards 9th,10th, 11th, and 12th in the government school.
1. Girl students should belong to those below the poverty line (BPL).

scheme_name: Maternity Benefit
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be a registered female member or wife of a male member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
 - The applicant should be pregnant.

scheme_name: Magnifiers - Tamil Nadu
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a student. 
1. The beneficiary should be a person with low vision.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bagwani Mission: Aam Vikas Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Bihar.
 1. The applicant should be a ryot farmer.
 1. The applicant must have agricultural land minimum of 0.25 acres (0.1 ha) and a maximum of 10 acres (4 ha).
 1. The applicant must be registered on the DBT portal.

&gt; **Note 1: Beneficiary allocation:**

 - General Category: 78.56%.
 - Scheduled Castes: 20%.
 - Scheduled Tribes: 1.44%.
 - Women Participation: 30%.

&gt; **Note 2:** The benefits of this scheme can be taken by ryot farmers on the basis of land documents and non-ryot farmers on the basis of contract form.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bagwani Mission: Anjeer Vikas Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Bihar.
 1. The applicant should be a ryot farmer.
 1. The applicant must have agricultural land a minimum of 0.25 acres (0.1 ha) and a maximum of 10 acres (4 ha).
 1. The applicant must be registered on the DBT portal.

> **Note 1: Beneficiary allocation:**

 - General Category: 78.56%.
 - Scheduled Castes: 20%.
 - Scheduled Tribes: 1.44%.
 - Women Participation: 30%.

> **Note 2:** Beneficiaries will be selected on first come first serve basis.

scheme_name: Makhana Beej Vitaran
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a farmer.
 1. The applicant should belong to any of the following districts of Bihar: Katihar, Purnia, Madhubani, Kishanganj, Supaul, Araria, Madhepura, Saharsa, Darbhanga, Sitamarhi and Khagaria.
 1. The applicant must have agricultural land a minimum of 0.25 acres (0.1 ha) and a maximum of 10 acres (4 ha).
 1. The applicant must be registered on the DBT portal.

> **Note 1****: Beneficiary allocation:** Women Participation: 30%.

> **Note 2:****** The benefits of this scheme can be taken by ryot farmers on the basis of land documents and non-ryot farmers on the basis of contract form.

> **Note 3:****** Beneficiaries will be selected on first come first serve basis.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bagwani Mission: Dragon Fruit Vikas Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Bihar.
 1. The applicant should be a ryot farmer.
 1. The applicant must be from any of the following districts: Bhojpur, Gopalganj, Jehanabad, Saran, Siwan, Supaul, Aurangabad, Begusarai, Bhagalpur, Gaya, Katihar, Kishanganj, Munger, Muzaffarpur, Nalanda, West Champaran, Patna, East Champaran, Purniyan, Samastipur and Vaishali.
 1. The applicant must have agricultural land a minimum of 0.25 acres (0.1 ha) and a maximum of 10 acres (4 ha).
 1. The applicant must be registered on the DBT portal.

> **Note 1: Beneficiary allocation:**

 - General Category: 78.56%.
 - Scheduled Castes: 20%.
 - Scheduled Tribes: 1.44%.
 - Women Participation: 30%.

> **Note 2:** Beneficiaries will be selected on first come first serve basis.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Bagwani Mission: Kela Vikas Yojana
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Mid Day Meals Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- Students in Government and Government-aided schools: The scheme primarily targets students studying in government and government-aided schools across India. Children enrolled in classes from the primary level up to the secondary level are eligible to benefit from the scheme.
- Age criteria: The scheme typically covers children between the ages of 6 and 14 years. However, specific age requirements may vary slightly from state to state or depending on the educational policies of different regions.
- Socio-economic criteria: The Mid Day Meals Scheme aims to benefit children from economically disadvantaged backgrounds. Typically, children belonging to families with below-poverty-line (BPL) status or those from marginalized communities are given priority for enrollment in the scheme.
- School attendance: One of the key eligibility criteria for availing the benefits of the scheme is regular school attendance. Students are expected to attend school regularly to receive the mid-day meals.
- Enrolled in recognized schools: Children must be enrolled in schools recognized by the appropriate education authorities to be eligible for the scheme. The scheme is primarily implemented in schools that adhere to the government's educational standards and guidelines.

1. The minimum number of ST candidates shall be enrolled is 40 students.

eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be MSME unit those who have obtained UAM/EM(Part-ll)/Permanent Registration.
2. The applicant should be a "Sick" Industrial Enterprise. "Sickness" is defined as follows at present and shall be revised as per RBI guidelines from time to time:

- Continuous decline in gross output compared to the previous two financial years.
- Delay in repayment of loan, taken from institutional sources, for more than 12 months.
- Erosion in net worth due to the accumulated losses to the extent of 50% of the net worth during the previous accounting year.

3. Other firms taking over a sick unit for rehabilitation will also be eligible.

scheme_name: Migrants Employment Generation Programme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should have returned to Tamil Nadu on & after 01-01-2020 due to the Covid-19 outbreak.
 1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 to 45 years in general and for special categories (Women/Minorities/ BC/MBC/SC/ST/Ex-servicemen/Transgender/Differently abled), the age relaxation is up to 55 years.
 1. The applicant should possess a minimum educational qualification of 8th standard pass.
 1. The family income of the applicant should be below ₹5.00 lakh.
 1. The maximum project costs for Manufacturing / Trading / Service projects should be ₹15.00 lakh / ₹5.00 lakh / ₹5.00 lakh respectively.
 1. The applicant’s/promoter’s contribution should be 10% of the project cost for the General category and 5% for the Special Category.

scheme_name: Modular Employable Skills
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a school leaver, existing worker, or ITI graduate seeking vocational training to improve employability.
 1. The applicant should be willing to undergo skill assessment, training, or certification under the scheme.
 1. The applicant should meet the course-specific eligibility norms prescribed by the concerned training institution or industry.

scheme_name: Matritva, Shishu Evam Baalika Madad Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
**Matritva Hitlabh (Maternity benefits):**

1. Beneficiary women must be a permanent resident of Uttar Pradesh.
1. Beneficiary women must be a registered construction worker.
1. Beneficiary women must be above 18 years of age.
1. Benefits will be provided only to the first two deliveries of the worker.

**Shishu Hitlabh (Child benefits)**

1. First girl child born in the family (up to one girl child).
1. Second girl child only if both first and second child are girls.
1. If more than one girl child in first and second delivery, all girls are eligible.
1. Applies to legally adopted girl child (first girl only) in childless families.
1. Birth of girl child must be registered.
1. Girl must remain unmarried until 18 to receive fixed deposit.
1. Benefits will not be provided eligible if recipient of another similar government scheme.

scheme_name: Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of Odisha.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be a registered member of the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board or the applicant should be a dependent of the member.
 - The beneficiary should be registered at least for one year in line of the Odisha State Treatment Fund.
 - The applicant should suffer from any major diseases.

scheme_name: Medical Expenses-Treatment for Major Ailments Including Surgery
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
 - The member or his/ her dependents should suffer from Cancer/ Heart disease/ Kidney disease/ T.B./ Eye disease/ Leprosy/ Nerve disease.

scheme_name: Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments of a Beneficiary
eligibility_text_source:
1. The construction worker should be registered under the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board. 
1. The construction worker's family members (spouse and children) are eligible for financial assistance under this scheme. 
1. The construction worker or family members should suffer from T.B., Cancer, Heart Disease, Kidney Disease, Eye Disease, Leprosy, HIV Disease and Nerve Disease.

scheme_name: Modular Functional Artificial Limbs
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an orthopedically differently-abled person.
1. The applicant should be a resident of the area/district of Tamil Nadu. 
1. The applicant should be any one of the following:

 - Student.
 - Employed.
 - Self-Employed.

scheme_name: Mobile Food Court Van (DAADC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.
1. The applicant should belong to an Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) family.
1. The applicant’s annual family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should provide two guarantors to get the loan.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Mini Fish Feed Mills of Production Capacity of 2 Ton /Day
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant/beneficiary should be a resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID).
 1. All operational, maintenance, and post-construction management costs of the infrastructure facilities shall be borne by the beneficiary.
 1. The beneficiary should ensure that the Fish Feed Mill remains in functional condition.
 1. The beneficiary should supply the feed produced from the government-assisted Fish Feed Mill/Plant to fishers and fish farmers at an affordable price.
 1. The applicant should either own the land or have it on a registered lease agreement valid for a minimum of 10 years.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Medium Fish Feed Mill of Production Capacity of 8 Ton/Day
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant should possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID).
1. The applicant should ensure that the medium fish feed mill is in working condition.
1. The applicant should ensure the feed produced from the unit is supplied at affordable price to fishers and farmers.
1. The fish feed mill should be on land owned by the applicant or on a long-term registered lease (minimum of 10 years).

scheme_name: Micro Finance (GBCDC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to a socially and educationally backwards class.
1. The total annual income of the applicant's family should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant's age should be between 21 and 45 years as on the date of application.
1. The applicant must have experience in a business requiring technical and other skills.

scheme_name: Micro Finance Scheme (GNDTDC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should belong to the Nomadic Tribe or Denotified Tribe.
1. The applicant's family's annual income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant's age should be between 21 to 50 years on the date of application.
1. The applicant should have experience & technical skill in the proposed trade/business.
1. The applicant will have to provide suitable collateral to get the loan.

**Note:** At least 50% of the total loan amount under this scheme will be specifically allocated to families with an annual income of up to ₹1,50,000/-.

scheme_name: Mission for Integrated Horticulture Development (MIDH)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a farmer
 - The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
 - The applicant should intend to cultivate horticultural crops or undertake related activities.

scheme_name: Monthly/Family Pension for Freedom Fighters and their Widows of Uttarakhand State
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The applicant must be either a Freedom Fighter or the widow of a Freedom Fighter.

scheme_name: Micro Finance Scheme- Credit Line 1 for Minority Community
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 and 55 years.
 1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹98,000/- (Rural Area) and ₹1,20,000/- (Urban Area).
 1. The applicant should belong to a minority community, i.e., Muslims, Sikhs, Christians, Buddhists, Parsis, and Jains.
 1. In an SHG 75% of members can be from Minority Communities and the remaining 25% members may be from other weaker sections like SC/Handicapped/Minorities etc. in very exceptional cases, groups with up to 60% members belonging to Minority Communities will also be eligible.
 1. The applicant should have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP ID).

scheme_name: Micro Finance Scheme- Credit Line 2 for Minority Community
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 and 55 years.
 1. The annual family income of the applicant should be between ₹98,001/- to ₹8,00,000/- (Rural Area) and ₹1,20,001/- to ₹8,00,000/- (Urban Area).
 1. The applicant should belong to a minority community, i.e., Muslims, Sikhs, Christians, Buddhists, Parsis, and Jains.
 1. In an SHG 75% of members can be from Minority Communities and the remaining 25% members may be from other weaker sections like SC/Handicapped/Minorities etc. in very exceptional cases, groups with up to 60% members belonging to Minority Communities will also be eligible.
 1. The applicant should have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP ID).

scheme_name: Micro Finance Scheme of NMDFC
eligibility_text_source:
a) The scheme is implemented through Self Help Groups (SHGs) or Thrift and Credit Groups (TCGs) comprising members of minority communities.
b) At least 75% of the members of each SHG/TCG must belong to notified minority communities. The remaining up to 25% may belong to weaker sections, including Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Other Backward Classes, or Persons with Disabilities.

In exceptional cases, groups with minimum 60% minority members may also be considered.
c) The annual family income of each beneficiary must not exceed:
Rs. 3.00 lakh under Credit Line–1.
Rs. 8.00 lakh under Credit Line–2.
d) The SHG should have a satisfactory record of thrift and credit activity for at least six months.
e) The beneficiaries should not be defaulters under any other loan scheme.
f) Loans shall be used for income-generating activities such as petty trade, handicrafts, food processing, and service enterprises.
g) Preference shall be given to women SHGs and groups operating in rural and minority-concentrated areas.

scheme_name: Microcredit Finance Scheme- Self Help Groups (DAADC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must belong to a Scheduled Caste (SC) community.
1. The applicant must be part of an Antyodaya (AAY) family.
1. The applicant’s annual family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should be a self-help group of unemployed persons. 
1. The applicant must be a member of a registered self-help group (SHG) or apply individually with valid business intent.

scheme_name: Micro Finance Scheme for Self Help Groups (GTKVN)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should be a member of a Self Help Group (SHG).
1. The applicant should belong to the Thakor or Koli caste.
1. The applicant's age should be at least 21 years and not more than 50 years as on the date of application.
1. The applicant’s annual family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should have relevant experience in the proposed trade/business field.
1. The applicant will have to provide suitable collateral to get the loan.

> **Note:** At least 50% of the total loan allocation is reserved for those with family income up to ₹1,50,000/-.

scheme_name: Small Business (GBCDC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to a socially and educationally backward class.
1. The applicant's total annual individual/family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Gramin Awas Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must belong to rural area.
 1. The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, or Extremely Backward Class category.
 1. The applicant must reside in a cluster housing scheme built under previous government programs before January 1996.
 1. The applicant must have a house that is currently in a dilapidated condition.

scheme_name: Mahatama Gandhi Bunkar Bima Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The weaver should be earning at least 50% of his income from handloom weaving.
 1. All weavers, whether male or female, between the age group of 18 and 59 years are eligible to be covered under the scheme, including minorities, women weavers, and weavers belonging to NER.
 1. The weavers belonging to the State Handloom Development Corporations/Apex/ Primary Handloom Weavers’ Cooperative Societies will be covered under the Scheme.
 1. Weavers outside co-operative can also be covered under the scheme on a certificate from the State Directorate of Handlooms that they are fulfilling the eligibility conditions.
 1. The children of weavers who are covered under “Shiksha Sahyog Yojana” will also be eligible for scholarship and the benefit of scholarship is restricted to two children of the member covered. Both the children irrespective of gender based discrimination will be covered for scholarship.

**NOTE:**

 1. It will be the responsibility of the State Director in charge of Handlooms to verify the eligibility of the weavers who are proposed to be covered under the scheme.

scheme_name: Maintenance of Govt Boys and Girls Hostels (SC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant must have passed class 10th (SSC) or higher from GSEB/CBSE.1. The applicant must be enrolled in a regular course in a college within the same district as the hostel.
1. The boy student's annual family income must not exceed ₹2,50,000. No income limit for girl students.
1. The applicant must have scored at least 50% marks in the last annual exam.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Gambhir Bimari Upchar Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant’s gross annual income must be below ₹8,00,000/- for three consecutive years (not applicable for acid attack victims).
1. The applicant must suffer from one of the specified illnesses:

 - All types of cancer.
 - Kidney transplant.
 - Severe liver diseases.

scheme_name: Marriage Grant of Rs.51,000/- to Daughter/Sister of Personnel Killed/ Disabled in War/ Action/Terrorist Attack/Naxalite Attack/IS Duty
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a dependent (sister or daughter) of a martyred officer or soldier.
1. The deceased officer/soldier must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The martyrdom must have occurred during war, military operation, Naxalite attack, terrorist attack, or internal security duty.

scheme_name: Meghalaya Goat Farming Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
**Individuals**

1. The applicant should be between 18 and 58 years of age.
1. The entrepreneur should possess land, either self-owned or on lease, verified by the competent authority.
1. The entrepreneur should possess a training certificate on the sector applied for from a recognized Institution, preferably from a Government Institute.
1. The Preference for availing loans will be given to entrepreneurs and entities with prior experience of undertaking Goatery.
1. The entrepreneur should have access to adequate and quality feed for the goats.
1. The entrepreneur should have a vaccination Scheme in place for the goats.
1. The entrepreneur should have a plan for marketing the goats and their products.
1. The entrepreneurs village should be in an area where goat rearing is feasible.
1. The distance between the entrepreneur’s village and the nearest market must be reasonable.
1. The entrepreneur should not be a credit defaulter from any bank.

**Registered Entities**

1. Any group/entity registered in Meghalaya such as Cooperatives, Farmer Producer Companies (FPC), Farmer Producer Organization (FPO) and so on
1. Must provide an Incorporation Certificate or a registration certificate whichever is applicable
1. At least two members should possess a training certificate on the sector applied for from a recognized institution, preferably from a government institute
1. Should possess land, either self-owned or on lease, verified by the competent authority
1. Should possess a training

scheme_name: Mizoram Grant-in-Aid to Voluntary Organisations and Individuals
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Organizations/Institutions:**

- Must be a registered voluntary organization, society, trust, or institution.
- Minimum 3 years of experience in welfare activities (relaxable).
- Should have a proper management structure.
- Must be financially sound.
- Programmes must be non-discriminatory.

> **For Individuals:** The applicant must be any of the following:

- Senior citizens (65+ years, BPL).
- Destitute women and children.
- Persons with disabilities (≥40%).
- Victims of domestic violence or trafficking.
- Orphans, juvenile delinquents.
- Drug users, alcoholics, HIV/AIDS affected persons.

scheme_name: Maintenance Grant to Institutions and Organizations Engaged in Cultural Activities
eligibility_text_source:
1. A cultural organization should be engaged in cultural activities for a minimum period of two years. 
1. The institution's accounts should be duly audited and approved by a competent person or body, as per the rules outlined in the Memorandum of Association.
1. A cultural organization should not be run for profit to any individual or a body of individuals.
1. Institutions should have submitted utilization certificates for all previously received grants to be eligible for new grants.

scheme_name: Marang Gomke Jaipal Singh Munda Overseas Scholarship Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Jharkhand state.
1. The applicant must belong to Scheduled Tribes, Scheduled Castes, Minorities, or Backward Classes.
1. The applicant's maximum age should be 35 years at the time of application.
1. The applicant should hold a bachelor's degree and have outstanding academic performance.
1. The applicant must have an admission offer from a top 100 globally ranked university or a UK university listed in Annexure-A.

scheme_name: Marriage Grant for the Marriage of the Daughter of War Disabled Ex-Servicemen above 50% Disability
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be a war-disabled ex-serviceman with a disability assessed above 50%.
 1. The daughter should be at least 18 years old.
 1. The applicant can receive the benefit for up to two daughters only.

scheme_name: Marriage Grant for the Marriage of Daughters of Widows of Ex-servicemen (other than War Widows)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be the widow of an ex-serviceman who did not die in the war (other than a war widow).
 1. The daughter should be at least 18 years old.
 1. The widow can receive the benefit for up to two of her daughters.
 1. The widow should not have remarried. If remarried, she should only have married the real brother of her late husband.

**Note:** The entire verification of claimants will be carried out at the level of the Secretaries of Zila Sainik Boards as hither-to-fore.

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be at least 18 years of age.
1. The applicant must be residing in a Rural Area.

scheme_name: Marriage Grant to Orphan Daughter of Ex-Servicemen
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bona fide resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be a dependent orphan daughter of an ex-serviceman.
 1. The orphan daughter should be at least 18 years old.
 1. Assistance is available for up to two orphan daughters of an ex-serviceman.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Gramin Peyjal Nishchay Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of a rural area in Bihar.
 1. The applicant must not already have a piped water supply connection.
 1. The applicant should belong to a household requiring access to safe drinking water.
 1. The applicant must comply with the guidelines set by the Gram Panchayat.
 1. The applicant should be willing to contribute to the operation and maintenance of the water supply system.

scheme_name: Mill Gate Price Subsidy Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a handloom cooperative society, NGO, or development center.
- The applicant must purchase yarn from NHDC-recognized depots (e.g., Gujarat State Handloom & Handicrafts Development Corporation).

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Gram Parivahan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of the state.
 - The applicant must belong to a rural area.
 - The applicant should not have benefited from a similar scheme previously.
 - For ambulance purchases, applicants must be willing to use it for public health services.

scheme_name: Marriage Grant Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. To the daughters of poor individuals of other backward classes (excluding minorities in backward classes).
 1. The annual income of the applicant in urban areas should not exceed Rs. 56,460/- and in rural areas Rs. 46,080/-.
 1. The age of the girl and boy must be 18 years and 21 years or more respectively.
 1. Applicants of other backward classes will have to enter the serial number of the online Caste Certificate issued by the Tehsil in their online application form. 
 1. Priority will be provided to the destitute woman after the death of the husband or widow applicant and they will not be needing a caste certificate.
 1. Marriage grants will be allowed up to a maximum of 02 daughters from a family.

scheme_name: Marriage Gift Scheme - Telangana
eligibility_text_source:
> **For construction workers themselves (Women only)**

1. Construction worker should be registered under the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. 
1. Construction worker should be a woman.
1. Construction worker should be unmarried. 

> **For daughters of construction workers**

1. Construction workers (either one or both parents) should be registered under the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. A construction worker can receive the scheme benefits for up to two daughters.
1. The daughter(s) should have completed 18 years of age at the time of application.
1. If both parents are registered workers, only one parent is eligible for the scheme amount.

scheme_name: Marriage Gift Scheme (APBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
> **For construction workers themselves (Women only)**

1. Construction worker should be registered under the Andhra Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. 
1. Construction worker should be a woman.
1. Construction worker should be unmarried. 

> **For daughters of construction workers**

1. A construction worker can receive the scheme benefits for up to two daughters.
1. The daughter(s) should have completed 18 years of age at the time of marriage.

scheme_name: Museum Grant Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
The applicant should belong to Category I, II, or III as defined:

- Category I: Central or State Government-owned museums in state capitals, including Public Sector Undertakings (PSU) museums.
- Category II: Central or State Government-owned museums, including PSU museums, not necessarily in state capitals.
- Category III: All other museums, including PSU museums.

1. The applicant should be located in the capital of the state or union territory (for certain categories).
1. The applicant should have a significant collection of historical or cultural objects/artifacts.
1. The applicant should be a museum of high repute in its region.
1. The applicant should have an annual footfall of at least 1 lakh visitors per year in the preceding two years.
1. The applicant should have been in existence for at least three years prior to application.
1. Note: This requirement may be relaxed in exceptional cases, with written justification by the Expert Committee.
1. The applicant should have a well-defined constitution and laid-down rules/bye-laws for functioning.
1. The applicant should not be run for personal profit.
1. The applicant should own and possess a substantial collection of objects for display.
1. The applicant should be able to maintain the museum and bear all recurring costs, including utilities, maintenance, and staff.
1. The applicant should have adequate infrastructure, resources, and trained personnel to execute the work for which the grant is requested.
1. The applicant

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Gramin Street Vendor Rin Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must reside in Madhya Pradesh.
- The applicant must reside in a rural area.
- The applicant must be between 18 and 55 years of age.
- The applicant must belong to the poor class.
- The applicant must be a migrant worker or a small trader (Street Vendor).
- The applicant must be applying for a loan for an existing enterprise located in a rural area.
- The applicant must be a migrant worker establishing a new enterprise.

scheme_name: "Monthly Grant To Old Crafts Persons (Pension)" Component of the "Development of Handicrafts" Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be an artisan who has attained the age of 60 years.
- The artisan should be a native or resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
- The artisan should have practised the craft for at least 5 years in the Union Territory of Puducherry.
- The family income from all sources should not exceed ₹40,000 per annum.
- The applicant should not be a recipient of similar financial assistance from any other source.

scheme_name: Marriage Grant to War Widows’ Daughters of Ex-Servicemen
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Haryana.
- The applicant should be the widow of an ex-serviceman who died in the war.
- The daughter should be at least 18 years old.
- The widow can receive the benefit for up to two of her daughters.

scheme_name: Manav Garima Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
 1. The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state.
 1. The age limit of the applicant should be 18 to 60 years.
 1. The applicant from the Scheduled Caste Category should have an annual family income of up to ₹6,00,000/-.
 1. There is no income limit for applicants belonging to the Most Backward Caste among the Scheduled Castes category.
 1. If the beneficiary or any family members of the beneficiary have already benefited from this scheme, they are not eligible to avail of its benefits again.

scheme_name: Maintenance of Horti Hubs
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicants should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
1. The applicant should be a bonafide farmer engaged in agriculture.
1. The applicant/farmer should own or lease land of at least 0.2 hectares or more.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Hunar Vikas Yojana- Higher Education/Technical Education
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a beneficiary under the Palanhar Scheme of Rajasthan.
 1. The applicant should have completed 15 years of age.
 1. The applicant’s maximum age should not exceed 21 years or till course completion (whichever is earlier).
 1. The applicant should be pursuing education from a university/college/technical educational institution recognized by the Government of India/State Government.
 1. Financial assistance is applicable for regular higher or technical education at the graduate or postgraduate level.
 1. The applicant should not be receiving any scholarships or grants for higher/technical education from the Central or State Government.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Hunar Vikas Yojana- Professional/Technical Training
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a beneficiary under the Palanhar Scheme of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should have completed 15 years of age.
1. The applicant’s maximum age should not exceed 21 years, or till course completion (whichever is earlier).

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Hunar Vikas Yojana- Self-Employment/Business
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a beneficiary under the Palanhar Scheme of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should have completed skill training under the scheme.
1. The applicant’s age should be 21 years or above.
1. The applicant should be registered their business through Udyam Aadhaar Portal is required for self-employment assistance.

scheme_name: "Marketing Incentive" under the "Handloom Marketing Assistance" Component of the National Handloom Development Programme
eligibility_text_source:
The applicant must be one of the following:

- Handloom agencies including State level organizations such as Handloom Corporations, Apex Cooperative Societies and National Level Handloom Organizations, Primary Handloom Weavers Cooperative Societies, Producer Companies, Self Help Groups, Joint Liability Groups, Federations and Other eligible Handloom entities.
- The applicant must have made the final transaction of product sale to the consumer.
- The applicant must use Handloom Mark or India Handloom Brand for their handloom products.
- The applicant must have average sales turnover of handloom products for the last 3 years for incentive calculation.

scheme_name: Matrimonial Incentives
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Maharashtra State.
1. The applicant should be a Person with Disability (Visually Handicapped, Low Vision, Hearing Impaired, Orthopedically Handicapped, etc).
1. The percentage of the disability should be 40% or above.
1. The applicant should have been married to a non-disabled person.

scheme_name: Mission on Integrated Development of Horticulture- NHM and HMNEH
eligibility_text_source:
> The following categories of beneficiaries are eligible to receive assistance under various components of the scheme:

- Individual Farmers
- Farmer-Producer Organizations (FPOs)
- Farmer Interest Groups (FIGs)
- Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
- Cooperative Societies
- Partnership Firms
- Proprietary Firms
- Companies
- Corporations
- Local Bodies
- Agricultural Produce Market Committees (APMCs)
- State Government Entities (for eligible components)

scheme_name: Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture Sub Scheme Horticulture Mission for North East and Himalayan Region- Meghalaya
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Manav Kalyan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicants must be between 18 and 60 years of age to qualify for the scheme.
- The applicants from Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), or Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) nomadic tribes are eligible without any income proof requirement.
- The applicants from general categories must have a family annual income of ₹6,00,000/- or less, and must submit an income certificate as proof.

**Additional Requirement for Rural Applicants**
The rural applicants must - 
a) either be listed in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) list with a poverty score of 0 to 16.
b) or submit an income certificate issued by the relevant authority.

**Income Criteria Exemptions**
The income limit does not apply to the following:
a) Individuals from the 12 most backward sub-castes within the Scheduled Castes.
b) Members of the most backward, nomadic, or de-notified tribes under the SEBC category.
_*For all other applicants, the annual family income must not exceed ₹6,00,000/-, whether residing in rural or urban areas._
