1. Resident of Gujarat: Only women farmers who are residents of Gujarat are eligible to apply.
1. Age Criteria: There is no specific age limit for the scheme. Women farmers of all age groups can apply.
1. Income Criteria: There is no specific income criteria for the scheme. However, preference is given to women farmers who belong to economically weaker sections.
1. Land Holding: Women farmers who own or operate agricultural land are eligible to apply. The size of the landholding is not a criterion for eligibility.
1. Registration: Women farmers have to register themselves with the Department of Agriculture and Co-operation to apply for the scheme.
1. Group Formation: Women farmers can also form groups and apply for the scheme collectively..

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Krishak Samriddhi Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a registered farmer.
- The applicant must have registered for the e-Upajan Yojana.
- The applicant must be registered in the e-Upajan system within the specified period (e.g., between 28 March and 26 May for Wheat eligibility in Rabi Marketing Season 2017-18).
- The applicant must have cultivated and sold the eligible crops (Paddy, Wheat, Gram, Lentil, or Mustard) during the specified marketing season.
- The applicant must sell the produce up to the limit of their registration/productivity/production capacity.
- The applicant must have sold Wheat during the Rabi Marketing Season 2017-18 between 15 March 2018 and 26 May 2018 to receive the ₹265/- per quintal incentive.
- The applicant must have sold Gram, Lentil, or Mustard during the Rabi Marketing Season 2017-18 between 10 April 2018 and 9 June 2018 to receive the ₹100/- per quintal incentive.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Kalyani Vivah Sahayta Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a widowed woman (Kalyani). 
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh, and her spouse must also be a permanent resident.
1. The applicant must be 18 years or older, and her husband must be 21 years or older at the time of marriage.
1. The applicant must not be an income taxpayer.
1. The applicant must not be a government employee of the state, central government, or government-funded corporations and institutions.
1. The applicant must not be receiving a family pension from any government source.
1. There is no restriction of BPL under the scheme.
1. The husband must not have any living wife from a previous marriage.
1. There will be no compulsion to marry in a mass marriage ceremony for the Kalyani Vivah Yojana; individual marriages will also be considered valid.
1. The Kalyani has minor children, the responsibility for the children's upkeep will be jointly borne by the Kalyani and her husband.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Kanya Vivah Yojana- Madhya Pradesh
eligibility_text_source:
1. The parents of the bride/groom should be natives of Madhya Pradesh.
 1. Financial assistance will be provided to girls, widows, and abandoned women from destitute or poor families in the state at the time of their marriage.
 1. The bride should be at least 18 years old, and the groom should be at least 21 years old.
 1. The applicant should belong to the General, Other Backward Class, Scheduled Caste, or Scheduled Tribe category.
 1. There are no income restrictions for receiving assistance under the scheme.
 1. The bride and groom should participate in the mass marriage ceremony organized on the prescribed dates.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will not be available for single marriage ceremonies.

**Note 1:** Abandoned women who have been legally divorced are only eligible under the scheme.
**Note 2:** If the groom is from outside the state, the bride will still be eligible for the benefits of the scheme. However, if the parents of either the bride/groom are not natives of the state, the benefits under the scheme will not be available.
**Note 3:** The bride should not have been previously married. This means that the marriage of the bride and groom must take place solely during a mass marriage ceremony organized under the scheme, and they should not have been married through a single or any other mass marriage ceremony before.
**Note 4:** A mass marriage ceremony will be organized only if applications are received from a minimum of 5 couples.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Kanyadaan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. Girl whose father is not alive and whose guardian’s total income does not exceed ₹35,000/- per annum (excluding MNREGA income).
1. Girl whose father is incapacitated or bedridden due to physical or mental disability, including prolonged illness, and whose family income does not exceed ₹35,000 /- per annum (excluding MNREGA income).
1. Orphan girls/neglected girls and women, including inmates of Nari Niketan and former inmates of Balika Ashrams, with no regular source of income.
1. Deserted/divorced women whose income does not exceed ₹35,000/- per annum (excluding MNREGA income).
1. Girls/women in moral danger or rescued from immoral trafficking.
1. Destitute women/girls released after imprisonment under any law.
1. Daughters of divorced/deserted women whose annual income does not exceed ₹35,000/- per annum (excluding MNREGA income).

scheme_name: MMKY-Financial Assistance for New Boat, Engine, and Net
eligibility_text_source:
**The following are the eligibility criteria of the beneficiary:**

 1. The beneficiary should be a resident of Odisha and must possess identity and residential proof including an Aadhaar Card.
 1. The beneficiary should have a Bank Account in his/her name in the State of Odisha.
 1. The beneficiary should have QR Coded Aadhaar Card.
 1. The beneficiary should not have received any financial assistance from Government /any Agency towards the purchase of any new boat/engine in the last 5 Years (the beneficiary should not have availed subsidy after 1st April 2018) and net in the last 3 Years (the beneficiary should not have availed subsidy after 1st April 2020).
 1. While availing subsidy, the beneficiary family will be considered instead of individual members of the family. One beneficiary family should not be provided with multiple benefits under one particular scheme. A family will not be eligible for subsidy under the same scheme if any of its members have received a subsidy during the last three years.

scheme_name: "Input Assistance to Farmers for Taking up Fish Farming in Farm Ponds" Component of the "Mukhyamantri Maschyajibi Kalyan Yojana" Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Odisha.
1. The applicant must have a bank account in his/her name in Odisha.
1. The farm pond should have been excavated by the applicant.
1. The farm pond should have been excavated under the various Government schemes including Watershed Development Projects & MGNREGA through the Directorate of Soil Conservation & Watershed Development.
1. The farm pond should have a minimum 5 feet water depth.
1. The farm pond should have sufficient water retaining capacity for a minimum 5 months of culture period.
1. While availing subsidy, the beneficiary family will be considered instead of individual member of the family.

scheme_name: MMKY-New Tank Excavation Under Brackish Water Aquaculture
eligibility_text_source:
**The following are the eligibility criteria for the beneficiaries:**

 1. The beneficiary should be a resident of Odisha and must possess identity and residential proof including an Aadhaar Card. 
 1. The beneficiary should have a bank account in his/her name in the State of Odisha.
 1. The beneficiary must have requisite land free from all encumbrances. 
 1. The beneficiary who has not availed assistance under this component shall be eligible for a subsidy maximum of up to 2.00 Ha water spread area.
 1. The beneficiary who has availed similar subsidy earlier for excavation of Brackish water ponds under different schemes of Govt. of India or the State Government for less than 2.00 Ha. water spread area, he/she shall also be eligible to avail further subsidy for balance area keeping an overall maximum limit of up to 2.00 Ha. under the scheme.
 1. While availing subsidy, the beneficiary family will be considered instead of individual members of the family. One beneficiary family should not be provided with multiple benefits under one particular scheme. A family will not be eligible for subsidy under the same scheme if any of its members have gotten a subsidy during the last three years.

**Note 01:** Preference will be given for the selection of beneficiaries in cluster mode.
**Note 02:** The documentary evidence in support of the suitable land needs to be provided by the beneficiary. (Copy of the RoR/Copy of the lease agreement in case of lease land for a minimum period of se

scheme_name: "Reactivation Of Fisheries Cooperative Societies" Component of the "Mukhyamantri Maschyajibi Kalyan Yojana" Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be an active Primary Fisherman Cooperative Society (PFCS).
- The PFCS should have completed at least 1 year from the date of its registration under "The Odisha Cooperative Societies Act-1962".
- The PFCS should be in working condition.
- The PFCS should not have availed the revolving fund before.
- The PFCS should be conducting Committee of Management meetings and AGB meetings in time as per the OCS Act and Rules framed there under the Bylaws of the Society.
- The PFCS should have paid the Government lease value of the reservoir/ pond if taken on lease as per the Rules.
- The PFCS should be able to produce last year's audited balance sheets with Profit Loss accounts of the Society from AGCS-empanelled CA firms/auditors.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Mahalakshmi Kit Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The beneficiary must be a pregnant woman registered with an Anganwadi Centre and eligible to receive a Mahalakshmi Kit after delivery.
 1. The beneficiary must not be an income tax payer.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri - Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a female.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Maharashtra state.
1. The applicant's age should be between 21-65 years.
1. The applicant should have their bank account with an Aadhaar link.
1. The annual income of the applicant's family should not exceed ₹2,50,000/-. 
1. Outsourced employees, voluntary workers, and contract workers with income up to ₹2,50,000/- are eligible.
1. The applicant should be any one of the following:

 - Married Woman 
 - Widowed 
 - Divorced Woman
 - Abandoned and Destitute Women 
 - One Unmarried Woman in the family

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Alpasankhyak Medhwi Balika Protsahan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must belong to a minority community in Uttarakhand.
2. The applicant must be a girl student.
3. The girl student must have secured 60% or more marks as an institutional candidate in the High School Examination of the Uttarakhand Secondary Education Board / Madrasa Board or its equivalent (Munshi/Maulvi), or in the Intermediate Examination or its equivalent (Alim).
4. The applicant’s mother/father/guardian should belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category, or their annual family income from all sources should not exceed:

 - ₹81,000 in rural areas
 - ₹1,03,000 in urban areas

5. The girl student must be unmarried.
6. The age of the girl student should not be more than 20 years.
7. A maximum of two daughters from the same family can avail of this benefit.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Mahila Nirman Shramik Sammaan Yojana (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a woman engaged in building or construction work.
 1. The applicants should be registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered woman worker must have a minimum one-year regular membership.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will be given to the registered female worker on renewal of her membership every year.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Mahila Sashaktikaran Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a rape victim woman or girl. 
 1. The applicant should be a BPL woman saved from trafficking. 
 1. The applicant should be an acid attack victim. 
 1. The applicant should be a woman who completed their prison sentence. 
 1. The applicant should be a BPL woman who is abandoned/ divorced. 
 1. The applicant should be a girl/woman in distress residing in government and non-government shelter homes, girls’ homes, maintenance homes, etc.
 1. The applicant should be a dowry victim woman.
 1. The applicant should be a victim of child marriage.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Matritva Sahayata Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
> **The following are eligible to apply for the scheme:**

 - Women belonging to the Scheduled Castes(SC) and the Scheduled Tribes (ST).
 - Women who are partially (40%) or fully disabled (Divyang Jan).
 - Women holding of BPL ration card.
 - Women beneficiaries under Pradhan Mantri Jan Aarogya Yojana (PMJAY) under Ayushman Bharat.
 - Women holding E-shram card.
 - Women farmers who are beneficiaries under Kishan Samman Nidhi.
 - Women holding MGNREGA job card.
 - Women whose net family income is less than Rs. 8 lakh per annum.
 - Pregnant and Lactating AWWs/ AWHs/ ASHAs.
 - Any other category as may be prescribed by the Central Government.

> **Conditions for Disbursement:**

 - The child born on or after 08.03.2022 in any family in state of Haryana.
 - Child birth is registered.
 - Child has received first cycle of BCG, OPV, DPT and Hepatitis-B or its equivalent/substitute.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Madad Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
 1. The beneficiary should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category.
 1. The beneficiary should reside in a rural area.
 1. No family member should be employed in government service (Class 1/2/3) or hold a high-ranking post.
 1. The beneficiary must reside in one of the 20 identified tribal districts of Madhya Pradesh: Mandla, Dindori, Balaghat, Shahdol, Anuppur, Umaria, Jhabua, Alirajpur, Barwani, Ratlam, Hoshangabad, Betul, Sidhi, Seoni, Chhindwara, Dhar, Khargone, Khandwa, Burhanpur, and Sheopur.

**Note:** Benefits under the scheme are provided for the birth of the first or second child of the couple.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantrir Nijut Moina Aasoni
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a girl student domiciled in Assam irrespective of economic status.
- The applicant must be enrolled in government and venture educational institutions for Higher Secondary, Graduation (Bachelor of Arts/Bachelor of Science/Bachelor of Commerce), or Post-Graduation (Master of Arts/Master of Science/Master of Commerce/Bachelor of Education) courses.
- The applicant must be a regular student (no correspondence or part-time students).
- The applicant must be unmarried except for those enrolled in Post-Graduation and Bachelor of Education categories.
- The applicant must maintain institutional discipline and adhere to all rules and regulations.
- The applicant must attend classes regularly and maintain 75% attendance for existing beneficiaries.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Nikah Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The parents of the bride and groom should be natives of Madhya Pradesh.
 1. Financial assistance will be provided to needy girls, widows, and abandoned women from destitute or poor Muslim families in the state at the time of their marriage.
 1. The bride and groom should belong to Muslim families.
 1. The bride should be at least 18 years old, and the groom must be at least 21 years old.
 1. There are no income restrictions for receiving assistance under the scheme.
 1. The bride and groom should participate in the mass marriage (Nikah) ceremony organized on the prescribed dates.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will not be available for single marriage (Nikah) ceremonies.

**Note 1:** Abandoned women who have been legally divorced are only eligible under the scheme.
**Note 2:** If the groom is from outside the state, the bride will still be eligible for the benefits of the scheme. However, if the parents of either the bride or groom are not natives of the state, the benefits under the scheme will not be available.
**Note 3:** The bride should not have been previously married. This means that the marriage of the bride and groom must take place solely during a mass marriage (Nikah) ceremony organized under the scheme, and they should not have been married through a single or any other mass marriage (Nikah) ceremony before.
**Note 4:** A mass marriage (Nikah) ceremony will be organized only if applications are received from a minimum of 5 couples.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Prakhand Parivahan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a resident of the block where they apply.
1. The age of the beneficiary should not be less than 18 years.
1. The beneficiary must have a driving license. 
1. The beneficiary should not be working/employed in government service.
1. The beneficiary should belong to the specified categories SC, ST, EBC, BC, Minority, and General.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Pichhda Varg Evam Ati Pichhda Varg Kaushal Vikas Prashikshan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Bihar.
1. The applicant should belong to the Backward Class or Extremely Backward Class category.
1. The applicant should be between 18 and 45 years of age.
1. The applicant should possess the Aadhaar number (mandatory for Aadhaar-based registration).

scheme_name: Mukhiya Mantri Protsahan Yojna
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student should be a native of Himachal Pradesh.
1. The student, who is selected and take admission for a degree course in any Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) or All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS) or for a Post Graduate Diploma Course in any Indian Institute of Management (IIM) or Indian School of Mines (ISM) Dhanbad or Indian Institute of Science (IISc), is eligible to apply for award under this scheme.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Protsahan Yojana Jharkhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be unemployed (not employed in the public/private sector or self-employed).
1. The applicant must be registered in a Jharkhand employment exchange.
1. The applicant should have completed a skill training course conducted by a government ITI, polytechnic, or other government vocational institutes aligned with NSQF.
1. The applicant must not have availed of any similar incentive under another scheme.
1. The applicant must be a domicile of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant should have a valid bank account and Aadhaar card.
1. The applicant should not have been convicted of a crime with a sentence of 48 hours or more.
1. The applicant must be between 18 to 35 years old on the date of employment exchange registration (relaxation as per government norms for SC/ST candidates).

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Mahila Poshan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a pregnant or lactating woman.

scheme_name: Mukhyamatri Rojgaar Srijan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant's age should be between 18 to 50 years.
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹5,00,000/- for rural and urban areas.
1. The applicant should not be employed in any government or semi-government job.
1. The applicant must not be a loan defaulter in any bank.
1. Only environment-friendly business proposals are acceptable (businesses related to alcohol, drugs, or polythene are not allowed).
1. If the applicant is disabled the percentage of disability should not be less than 40%.

scheme_name: Meghalaya Motorcaravan Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
**Individuals**

1. The applicant must be between 18 and 58 years of age.
1. The applicant must hold a valid driving license.
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
1. The applicant must have a minimum of 3 years of experience in the tourism service sector.
1. The applicant shall not be a credit defaulter from any bank.

**For Registered Entities:**

1. The entity must be a recognized and registered tourism operator or tourism society with the Tourism Department, Government of Meghalaya.
1. The entity must have a minimum of 3 years of experience in the tourism service sector.
1. The entity shall not be a defaulter at any financial institution.
1. The entity must provide an Incorporation Certificate or registration certificate, whichever is applicable.
1. The entity shall not be a credit defaulter from any bank.

**For Unregistered Entities:**

1. The entity can be any unregistered organization such as village organizations, self-help groups, or traditional communities based in Meghalaya.
1. The entity must be actively involved in the tourism sector.
1. The entity must have a minimum of 3 years of experience in the tourism service sector.
1. The entity shall not be a credit defaulter at any financial institution.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Shahri Ajeevika Guarantee Yojna
eligibility_text_source:
1. All adult members of the household who register under this scheme will be eligible to work. To register they have to be:

 - Local resident of the Urban Local Body. (Meaning thereby they should be residing within the jurisdiction of the Urban Local Body either in their own house or on rent).
 - Willing to do unskilled work at projects being executed or in Sanitation Services being provided by the Urban Local Bodies.

2. The household will comprise of husband, wife, and their minor children. However, only adult members of the household shall be eligible to work.
3. The upper age limit for providing work shall be 65 years.
> **Permissible Activities:**

1. All eligible beneficiaries will be employed in the following permissible activities in ULBs:

 - In any ongoing or new admissible work under any Govt. of H.P./Govt. of India scheme for which funds are available with ULB.
 - In any admissible work under the 15th Finance Commission or 5ffi State Finance Commission for which grant-in-aid is provided to the ULBs.
 - Sanitation Works/Services as admissible under Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016 and Swachh Bharat Mission. The sanitation works/services may include activities like street sweeping, garbage collection, processing/removal of legacy waste, waste segregation, cleaning of garbage from hill slopes or other litter points, cleaning and maintaining municipal parks, and cleaning of municipal parking.

2. The Urban Local Bodies shall not start any muster-roll linked work/ac

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Shiksha Alankar Yojana for Students of Economically Weaker Section
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
1. The student should belong to the Economically Weaker Section (EWS).
1. The student is studying in government schools in Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in the 10th or 12th class.1. The student should have secured the highest marks in the 10th and 12th class board examinations conducted by the Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education, Ajmer (for Arts, Commerce, and Science streams).
**Note:** Students benefiting from any other incentive schemes run by the state and central government will also be eligible for benefits under the scheme.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Samekit Chaur Vikas Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an individual, family, group beneficiary, or entrepreneur.
1. The applicant should be a private landowner or lessee in Chaur-dominated districts.

scheme_name: Maanki Munda Scholarship for Female Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Jharkhand state. 
1. The applicant should be a female student.
1. The student should be enrolled in B.Tech. Programme at BIT Sindri in 2nd, 3rd, and 4th year. 
1. The student already availing/applied for any other scholarship from any other source viz state govt. or central govt. cannot apply for this scholarship.
1. The student should have obtained a minimum of 50% marks in the previous year without any back paper.
1. The student's annual family income from all sources should not exceed ₹8,00,000/-. 
1. The student must have passed the 10th and 12th examinations from schools based in Jharkhand state. For lateral entry, the student must have passed their class 10th and diploma or equivalent courses from schools/institutes based in Jharkhand state.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Seekho Kamao Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be between 18 to 29 years of age.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be educated with a minimum qualification of 12th pass, ITI, or higher.
1. The applicant should be designated as a “Student-Trainee” upon selection.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Shramyogi Prathibhavan Vidyarthi Yojana (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be engaged in building or construction work.
 1. The applicants should be registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered worker must have a minimum of one year’s regular membership.
 1. The worker must have an active membership with the Board.
 1. The worker's child must have scored 85% in the senior secondary (10+2) board examination or 75% or more in the bachelor's degree course and is continuing studies as a regular student in the next class or course.
 1. For students pursuing commercial pilot training courses and type renting courses, all institutions approved by the Director General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) are also eligible for reimbursement under the scheme.
 1. Applicants under this Scheme will not be eligible for benefits under already approved financial assistance, higher education, technical and professional courses, or any other similar scheme of the Board.
 1. This financial assistance will be payable up to the first three children of the worker.
 1. The worker has neither applied again for the same class of his/her son/daughter nor received similar benefits/financial assistance from any other government department/board/corporation, nor have they made any application for the same.

**Note 01:** Under this scheme all IIMs/IITs/IIITs/AIIMS/NITs/NIFT/NID/Indian Institute of Hotel Management, National Law University, and other institutions of Central Government, approved by statutory bodie

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Shiksha Protsahan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Jharkhand state.
1. The student should have passed class 10th from any recognized school in the state of Jharkhand **or** the student should possess a local residence certificate of Jharkhand. 1. The student must meet the exam's eligibility criteria for coaching under this scheme. For exams requiring Class 12th qualification, Class 11th students at the time of application are also eligible.
1. The student's annual family income should be less than ₹8,00,000/- **or** the student should be covered under the National/State Food Security Scheme. 1. An applicant who has already received similar benefits from another department is not eligible for dual benefits.
1. The student can avail the benefit only once under this scheme.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Shramik Seva Prasuti Sahayata Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a pregnant woman.
1. The applicant should be over 18 years of age.
1. The applicant should be registered as an unorganized worker.
1. The applicant should have the delivery in a government hospital.
1. The applicant should not already have had two live births.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Shramshakti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Bihar.
 1. The applicant should belong to a minority community.
 1. The applicant's age should be between 18 and 45 years.
 1. The applicant’s annual family income should be less than ₹4,50,000/-.

**Note 1:** Minimum educational qualification for most training courses is 8th/10th pass. The minimum educational qualification required for various training programs will be determined by the Bihar State Minorities Financial Corporation Limited, depending on the course syllabus.
**Note 2:** There is a 30% reservation for women and 3% reservation for disabled.
**Note 3:** If the reserved seats for women or disabled persons are not filled, they will be allocated to applicants from other minority categories.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Sharmik Surksha Yojana- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be the widow/dependent of the deceased worker.
 1. The deceased worker should have been employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. There is no fixed service period required for the worker.
 1. There is no fixed monthly salary requirement for the worker.
 1. Applications must be submitted within two years from the date of the worker's death.
 1. Financial assistance is provided under this scheme for the death of a worker primarily caused by workplace incidents within the organization's premises or on the way to the hospital for treatment. This also covers deaths resulting from other causes such as natural disasters, heart attacks, snake or other poisonous creature bites, fire, or building collapses within the organization.
 1. Assistance is also provided to widows/dependents of unregistered workers who die or become disabled due to incidents like fire, building collapse, etc. within the establishment.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Samajik Suraksha Yojana: Death & Funeral Assistance (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a nominee/legal heir of the deceased construction worker.
 1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The scheme allows for application only once.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Swasthya Sahayata Yojana Jharkhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe, Scheduled Caste, or Backward Class categories.
1. The applicant must be suffering from the illness or surgery.
1. Applicants already availing financial assistance from other government schemes are ineligible.
1. The applicant must be a holder of one of the following ration cards:

 - Priority Household Ration Card (PHH)
 - Antyodaya Ration Card
 - Green Card

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Sarvajan Uchcha Shiksha Chhatravriti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
1. The annual income of the parent/guardian of the student/applicant from all sources (including the applicant’s own income, if any) should be less than ₹5,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in IITs, IIMs, NITs, AIIMS, or other [national institutions listed](https://sje.rajasthan.gov.in/Default.aspx?PageID=198) under the scheme.1. The applicant should not be availing of any other government scholarship for the same course.
1. The applicant should maintain academic performance as required for renewal of the scholarship.
1. An employee pursuing full-time studies while on leave without pay for the entire duration of the course will also be eligible for the scholarship.
1. The student studying outside Rajasthan in the [listed national institutions](https://sje.rajasthan.gov.in/Default.aspx?PageID=198) are eligible.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Sahara Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
2. The applicant must belong to the economically weaker section of society.
3. The applicant must be suffering from specified diseases such as Parkinson's disease, Malignant Cancer, Paralysis, Muscular Dystrophy, Hemophilia, and Thalassemia, or chronic renal failure or any other disease that renders a person permanently incapacitated.
4. The applicant must not be a beneficiary of any other government-sponsored pension scheme.

scheme_name: Mukhymantri Sarthi Yojana - Jharkhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant's age should be 18-35 years.
1. The applicant must belong to one of the priority groups (SC/ST, women, PwD, transgender).

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Swarozgar Yojana - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a native or permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The applicant must be at least 18 years of age.
 1. No educational qualification is required for availing the benefits under this scheme.
 1. Under the scheme, financing is available for industrial, service, and business sectors. It is also available for projects related to apple cultivation, orchids, animal husbandry, and agriculture activities.

eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Nirmaan Shramik Pension Sahayata
eligibility_text_source:
1. Beneficiaries should be 60 years old or more. 
1. Beneficiaries must be permanent residents of Chhattisgarh.
1. Beneficiaries must have registered members as construction workers in the Board for 10 years.
1. Beneficiaries cannot receive benefits under this scheme if they are already receiving benefits from any other parallel scheme of the State/Central Government.
1. Registered construction workers who have availed the benefit of the Chief Minister Syan Sahayata Yojana are ineligible for this scheme.
1. Beneficiaries must submit a survival certificate every year in March to continue receiving the assistance amount.
1. If both husband and wife are registered workers and both are receiving pension benefits, only one spouse will continue to receive pension after the death of the other. Family pension will not be payable to dependents.
1. If both husband and wife are registered workers and the husband dies before the wife turns 60, the wife will receive family pension until she turns 60. After turning 60, she can apply for pension under the pension scheme, and the family pension will automatically stop.
1. Beneficiaries have six months from the date of completing 59 years and three months to 60 years to apply for the scheme. A survival certificate must be submitted in the month when the first pension amount is to be paid.

Unemployed youth/women applying for the amount of self-help allowance will have to compulsorily get training in language communication and basic computer knowledge (skilled youth program) conducted by Labor Resources Department and they will get a total amount of self-help allowance for the last 5 months will not be released until they successfully surrender the certificate of the said training.

1. The age of the daughter should be at least 18 years and should not be more than 21 years.

scheme_name: Madhya Pradesh Fasal Vividhikaran (Crop Diversification) Encouragement Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a Company, Firm, or Institution.
- The applicant must maintain all records and qualifications as a qualifying Company, Firm, or Institution.
- The applicant must advise the farmer regarding the sowing of the 'Eligible Crop' and the associated Agriculture Practice (Krishi Karyapranali).
- The applicant must confirm the purchase of the 'Eligible Crop'.
- The applicant must execute the crop diversification work.
- The applicant must make a mandatory tie-up with another Company, Firm, or Institution for the purchase of the 'Eligible Crop'.
- The applicant must ensure they themselves do not purchase the crop.

eligibility_text_source:

1. The applicant should be a physically disabled artist or litterateur.
1. The applicant should be 21 years of age or above.
1. The applicant’s monthly income from all sources, including spouse income, should not exceed ₹10,000.
1. The total monthly family income, including dependents, should not exceed ₹20,000.

> **NOTE:** Income limits are revised annually with a 6 percent increase for eligibility calculation.

1. The writer/artist's fully dependent spouse, fully dependent parents, dependent minor brothers and sisters, dependent minor children, dependent widowed daughter, and dependent differently-abled brothers and sisters are eligible to get assistance.

scheme_name: Madhya Pradesh Lok Vaniki Yojana
eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant must apply to the Tehsildar (Revenue) for the demarcation and management of the land as "Lok Van" (Public Forest), if the land is tree covered revenue land.
- The applicant must submit the management proposal to the Divisional Officer (Van Mandal Adhikari) or the Range Officer (Van Kshetra Adhikari).
- The applicant (Landowner) must attach a declaration regarding land title/possession, including an attested photocopy of the latest land records (Khasra).
- The applicant (Gram Panchayat/Gram Sabha) must submit a resolution to the Tehsildar (Revenue) after getting the land demarcated and handed over for management as "Lok Van".

scheme_name: Mushroom Production and Marketing Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a farmer interested in mushroom cultivation.

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for Students belonging to Scheduled Caste Community
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be permanently settled in Mizoram.
- The applicant must belong to Scheduled Caste.
- The income of the parents/guardians of the applicant (from all sources) should not exceed ₹2,50,000/- per annum.
- The applicant's ongoing educational qualification must be between 11th Standard and Post-Doctoral Fellowship.

scheme_name: Madhya Pradesh Non-Government Institution Grant Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a non-governmental cultural institution.
1. The institution should be registered under the Madhya Pradesh Society Registration Act, 1973, or other applicable law.
1. The institution should be continuously active for at least 3 years prior to the application year.
1. The institution should be engaged in art, culture, literature, training, research, or performances.
1. The institution should have cultural and literary objectives clearly mentioned in its registered constitution.
1. The institution should be operating within Madhya Pradesh.

scheme_name: Medhavi Putra/Putri Chhatravritti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a domicile in the state of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant should be a Student.
1. The applicant should have passed the previous examination with atleast Second Division or 45% marks in any examination (except Class 1st to 5th) OR have got admitted/enrolled in the course on the basis of a competitive examination.
1. The applicant should be the child of a Worker registered with the 'Jharkhand Building and Other Construction Worker Welfare Board (JBOCWW Board)'.
1. The scholarship can be availed by upto two children of the Registered Worker.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Pragatishil Pashupalak Protsahan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Uttar Pradesh.
1. The age of the applicant should be 18 years or above.
1. Only individual cattle farmers will be eligible, but groups, firms, or organizations will not eligible.
1. Only indigenous breed of cows like Gir, Sahiwal, Haryana, Gangatiri, and Tharparkar species are eligible.
1. The cattle farmers of the state can avail the benefit of this incentive amount only once for a maximum of 02 cows.
1. The cattle farmers will be eligible to receive an incentive amount only once in the lifetime of the cow.

scheme_name: Media Person Pension Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The accredited media persons of daily, evening, weekly, fortnightly, and monthly newspapers, news agencies, Radio stations, and news channels with at least 20 years of experience in the field of journalism are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. The age of the applicant/media person should be above 60 years.
 1. The media person should have been accredited with the Information, Public Relations & Languages Department, Haryana for at least the last five years.
 1. The beneficiary media person will have to open an Aadhaar-linked savings bank account in any nationalized bank for depositing the amount of pension in their bank accounts.
 1. There should be no criminal proceedings pending against the beneficiary media person.
 1. The media person, getting a pension or honorarium of any kind from any other State Government or news organization will also be eligible. However, if an otherwise eligible media person is getting a pension from any other State Government of an amount less than ₹10,000/- per month, the entitlement of pension under this scheme would be reduced by that amount.

**Note 01:** The beneficiary media person has to give a live certificate in the month of January every year.
**Note 02:** The pension under this scheme will be discontinued in case of registration of any criminal case against the beneficiary media person at any time or if the conduct of the media person is found against the set principles/ethics of journalism.

scheme_name: Madhya Pradesh Professional Talent Examination Award Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
- The applicant should belong to Other Backward Classes (OBC) as per the list declared by the Government of Madhya Pradesh.
- The applicant should have appeared and secured first, second, or third position in entrance examinations (PET, PPT, or MCA) conducted by the Professional Examination Board.
- The applicant should be pursuing technical and vocational subjects.

scheme_name: Post-matric Scholarship Scheme- Uttar Pradesh
eligibility_text_source:
1. Native and under study in Uttar Pradesh.
 1. The annual income of parents/guardian of the student is upto Rs. 2 Lakh.
 1. Students from Other Backwards Classes study in all government-aided and recognized state educational institutes (subject to budget availability).

scheme_name: Matrutva Prasuvidha Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a domicile in the state of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant should be a female.
1. The applicant should be pregnant.
1. The applicant should be a Worker registered with the 'Jharkhand Building and Other Construction Worker Welfare Board (JBOCWW Board)'.
1. The applicant should be between 18-60 years of age.
1. The applicant should be engaged in construction work such as masons, carpenters, electricians, porters, painters, etc.
1. The scheme is applicable only for the first two deliveries.

scheme_name: Matsya Pokhari Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Odisha. 
1. The applicant should possess identity and residential proof including an Aadhar card.
1. The applicant should have a bank account in his/her name in the State of Odisha.
1. The applicant should have requisite land free from all encumbrances. 
1. The pond area must be between 0.2 to 2.00 hectares with a minimum water depth of 6 feet.
1. Previous beneficiaries of similar subsidies are eligible for additional subsidies up to a total of 2 hectares.

scheme_name: Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar Ninaivu Marriage Assistance Scheme - II
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a girl.
1. The bride should be 18 years or above at the time of marriage.
1. The bride should be a degree holder from a regular college, Distance Education / Government recognized Open University Or diploma holder should have qualified from Institutions recognized by the Directorate of Technical Education, Government of Tamil Nadu.
1. The bride's family income should not exceed ₹24,000/- per annum. 
1. The assistance is given to only one girl in a family.

scheme_name: Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar Ninaivu Marriage Assistance Scheme-1
eligibility_text_source:
1. The annual income of the family of the applicant should not exceed ₹72,000/-.
1. The girl should be of the legal marriage age, i.e. 18 years or above.
1. The boy should be of the legal marriage age, i.e. 21 years or above.
1. The girl should have studied upto 10th Standard. As a special case, a bride from the Scheduled Tribe community, with a minimum education of 5th Standard, can also avail of this scheme.
1. Only one girl from a family is eligible.

scheme_name: Mai Ramabai Ambedakar Saat Phera Samuh Lagna Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
 1. The annual income limit in this scheme is ₹6,00,000/- in rural areas and ₹6,00,000/- in urban areas.
 1. In case of remarriage, the benefit will not be available under this scheme.
 1. The age limit of the girl at the time of marriage should be 18 years and the age of the boys should be 21 years. Proof of age of bride and groom to be married will be mandatory.
 1. Applications for assistance must be made within two years of the marriage.
 1. The beneficiary girl participating in the group marriage will be eligible for the benefits under this scheme.
 1. The assistance of the scheme will be available to girls from Kunwar Bai Nu Mameru Yojana.
 1. Marriage registration certificate has to be submitted compulsorily to avail the benefits of this scheme.
 1. The organization/institution organizing the group wedding has to apply to the concerned authority within two years from the date of marriage.
 1. The bride must produce a certified copy of the caste certificate obtained from the competent authority of the bride.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Bridhhabasta Pension Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Jharkhand.
 1. The applicant should have attained the age of 60 years or more.
 1. The applicant should have a Voter ID card and Aadhar Card.
 1. The applicant should not be a beneficiary under any other Social Security pension scheme.
 1. The applicant or their spouse should not be a Retired or permanently employed in Central and State Government or in public sector undertakings.
 1. The applicant or their spouse should not be a recipient of Government pension or pension from Central and State Government or from public sector undertakings.
 1. Applicant’s family is not an income tax payee.

scheme_name: Mentally Retarded Children Benefit Scheme (HPBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
***For Registration***

1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a Building or Construction Worker.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have worked in building or construction activities for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months.

_*The registration should be renewed annually._
***For Application***

1. The worker should be engaged in building or construction work under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The worker should be registered with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The worker's membership with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board must be active.
1. The child of the worker should be mentally retarded/disabled and dependent on the registered worker.

scheme_name: Mentally Retarded Children Benefit Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

 - The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
 - The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
 - The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

 - The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab.
 - The member should be depositing his contribution to the Board regularly.
 - The child of the member should be mentally retarded or disabled.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Raut, Charavaaha Evan Doodh Duhane Vaale Saayakal Sahaayata Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant must be registered as an unorganized worker in any district of the state. 
1. Applicant must be between the ages of 18 and 60. 
1. Unorganized workers who are receiving benefits from any other parallel scheme of the State Government are not eligible for the scheme.

scheme_name: Mizoram Research Fellowship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a bonafide permanent residents of Mizoram.
1. The applicant must be prosecuting higher studies or research at a university/institution recognized/established by the law.
1. The applicant must not be guilty of misconduct or breach of discipline or participating in strikes, demonstrations, picketing, and the like for unfair demands from any high-level authority.
1. (Unless otherwise relaxed by the awarding authority) The applicant must secure at least 50% on average in the last university examination.
1. The applicant must not be in receipt of any scholar or stipend except merit scholarship, from any sources.

scheme_name: Modular Reflective Folding Sticks (Smartcane)
eligibility_text_source: - The applicant should be a visually impaired person.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Krishi Vikas Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a farmer cultivating sugarcane.
1. The applicant must be a permanent member of a cooperative sugarcane development society.
1. The applicant must practice advanced sugarcane farming.
1. The applicant’s name must be registered in the land records of the state.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Nirashrit Mahila Samman Pension Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a permanent resident of Jharkhand.
- The applicant should have attained the age of 18 years or more.
- The applicant must belong to any of the following.

1. A woman whose husband has died.
1. An abandoned woman.
1. Single Woman aged 45 years or more.

- The applicant should have a Voter ID card and Aadhar Card.
- The applicant should not be a beneficiary under any other Social Security pension scheme.
- The applicant should not be retired or permanently employed in Central and State Government or in public sector undertakings.
- The applicant or their spouse should not be a recipient of any family pension or pension from Central and State Government or from public sector undertakings.
- Applicant’s family is not an income tax payee.

scheme_name: Monetary Relief to the Victim of Atrocities- Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant must be a victim, or a family member/dependent of a victim, of an atrocity committed by non-Scheduled Caste persons.
 1. The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe community.
 1. An FIR must be lodged under the SC/ST Prevention of Atrocities Act, 1989.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Rajshri Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The girl child must be a resident of Rajasthan.
1. She must be born on or after June 1, 2016.
1. The girl child must be born in a government or private medical institution registered with Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY).
1. Only two girl child will be eligible.
1. Girl's parents must have an Aadhar Card and a Bhamashah Card.

scheme_name: Manabik Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant must be a disable person with 50% of disability. 
1. Applicant must be a resident of the state namely West Bengal for not less than 10 Years on the date of making application for Pension.
1. Family income of the beneficiary should not exceed ₹1,00,000/- (One thousand) per month.
1. The differently abled person should be declared unsuitable for physical work by the respective Medical officer.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Shramik Aujaar Sahayata Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The Applicant must be a native of Chhattisgarh.
1. The beneficiary should be a construction worker.
1. The beneficiary should be registered under the Building and Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The age of the registered worker should not be less than 18 years.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Sukh Ashray Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Himachal Pradesh.
1. Orphans, abandoned, or specially-abled children under 27 years of age.
1. Destitute women who are either unmarried or whose husbands have been missing for over 7 years.
1. Senior citizens who do not have any support are also eligible under this scheme.

scheme_name: Mission Solar Charkha
eligibility_text_source:
> **Criteria for selection of Promoter Agency/SPV:**

***A. Existing Khadi and Village Industry Institution (KVI) could apply for setting up such a Cluster. However, the following parameters must be fulfilled:***

 1. KVI having a positive balance sheet and assets in favour of the institution.
 1. KVI having an artisan base of not less than 200.
 1. KVI having a sales turnover of not less than ₹1.00 Crore in each of the preceding three financial years.
 1. Should have been an increase in a number of new artisans in the last three years.

***B. Other Institutions such as SPV, Society, Trust, Section 8 Company or LLP under the Companies Act, 2013 registered under respective statutes could also apply for setting up a new Solar Charkha Cluster, with the following criteria:***

 1. Vision and Mission
 1. Board and Governing Structure with sufficient experience.
 1. Proper Management Information System (MIS).
 1. Financial resources-equity and debt.
 1. Financial Performance for last three years-profitability and IRR.

***C. First Timers who want to join the village industry movement through solar charkha clusters could apply for a new cluster, with the following criteria:***

 1. Highest Commitment to the cause of Social and rural upliftment.
 1. Funding commitment from Scheduled Commercial Banks/NBFC/Venture Capital Fund/Private Equity funds.
 1. Proper Management Information System (MIS).
 1. Any other criteria to be approved by the governing council.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Sports Development Scheme - Retired Person
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Tripura.
1. The applicant should be a retired sportsperson. 
1. The applicant's age should be less than 64 years.

scheme_name: Mobility Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- Woman scientists/academicians working at permanent position in any Government Institution/R&D Lab/University and willing to resign from their permanent position or take a long leave to avail the fellowship and after completion of the tenure return back to the parent organization.
 - The new place should be located beyond 500 Km from present city.
 - Upper age limit is 50 years to apply.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Sports Development Scheme - Unemployed Young Professional Sportsperson
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a resident of Tripura.
- The applicant should be a young sportsperson.
- The applicant should be an experienced professional.
- The age of the applicant should be between 18 to 40 years (a relaxation of 5 years in the upper age limit for ST/SC candidates).

scheme_name: Merit Scholarship: Engineering University
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a native resident student of Chhattisgarh.

- The applicant must be currently studying in a recognized engineering college in Chhattisgarh.
- The applicant must be pursuing an engineering degree course.
- Students who have passed their 10th and 12th standard examinations with a minimum of 60% marks from any recognized institution located in Chhattisgarh will be eligible.
- Diploma holders newly admitted to the second year of B.E. must have passed the final year examination of their diploma with a minimum of 60% marks will also be eligible.

**Other Conditions -** 

- Without special approval from the government, no student will receive more than one scholarship at the same time. However, if the institution where he is studying has given him any fee concession, then he will be entitled to continue receiving it.
- If any scholarship becomes vacant during its duration, then the approving authority can give such scholarships for its unexpired period to the next eligible and deserving candidate from the merit list.
- Only those students will be eligible to receive scholarships who have passed the qualifying examination from any testing body of Chhattisgarh. But children of such permanent government employees of Chhattisgarh can be exempted from this condition who are on deputation to the Government of India or any other state government.
- Such students will not be eligible to receive scholarships who are in full-time employment. In this regard, only one co

scheme_name: Self-Employment Scheme for Unemployed Minority Communities
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must belong to a minority community (Muslim, Sikh, Christian, Parsi, Buddhist, or Jain).
1. The applicant’s age should be between 18 and 55 years.
1. The annual family income from all sources should not exceed ₹2,50,000/-, or the applicant should be a member of a BPL family.

scheme_name: Mahila Samruddhi (Gujarat Backward Caste Development Corporation)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be female.
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should belong to a socially and educationally backward class, specifically Other Backward Classes (OBC).
1. The total annual income of the applicant's family should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant's age should be between 21 and 45 years on the date of application.
1. The applicant must have experience in a business requiring technical and other skills.

scheme_name: MICRO FINANCE (Gopalak Nigam)
eligibility_text_source: 1. The applicant must be a **permanent resident of Gujarat.**1. The applicant must be a woman or man belonging to the **Rabari or Bharwad caste.**1. The applicant must belong to a **socially and educationally backward class** as recognized by the Gujarat government.1. The applicant must be between **21 and 45 years of age.**1. The applicant’s annual family income must **not exceed ₹3.00 lakh.**1. The applicant must be planning to **start or expand a small business.**

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Svadeshi Gau Samvardhan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Uttar Pradesh.
1. The age of the applicant should be 18 years or above. 
1. The milk producer/animal rearer should have adequate space/shed available for keeping the animals.
1. The milk producer/animal husbandry should not already have more than 02 cows of indigenous advanced breed like Gir, Sahiwal, Haryana, Tharparkar and F-1 cow of hybrid breed.
1. The applicant should purchase an indigenous improved breed of cows from outside the state. 
1. The applicant should purchase cows of indigenous improved breed of first or second calving.
1. The applicant should purchase disease-free and healthy cows. 
1. The applicant should take animal insurance for all the cows purchased for 03 years. 
1. The applicant should take the transit insurance for the cattle rearer to get for bringing the cows from the place of purchase/state to the place of establishment of the unit.

scheme_name: Mizoram Sports Incentive Cash Award Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- A person must be a resident of Mizoram, representing any state of the union, the Sports Control Boards, or the Country.
 - Winner of first, second, or third position in the specified championship as enumerated in column 3 of Annexure.
 - Any sports-person participating in the International Championship.
 - Officials, Manager, Coach, Masseur, Physiotherapist, and Doctor will also be entitled to receive 25% of the award applicable to the sports- person.
 - Any sports person of Mizoram representing the Country in any recognised sports discipline in any international sports or championship will be entitled to receive Rs. 20,000/- as sports kit allowance.
 - For the purpose of awarding the incentive cash award, there can be only one award in one sports discipline in a championship or tournament.

scheme_name: Margadeepam Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Kerala.
- The applicant must be studying in Class 1st to Class 8th.
- The applicant must be studying in a Government or Government-aided school.
- The applicant must belong to a minority religious community (Hindu, Christian (all denominations), Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Parsi).
- The applicant's family annual income must not exceed ₹2,50,000/-.

_*If adequate applicants are not available in one category, students from other categories may be considered._
##### **Earmarking**

- 30% scholarships are reserved for female students. 
- The applicant (male students) may be considered for female-reserved positions if adequate female applications are not received.

##### ***Additional Merit Consideration***

- **PwD:** The applicant should provide disability certificate if having 40% and above physical/mental disability.- **Orphan:** The applicant should provide death certificate if orphaned (father/mother/both parents deceased).
##### ***Merit Point Calculation System***
Family Income: Formula = (2,50,000 - Actual Income)/25,000 (Maximum 10 points).
Academic Grades: Maximum 12 points (A+ Grade = 12, A Grade = 11, B+ Grade = 10, B Grade = 9, C+ Grade = 8, C Grade = 7, D+ Grade = 6, D Grade = 5).
Orphaned Students: 4 points maximum (Both parents deceased = 4, Single parent deceased = 2).
Physical/Mental Disability: 4 points (40% and above disability and category 2 disability).
Total Maximum Points: 30.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Sudharit Kamdhenu Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a bonafide resident of Goa for at least 15 years.
- The applicant must possess a pucca cattle shed with cement flooring for housing the animals proposed to be reared.
- The applicant must undertake to strictly stall feed the animals and not allow open grazing except in open paddock surrounding the cattle shed.
- The applicant must have knowledge or experience in Cattle or Buffaloes farming or trained by the Department.
- New applicants must compulsorily undergo dairy training as decided by the Department of Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Services (AH & VS) at the designated training centre.
- The applicant must have the cattle shed constructed on their own land.
- The applicant must ensure only one cowshed is permitted under one survey number having area of less than 400 square meters.
- The applicant must be a member of the Local Dairy Society/Bachat Gat/Self help groups, unless they are interested in branding their dairy products without membership of local milk unions.
- The applicant must obtain prior written permission well in advance from the Department before undertaking purchases through a registered dealer.
- If the applicant's spouse is a Government employee, the applicant must submit a declaration on their behalf stating the Department of the spouse, designation, place of posting, and mobile number.
- If the applicant has more than 25 cattle, the applicant (owner of the cattle shed) must apply for and produce the consent of the Goa State Poll

scheme_name: Shaikshanik Chatravriti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The parents of the applicant must be working in a factory or institution established in Madhya Pradesh under the Labor Welfare Fund Act 1982.
1. Only 2 children in a family are eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
1. Children of officers and employees in managerial capacities working in institutions cannot be benefited from this regime.

scheme_name: Msme Skill Training And Employment Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an MSME unit in the state who has the requisite infrastructure.
1. Only in respect of vocation/trade in which MSMEs have expertise will be encouraged, MSME units should have experience, expertise, and running business.
1. The trainees selected by the applicant (MSME) shall be between 18 to 25 years of age.
1. The trainees selected by the applicant shall have an engineering degree, a polytechnic diploma, or an Industrial Training Institute (ITI) certificate, or qualification as prescribed by the respective sector skill council.
1. The applicant shall provide the trainee with a stipend of ₹5000/- per month, for a maximum of 6 months.
1. The applicant, MSME Unit, should not have been blacklisted at any point in time by any Government Norms/Rules/Acts. 

**Note:** The scheme is funded by Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation (TNSDC)

scheme_name: Mata Shabri Mahila Sashktikaran Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a woman.
1. The applicant should belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
1. The applicant should belong to a scheduled caste or scheduled tribe family. 
1. The applicant's family income should not exceed ₹35,000/-.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Medium Scale Ornamental Fish Rearing Unit (Fresh Water) - Haryana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant should possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID).
1. The applicant should have at least 150 square meters of vacant land with adequate water supply.
1. The applicant should either own the land or possess a registered lease deed valid for at least 7 years.

scheme_name: Map Simplification Process
eligibility_text_source: 1. All landowners in the Haridwar district of Uttarakhand are eligible.

scheme_name: Mandahasam Scheme To Provide Artificial Dentures To Senior Citizens
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native and resident of Kerala State.
 1. The applicant’s age has to be 60 years or above.
 1. The applicants should be from Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
 1. The persons who have completely lost their teeth or those who have partially lost their teeth and are in a position to have them extracted as they are unusable are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. The persons who have been certified by a qualified dentist in the prescribed form as unfit for placing artificial teeth.

**Note:** The priority will be given to the oldest person under the scheme.

scheme_name: Minimum Support Price for Aromatic Oils/Products - Centre for Aromatic Plants
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The farmer must be engaged in the cultivation of aromatic plants.
 1. The farmer must be registered with the Centre for Aromatic Plants (CAP), Selaqui, Dehradun.

scheme_name: Merit Scholarship: Polytechnic Institutions
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a native resident student of Chhattisgarh.

- The applicant must be currently studying in a recognized polytechnic institution in Chhattisgarh.
- The applicant must have secured a minimum of 60% marks in the qualifying examination.
- Students who have passed the 10th/12th class examination under the 10+2 system from any recognized institution located in Chhattisgarh with a minimum of 60% marks will be eligible.
- Students newly admitted to the second year of the Diploma program who are ITI / 12th vocational certificate holders must have passed the qualifying examination with a minimum of 60% marks.

**Other Conditions -** 

- Without special approval from the government, no student will receive more than one scholarship at the same time. However, if the institution where he is studying has given him any fee concession, then he will be entitled to continue receiving it.
- If any scholarship becomes vacant during its duration, then the approving authority can give such scholarships for its unexpired period to the next eligible and deserving candidate from the merit list.
- Only those students will be eligible to receive scholarships who have passed the qualifying examination from any testing body of Chhattisgarh. But children of such permanent government employees of Chhattisgarh can be exempted from this condition who are on deputation to the Government of India or any other state government.
- Such students will not be eligible to receive scholarships

scheme_name: Matribhasha Satyagrahi Pension Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Matribhasha Satyagrahi of Hindi Aandolan-1957 and had been imprisoned for participating in Matribhasha Hindi Satyagraha (Hindi Andolan).
 1. The beneficiary Satyagrahis will have to open an Aadhaar-linked savings bank account in any nationalized bank for transferring the pension amount to their bank accounts.
 1. The beneficiary Satyagrahis have to give a live certificate in the month of January every year.
 1. A Satyagrahi, getting a pension or honorarium of any kind from any other State Government will also be eligible. However, if an otherwise eligible Satyagrahi is getting a pension from any other State Government for the same purpose of an amount less than ₹10,000/- per month, the entitlement of pension under this scheme would be reduced by that amount.

**Note 01:** The Matribhasha Satyagrahis were identified by constituting committees in all the districts of the State under the Chairmanship of the respective Deputy Commissioners concerned.
**Note 02:** The Deputy Commissioners, being the chairpersons of the committee, had got the record of Matribhasha Satyagrahis verified by the concerned Superintendents of Police by checking/verifying their record from the jails.
**Note 03:** The Committees, already constituted under the Chairmanship of respective Deputy Commissioners of all the districts, will examine fresh applications, as and when receive, and send their recommendations to the State Government whose decision shall be final.

**Note:** An account opened under this Scheme shall be a single-holder type account.
> **Deposits:**

 - An individual may open any number of accounts subject to the maximum limit for deposit and a time gap of three months shall be maintained between the existing account and the opening of other accounts.

> **Payment on maturity:**

 - The deposit shall mature on completion of two years from the date of the deposit and the Eligible Balance may be paid to the account holder on maturity.
 - In calculating the maturity value, any amount in fraction of a rupee shall be rounded off to the nearest rupee, and for this purpose; any amount of fifty paisa or more shall be treated as one rupee, and any amount less than fifty paisa shall be ignored.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Old and Infirm
eligibility_text_source:

- The age of the applicant should be 60 years or above.
- If the family has children in the age group of 6 to 14 years, those children must be enrolled in a school recognized by the Government of Tripura.

**For Infirm Applicants**

- The applicant should be a resident of Tripura.
- The annual income of the family of the applicant should not exceed ₹1,00,000/-
- The applicant should be affected by Cancer/ AIDS/ Leprosy.
- If the family has children in the age group of 6 to 14 years, those children must be enrolled in a school recognized by the Government of Tripura.

**NOTE:** The assistance under this scheme will be stopped immediately in the following cases:
1. Death of Beneficiary.
2. Fulfillment of any exclusion criteria during the lifetime of a beneficiary:

- Remarriage of a widow.
- A member of the family of the beneficiary getting a Government Job.
- The annual family income of the beneficiary exceeds ₹1,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Tirth Yatra Yojana

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Tirth Yatra Yojana - Delhi

scheme_name: Dr. A.N. Mukhopadhyay Needy Student Fund (B.Sc. Agriculture - Final Year Student)

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Vidyarthi Kalyan Yojana

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Vriksharopan Protsahan Yojana

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Vyavsayik Pathyakram Margdarshan Evam Utpreran Yojana

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Vidyarthi Pratibha Yojna
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of GNCT of Delhi.

 1. Annual Income Limit – Criteria for annual family income ₹ 8 Lacs at par annum with the EWS income limit, subject to exclusions notified by the Government of India. If the student scores 75% and above marks in the previous class, no income limit is applicable for SC/ST/OBC category.
 1. The applicant should upload a Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC) issued in the name of the student or his father’s name issued by the SDM/Deputy Commissioner (Revenue), GNCTD. However, the Domicile Certificate of Delhi from the Revenue Deptt. Will be entertained for the SC beneficiary only, if the caste certificate is issued by the outside state authority instead of the competent authority of Govt. of NCT of Delhi.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Vridhjan Pension Yojna

scheme_name: Mahadevi Verma Shramik Pustak Kraya Dhan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:

1. In case the worker is a woman herself, she will be eligible for the benefit.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Vriddhjan Samman Pension Yojana
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Maths Wizard & Spelling Genius - Uttarakhand

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Yuva Annadoot Yojana

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Yuva Swavalamban Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility**

 1. The State Government has approved the validity of the income certificate for three financial years from the date of issue. Accordingly, a candidate who has a valid income certificate need not have to issue it again for the next three years financial years.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Yuva Swarojgar Yojana
eligibility_text_source:

 1. Applicant must have passed minimum 8th class.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Yuva Udyami Yojana

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Yuva Udyami Yojana

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Yuba Yogayog Yojana-Special
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: National Post Doctoral Fellowship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian citizen.
 1. The applicant must have obtained PhD/M.D/M.S degree from a recognized University. Those who have submitted their PhD/M.D/M.S. thesis and are awaiting the award of the degree are also eligible to apply. However, such candidates, if selected, will be offered a lower fellowship amount till they qualify for the eligible degree.
 1. The upper age limit for the fellowship is 35 years at the time of the submission of the application, age will be calculated by taking the date of closure of the respective call. Age relaxation of 5 (five) years will be given to candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC/Physically Challenged & Women candidates.
 1. NPDF can be availed only once by a candidate in his/her career.
 1. The mentor must hold a regular academic/research position in a recognized institution in India. Should hold a Ph.D. degree in Science or Engineering.
 1. A mentor shall not have more than two SERB NPDF fellows at any given time.

**Note 01:** The fellowship is tenable only in India and can be implemented in any of the recognized academic institutions, national laboratories, and other recognized R&D institutions. The host institution should provide the necessary administrative and infrastructural support.
**Note 02:** The fellows will not be allowed to work with the Ph.D. guide/co-guide. It is also normally not availed at the same Department/institution where the candidates have earned their PhD/M.S./M.D degree.

scheme_name: Swanirbhar Naari
eligibility_text_source:
1. The weaver must be a resident of Assam.
1. The weaver must have at least one handloom in their household.
1. The weaver must have a valid bank account with Core Banking Solution (CBS).
1. The weaver should possess a valid yarn passbook issued by the Directorate of Handloom & Textiles, Assam (if available).
1. Indigenous weavers from Assam and intended weavers are allowed to register, subject to physical verification by concerned Inspectors of Handloom and Textile, Assam.

scheme_name: National AIDS Control Organisation (NACO) Internship Programme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be pursuing undergraduate, postgraduate, M.Phil, or PhD courses.
 - The applicant must be studying in disciplines such as Public Health, Preventive & Social Medicine, Health Management, Statistics, Social Sciences, or related fields.
 - The applicant must be enrolled in a recognized university or institution.

> **Reservation / Preference / Priority:**

- Preference will be given to applicants having an excellent academic track record and to research scholars, and candidates bringing project work relevant to NACO’s activities or concomitant with NACP.
- There shall be no bar for candidates to apply for a second internship with NACO.

> **Number of Interns:**

The number of interns can vary depending on the applications and requirements of NACO. However, at any point in time, the number of interns shall not be more than 10.
> **Placement:** 

The Interns will be placed in selected programme divisions within NACO or rotated through different divisions based on the prerequisites of the study programme.

scheme_name: National Awards for e-Governance
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a central ministry or department, state or union territory government, district, or urban local body.
1. The applicant should be an academic institution, research organization, or startup (as per the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade definition).
1. The applicant should have implemented an e-governance project using information and communication technology.
1. The applicant should ensure that the project was launched between 01.10.2023 and 30.09.2025.
1. The applicant should ensure that the project is fully operational as on 01.12.2025.
1. The applicant should submit only one project under one category.
1. The applicant should ensure that the project demonstrates innovation, efficiency, transparency, and citizen-centric service delivery.

scheme_name: New Akanksha Scheme (Educational Loan) (GNDTDC)
eligibility_text_source:
