**Note:** At least 50% of the total loan amount will be allocated for families having an annual income of up to ₹1,50,000/-.

scheme_name: National Awards For Empowerment Of Persons With Disabilities: National Awards For Institutions Engaged In Empowering Persons With Disabilities: Divyangjano Ke Liye Sarvshrestha Niyoktha
eligibility_text_source:
The "Divyangjano Ke Liye Sarvshrestha Niyoktha" award is given by DoEPwD to the outstanding employers of the disabled under: 
1. Government organization.
2. Public Sector Undertaking or Autonomous Body or Local Government Body.
3. Private or Non-Governmental Organization.

> **Selection Criteria & Weightage**

1. That at least 10% of the employees are disabled subject to a minimum of disabled persons in a given establishment. (In the case of a large Establishment employing 100 disabled persons or more, the condition of 10% need not be rigidly adhered to) - 20% 
1. That wherever necessary - 5%
1. That necessary environmental modifications at the worksite have been made including barrier-free access - 10%
1. That the employees with disabilities are offered the same conditions in service, including the rates of pay as given to other employees for similar work - 15%
1. That the employers have shown a sympathetic understanding of the problems of the disabled - 5%
1. That when necessary and feasible, such additional facilities such as accommodation and transport etc, are provided - 5%
1. Retention Rate - 10%
1. Employees Assessment - 10% 
1. Productivity Ensured - 10% 
1. x Screening Committee may devise any other assessment parameter which may facilitate shortlisting of applicants - 10%

> **Agencies Eligible to Make Nominations**

Any State/UT/ Autonomous Body / PSU and nongovernmental organization registered under the Trust Act/Society Act/Section 8 of Company Act, 2013.

scheme_name: National Awards For Empowerment Of Persons With Disabilities: National Awards For Institutions Engaged In Empowering Persons With Disabilities: Divyangjano Ke Liye Sarvshrestha Placement Agency
eligibility_text_source:
The "Divyangjano Ke Liye Sarvshrestha Niyoktha" award is given by DoEPwD to the outstanding employers of the disabled under: 
1. Government organization.
2. Public Sector Undertaking or Autonomous Body or Local Government Body.
3. Private or Non-Governmental Organization.

> **Selection Criteria & Weightage**

1. Placed at least 50% registered unemployed disabled persons in employment during the last five years and that at least 30% of them were women - 20% 
1. Total number of persons with disabilities placed during the last five years - 10%
1. His/her follow-up during the last five years coupled with placements of the people registered with him/her up to the end of the last year is outstanding - 25%
1. The percentage of dropout employees should not exceed 20% in a given year - 15%
1. The placement officer shall provide placement to all categories of disabled persons and maintain a balance amongst them while assisting for placement - 10%
1. Screening Committee may devise any other assessment parameter which may facilitate short-listing of applicants - 20%

scheme_name: National Awards For Empowerment Of Persons With Disabilities: National Awards For Institutions Engaged In Empowering Persons With Disabilities: Divyangjanon Ke Sashaktikaran Men Karyrat Sarveshrestha Sansthan (Private Organization, NGO)
eligibility_text_source:
> **For the Nominee (Institution)**

Any organization working for the empowerment of Persons with Disabilities for a minimum period of 10 years after registration under Society Act/ Trust Act / Section 8 of Companies Act 2013.
1. One Award for providing holistic comprehensive Rehabilitation Services. 
2. One Award for Promoting and Imparting Inclusive Education or Educational Institutions That Are Preparing Children With Disabilities for Inclusive Education for Higher Studies.

> **Selection Criteria & Weightage**

1. The Institution has started comprehensive service for people with various disabilities: 10% 
1. The Institution has adopted new equipment: 10%
1. The Institution has provided new services: 15%
1. The Institution has undertaken new strategies for improving existing services: 10%
1. The Institution has followed up with the clients in rehabilitation: 5%
1. The Institution should have at least 10 years of experience in the field in the area of education/training/ rehabilitation etc: 10%
1. The contribution of the institution in the extension of outreach services in and around its working headquarters: 5%
1. Motivation, involvement and participation particularly in rural areas: 10%
1. While selecting the institutions due weightage would be given to voluntary action through the participation of the local public and efforts for rendering rehabilitation services to persons with disabilities: 5%
1. The geographical area in which the Institution is providing services: 5%

scheme_name: National Awards For Empowerment Of Persons With Disabilities: National Awards For Institutions Engaged In Empowering Persons With Disabilities: Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan Ke Karyanvayan/badhamukta Vatavaran Ke Srijan Mein Sarvshrestha Rajya/ Ut/ Zila
eligibility_text_source:
> **Criteria 1, Weightage: 15%**

1. Total number of Public buildings in the State* 
1. Total Number of Public buildings* identified for the creation of the barrier-free environment. 
1. Total number of public buildings out of the above made accessible from Accessible India Campaign funds. 

* Offices, Cinema Halls, theatres, Parks, Hospitals, Museums, Police Stations, tourist places, Monuments, Educational Institutes, Banks, Post offices, ATMs, Commercial Complexes, Market places, Streets, Libraries, Courts etc.
> **Criteria 2, Weightage: 15%**

1. Total number of cost estimates sent to the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities 
1. Total number of buildings for which funds have been given and utilized. (financial year-wise details to be given beginning 2015-16 onwards)

1. The total number of buildings retrofitted with the following details:

scheme_name: National Award for Individual Excellence

- The applicant should demonstrate capability to serve emergency cleaning operations within 75 kilometers radius including smaller clustered towns.

eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Nari Adalat
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: National Awards to Teachers
eligibility_text_source:

- Central Govt. Schools, i.e. Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVs), Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas (JNVs), Sainik Schools run by the Ministry of Defence (MoD), Schools run by Atomic Energy Education Society (AEES) and Eklavya Model Residential Schools (EMRS) run by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs.
- Schools affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) (other than those at (a) and (b) above).
- Schools affiliated to the Council for Indian Schools Certificate Examination (CISCE) (Other than those at (a), (b) and (c) above).

2. Normally, retired teachers are not eligible for the award, but those teachers who have served a part of the calendar year (at least for four months, i.e. up to 30th April in the year to which National Awards relate) may be considered if they fulfil all other conditions.
3. Only regular Teachers and Heads of Schools with a minimum of ten years of service are eligible.

eligibility_text_source:

eligibility_text_source:

1. A one-time awardee is ineligible to apply for the subsequent four years.
1. The applicant should not submit an abridgment, adaptation, translation, or paraphrase of another text for the competition. 
1. Essay and thesis submitted for a university degree or examination are not eligible for this award.

scheme_name: New Akanksha Scheme (Higher Education Loan) (GGDC)

scheme_name: National Family Assistance Scheme- Chhattisgarh
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Chhattisgarh.
 1. The applicant should be a family member of the deceased person.
 1. The deceased person should have been the primary breadwinner on whom the family primarily depended for income.
 1. The family should have lost the primary breadwinner due to natural, unnatural, or accidental death.
 1. The family of the deceased primary breadwinner should be living Below Poverty Line (BPL).
 1. At the time of death, the deceased primary breadwinner should have been between 18 and 59 years old.

**Note 1:** The term "family" includes the husband, wife, minor children, unmarried daughters, and dependent parents.
**Note 2:** If there is more than one earning member in the family, assistance will be provided only upon the death of the member whose income contributes the most to the family.

scheme_name: National Family Assistance Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
 1. The applicant should be a family member or a dependent of the deceased person.
 1. The deceased person should have been the primary breadwinner (an earning member of the family) on whom the family primarily depended for income.
 1. The family of the deceased should be living Below the Poverty Line (BPL).
 1. At the time of death, the deceased should have been between 18 and 60 years old.

scheme_name: Naye Fal Bagicho Ki Sthapana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a farmer.
 1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
 1. The applicant must have agricultural land ownership and irrigation sources.
 1. Farmers with land between 0.4 and 4 hectares are eligible for the grant.
 1. For Scheduled Caste/Tribe farmers and tribal areas, the minimum area limit is 0.2 hectares.

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a citizen of India.
1. The family of the applicant must be living Below Poverty Line (BPL).
1. The primary breadwinner of the applicant's family must have been deceased.
1. The age of the deceased breadwinner must have been more than 18 years and less than 60 years.
1. The applicant must be the succeeding primary breadwinner of the family.

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme- Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a family member of the deceased primary breadwinner.
1. The deceased primary breadwinner must have been a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The family must be classified under the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
1. The deceased must have been between 18 and 59 years of age at the time of death.

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme - Sikkim
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of the state of Sikkim.
 1. The applicant should be from Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
 1. In the event of the death of the breadwinner of a family, the family will become eligible to apply for the scheme.
 1. The financial assistance will be paid to the household (includes spouse, minor children, unmarried daughters, and dependent parents) of the deceased person.
 1. The applicant should have opened the Bank Account under “Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana”.

**Note:** A woman in the family, who is a homemaker, is also considered as a "Breadwinner".

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme- Bihar
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Bihar.
 1. The applicant should be a family member or dependent of the deceased person.
 1. The deceased person should have been the primary breadwinner (an earning member of the family) on whom the family primarily depended for income.
 1. The family of the deceased should be living Below the Poverty Line (BPL).
 1. At the time of death, the deceased should have been between 18 and 60 years old.

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme for BPL Families
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 - The applicant's name should be registered in the BPL list or classified as destitute.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 59 years of age.
 - The applicant should not have any other source of income.
 - If eligible, both husband and wife are entitled to receive the benefit individually.
 - The applicant should not be receiving assistance or pension under any other government scheme.
 - The application must be submitted within one year from the date of death of the breadwinner.
 - The applicant must be the head of the family or a surviving family member considered the head after investigation.

> **Note:**

 - Benefit will be given to such surviving members in the family of the deceased, who after investigation, will be considered as the head of the family.
 - The word family means husband/wife/minor children/unmarried daughters and dependent parents.

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme - Gujarat
eligibility_text_source:

1. The deceased (male or female) should have been the primary income earner of the family.
1. The death of the primary income earner should have occurred due to natural or accidental causes.
1. The age of the deceased at the time of death should have been between 18 and 60 years.
1. The application should be submitted within two years from the date of death to be eligible under the scheme.

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme (Jammu and Kashmir)

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme (Maharashtra)

The research work will be assessed as per the UGC norms. The total period of award of JRF and SRF shall not exceed a period of 5 years.

scheme_name: National Food Security Mission NFSM
eligibility_text_source:
> All farmers can apply for the scheme.

**Criteria for Identification of Areas and Beneficiaries**
1) In accordance with the decision of the GoI regarding implementation of Special Component Plan (SCP) for Scheduled Castes and Tribal SubPlan (TSP) for Scheduled Tribes, 16% of the total allocation for SCP and 8% for TSP will be earmarked. However, States will be allowed to make allocation to SC/ST farmers proportionate to their population in the States/districts.
2) At least 33% allocation of the fund is to be made for small and marginal farmers.
3) At least 30% allocation of the fund is to be made for women farmers.
4) All the farmers are entitled to avail the assistance for various components of the Mission limited to 5 hectares in a season.
**Criteria for selection of districts:**
1) Districts with area more than 50,000 ha and yield below State average yield under NFSM-Rice and NFSM-Wheat have been selected.
2) All districts of north-eastern states (except Assam) with at least 5000 ha area under rice have been selected.
3) Districts with at least 15000 ha area under rice or wheat in hill states of Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir and Uttarakhand have been selected.
4) All districts of 29 states have been included under NFSM pulses.
5) In case of coarse cereals (Maize and Barley), the districts covering 70% of the total coarse cereals area of the state have been selected.
6) All States growing Jowar, Bajra, Ragi and Small Millets will be covered under the programme of NFSM

eligibility_text_source:

 - A household whose total irrigated land, as per revenue records, is less than 2 hectares; or has less than 1 hectare irrigated and 2 hectares unirrigated; or has a total area of less than 4 hectares of unirrigated land.
 - A person engaged in occupations such as rickshaw pulling, porter work, labor, rag picking, cobbling, blacksmithing, carpentry, rural artisanal work, domestic service, or sanitation work.
 - A household that cultivates land belonging to another farmer.
 - A household residing in a slum or hut in an urban area, living in Uttarakhand since or before the date specified in the relevant Government Order.
 - A household whose annual income does not attract liability under the Income Tax Act.
 - A person who is homeless and separated from their social class, residing in organizations or ashrams such as widows’ ashrams, child reform homes, beggars’ homes, leprosy ashrams, orphanages, ashrams for the mentally ill or handicapped, or old age homes.

scheme_name: Nashamukt Gram Panchayat Puraskar Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must represent a Gram Panchayat of Madhya Pradesh.
- The applicant must submit their application based on work performed and achievements realized in the previous year.
- The Gram Panchayat must have one hundred percent (100%) of its population living a drug-free life after giving up intoxication.
- The Gram Panchayat must include all villages under its jurisdiction.
- The Gram Panchayat must have taken action under the Cigarettes and Other Tobacco Products (Prohibition of Advertisement and Regulation of Trade and Commerce, Production, Supply and Distribution) Act 2003.
- The Gram Panchayat must have provided treatment for habitual addicts by admitting them for medical care in De-addiction cum Rehabilitation Centers.
- The Gram Panchayat must ensure that students studying in all schools operating within the Panchayat area are motivated against and resolved not to consume intoxicants.
- A news item should have been published about the drug-free Gram Panchayat in daily newspapers or magazines.

eligibility_text_source:

3. The benefit is applicable for up to two girls per family.
4. It is mandatory for the mother/father/guardian to submit an affidavit regarding availing or not availing the benefit of the scheme for other girl children in the family in the past. The affidavit must include the following declarations:

 - All information regarding movable and immovable properties and other details sought by me have been provided correctly.
 - I certify that this benefit is being availed for my first/second daughter.
 - No facts have been concealed by me.
 - I have provided the details of all family bank accounts along with bank statements for the last one year.

5. Girls living in Nari Niketan, Balika Niketan, orphanages, or other state government-aided homes will be exempted from the requirement of birth certificate, permanent residence certificate, income certificate, or parent’s name to avail the benefit after passing Class 12.

- Scholastic Aptitude Test (SAT), 90 minutes duration: 90 multiple-choice questions covering subjects, namely, science, social studies, and mathematics, as taught in classes 7th and 8th.

scheme_name: National Science Chair- Scientific Excellence
eligibility_text_source:
> **Eligibility for nomination:**

 1. The nominee should be an active resident Indian scientist/academician, with an outstanding research track record in any one or more areas of STEM and medicine, particularly during the last five years.
 1. The nominee must have received recognition for his/her work from national/international scientific bodies such as fellowship in prestigious academies and S&T awards.
 1. The nominee could be affiliated with an academic institutions/R&D lab or should have consent on the subject from the future host institution.

**Note:** The nominee should not hold any administrative roles and functions and is not in receipt of any honorarium/fellowship/salary from any other institute or organization at the time of initiation of the NSC award if offered by SERB. 
> **Assessment Criteria:** The assessment criteria for the selection of the awardee will be decided by NSCAC and it will be generally based on the following considerations:

 1. Outstanding research track record of the nominee and international standing in the field.
 1. Quality/originality of proposed research plan.
 1. Ability to mentor young researchers and implement proactive approaches towards the advancement of S&T talent in the country.

scheme_name: National Science Chair- Science Leadership
eligibility_text_source:

 1. The nominee must be a Science Leader and have recognition for his/her work from national/international scientific bodies such as fellowship in prestigious academies and S&T awards.
 1. The nominee could be affiliated with an academic institutions/R&D lab or should have consent on the subject from the future host institution.

**Note:** The nominee should not hold any administrative roles and functions and is not in receipt of any honorarium/fellowship/salary from any other institute or organization at the time of initiation of the NSC award, if offered by SERB.
> **Assessment Criteria:** The assessment criteria for the selection of the awardee will be decided by NSCAC and it will be generally based on the following considerations:

 1. Outstanding research track record of the nominee and international standing in the field.
 1. Quality/originality of proposed research plan.
 1. Ability to mentor young researchers and implement proactive approaches towards the advancement of S&T talent in the country.

scheme_name: National Savings Certificates (VIII-Issue) Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Any individual who is a resident of India can avail of the benefits under the scheme.
 1. On behalf of a minor or a person of unsound mind, the guardian can apply under the scheme.
 1. The minimum age of minors should be 10 years.

> **Deposits:**

 - A minimum of ₹1000/- and any sum in multiples of ₹100/- may be deposited in an account.
 - There shall be no maximum limit for deposits in an account or in accounts held by an account holder.
 - An individual may open any number of accounts.

> **Payment on Maturity:**

 - The deposit shall mature on completion of 5 years from the date of the deposit. The amount of maturity may be repaid to the account holder after submitting the specified form to the accounts office.
 - The maturity value of an account opened with one thousand rupees shall be One thousand four hundred sixty-two rupees and fifty-four paisa and proportionate for deposits made with any other sum. In the calculation of maturity value, any amount in fraction of a rupee shall be rounded off to the nearest rupee and for this purpose, any amount of fifty paisa or more shall be treated as one rupee, and any amount less than fifty paisa shall be ignored.
 - A certificate of annual accrual of interest shall be issued by the accounts office, on demand, to the account holder.

scheme_name: Nirman Shramik Pension Yojana (NSPY) (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

 - The applicant should be a resident of Odisha.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

 - The applicant should be any of the following:

 1. a construction worker and validly registered with the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers’ Welfare Board and has completed 60 years of age (Old Age Pension-OAP).
 1. a registered construction worker who is a widow (irrespective of age) (Widow Pension-WP)
 1. a widow of a registered construction worker (irrespective of age).
 1. a registered construction worker who is a leprosy patient with visible signs of deformity (CLP) as per rule.

scheme_name: National Scheme Of Welfare Of Fishermen
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility Criteria for Inland Fishermen**

1. Fishermen living in coastal areas and with whom the respective States and Union Territories have officially licensed are eligible for this scheme.
1. The age of fishermen must be below 60 years old.
1. Applicant fishermen must belong to the BPL (Below Poverty Line) category.
1. They must be engaged in full-time activities inland.

**Eligibility Criteria for Marine Fishermen**

All marine fishermen who operate under State, Union Territories, and FISHCOPFED are eligible for this government scheme for fishermen. However, there are other eligibility parameters marine fishermen must fulfill. These include -

1. Their respective States and Union Territories must officially license marine fishermen.
1. They must involve themselves in full-time activities at sea.
1. They must be a member of the Welfare Society or Federation or Cooperative Society.
1. Please note that the fishermen under FISHCOPFED can avail of funds available under the insurance component only.

scheme_name: Noni Suraksha Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The girl must have been born on or after April 1, 2014.

- The girl's parents must be permanent residents of Chhattisgarh.
- The girl must belong to a family living below the poverty line. The name of the family's head must be present in the current poverty line survey list of the Rural Development Department (currently, the 2002 survey list for rural areas and the 2007 survey list for urban areas is valid).
- The benefit under the scheme is limited to two daughters. If there are two sons, a third daughter is not eligible for the benefit.
- In the case of the second girl child, it is mandatory for the mother or father to have adopted a permanent family planning method before applying. 
- All girls born as twins or more than one child in the first/second delivery will receive the benefit of the scheme. 
- If a third girl child is born after the benefit has been given to twin girls born in the first delivery, the third girl child will not receive the benefit of the scheme.
- If the family has legally adopted a girl, she will be considered eligible and given the benefit of the scheme if she meets the other criteria. 
- The girl must not marry before the age of 18, and she must complete her 12th standard education to be eligible for benefits under the scheme. 

_*The decision to determine eligibility and approve/disapprove cases rests entirely with the concerned District Collector._

Girls born to HIV-affected couples (including A.P.L. families) after Ap

scheme_name: National Talent Scholarship Postgraduate
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be pursuing a Master's degree programme in Agriculture or allied disciplines.

1. The applicant should be enrolled in an Agricultural University located outside his/her State of domicile.
1. The applicant should be studying in an Agricultural University funded under the ICAR scheme "Strengthening and Development of Higher Agricultural Education in India".

> **Conditions for Continuation of Scholarship**

1. The student must maintain merit and good conduct as certified by the Head of the College/University/Institution.
1. The student must attend studies on a full-time basis and not undertake any employment.
1. The student must not remain absent without prior permission or participate in strikes/agitations.
1. The student must not receive any other scholarship during the tenure of the programme.
1. The student must not leave the course within one month of admission.
1. The student must not change the discipline/subject for which the scholarship was awarded.
1. The student must not change the University during the tenure of the scholarship.
1. The student must secure a minimum OGPA/CGPA of 7.00 out of 10.00 (6.50 out of 10.00 for SC/ST students).
1. The student must clear all papers in every semester and appear in regular examinations.

scheme_name: National Talent Scholarship Undergraduate

1. The applicant should be studying in an Agricultural University funded under the ICAR scheme "Strengthening and Development of Higher Agricultural Education in India".

**Conditions for Continuation of Scholarship**

1. The student must maintain merit and good conduct as certified by the Head of the College/University/Institution.
1. The student must attend classes regularly and not remain absent without prior permission.
1. The student must not participate in strikes, agitations, or activities that disrupt academic studies.
1. The student must pursue studies on a full-time basis and not undertake any part-time or full-time employment.
1. The student must not receive any other scholarship or financial assistance during the course of study.
1. The student must not leave the course within one month of admission.
1. The student must not change the discipline/subject initially allotted for which the scholarship was awarded.
1. The student must not change the University during the tenure of the scholarship.
1. The student must secure a minimum OGPA/CGPA of 7.00 out of 10.00 at the end of the first year (6.50 out of 10.00 for SC/ST students).

scheme_name: Nagar Van Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a Municipal Corporation, Municipal Council, Municipality, Urban Local Body, State Forest Department, Educational Institution, University, Government Organisation, or Non-Government Organisation.
- The applicant must have possession of the proposed land.
- The applicant must provide a commitment to bear the balance cost of the project beyond the provided grant.
- The applicant must ensure that the proposed area is situated on forest land or other vacant non-forest public land.
- The applicant must formulate a Development cum Management Plan with year-wise details of works under different components.

##### ***For Nagar Van***

- The applicant must develop the Nagar Van over a minimum area of 10 hectares.
- The applicant must restrict the maximum area for grant calculation to 50 hectares.
- The applicant must ensure the site is located within a 5-kilometre limit of the Municipal Corporation, Municipal Council, or Municipality.
- The applicant must maintain a minimum of 2/3rd of the Nagar Van area under woodland or tree cover.

##### ***For Nagar Vatika***

- The applicant must develop the Nagar Vatika in a minimum area of 1 hectare.
- The applicant must restrict the maximum area of the Nagar Vatika to 10 hectares.
- The applicant must ensure the site is located strictly within the city limits or congested parts of the cities.

##### ***Post-Selection Conditions***

- The applicant must keep all Nagar Vans and Nagar Vatikas open and accessible to the city

In order to dovetail this scheme with the PMFME scheme - existing enterprises supported under the PMFME scheme shall also be eligible for assistance under the "One Family One Enterprise (OFOE)" scheme provided the unit commenced production after expansion/ modernization/ diversification on or after 01/04/2022.

scheme_name: Odisha State Treatment Fund
eligibility_text_source:

However this will not restrict : Cancer patients from getting chemo in Govt. Day Care Centre without being formally admitted & Patients who have undergone kidney transplantation at Govt. Hospitals from getting post-operative care including supply of free medicines and investigation up to 6 months from the date discharge.

eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant must be located entirely in Assam
- The applicant must not have any part of the plantation or manufacturing facility located outside the State of Assam

**Production Subsidy Specific Eligibility**

- The applicant must be manufacturing Orthodox tea, Green tea, White tea, Oolong tea, Singhpo tea, Purple Tea, Yellow tea or other Speciality tea (not manufactured through Crush, Tear, Curl process).
- The applicant must have started production on or after 1st of April, 2020.

scheme_name: Out of Turn Employment to Sportspersons in State Services
eligibility_text_source:

Players/sportspersons participating in any of the following sports will be considered eligible: Athletics, Archery, Badminton, Basketball, Boxing, Canoeing & Kayaking, Cycling, Diving, Equestrian, Fencing, Football, Golf, Handball, Hockey, Judo, Lawn Tennis, Shooting, Rowing, Sailing, Swimming, Table Tennis, Taekwondo, Volleyball, Weightlifting, Wrestling, Baseball, Chess, Cricket, Kabaddi, Karate, Kho-Kho, and Mallakhamb.

scheme_name: One Time Financial Support for Economically Weaker Meritorious ST Students

The student must have taken admission in any professional course listed in [Annexure-A and Annexure-B](https://twd.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/Notification_for_Public_Domain-OTFS.pdf) during the academic year 2022-23, with a course duration of at least 2 years.

1. The applicant must have at least fifteen years of experience in the field of Journalism.
1. The applicant should not have been convicted for any criminal offense or punished by any Court of Law or reprimanded by the Press Council of India for misconduct or violation of ethics of journalism or for any other similar reason.
1. The Annual income of the applicant should be less than ₹ 5,00,000.

**NOTE:** Canvasing for the grant in any form will disqualify the candidature. Anybody found canvassing in his/her favor shall be debarred from consideration for a grant.

scheme_name: Overseas Visiting Doctoral Fellowship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The scheme is open to Indian nationals only.
 1. The applicant should have registered for a full-time Ph.D. Degree in any of the recognized Institutions / Universities in India in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (including Medicine, Pharma, Agriculture, and related S&T areas) disciplines.
 1. Part-time and sponsored students are not considered under the scheme.
 1. The students who have submitted their thesis for the award of the Degree of Ph.D. are not eligible to apply.

**Note 01:** The selected fellows while taking up the SERB overseas fellowship should not draw their regular research fellowship in India.
**Note 02:** The exchange visit of the supervisors should be during the tenure of the Ph.D. work of the applicant. Both supervisors should ensure a residency period of at least two weeks and not exceeding 30 days in their respective foreign institutes.
**Note 03:** The Indian Institution should ensure the availability of necessary facilities including scientific equipment, infrastructure, research personnel, etc. for facilitating the visit of overseas scientists for undertaking collaborative research. The institution should also extend all necessary administrative support to the proposed work.
**Note 04:** Purdue faculty may support only one OVDF application per call.

**Note:** "Permanent Incapacitation" means incapacitation on account of accident, disease, or any other reason which renders the working Journalist wholly unfit for working as such.

scheme_name: Production of Audio Albums of songs on Patriotism & National Integration
eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant must submit a typed copy of lyrics of songs to Tiatr Academy of Goa along with the application form.
- The applicant must obtain approval of lyrics from the Recommendation Committee appointed by Tiatr Academy of Goa.
- The applicant must receive official letter of acceptance from Tiatr Academy of Goa before production.
- The applicant must not make any adverse remark in any form either written or verbal against any Governments/Individuals/Institutions or anything against caste, creed and religion in the lyrics of songs which may hurt the sentiments of anybody.

##### ***Album Content Requirements***

- The audio album must include at least 10 songs of Patriotism & National Integration.
- The album should not have solo songs only.
- 50% of the songs must be group songs and children's songs.
- Melody and musical arrangement must be appropriate to the content of the song.
- The applicant will have to give 300 copies of Audio albums to Tiatr Academy of Goa.

##### ***Quality Control Process***

- Lyrics will be scrutinized by the Recommendation Committee.
- Committee will be free to advise the lyricists to improve the quality of lyrics.
- Tiatr Academy of Goa reserves the right to disapprove the lyrics as per the opinion given by Recommendation Committee.

- The applicant must ensure their performance is between 4 to 7 minutes duration.

##### ***Content Requirements***

- The applicant must ensure their script/performance does not contain any adverse remarks against any governments, individuals, institutions, or anything against caste, creed and religion.

##### ***Competition Categories Available***

- The applicant can participate in Monologue (Comedy Acting) category.
- The applicant can participate in Dialogue (Comedy Acting) category.
- The applicant can participate in Monologue (Non-Comedy Acting) category.
- The applicant can participate in Dialogue (Non-Comedy Acting) category.

##### ***Judging Criteria***
Acting abilities are evaluated based on six specific criteria:

- Delivery of dialogues
- Facial expression
- Reaction to others on the stage
- Movement on the stage
- Costumes
- Overall impact

##### ***Performance Guidelines***

- Light effects and background music are not considered dur

scheme_name: Post Doctoral Fellowship to Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Candidates
eligibility_text_source:
Target Group: Unemployed candidates from the deprived section of the society with Ph.D. Degree awarded and aptitude for research.

 1. The candidate must have a doctorate degree in the relevant subject preferably with published research work to their credit. (Provisional degree certificate may be accepted if degree is not awarded)
 1. The candidates should belong to the Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe category.
 1. Only Ph.D. awarded unemployed candidates will be eligible for the award of Post Doctoral Fellowship.
 1. The upper age limit for male applicants is 50 years as of the last date of receipt of online applications and 55 years in the case of female candidates.
 1. The candidates of SC/ST categories having 50% marks at the UG level and 55% marks at the PG level are eligible to apply under the Scheme.
 1. The candidate must upload the No Objection Certificate from the proposed research place duly forwarded by the Head of Department and Registrar/Director/Principal in the online application.
 1. Transgender candidates are eligible to apply under the scheme. The reservation shall be followed as per government of India norms.

**Note:** There shall be reservations for SC/ST/Persons with disability candidates as per provision of the Constitution and instructions issued by the Govt. of India from time to time.

scheme_name: Post Doctoral Fellowship To Women Candidates
eligibility_text_source:
1. The candidate should be unemployed.
 1. The candidates must have been awarded the Ph.D. degree to be eligible to apply (Provisional Certificate may be accepted in case the degree is not awarded).
 1. The candidate should be 55 years of age as of the last date of submission of the online application. There shall be age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PWD (Persons with Disabilities for 5 years.
 1. In the case of the general category minimum of 55% of marks at the UG level and a minimum of 60% of marks at the PG level. For reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PWD), a minimum of 50% of marks at the UG level and a minimum of 55% of marks at the PG level.
 1. Candidates applying for PDF must upload the No Objection Certificate from the proposed research centre duly signed by the HOD and Registrar/Principal/Director of the University/College/Institute.

scheme_name: Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship

1. Age Limit: The age limit to apply for this scheme is 21 years for undergraduate courses, and 35 years for postgraduate and doctoral programs.
1. Qualifying Marks: Candidates applying for the scholarship must have scored at least 55% marks or equivalent grade in their qualifying degree exams for postgraduate and doctoral courses, and 80% marks or equivalent grade in qualifying exams (PUC/12th class) for undergraduate courses.
1. Standardized Test Scores: The candidates should have cleared standardized tests like GRE/GMAT/TOEFL/IELTS exams with minimum score marks before applying for the scholarship.
1. Admission: Applicants should have secured admission into a full-time degree program at an overseas university/college.
1. Documents: The candidates must have the necessary documents like Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Latest Income Certificate, Last 2 years IT Returns, Degree/Master Degree Convocation Certificate, Degree/Master Degree All Semester Marks Card, Offer letter from the university obtained, Copy of the Passport, and Tuition fee and maintenance fee as sent by the university.
1. Field of Study: The scholarship covers various fields of study, including Engineering and Management, Pure Sciences and applied sciences, Agriculture sciences and medicine, International Commerce, Economics, Accounting Finance, Humanities, Social science, fine arts, and law.

 - The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

scheme_name: Promotion And Development Of Tea Tourism Infrastructure In Assam
eligibility_text_source:
While selecting the tea gardens for boosting tea tourism in the state, the following selection criteria would be followed:-

 1. Private Tea Garden companies’ Garden owners can apply.
 1. If the tea garden is on a lease, then, the leaseholder can also apply subject to the lease period in hand being a minimum of 15 years from the date of application.
 1. Tea Garden company/Garden owner/ lease holder must be willing to undertake a tea tourism scheme.
 1. Tea Gardens which are iconic/heritage will be preferred.
 1. The ideal size of the tea garden should be 50 hectares and above. In the case of the hill district, the ideal size may be reduced to 25 hectares.
 1. Tea Gardens must be free from all kinds of disputes, free from encroachment, and industrial pollution.
 1. Tea Gardens situated nearest to the reserved forest, wildlife sanctuaries, and National Parks as well as other existing major tourist destinations/circuits, will be preferred.
 1. Tea gardens which have many more existing tourist infrastructures, especially iconic bungalows, golf courts, etc. shall be preferred.
 1. Assistance will be given only for the renovation of iconic bungalows and newly created tourism infrastructures.
 1. For employment purposes in tea tourism-related activities dependent on Tea Garden, workers will be given preference.

eligibility_text_source:

- MSMEs taking up subsequent energy audits after 3 years.
- Enterprises having more than one unit may also avail of this concession, separately for each unit.
- There should be a gap of a minimum of three years between the previous energy audit and the subsequent energy audit i.e. present energy audit.

> **Compotients Eligible for Subsidy**

The expenditure incurred towards professional charges of conducting an Energy Audit alone is eligible.

scheme_name: Promotion of Energy Audit and Conservation of Energy (PEACE): Incentive for Implementing Energy Audit Recommendations
eligibility_text_source:
- AIl manufacturing MSMEs in the state which have undertaken energy audits arid have achieved at least 10% energy savings in terms of the number of units of energy consumed per unit of product manufactured with reference to the last 12 months average.
- Enterprises having more than one unit may also avail of this concession, separately for each unit.
- There should be a gap of a minimum of three years between the previous energy audit and the subsequent energy audit i.e. present energy audit.

***The percentage of savings in specific energy consumption (Kwh/Kcal per unit of product) shall be reckoned on twelve-month average energy consumption before the implementation of energy audit recommendations and a minimum three-month average after the implementation of the recommendation of the energy audit.**

> **Eligible Components**

- Cost of technology acquired for energy saving.
- Cost of replacement of equipment for energy conservation/ cost of machinery/accessories newly purchased & installed for the purpose.
- Cost of modification/ alteration made in the existing machinery/equipment/retrofit equipment for energy conservation.

**Note 07:** IGSTC will not be responsible for violation of any Rules and regulations by him/her.

If a beneficiary has availed subsidy earlier for installation of Bio-floc unit under different schemes of Government of India and State Government, for less than 6 nos. of tanks, He/ She shall also be eligible to avail further subsidy under this scheme. However, in such cases, the subsidy shall be limited to a maximum of 6 tanks which will include the installation of tanks earlier

scheme_name: Pension to Indigent Sportspersons of Odisha
eligibility_text_source:

Applicant should be at least 50 years of age if a male and at least 40 years of age if a female, provided that the age limit, in either case, may be relaxed by a maximum period of 10 years if the sportsperson has been suddenly incapacitated by an injury or a physical handicap or a serious ailment resulting in premature withdrawal from active sports (Medical Board Certificate is mandatory for this case).
 1. Applicant should have represented the State of Odisha in the sporting disciplines recognized by Sports & Youth Services Department as in [Annexure-I](https://sports.odisha.gov.in/sites/default/files/2021-11/guidelinesforsportspension.PDF). 1. - Applicant must possess a valid Aadhaar card (exemption for the candidates of such States which have not implemented Aadhaar completely).

> **Target Groups:**

 - **At

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a household belonging to the Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) category or the Priority Households (PHH) category.
1. Priority Households (PHH) are to be identified by the state governments/union territory administrations as per the criteria evolved by them.
1. Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) families are to be identified by the States / Union Territories as per the criteria prescribed by the Central Government, as listed below.

> **Criteria for Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) Families:**

1. The applicant should be a household headed by a widow, a terminally ill person, a disabled person, or a person aged 60 years or more, with no assured means of subsistence or societal support.
1. The applicant should be a widow, a terminally ill person, a disabled person, a person aged 60 years or more, a single woman, or a single man, with no family or societal support, and with no assured means of subsistence.
1. The applicant should belong to a primitive tribal household.
1. The applicant should be a landless agricultural laborer, a marginal farmer, or a rural artisan or craftsman (such as a potter, tanner, weaver, blacksmith, or carpenter); a slum dweller; or a person earning a livelihood on a daily basis in the informal sector (such as a porter, coolie, rickshaw puller, handcart puller, fruit and flower seller, snake charmer, rag picker, cobbler, or destitute person); or a person in other similar categories in both rural and urban areas.
1. The applicant should belong t

scheme_name: PM Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM JANMAN)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must belong to a Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) community.
- The applicant must reside in one of the 18 States and Union Territories having a notified Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group population.
- The applicant household must be identified as eligible through the physical survey conducted by state governments using the mobile application on the PM Gati-shakti Portal.

***Eligibility Specific to Interventions***

- The applicant household seeking a pucca house must currently reside in a kaccha house.
- The applicant household seeking solar power solutions must be an unelectrified household not covered under the Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme (RDSS).
- The applicant household seeking an on-grid electricity connection shall be an identified beneficiary household under PM-JANMAN, irrespective of standard eligibility criteria under the Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme (RDSS) for the State and Electricity distribution company (DISCOM).

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
eligibility_text_source: - All landholding farmers' families, which have cultivable land holding in their names are eligible to get benefit under the scheme.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha Evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan Yojana (PM-KUSUM) - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The beneficiary must be a farmer engaged in agricultural activities.
 1. As per the guidelines of MNRE, Government of India:

 - The farmer must agree to contribute 20% of the cost of the solar pump set as the farmer’s share.
 - The farmer must have his own borewell.
 - The farmer should currently be using a diesel pump set for irrigation.
 - A farmer who wishes to install a USPC along with the solar pump set must agree to make 100% payment for the said work.

scheme_name: PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant must be studying in a Primary or Upper Primary class (Class 1st to Class 8th) in a Government or a Government-aided school.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti Nirman - DNH&DD
states: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
eligibility_text_source:
1) The applicant must be a school-going child enrolled in a Government (Balvatika–VIII), Government-aided (VI–VIII) or Local Body (Balvatika–VIII) school.
2) The child should be studying in Balvatika, Primary classes I–V, or Upper Primary classes VI–VIII.
3) No income criteria are applicable; all enrolled children are eligible.
4) Children studying in Special Training Centres (STCs) under the RTE Act and CwSN enrolled under Home based Education are also eligible.

scheme_name: PM Street Vendor’s AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
eligibility_text_source:
- Street vendors in possession of Certificate of Vending / Identity Card issued by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
 - The vendors, who have been identified in the survey but have not been issued Certificate of Vending / Identity Card.
 - Street Vendors left out of the ULB-led identification survey or who have started vending after completion of the survey and have been issued a Letter of Recommendation (LoR) to that effect by the ULB / Town Vending Committee (TVC).
 - The vendors of surrounding development/ peri-urban/rural areas vending in the geographical limits of the ULBs, who have been issued a Letter of Recommendation (LoR) to that effect by the ULB / TVC.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be an unorganised worker.
1. The applicant must have an entry age between 18 and 40 years.
1. The applicant must have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or less.
1. The applicant must possess a Savings Bank Account or Jan Dhan account number with an Indian Financial System Code.
1. The applicant must possess a mobile phone.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Uchchatar Shiksha Protsahan Yojana - Special Scholarship Scheme for Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh
eligibility_text_source:
1. Candidates should be domicile of Union Territories of J&K and Ladakh.
1. Candidates must have passed 12th examination from JKBOSE or CBSE affiliated schools located in UTs of J&K and Ladakh.
1. The family income of the Candidate should be less than or equal to ₹8,00,000 per annum.
1. The candidate who wants to take admitted under the Lateral Entry Scheme have to pass a Diploma in Engineering from the Polytechnic Institutes recognized by the Directorate of Technical Education(DTE), UTs of J&K and Ladakh.
1. Candidates are compulsorily required to apply online on the AICTE web-portal and verify their documents through nearest Facilitation Centers of UTs of J&K and Ladakh within time frame. 
1. Admission under the Lateral Entry Scheme will be subject to allocation of seats / vacant seats of the preceding year in the Engineering Stream and in the relevant stream only. 
1. Merit list will be prepared for HSC and Lateral Entry students and the same shall be displayed on the student’s portal only for conduction of online counselling for final allotment of seats in colleges to eligible candidate’s subject to choice filling/availability of scholarships.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Uchchatar Shiksha Protsahan Yojana: Central Sector Interest Subsidy Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Education Loans taken under the IBA Model Education Loan Scheme.
 1. The benefit of the Scheme is applicable to students belonging to economically weaker sections having gross parental/ family income up to ₹4.5 lakhs per annum from all sources.
 1. Students enrolled in professional/technical courses only from NAAC accredited Institutions or professional/technical programmes accredited by NBA or Institutions of National Importance or Central Funded Technical Institutions (CFTIs). Those Professional Institutions/programmes, which do not come under the ambit of NAAC or NBA, would require approval of the respective regulatory body.
 1. Admissible only once either for Undergraduate or Post Graduate degree courses. This is also admissible for Integrated Courses (Graduate + Post Graduate).
 1. Students availing any other Central /State Government Scholarship or Fee reimbursement shall not be eligible for availing benefits under the CSIS Scheme.
 1. Interest Subsidy under this Scheme shall not be available to those students who discontinue their course midstream, or who are expelled from the Institution on disciplinary or academic grounds. However, the interest subsidy would be available only if discontinuation is due to medical grounds for which necessary documentation to the satisfaction of the Head of an educational institution needs to be provided.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Virasat ka Samvardhan
eligibility_text_source:
- The Age of the applicant should be 14 to 45 years for skilling and education component and 18 to 45 years for Women Leadership and Entrepreneurship Component
 - 33% of total seats in Skilling and Training component, 50% of total seats in Education component and 100% of total seats in Women Leadership and Entrepreneurship component are earmarked for women. 
 - The education qualification for Skilling and Training component would be as per NSQF norms and Education component would be as per NIOS norms. Candidates with aptitude and leadership qualities will be selected for Women Leadership and Entrepreneurship component.

scheme_name: PM-YASASVI: Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC, EBC and DNT Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. The scholarships will be open to Indian nationals belonging to OBCs, EBCs, and DNTs, as notified by the Central Govt./State Govt./ UT Admin. 
 1. These scholarships will be given for the study of all recognized post-matriculation or post-secondary courses pursued in recognized institutions as specified in para 3 of the [Guidelines](https://socialjustice.gov.in/public/ckeditor/upload/65661651839791.pdf). 1. Only those candidates who belong to OBCs/EBCs/DNTs so specified in relation to the State/UT to which the applicant actually belongs, i.e. permanently settled and who have passed the Matriculation or higher secondary or any higher examination of a recognized University or Board of Secondary Education will be eligible.
 1. Students studying in the XI Class of the Higher Secondary School courses or the XII class of the Multipurpose High School will not be eligible for it being a continuous school course. However, in cases, where the 10th Class examination of such courses is treated as equivalent to Matriculation and students who after passing the 10th Class join other courses, such students will be treated as post-matric students and will be eligible for the award of scholarships.
 1. Students pursuing Post-graduate courses in medicine will be eligible only if they are not allowed to practice during the period of their course.
 1. Students who after failing or passing the Under Graduate/Post-Graduate examinations in Arts/Science/Commerce join any recognized professional or

scheme_name: PM-YASASVI: Top Class College Education for OBC, EBC and DNT Students
eligibility_text_source:
> **Eligibility of Students:**

 1. The Scheme will cover OBC/EBC/DNT students for pursuing studies beyond Class XII.
 1. Those OBC/EBC/DNT students having total annual family income from all sources up to ₹2.50 lakhs and have secured admission in a full-time prescribed course in any of the notified institutions according to the general selection criteria prescribed by the concerned institution, will be eligible for a scholarship under the Scheme to the extent of the number of scholarships (slots) allocated to the institution for the course.
 1. The benefit of the Scheme will not be provided to more than 2 siblings in a family. The students will submit an affidavit to the Institution to certify that he/she is not the third sibling of the family who is availing of benefits under the scheme.

**Note 01:** In case the institution finds that the number of eligible candidates in the 1st year is less than the number of slots allotted to it, the balance slots may be offered to eligible students studying in 2nd, the 3rd and 4th year, etc. on the basis of inter-se merit of previous year’s result giving priority to those with a higher number of years left to complete their respective course i.e. 2nd year students are to get priority over the 3rd year students and soon.
**Note 02:** 30% of slots allotted to the Institution shall be reserved for eligible girl students as per their inter-se merit. In the absence of a sufficient number of girl students, the slots may be transferred to elig

scheme_name: PM-YASASVI: Top Class School Education for OBC, EBC and DNT Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. All the OBC, EBC, and DNT students studying in the Top Class Schools identified as per the modalities of the scheme with a household annual income of less than ₹2.5 lakhs would be eligible for the scheme.
 1. The meritorious students studying from Class 9 onwards till they complete Class 12 can avail of the benefits of the scheme.

> **Other Conditions:**

 - The students must have a Bank Account and while applying under the scheme should provide all necessary details.
 - The shortlisted School should have an Aadhaar-Based Attendance System that can be integrated into a central portal if required.
 - Students studying in schools assisted under this Scheme shall not be eligible for assistance under any other scheme of the Government for the items/purposes already covered under this Scheme.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Adi Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAAGY)
eligibility_text_source:
> **General Eligibility**

- The village must have at least 50% Scheduled Tribe (ST) population.
- The village must have a population of at least 500 in plain areas; 250 in hilly/tribal areas.
- The village must be selected by the State/UT Government based on criteria defined in the guidelines.

> **Preference / Priority**

- Preference is given to villages with higher ST population concentration.
- Priority is given to villages with significant infrastructure gaps.

scheme_name: Prime Minister's Award for Yoga
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should have rich experience and a deep understanding of Yoga.
1. The applicant (individual category) must be at least 30 years of age.
1. The applicant should have a minimum of 10 years of service with an excellent track record in promoting and developing Yoga.

**Note:** The applicant can apply or be nominated for only one category (Individual or Institutional) in a particular year.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. Village must have ≥50% Scheduled Caste (SC) population, but villages with ≥40% SC population may also be included in some states.
1. The village must be recognized in the latest Census data.
1. Ideally villages with ≥500 total population.
1. Villages already covered under PMAGY earlier phases may not be reselected.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Anusuchit Jaati Abhyuday Yojana (PM-AJAY) - Adarsh Gram
eligibility_text_source:
- The village must have more than 40% Scheduled Caste population as per the latest available Census data.

- The village must have total population ≥ 500 as per the latest available Census data.
- The village must be willing to participate in convergence implementation with other Central and State Government schemes.
- The village must constitute Village Level Convergence Committee headed by Sarpanch or Pradhan of the village.
- The village must complete Need Assessment exercise for all 50 monitorable indicators across 10 developmental domains.
- The village must prepare comprehensive Village Development Plan through online portal https://pmagy.gov.in.
- The village must obtain approval of Village Development Plan from Gram Sabha.
- The village must get Village Development Plan approved by District Level Project Appraisal cum Convergence Committee.
- The village should commit to utilize Gap-filling funds within 2 years from date of release.
- The village should agree to continue convergent implementation for additional 3 years after fund utilization period.

***Selection Process***
Villages will be selected in descending order of their Scheduled Caste population percentage.
***Priority***
Villages having less than or equal to 40% Scheduled Caste population but overall high number of persons belonging to Scheduled Castes shall be taken up after covering eligible villages with more than 40% Scheduled Caste population.

scheme_name: Post Matric and Top Class Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant Should be studying in graduate/post-graduate courses.
1. The maximum annual income of the guardian should be ₹2,50,00/- for post-matric and ₹6,00,000/- for top class.
1. The applicant has 40 percent or more disability.

scheme_name: Maintenance Allowance To VJNT And SBC Students Studying In Professional Courses And Living In Hostel Attached To Professional Colleges
eligibility_text_source:
1. Students should belong to VJNT or SBC category.
1. Students should be studying in professional courses such as Medical, Engineering, Animal Husbandry, etc.
1. Students should submit an Application form for admission in Govt. Hostel.
1. Students should have been admitted to the hostels attached to professional colleges or approved private hostels.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Gramin
eligibility_text_source:
The universe of eligible beneficiaries under PMAY-G will include all the houseless households living in zero, one, or two-room houses with kutcha walls and kutcha roofs (as per SECC data, and subject to the exclusion process).
> **Criteria for Automatic/Compulsory Inclusion -** 

1. Households without shelter
2. Destitute/ living on alms
3. Manual scavengers
4. Primitive Tribal Groups
5. Legally released bonded laborer
> **Prioritisation within the Universe**

i. There will be multilayered prioritization within the universe of eligible PMAY-G beneficiaries. Priority will first be assigned on the basis of parameters reflecting housing deprivation in each category viz., SC/ST, Minorities, and Others. 
ii. To begin with, households will be prioritized based on houselessness followed by the number of rooms; zero, one, and two rooms, in that order. 
iii. In a particular social category viz., SC/ST, Minorities, and Others, households that are houseless or living in houses with a lower number of rooms shall not be ranked below households living in houses with a higher number of rooms.
Within the above priority groups, households that fulfill the criteria of "compulsory inclusion", will be further elevated. Automatically included households shall not rank lower than other households within a priority group. Inter-se priority within the two subgroups viz. households which are automatically included and otherwise will be determined based on their cumulative deprivation scores.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban
eligibility_text_source:
1. The family identifies as one of the following -
a) Economically Weaker Section (EWS): households with annual income up to ₹3,00,000.
b) Low Income Group (LIG): households with annual income between ₹3,00,001 and ₹6,00,000.
c) Middle Income Group-1 (MIG-1): households with annual income between ₹6,00,001 and ₹12,00,000.
d) Middle Income Group-2 (MIG-2): households with annual income between ₹12,00,001 and ₹18,00,000.
2. The applicant or his/her family members must not own a pucca house in any part of the country.
3. The family must comprise of husband/wife and unmarried children.
4. The town/city in which the family resides must be covered under the scheme.
5. The family MUST NOT have previously availed the benefits of any housing-related schemes set up by the Government of India.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Gramin, Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The selection of eligible beneficiaries for housing is done based on the “Social, Economic and Caste Census 2011 (SECC-2011)” and the Awaas Plus Permanent Waiting List.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP)
eligibility_text_source:
**Individuals:** 

- The applicant must hold D. Pharma/B. Pharma degree or employ qualified pharmacists.

**Organizations/NGOs:** 

- The applicant must employ B. Pharma/D. Pharma degree holders.

**Government hospital premises (including medical colleges):** 

- Preference to reputed NGOs/charitable organizations though individuals are also eligible.

scheme_name: Post Matric College Scholarship (Scheduled Caste)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a student.
1. The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
1. The student must come from a family with an income limit of up to ₹6.00 lakh to qualify.

**Note:** One-third of the subsistence allowance will be payable to students residing in departmental hostels who are provided with free lodging/boarding facilities.

scheme_name: SC/ST Pre Matric Scholarship - Uttar Pradesh
eligibility_text_source:
1. Beneficiary must be a resident of Uttar Pradesh.
1. Beneficiary must belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe(ST) category.
1. Beneficiaries who are dependents of people in occupations traditionally considered untouchable.
1. Financial:

 - Component 1: Beneficiaries' parents' annual income should not be more than ₹2,50,000/-.
 - Component 2: No income criteria.

1. All the children of the applicant's parents will be eligible.
1. Students cannot receive other scholarships alongside this one (with some exceptions).
1. Beneficiary's age limit is 12 to 20 years old (as of July 1st).

scheme_name: Prime Minister Early Career Research Grant
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian citizen.
 1. Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) or Persons of Indian Origin (PIO) working as regular employees in any publicly funded and recognized R&D laboratories/institutions in India are eligible to apply as Principal Investigator (PI), in collaboration with an Indian Co-PI, provided they meet the eligibility criteria specified by ANRF (ANRF Funding Guidelines for PIO/OCI).
 1. The applicant should hold a Ph.D. degree in Science or Engineering or an MD/MS/MDS/MVSc degree.
 1. The applicant should be in a regular academic/research position at a recognized academic institution, national laboratory, or any other recognized R&D institution.
 1. The applicant is eligible to apply within two years of joining the institution.
 1. The upper age limit is 42 years, with a relaxation of three years for SC/ST/OBC/Physically Challenged and women candidates.
 1. The applicant should not have received funding under YSS, ECR, SRG, CRG (erstwhile EMR), EMEQ, or any other project grant from the erstwhile SERB or ANRF.

***Other conditions***

 - An applicant is eligible to apply only one proposal during a call.
 - Only a one-time career grant will be provided.
 - Extension of the project tenure is not permitted under this programme.

***Termination and transfer of the project:***

 1. If any Principal Investigator (PI) wishes to terminate the project, they shall inform ANRF immediately with proper justifications. The implementing institute should not inc

scheme_name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme
eligibility_text_source:
> **For PMEGP new enterprises (Units)** 

1. Any individual, above 18 years of age. 
1. There will be no income ceiling for assistance in setting up projects under PMEGP. 
1. For setting up of project costing above Rs.10 lakh in the Manufacturing sector and above ₹ 5,00,000 in the Business /Service sector, the beneficiaries should possess at least VIII standard pass educational qualification. 
1. Assistance under the scheme is available only for new projects sanctioned specifically under the PMEGP. 
1. Existing Units (under PMRY, REGP, or any other scheme of the Government of India or State Government) and the units that have already availed of Government Subsidy under any other scheme of the Government of India or State Government are not eligible. 

> **For up-gradation of existing PMEGP / REGP / MUDRA units**

1. Margin Money(subsidy)claimed under PMEGP has to be successfully adjusted on the completion of the lock-in period of 3 years. 
1. The first loan under PMEGP/REGP/MUDRA has to be successfully repaid in the stipulated time. 
1. The unit is profit-making with good turnover and has the potential for further growth in turnover and profit with modernization/upgrading of the technology. 

**Reservation / Preference / Priority**
Priority will be given to the persons affected by natural calamities/disasters in the areas which are declared as affected by "disaster" as defined under Section 2(d) of the Disaster Management Act, 2005 by the Ministry of Home Affairs.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojna (PMFBY)
eligibility_text_source:
1. All farmers, including tenant farmers and sharecroppers growing notified crops in notified areas.
1. Farmers must have an insurable interest in the insured crops.
1. Farmers must possess a valid and authenticated land ownership certificate or a valid land tenure agreement.
1. The farmer must be a cultivator or sharecropper on the insured land.
1. The farmer must apply for insurance coverage within the prescribed time frame, usually within 2 weeks of the start of the sowing season.
1. Farmers must not have received compensation for the same crop loss from any other medium or source.

scheme_name: Prime Minister’s Fellowship for Doctoral Research
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be Indian.
 - The applicant should be a full-time PhD scholar in any recognized Indian university/institute/research laboratory.
 - The scholar’s PhD registration date should not be more than 14 months from the date of submission of application i.e. the applicant should apply within 14 months of PhD admission/registration whichever date is earlier.
 - The applicant should have a valid industry partner who should be ready to support the research project financially as well as provide guidance and mentorship.
 - The mutually agreed topic of research, between the applicant and the supporting company, should be innovative, have practical relevance, and have industrial application.
 - The selected applicant should be willing to work with/in the supporting company as and when required during the project period.
 - The applicant should not draw any other fellowship after the selection.

scheme_name: President’s Medal for Distinguished Service
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a member of the Police Forces, CAPFs, CPOs, Fire Services, Prison Administration, Home Guards, or Civil Defence.
1. The applicant should have a clean service record; no major penalties in the review period.
1. The applicant should demonstrate exceptional ability, merit, or devotion to duty.
1. The applicant should have completed a minimum 25 years of service.
1. The applicant should have at least 10 years of ACR/APAR records under review.
1. The applicant should have at least 8 Very Good/Outstanding ratings; at least 5 must be Outstanding.
1. The applicant should not have more than 1 missing ACR; last year ACR must be available.
1. The applicant should maintain SHAPE 1 physical fitness (relaxation possible in exceptional cases).
1. The applicant should have a gap of at least 6 years after receiving MSM (relaxable to 4 years in special cases).

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Formalisation Of Micro Food Processing Enterprises
eligibility_text_source:
> **The Eligible Borrowers for PMFMPE can be:**

1. Farmer Producer Organization (FPO)
2. Self-Help Groups
3. Co-operatives
4. Existing Micro Food Processing Entrepreneurs
5. New Units, whether for individuals or groups would only be supported for One District One Product (ODOP)
