1. Existing micro food processing units in operations.
2. Existing units should be those identified in the SLUP for ODOP products or by the Resource Person on physical verification. In the case of units using electrical power, the electricity bill would support it being in operation. For other units, existing operations, inventory, machines, and sales would form the basis.
3. The enterprise should be unincorporated and should employ less than 10 workers.
4. The enterprise should preferably be involved in the product identified in the ODOP of the district. Other micro-enterprises could also be considered.
5. The applicant should have ownership rights of the enterprise.
6. Ownership status of the enterprise could be proprietary / partnership firm.
7. The applicant should be above 18 years of age and should possess at least VIII standard pass educational qualification.
8. Only one person from one family would be eligible for obtaining financial assistance. The “family” for this purpose would include self, spouse, and children.
9. Willingness to formalize and contribute10% of project cost and obtain Bank loan.
10. Cost of the land should not b

scheme_name: Police Medal for Meritorious Service
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a serving police officer or personnel of CAPFs/CPOs.
1. The applicant should have a clean service record without major penalties.
1. The applicant should demonstrate meritorious and consistent service.
1. The applicant should have a minimum service of 18 years.
1. The applicant should have ACRs available for the last 10 years.
1. The applicant should have at least 8 Very Good or above ACRs; at least 1 should be Outstanding.
1. The applicant should not have any average ACR during the review period.
1. The applicant should have at least 1 ACR after the Non-Initiation Certificate (NIC) period (if applicable).
1. The applicant should not have more than 1 missing ACR.
1. The applicant should ensure the last year ACR is available.
1. The applicant should be physically fit and fall under SHAPE 1 category; relaxation to SHAPE 2 may be given in exceptional cases.
1. The applicant should not have more than 3 nominations (for Group A officers).
1. The applicant should not have adverse vigilance or disciplinary issues.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Package : Insurance Scheme For Health Workers Fighting COVID-19
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a healthcare worker; including doctors, nurses, paramedics, and support staff.
- The applicant must be directly involved in COVID-19 related duties.
- The applicant must be a citizen of India.
- The applicant must be working in a COVID-19 designated hospital or care facility.
- The applicant must be registered under a government health program.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. A region must be a habitation. It cannot be a hamlet or revenue village to be eligible for the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY).

2. The Central Government describes habitation as a population cluster residing within an area that remains constant over time. Some of the local words used to describe a habitation are:
Majras, Desam, Hamlets, Tolas, Dhanis

3. The hamlet must have a higher population as per the 2001 Census to be eligible for road connection. The eligible habitations have a population of above 500 persons in plain areas and around 250 persons and above in hilly areas.

scheme_name: Prime Minister’s Internship Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The candidate belongs to Indian nationality.
 - The candidate’s age should be between 21 to 24 years (as on the last date for submission of the application). 
 - The candidate should not be full-time employed.
 - The candidate should not be engaged in full-time education.
 - The candidate enrolled in online/distance learning programmes are eligible to apply.
 - The candidate should be completed High School, Higher Secondary School, possess a certificate from an ITI, hold a diploma from a Polytechnic Institute, or are graduates with degrees such as BA, B.Sc, B.Com, BCA, BBA, B.Pharma, etc.
 - The candidate family income should not exceed ₹8,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian National.
1. If minors above ten years apply, they will require support from their legal guardians for administering their PMJDY account.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The age of the applicant must be between 18 to 50 Years.
1. The applicant must hold an individual bank / post office account.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Vikas Karyakram
eligibility_text_source:
- The projects under PMJVK can be proposed in the identified areas as approved and certified by the SLC (where the minority population is more than 25% of the total population in the catchment area - 15Km radius).
 - The State Level Committee (SLC) shall consider viability af all the projects before recommending the projects to the Ministry. The proposed projects should be sustainable and clear provision should be made by the State Government/ UT Administration regarding the recurring cost and maintenance / operationalization cost as well as manpower requirement, so that there is no such problem once the infrastructure project has been completed.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Vikas Mission (PMJVM)
eligibility_text_source: - **MSP Support:** Fixation and procurement of non-nationalised, non-timber Minor Forest Produce (MFPs) to ensure a fair price to tribal gatherers. - **Infrastructure Development:** Creation and development of Haat Bazars, storage godowns, and other facilities, including special projects for tribal trade and storage. - **Value Addition:** Support for processing and value addition to various MFPs, non-MFPs, and other tribal produce/products to enhance income. - **Enterprise Creation:** Establishment of Van Dhan Vikas Kendras (VDVKs) and Van Dhan Producer Enterprises (VDPEs) for organised tribal business activities. - **Marketing Support:** Facilitation of sale and purchase of products made by VDVKs/VDPEs and other tribal products such as handloom and handicrafts. - **Branding & Promotion:** Advertisement, branding, and promotion of tribal products through participation in festivals, exhibitions, tribal artisan melas, and Mahotsavas. - **Skill & Entrepreneurship Development:** Training and capacity building for tribal beneficiaries to develop skills and entrepreneurial capabilities. - **Geo-Tagging of Assets:** Geo-tagging of VDVKs, VDPEs, and other assets created under the scheme for better monitoring and tracking. - **Geographical Indication Support:** Promotion and support for Geographical Indication (GI) tagging of tribal produce/products. - **IT & E-Commerce Development:** Building and upgradation of IT infrastructure, e-commerce platforms, and digitisation of operations.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maandhan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Small or Marginal Farmer.
 1. The applicant should have cultivable landholding up to 2 hectares as per State or Union Territory land records.
 1. The applicant should be between 18 and 40 years of age at the time of entry.
 1. The applicant’s name should appear in land records as on 01.08.2019.
 1. The applicant should possess an Aadhaar card.
 1. The applicant should have a savings bank account or PM-KISAN account.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana - Har Khet Ko Pani (Surface Scheme) Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The beneficiary must be a farmer engaged in agricultural activities.
 1. As per the guidelines issued by the Government of India:

 - For the construction of collective irrigation schemes, clusters will be formed within a radius of 5 km from the proposed village, with a command area of 20 hectares or more.
 - In the case of an individual scheme, the command area should be 10 hectares or more.
 - The cost of the scheme must not exceed ₹4.00 lakh per hectare.
 - The benefit-cost ratio of the scheme must be greater than 1.0.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana - Har Khet Ko Pani (Groundwater Irrigation Scheme) Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The beneficiary must be a farmer engaged in agricultural activities.
 1. As per the guidelines issued by the Government of India:

 - For the construction of collective irrigation projects, a command area of 5 hectares must be available.
 - According to the report of the Central Ground Water Board, Government of India, the command area must be classified under the safe category.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana: Per Drop More Crop
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. All the farmers of the State & Union Territory are eligible to take the benefits of this scheme.
1. The subsidy payable to the beneficiary will be limited to an overall ceiling of 5 hectares per beneficiary.

**Note 01:** The beneficiary has to purchase only BIS-marked systems/components under the scheme.
**Note 02:** The scheme is to be implemented through the mechanism of Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). Aadhaar details of the beneficiary are required to access the benefit of the programme.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0 - Recognition Of Prior Learning
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian national.
1. The applicant should possess a valid Aadhaar.
1. The applicant should be in the age group of 18 to 59 years.
1. The applicant should be a candidate with prior learning experience or skills, willing to get assessed and certified.
1. The applicant should have prior experience in the job role for which RPL certification is being sought, as specified in the job role.
1. The applicant should fulfil the eligibility criteria of the respective job role.
1. The applicant should not be enrolled again in the same job role in which he/she was already trained earlier.
1. The applicant, if already certified once under PMKVY, opting for a lower NSQF-level course in the same sector, shall be discouraged; however, a higher job role in the same sector is permitted.
1. The applicant may enrol not more than twice in the same sector, with a six-month gap between the certification date of the first course and the batch start date of the subsequent course.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0- Special Projects
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be of Indian nationality.
1. The applicant should possess a valid Aadhaar.
1. The applicant should fulfil the eligibility criteria of the respective job role.
1. The applicant should be in the age group of 15 to 45 years.
1. The applicant should belong to a marginalised or vulnerable group, or another group requiring special attention, or should be undertaking job roles with a focus on future skills.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0 - Short-Term Training
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian national.
1. The applicant should possess a valid Aadhaar.
1. The applicant should be in the age group of 15 to 45 years.
1. The applicant should fulfil the eligibility criteria of the respective job role, as defined in the course curriculum of the job role to be offered.
1. The applicant may be a candidate looking for fresh skilling, re-skilling, or upskilling.
1. The applicant may be an out-of-education candidate, a school/college dropout, or an unemployed youth.
1. The applicant should not be enrolled again in the same job role in which he/she was already trained earlier.
1. The applicant, if already certified once under PMKVY, opting for a lower NSQF-level course in the same sector, shall be discouraged; however, a higher job role in the same sector is permitted.
1. The applicant may enrol not more than twice for training in the same sector, with the second time being for a higher NSQF-aligned job role only, and with a six-month gap between the certification date of the first course and the batch start date of the subsequent course.

scheme_name: Procedure for Making Medical Certificate (for Certifying Disability)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The applicant must be a person with disabilities.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah-yojana (PM-MKSSY): COMPONENT 1B: One-Time Incentive for the Adoption of Aquaculture Insurance
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Post Matric Merit Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a bonafide permanent resident of Mizoram, or the child/ward of a bonafide permanent resident of Mizoram.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Backyard Ornamental Fish Rearing Unit - Gujarat
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
1. The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
1. The applicant must have their own bank account.
1. The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
1. The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport as proof of identity.

> **Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:**

- All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
- Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

eligibility_text_source:

1. The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
1. The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport as proof of identity.

> **Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:**

- All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
- Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant may belong to any caste category, including General, Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Other Backward Class (OBC), or be a woman.
1. The applicant must have availability of land and a water supply source.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be of at least 19 years old and a pregnant women.
 1. The applicant should be employed and experiencing wage-loss due to the pregnancy.
 1. The scheme is applicable only for the first live birth.
 1. Eligible beneficiary can apply for the benefit under PMMVY scheme within 270 days from the child birth. 
 1. If a beneficiary delivers twins/ triplets/ quadruplets in her second pregnancy, with one or more children being girl, she will receive incentive for second girl child as per the PMMVY 2.0 norms.

**Criteria for determining socially and economically disadvantaged sections of society are the following:**

 1. Women belonging to scheduled castes and scheduled tribes
 1. Women who are partially (40%) or fully disabled (Divyang Jan)
 1. Women holder of BPL Ration Card
 1. Women Beneficiaries under Pradhan Mantri Jan Aarogya Yojana (PMJAY) under Ayushman Bharat.
 1. Women holding e-Shram card
 1. Women farmers who are beneficiaries under Kisan Samman Nidhi
 1. Women holding MGNREGA Job Card
 1. Women whose net family income is less than Rs. 8 Lakh per annum
 1. Pregnant and Lactating AWWs/ AWHs/ ASHAs
 1. Women holding Ration Card under NFSA Act 2013.
 1. Any other category as may be prescribed by the Central Government

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
##### Eligible borrowers

- Individuals
- Proprietary concern.
- Partnership Firm.
- Private Ltd. Company.
- Public Company.
- Any other legal forms.

**Note 01:**The applicant should not be a defaulter to any bank or financial institution and should have a satisfactory credit track record.
**Note 02:** The individual borrowers may be required to possess the necessary skills/experience/ knowledge to undertake the proposed activity.
**Note 03:** The need for educational qualification, if any, is assessed based on the nature of the proposed activity and its requirement.

scheme_name: Procedure for Making "Orphan Certificate" for Getting Horizontal Reservation for Orphan Children
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
1. Children whose both biological or adoptive parents have died within 21 years from the child’s birth are eligible for reservation.
1. Orphan children residing in voluntary or government-run homes within the state are also eligible for reservation.

scheme_name: Pre-Matric & Post-Matric Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam.
- The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community.
- The applicant must produce a valid Caste certificate which is duly certified or issued by the District Commissioner of the concerned district or his or her designated officer.
- The applicant must submit an undertaking on the annual family income stating that it is not exceeding ₹5,00,000/- per annum.
- The Pre-Matric Scholarship is awarded to students from Class 9th to Class 10th.
- The Post-Matric Scholarship is awarded to students pursuing studies from Class 11th to normal Three Years Degree Courses (Bachelor of Arts, Bachelor of Business Administration, Bachelor of Commerce, Bachelor of Science, Integrated Graduate plus Post Graduate courses like integrated Master of Business Administration / Bachelor of Arts-Bachelor of Law / Bachelor of Arts-Bachelor of Education / Master of Social Work, etc.).
- The scholarships are available for all recognized post-matriculation or post-secondary courses in recognized institutions.

##### ***Special Provisions***

- All children of the same parents or guardians are eligible.
- Students studying through correspondence course are also eligible to apply.

scheme_name: Palak Mata-Pita Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a close relative, guardian, or foster parent taking care of the child.
 - The applicant must be caring for a child aged between 0 to 18 years.
 - The applicant must be taking care of a child whose both parents are deceased, or whose father is deceased and mother has remarried.
 - The applicant must ensure that the child between 3 to 6 years is enrolled in an Anganwadi center.
 - The applicant must ensure that the child above 6 years is enrolled in a school for compulsory education.
 - The applicant must submit a certificate from the school or institution every year confirming the child’s ongoing education.
 - The applicant must have an annual family income not exceeding ₹27,000/- in rural areas.
 - The applicant must have an annual family income not exceeding ₹36,000/- in urban areas.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar
eligibility_text_source:
1. A child should be an Indian Citizen.
1. A child above the age of 5 years and not exceeding 18 years (as of 31st July of respective year).
1. The act/incident/achievement should have been within 2 years of the last date of receipt of application/nomination for the year of consideration.
1. The child must have made outstanding contributions in any of the seven categories specified above

 - Bravery.
 - Art & Culture.
 - Environment.
 - Innovation.
 - Science & Technology.
 - Social Service.
 - Sports.

scheme_name: Prime Minister Research Chair (PMRC) Scheme - For Fellows
eligibility_text_source:
1. Eligible Fellow for the Scheme are:

 - Indian origin (Indian nationals, Overseas Citizens of India, or Persons of Indian Origin) researchers / scientists and professionals working at leading foreign HEIs, research laboratories, and research institutions, reputed companies and other industries.
 - The Fellowship is in-person and they will be expected to work at Host Institutions. The Scheme will support Fellows for a pre-determined period of duration, with a maximum tenure of 5 years based on performance of the candidate.

2. Fellows must be able to demonstrate a distinguished track record in research, as evidenced by publications, patents, awards, citations, or other internationally recognised indicators of excellence. They must also be willing to commit to the prescribed engagement period and associated responsibilities including research, teaching, and mentorship. It should be noted that candidates currently based in India are also eligible, provided they meet the Indian-origin criterion and other requirements as determined by the Empowered Committee.
3. PMRC Fellows will be expected to:

 - Conduct original, high-impact research in the identified priority sector(s) in collaboration with the Host
 - Teach and co-design courses, contributing to curriculum enrichment
 - Mentor doctoral, postdoctoral, and postgraduate students and junior researchers
 - Facilitate international collaborations, publications, and co-funded R&D projects
 - Engage with industry partners to prom

scheme_name: Prime Minister’s Rural Development Fellows Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant's age must be between 22 years and 27 years at the time of application.
- The applicant must hold at least a graduate degree from a recognized University with a four-year degree course.
- The applicant must hold a post-graduate degree from a recognized University in case the graduate degree course duration was less than four years.
- The applicant's one-year post graduate course will also be considered valid for eligibility if it is regular, full time, and if the degree/equivalent diploma is recognized by the All India Council for Technical Education/Medical Council of India/University Grants Commission.
- The applicant must have achieved at least 50% marks in the aggregate or equivalent grade at the graduate or Post-graduate level, whichever is qualifying.
- The applicant must possess physical fitness, certified by Government Medical Hospital, for the arduous nature of the work.
- The maximum age limit will be 32 years for candidates belonging to the Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe category.

> ***Other Conditions***

_*Fifteen percent (15%) of seats are reserved for Scheduled Castes._
_*Fifteen percent (15%) of seats are reserved for Scheduled Tribes._

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
1. Native and studying in Uttar Pradesh. 
1. Annual income of the parents/guardians of the students upto ₹ 2 Lakh.
1. Students studying in government schools, aided and recognized educational institutes (provided the availability of the budget).

scheme_name: Post Metric Scholarship For OBC Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
 1. Annual family income is up to Rs 3,00,000/-
 1. Have passed Class 10.
 1. Belong to the OBC category.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana
eligibility_text_source: Individual bank account holders of participating banks aged between 18 years (completed) and 70 years (age nearer birthday) who give their consent to join / enable auto-debit, will be enrolled into the scheme.

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for Class 11th 12th And College
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be from the Scheduled Tribe category.
1. The applicant should be studying in class 11th, 12th, college, PhD, or professional courses.
1. The applicant should be studying in a government or government self-financed educational institution (income limit restriction has been abolished for these institutions).
1. The parents/guardians of the applicant should have an annual income of up to ₹6.00 lakh if the student is studying in a non-government institution (non-professional courses).
1. The applicant should be eligible for the payment of half the non-refundable compulsory fees, including tuition fees, at prescribed rates for government-related courses.

scheme_name: Pashudhan Mitra Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Chhattisgarh.
1. The applicant should have experience in animal husbandry or artificial insemination.
1. The applicant should be physically fit for fieldwork.
1. The applicant should possess basic knowledge of vaccination and castration procedures.

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarships to the Children of Those Engaged in Occupations Involving Cleaning and Prone to Health Hazards
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility**
The scholarship will be admissible to the children/wards of Indian Nationals who, irrespective of their religion belong to one of the following categories:

1. Tanners & Flayers.
1. Waste Pickers.
1. Persons who are Manual Scavengers as defined under section 2(I) (g) of the Manual Scavengers Act 2013.
1. Persons engaged in hazardous cleaning as defined in Section 2(I) (d) of the Manual Scavengers Act 2013.

Other Conditions

1. The students may not be allowed to take up employment or render help to parents in their work during the course of their studies.
1. The award will be discontinued if a student fails to secure annual promotion except in case of unavoidable reasons to be certified by the Head of the Institution in which the student is studying.
1. If a student violates school discipline or any other terms and conditions of the scholarships, the scholarship may be suspended or even cancelled, subject to the satisfaction of the competent school authority. 
1. Migration of students from one institution to another would not normally be allowed during the course of the academic year except under exceptional circumstances and in the interest of the student's academic career.
1. The scholar obtaining benefits under this scheme is not allowed to avail of benefits under any other pre-matric scholarship scheme.
1. The students should be regular in attendance for which the yardstick is to be decided by the competent authority of the school.

scheme_name: Post Metric Scholarship (Denotified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be from a Denotified, Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic Community.
- The applicant should be from a family with an annual income of less than or equal to ₹2,50,000/-.
- The applicant should be a Student.
- The applicant should be enrolled in a government institution where the mandatory fees, including prescribed tuition and examination fees, are paid.
- The applicant should not have failed in any academic year.

eligibility_text_source:

1. The applicant should have a minimum employee strength of 500.
1. The applicant should commit a minimum industry contribution of ₹40,00,00,000 (₹40 crore) per NSTI over a five-year period.
1. In the case of a consortium, the prescribed minimum requirements of annual turnover and employee strength shall be fulfilled exclusively by the Lead Industry Partner.

1. CITS (National Craft Instructor Certificate): The applicant should have completed CTS (ITI), NAC (National Apprenticeship Certificate), Diploma, or Degree.
1. Short-term Courses on Advanced Skilling: Eligibility depends on industry requirements.
1. Diploma: The applicant should have passed Class 10th or equivalent.
1. Advanced Diploma (Technical and

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Skilling and Employability Transformation through Upgraded ITIs (PM‑SETU) : Component I
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Government ITIs:**

1. The ITIs should be Government Industrial Training Institutes.
1. The selection of ITIs shall be carried out by the respective State or Union Territory.
1. The selection should be based on factors such as emerging skill requirements, local industry demand, and potential for industry participation.

> **For Anchor Industry Partner (AIP) / Industry Partner:**

1. The applicant should be a credible company or manufacturer with established operations in India.
1. The applicant may be an industry association, consortium, industry-led foundation, or an academic institution promoted by industry.
1. The applicant should have the capability to participate in governance, curriculum design, and training delivery.

> **Minimum Eligibility Conditions (As Defined by State/UT):**

1. The applicant should meet the minimum eligibility criteria such as turnover, number of employees, and sectoral experience, as defined by the State or Union Territory.
1. The applicant should demonstrate the ability to ensure meaningful industry participation.
1. The applicant should not be a small or non-serious operator.

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Backward Class Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student of classes 6th to 10th.
1. The parents/guardians of the applicant should not be income taxpayers.
1. The parents/guardians of the applicant should not own more than 10 acres of land.

scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC students
eligibility_text_source:
1. The scholarships are open to Indian nationals only.
1. Only candidates who belong to Scheduled Castes and have passed the Matriculation, Higher Secondary, or a higher examination of a recognized university or Board of Secondary Education are eligible.
1. Scholarships will be paid to students whose parents' or guardians' income from all sources does not exceed ₹ 2,50,000/- per annum.
1. The ceiling on age limit for admission as a regular student in different classes should be decided by the concerned institutions.
1. Candidates who are studying in the same stage of education in a different subject after passing one stage of education are not eligible.
1. Students who pursue their studies through correspondence or online courses are also eligible.
1. All children of any parent or guardian are entitled to receive benefits of the scheme.
1. A scholarship holder under this scheme will not hold any other scholarship or stipend.

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC students (Delhi)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Student.
- The applicant should be from a Scheduled Caste.
- The annual income of the family of the applicant should not exceed ₹2,50,000/-.
- The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized post-matriculation/post-secondary course in India.
- The applicant should not be availing of any other scholarship for the same course from any other source.

scheme_name: PM – Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
- The household must be an Indian citizen. 
- The household must own a house with a roof that is suitable for installing solar panels.
- The household must have a valid electricity connection.
- The household must not have availed any other subsidy for solar panels.

scheme_name: Scheme of Post Matric Scholarship to Lower Income Group Student for Studies in India
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a national of India.
1. The applicant should permanently reside in Haryana.
1. The applicant should belong to the lower-income group as defined under the scheme guidelines.
1. The applicant should have passed the matriculation or equivalent examination from a recognized board or university.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized post-matriculation course, such as:

- National Fire Service College, Nagpur.
- Aircraft Maintenance Engineer's Course.
- Courses at Training Ship Dufferin or Military College, Dehradun.
- Pre-examination training courses for IAS/IPS, etc.

_*Students in Class XI/XII under continuous school courses are ineligible unless the 10th class is equivalent to matriculation._

scheme_name: Post Metric Scholarship - Madhya Pradesh
eligibility_text_source:
• The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
• If the applicant studying in non-government institutions, the annual income limit of parents/guardians is up to ₹6,00,000/-.
• There is no income limit for government institutions.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a pregnant woman in her 2nd or 3rd trimester of pregnancy.
- The applicant must be an Indian citizen residing in India.
- The applicant must be willing to visit designated government health facilities on the 9th of every month.

##### ***Trimester Specific Criteria***

- The applicant must have completed the first trimester of pregnancy (beyond 12 weeks of gestation).
- The applicant must be in 2nd trimester (13-28 weeks) or 3rd trimester (29-40 weeks) of pregnancy.

##### **Special Categories**

- The applicant identified as high-risk pregnancy cases receive priority attention and follow-up care.
- The applicant who are dropouts from regular antenatal care services are encouraged to participate.

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for OBC, EBC and DNT Students - DNH & DD
states: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

 1. They should belong to either of the OBC, EBC, or DNT (who are not covered under SC, ST, or OBC) categories through a valid certificate.
 1. A scholarship holder under this scheme will not hold any other educational scholarship for the purpose of studying in Class IX and X. If awarded any other educational scholarship for studying in classes IX and X, the students can avail of either of the two scholarships as per his/her choice and should inform the awarding authority to the Head of the Institution about the option made.
 1. Income Certificate is required to be taken once only, i.e. at the time of admission to courses.

scheme_name: Post Matric Students of Class 11th &12th ( Scheduled Caste)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.
1. The student should be enrolled in Class 11th or 12th in a recognized educational institution.
1. The student should provide a valid income certificate of the family, confirming that the annual income is below the prescribed limit.
1. The guardian should provide a declaration stating that there has been no increase in the family income for the last financial year.

scheme_name: Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC, EBC  and DNT Students for studying in India
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be from Other Backward Classes (OBC) / Economically Backward Classes (EBC) or from Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNT).
- The applicant should be studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage of education.
- The applicant should be permanently settled in the State/UT where they are applying.
- The applicant should be studying in an institution recognized by the All India Survey on Higher Education (AISHE) or Unified District Information System for Education (UDISE).
- The applicant's institution should have National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) or National Board of Accreditation (NBA) accreditation.
- The applicant should maintain Aadhaar-based attendance in the institution.
- The applicant must have a valid "Freeship Card" issued by the State/District Authority.

**Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:**
A maximum of 20 slots per annum are reserved for Commercial Pilot License courses, including helicopter and multi-engine training.

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The beneficiary should be a student.
 - The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
 - The beneficiary should belong to the Backward Classes (BC), Most Backward Classes (MBC), and Denotified Communities (DNC).
 - Parents' income should not exceed ₹1,00,000/- (for Backward Classes (BC) students).
 - There should be no graduate in the family (for Backward Classes (BC) students).
 - The beneficiary should be studying in 11th or 12th standard in an English medium school.

scheme_name: Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Students who have taken admission in 1st year i.e. current financial year (except Lateral Entry) are only eligible to apply for PMSS.
 1. Students should have scored 60% and above in Minimum Educational Qualification (MEQ) i.e. 10+2/ Diploma/ Graduation.
 1. Students who are Dependent Wards/ Widows of Ex-servicemen & Ex-Coast Guard personnel are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. Master Degree Courses are not eligible for PMSS except MBA / MCA Courses.
 1. Scholarship can be availed by student for One Course only.

***Priority for Grant of Scholarship:***

 - **Category 1** - Wards / Widows of ESM/ Ex-Coast Guard personnel killed in action. - **Category 2** - Wards / Widows of ESM/ Ex-Coast Guard personnel disabled in action and boarded out of service with disability attributable to Military/ Coast Guard service. - **Category 3** - Wards / Widows of ESM/ Ex-Coast Guard personnel who died while in service for causes attributable to Military / Coast Guard Service. - **Category 4** - Wards / Widows of ESM/ Ex-Coast Guard personnel disabled in service with disability attributable to Military/ Coast Guard Service. - **Category 5** - Wards / Widows of ESM/ Ex-Coast Guard personnel in receipt of gallantry awards. - **Category 6** - Wards / Widows of ESM/ Ex-Coast Guard personnel (PBOR Only).

scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme-Bihar
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Bihar state. 
 1. The caste of the applicant should be included in the list of Backward Class and Extremely Backward Class issued by the state government.
 1. The applicant should be studying in higher classes after passing the Matriculation/Entry Examination.
 1. The family’s annual income must not exceed the below-mentioned limit:

 - Extremely Backward Classes (EBC): ₹2,50,000/-.
 - Other Backward Classes (OBC): ₹3,00,000/-.

 1. The scholarship will be awarded to only two (2) sons of the applicant's parents. This rule will not apply to daughters.

scheme_name: Pre Matric Scholarship for Students belonging to Scheduled Caste Community
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be permanently settled in Mizoram.
1. The applicant must belong to Scheduled Caste.
1. The income of the parents/guardians of the applicant (from all sources) should not exceed ₹2,50,000/- per annum.
1. The applicant must be studying either in 9th or 10th standard.

scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarship for the Students Belonging to Scheduled Tribe for Studies in India
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe, Other Backward Classes, or More Other Backward Classes as specified in relation to the State or Union Territory to which they actually belong.
- The applicant must have successfully passed the Matriculation or Higher Secondary or any higher examination of a recognized University or Board of Secondary Education.
- The applicant must take admission in recognized post-matriculation or post-secondary courses pursued in government, government-aided, or approved private institutions.
- The applicant must possess a valid, active bank account in a Scheduled Bank.
- The applicant must link their bank account securely with their Aadhaar number and their active mobile number.
- The applicant should preferably continue using the identical mobile number linked with the bank account for the entire duration of the course.

***Income Criteria***

- The applicant must belong to a family whose total gross income from all sources does not exceed ₹2,50,000/- per annum without any exemption or deductions.
- The applicant must calculate the combined income of both parents from all sources if both the father and mother are currently working.
- The applicant must compute the income of only one parent if only one parent is alive and supporting the family.
- The applicant must not include the income of siblings or other earning members of the family while computing the total family income.
- The applicant must be fully exempt from the family income c

scheme_name: Additional Financial Assistance for the Children of Persons Engaged in the Cleaning Profession
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student.
 1. The applicant should be eligible for or currently receiving a scholarship under the Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme.
 1. The applicant should belong to Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Other Backward Classes (OBC), Economically Backward Classes (EBC), or Extremely Backward Classes (EBC).
 1. The applicant's parents or guardian should be engaged in the cleaning profession and belong to the scavenger class.
 1. If the applicant's parent is not alive, the guardian engaged in the cleaning profession and belonging to the scavenger class is eligible to apply.

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for the Children of Parents/Guardians Engaged in Unclean and Hazardous Occupations - Goa
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student.
2. The applicant should be a resident of Goa.
3. The applicant’s parents/guardians should be engaged in an unclean or hazardous occupation.
4. The student should be studying in Class I to X on a full-time basis.
5. Scholarship will be admissible to the children/wards of parents/guardians who, irrespective of their caste/religion belong to one of the following categories:

 - Persons who are Manual Scavengers as defined under section 2(I) (g) of Manual Scavengers Act 2013
 - Tanners & Flayers;
 - Waste pickers and
 - Persons engaged in hazardous cleaning as defined in Section 2(1) (d) of [Manual Scavengers Act 2013](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/8868/1/a2013-25msact.pdf). For the purpose of classification in this category, the eligible candidates will be required to submit a certificate from District Social Welfare officer/ identified officer of Local body/ Civic Agency/any such authority as designated by the State Government.
6. There is no family income ceiling under this component of the Scheme.
7. A scholarship holder under this scheme will not hold any other educational scholarship for the purpose of studying in class I to X. If awarded any other educational scholarship for studying in classes I to X, the students can avail of either of the two scholarships as per his/her choice and should inform the awarding authority to the Head of the Institution about the option made.

scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarships for Scheduled Castes Students- Gujarat
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste Category of Gujarat.
 1. The students who have passed the Matriculation or Higher Secondary or any higher examination of a recognized University or Board of Secondary Education will be eligible.
 1. Scholarships will be paid to the students whose parents/guardians' income from all sources does not exceed ₹2,50,000/- during the last Financial Year.
 1. Candidates who, after passing one stage of education are studying in the same stage of education in a different subject e.g. Intermediate Science after Intermediate Arts or B.Com after B.A. or M.A in one subject after M.A in other subjects will not be eligible.
 1. Students, who pursue their studies through correspondence/online courses, are also eligible. The term correspondence includes distance and continuous education for courses in Central/ State Universities only. Non-refundable fees shall be paid and no academic allowance will be paid.
 1. All the children of any parent/guardian will be entitled to receive the benefits of the scheme.
 1. A scholarship holder under this scheme will not hold any other scholarship/stipend.
 1. If awarded any other scholarship/stipend, the student can exercise his/her option for either of the two scholarships/stipends, whichever is more beneficial to him/her, and should inform the awarding authority through the Head of the Institution about the option made. No scholarship will be paid to the students under this scheme from the date he/she ac

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship To Scheduled Tribe Students Of Nagaland
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be pursuing post-matric studies (above Class X) and belong to the Scheduled Tribe of Nagaland.
- The applicant should have parents’/guardian’s annual income not exceeding ₹2.50 lakh from all sources.
- The applicant should be studying in an institution with a valid AISHE/UDISE code.
- The applicant should not be a repeater (students who failed in the last examination are not eligible).
- The applicant should not have availed this scholarship at the same stage of education in a different stream or subject.
- The applicant should not have a study break of more than two years.

scheme_name: Preservation of Tiatr Literature
eligibility_text_source:
##### **General Eligibility**
The applicant must be a tiatr artiste or the legal heir of a deceased tiatr artiste.
##### **Category-wise Eligibility**
***Category I:***

- The applicant must be a tiatr artiste who has expired or is living but not active on the tiatr stage.
- The applicant must submit scripts of Tiatr/Khell tiatr/Khell/One Act Plays.
- The applicant must have a minimum collection of 50 songs with or without musical scores if submitting only songs (kantaram).

***Category II:***

- The applicant must be a tiatr artist who is active on the tiatr stage.
- The applicant must submit scripts of Tiatr/Khell tiatr/Khell/One Act Plays.
- The applicant must have a minimum collection of 100 songs with or without musical scores if submitting only songs (kantaram).

***Category III:***

- The applicant must be an individual artist/publisher who wishes to publish Scripts of Tiatr/Khell tiatr/Khell/One Act Plays on his/her own.
- The applicant must ensure the book conforms to all norms of the scheme.

##### ***Preference***
Scripts/lyrics/music written by tiatr artistes of yesteryears will be given first preference.

scheme_name: Promotion of Tiatr Music
eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant must serve the tiatr stage by playing in any tiatr band or musical group for at least one year after completion of the course.

scheme_name: National Livestock Mission: Promotion of Piggery Entrepreneur
eligibility_text_source:

 1. The applicant should be an Individual Entrepreneur, Self Help Group (SHG), Farmers Producer Organization (FPO), Farmers Cooperative (FCO), Joint Liability Group (JLG), or Section 8 Company.
 1. The applicant should be interested in setting up a breeder farm with at least 100 sows and 25 boars.
 1. The applicant should obtain breeding animals from Central/State Government farms, universities, or local farmers with high genetic merit.
 1. The applicant should arrange the remaining funds through a bank loan, financial institution loan, or self-financing.

**Essential requirements of beneficiaries to avail benefit under the submission:**

 - Entrepreneurs/ Eligible Entities either have obtained training or have trained experts or have sufficient experience in the relevant field in managing and running the project or have technical experts with sufficient experience in the relevant field of managing and running the project.
 - The Entrepreneurs/ Eligible Entities have got the sanction loan for project by the bank or financial institutions furnished bank guarantee from scheduled bank along with appraisal of project for its validity by bank where it is holding the account.
 - The Entrepreneurs/ Eligible Entities should have own land or lease land where the project will be established.
 - The Entrepreneurs/ Eligible Entities have all the relevant documents for KYC.

**Notes:**

1. The applicant should not seek subsidy for land purchase, rent, lease, work

scheme_name: Public Provident Fund Account(PPF)
eligibility_text_source:

1. An individual may also open one account on behalf of each minor or a person with mental illness or intellectual disability of whom he is the guardian: Provided that only one account shall be opened in the name of a minor or a person with mental illness or intellectual disability by any of the guardian.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Provision of Polythene lining for the Construction of New ponds for Saline/Alkaline area-Haryana
eligibility_text_source:

1. The polythene lining cost shall be provided for new water bodies created under the various state government.
1. Beneficiaries shall be provided with central assistance for the polythene lining cost for the initial crop only in newly constructed.
1. Central assistance for the Polythene lining cost shall be released only after the ponds are ready for fish/shrimp culture.
1. The projects having land on long-term lease shall also be considered for Central financial assistance. However, the registered lease period/agreement shall not be a minimum period of 7 (seven) years.

scheme_name: Padho Pardesh
eligibility_text_source:
1. Students must have been admitted to an approved master's, M.Phil, or Ph.D. program abroad.
1. Students must have been admitted to a recognized university or institution abroad.
1. Student's family income must not exceed ₹ 6,00,000 per annum.
1. Students must have obtained an education loan from a scheduled bank under the Indian Banks Association (IBA) Education Loan Scheme.
1. Students must apply for the scheme's benefits during their first year of study. Applications received in the second year or later will not be accepted.
1. Priority will be given to applications from states and union territories with lower bank interest rates.
1. Payment of financial benefits may be linked to the student's Aadhaar number.

scheme_name: Pension Plan (MBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:

4. The registered workers who have been working as building workers for not less than one year shall be eligible to receive pension benefits under the scheme.
5. The registered worker shall become eligible to avail pension benefits upon attaining the age of 60 years.

scheme_name: Promotion of Post-Harvest Management, Processing, and Value Addition of Aromatic Plant Produce to Farmers/Institutions/Groups in the State - Centre for Aromatic Plants

scheme_name: Promotion of Patriotism/National Integration/Communal Harmony - Interschool Choreographic Dance Competition
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be an educational institution.
- The applicant must form a dance group consisting of not less than 6 dancers.
- The applicant must form a dance group consisting of not more than 10 dancers.
- The applicant must choose to participate in either Standard 1st-4th category or Standard 5th-10th category.
- The applicant must perform on only one patriotic song in Konkani language.
- The applicant must not use fusion of different songs in their performance.
- The applicant must provide a recorded copy of the song on Compact Disc along with the application form.

***Compliance Requirements***

- The applicant must read and understand all rules and regulations of the organization.
- The applicant must abide by all competition rules and regulations.
- The applicant must accept that the decision of the panel of judges will be final and binding.
- The applicant must accept that Tiatr Academy of Goa reserves the right to make any changes in the rules.

scheme_name: Promotion of Patriotism/National Integration/Communal Harmony - Production of Musical Programme or Tiatr
eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant must propose an activity that is a tiatr/musical programme on the theme of patriotism/national integration/communal harmony.
- The applicant must ensure the duration of the musical programme or tiatr is not less than two hours.
- The applicant must submit the application along with typed script/lyrics at least 30 days before the date of staging the programme.
- The applicant must attach No Objection Certificate from the lyricist/writer/director concerned if they are not the lyricist/writer/director of the proposed activity.
- The applicant must ensure scripts/lyrics do not contain any adverse remarks against any Governments/Individuals/Institutions or anything against caste, creed and religion.

scheme_name: Pre Matric Scholarship For Students With Disabilities
eligibility_text_source:

1. The applicant should be a regular, full-time student studying in Classes IX and X in:

 - A Government school; or
 - A school recognised by the Government; or
 - A school recognised by the Central/State Board of Secondary Education.

1. The applicant’s parents/guardian’s income from all sources should not exceed ₹2,50,000/- per annum.
1. Not more than 2 children with disabilities of the same parents will be eligible for benefits under the scheme. In case the second child is a twin, the scholarship will be admissible to both twins.
1. Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation
1. 50% of the total scholarships available each year are reserved for female candidates. If adequate eligible female candidates are not available, remaining slots may be utilised for suitable male candidates.

1. The applicant repeating the same class is not eligible to receive the scholarship for that class for a second or subsequent year.

scheme_name: Petrochemicals Research & Innovation Commendation
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be an Indian citizen, institution, industry, organisation, or Start-up registered in India.
- The applicant must be an entity created by the Central government, State governments, or Union Territories.
- The applicant must limit the maximum number of members to five, in case of applying as a team or group.
- The applicant must present an innovation that is an original product, process, or an improvement on an existing product or process.
- The applicant must ensure the innovation increases the utility of the product or process by enhancing consumer advantages, accuracy, reliability, safety, product life, or versatility.
- The applicant must not submit a mere theoretical hypothesis, an innovation simply in the idea stage, or a perpetual motion machine.
- The applicant must present an innovation that is not older than three years at the time of submitting the application.
- The applicant must not be an awardee of previous editions of the National Petrochemicals Awards for the immediate three preceding editions or years.

scheme_name: PRISM (Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Start-ups and MSMEs): Category-1: Proof of Concept/ Prototypes/ Models
eligibility_text_source:
1. Any Indian citizen including student innovator can avail support to develop their novel idea into demonstrable models/prototypes.
1. The proposals shall preferably be in the following focus sectors: Green Technology, Clean Energy, Industrially utilizable smart materials, Waste to wealth, Affordable healthcare, water & sewage management, and any other technology or knowledge-intensive area.

**NOTE 1:** When a group of students want to pursue the project, then there has to be an agreement among the group about the lead applicant. Though financial support can be given only to a single applicant by name, a letter of appreciation can be given by the department to all the students of the team, after successful completion of the project. 
**NOTE 2:** The support is primarily aimed at encouraging student innovators. However, in general, any Indian Citizen having an innovative idea or an invention and wishing to demonstrate his idea in the form of a basic stage model/prototype/process thus providing proof of his concept can apply to this category.

scheme_name: PRISM (Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Start-ups and MSMEs): Category-2: Fabrication of Working Model/Process Know-how/Testing & Trial/Patenting/Technology Transfer
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be an Indian Citizen.
- The applicant must have an innovative idea or an invention.
- The applicant must propose a project where the project cost is more than ₹5,00,000/- and up to ₹35,00,000/-.
- The applicant must ensure the project duration does not exceed 24 months.
- The applicant must ensure the proposal involves the fabrication of a working model/process know-how/testing and trial/patenting/technology transfer.
- The applicant must ensure the proposal does not involve only software development.
- The applicant must ensure the proposal is not for basic scientific research having no immediate commercial implications.

##### ***Specific Conditions***

- **Students:** The applicant must provide a `No Objection Certificate’ from the head of their institution on the institution’s letterhead.- **Students:** The applicant must ensure the commitment from the institution that the project will be taken to its logical conclusion by the student/concerned faculty.- **Individuals Working in Organizations:** The applicant must furnish a `No Objection Certificate’ from their employer.
##### ***Desirable Criteria (Preference)***

- The proposals should preferably be in the following focus sectors: Green Technology, Clean Energy, Industrially utilizable smart materials, Waste to wealth, Affordable healthcare, Water & sewage management, or any other technology or knowledge-intensive area.
- Preference: Student innovators making it to the final of any competition are e

scheme_name: Physical Research Laboratory Award
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be an Indian citizen.
 - The applicant should be a scientist. 
 - The applicant's age below 45 years as of 1st January 2021 (born on or after 02.01.1976) 
 - The applicant's contribution to Earth and Planetary Systems, Oceanography, Atmospheric Sciences, or Meteorology.

> **Note:**

Nominations may be made by the Head of Academic & Research Institutions/ Vice-Chancellors/ Deans of Universities/ Fellows of the Indian Academies of Science and Engineering/ and past recipients of these awards.

scheme_name: PRL Junior Research Fellowships
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian citizen.
1. The applicant should have studied at recognized Universities/Institutes in India.
1. The applicant’s age should be 28 years as of 01st July 2024.
1. The applicant should complete a Bachelor’s and Master’s degree in science or engineering with at least 60% marks (or equivalent) at both levels.
1. Candidates from disciplines of any branch of Physics, Chemistry, Engineering Physics, Photonics, Space Physics, Atmospheric Science, Environmental Science, Oceanography, Meteorology, Geology, Geophysics, Earth Science and Remote Sensing are eligible to apply.
1. The applicant should be interested in pursuing a research career in the current research areas at PRL, and must have qualified in any one of the following exams:

 - CSIR-UGC-NET JRF/AP (2022 or later) in Physical Sciences/Chemical Sciences/Earth, Atmospheric, Ocean, and Planetary Sciences.
 - GATE (2022 or later) in relevant subjects.
 - JEST (2024) in Physics.
 - UGC-NET JRF/AP (2022 or later) in Environmental Sciences.

scheme_name: PRL's Vikram Sarabhai Postdoctoral Fellowship (VISHWAS)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian national.
1. PhD should have been awarded within the last four years (from the application deadline).
1. The applicant's age should be 35 years or below on the last date of application.
1. The applicant should have a first-class academic record throughout their academic career.
1. The applicant should submit a strong research proposal aligned with PRL’s research areas.

scheme_name: Indo-French Postdoctoral Research Programme for Women in Science
eligibility_text_source:
1. Women applicants must be Indian or French citizens (citizens residing in France) from a recognized university or research institution in India/France.
 1. Indian applicants should be holding a PhD degree & sanctioned/tenured post in a scientific/academic institution and willing to undertake collaborative research projects in French institutions/universities are eligible to apply.
 1. French women applicants holding a PhD degree and currently completing a postdoctoral project & willing to undertake collaborative research projects in Indian institutions/universities are eligible to apply.
 1. The mobility should take place during the employment period of the applicant in the concerned institution.
 1. The applicant should not have availed of any Postdoctoral fellowship in France/India during the last 5 years.
 1. This fellowship cannot be availed simultaneously with any other fellowship.

**Note 01:** Preference will be given to those candidates who don’t have any international exposure.
**Note 02:** Preference will be given to candidates having previous R&D experience & publications.
**Note 03:** CEFIPRA may relax the eligibility conditions in case of potential candidates.

scheme_name: Indo-French Visiting/Mobility Programme for Women in Science
eligibility_text_source:
1. Women applicants must be Indian or French citizens (citizens residing in France) from a recognized university or research institution in India/France.
 1. Indian applicants should hold a PhD degree & sanctioned/tenured post in a scientific/academic institution and be willing to undertake collaborative research projects in French institutions/universities.
 1. French women applicants holding a PhD degree and currently completing a postdoctoral project & willing to undertake collaborative research projects in Indian institutions/universities.
 1. The mobility should take place during the employment period of the applicant in the concerned institution.
 1. The applicant should not have availed of any visiting fellowship in France/India during the last 5 years.
 1. This fellowship cannot be availed simultaneously with any other fellowship.

**Note 01:** Preference will be given to those candidates who don't have any international exposure.
**Note 02:** CEFIPRA may relax the eligibility conditions in case of potential candidates.

scheme_name: Payment Related to Wheat Procurement
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a landholding farmer.
1. The applicant should have sold wheat at a government-operated procurement center during the notified procurement period.

scheme_name: Palanhar Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Orphan boy/girl.
1. Children of parents sentenced to death/life imprisonment under judicial orders.
1. Children of a widowed mother receiving a pension (maximum 3 children at a time).
1. Children of remarried widowed mothers.
1. Children of HIV/AIDS affected mother/father.
1. Children of parents suffering from leprosy.

> **Mode of Contribution:**

The annual contribution of each tapper under the scheme is ₹480/-. Rubber Board will contribute, 50 % of the annual contribution for an initial period of 5 years for all the members who regularly remit their contribution every year on time. Accordingly, each tapper has to contribute ₹240/- annually to the scheme for 5 years. After 5 years the tapper has to remit the full contribution till attaining the age of 60 years.

eligibility_text_source:

 1. Orphaned and destitute children, or orphaned children living with their next of kin or relatives, whose annual income is less than ₹60,000/- or who are below the poverty line.
 1. Children will also be considered orphaned and destitute if their mother or father has either passed away or become incapable of raising them due to mental disability, imprisonment, or any other judicial order. However, their eligibility will automatically end once the compelling situation is resolved.
 1. Children suffering from HIV(+), AIDS, Visible Deformities Grade-II (Leprosy), or children of parents suffering from these diseases.

**Note:** The benefits of this scheme will be granted for one year for the above categories of children aged 0-18 years, and will be automatically renewed each year until the child turns 18, provided no adverse report is received.

scheme_name: Research and Development Programme in Water Sector
eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant must be from a non-profit making recognized Indian Private Research and Development Institution registered with the Department of Scientific and Industrial Research.
- The applicant must propose a research topic that falls within the subject domain or priority areas related to the respective Indian National Committee.
- The applicant must ensure the proposed research has not been done elsewhere to avoid duplication.
- The applicant must identify the probable users of the new technology and end-users of the results of the proposed research.
- The applicant must not seek funding for foreign travel, salaries of regular employees of the institute, or honorariums for investigators.
- The applicant must not utilize the grant for the construction, maintenance, or repair of buildings, or the purchase of vehicles and air conditioners.
- The applicant must complete any previous ongoing research projects funded by the Ministry of Water Resources before a new research prop

scheme_name: Rajya Dairy Udhyamita Vikas Yojna
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Chhattisgarh for at least five years.
1. The applicant should be purchase a maximum of two milch animals under the scheme.
1. The applicant should be entitled to 50% subsidy (up to ₹70,000/-) for general category and 66.6% subsidy (up to ₹93,200/-) for SC/ST category.
1. The applicant should be responsible for any additional costs beyond the scheme’s provisions, with no subsidy provided for the excess amount.
1. The applicant should ensure that the purchased animal is in milk production, not older than five years, and capable of producing at least 10 liters of milk per day at the time of purchase.
1. The applicant should have the animal insured for a minimum period of three years (total five years recommended) at the time of unit setup.
1. The applicant should be a landless, small or marginal farmer, BPL family member, milk co-operative society member, village resident on a milk collection route, Garva Yojana beneficiary, women SHG member, or an existing dairy farmer, as they will receive priority.
1. The applicant should be the only eligible beneficiary from a joint family.
1. The applicant should not own at least two high-breed dairy animals at the time of applying for the scheme.

scheme_name: Reimbursement of Expenditure for Occupational Diseases (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

 - The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
 - The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
 - The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

 - The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab or should be a family member of the worker.
 - The expenditure on treatment of the disease must be verified by the Civil Surgeon.
 - The applicant should have exhausted the benefit of ₹50,000/- for all his/her health ailments with the health card facility under the Bhagat “Puran Singh Sehat Beema Yojna” Scheme.

scheme_name: Revised Education Scheme For Children Of Artisans/Weavers Of Handicrafts And Handloom Departments
eligibility_text_source:

1. The parent should be registered with the Handicrafts and Handloom Departments having valid registration car/certificate.
1. The applicant should not have availed benefits under the following schemes being implemented by the departments: Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme; Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme; Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme; Any other scheme. The applicant shall seek NOC from the concerned Zonal Educational Officer and Tehsil Social Welfare Officer.

scheme_name: Reimbursement of Expenses for Startups Operating from Leased/Privately Owned Premises Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
All Start-ups certified by the Start-up Promotion Cell (SPC) having a valid start-up certificate number that are not using any government-developed co-working spaces/ incubators/ accelerators for their operations are eligible.

**NOTE:** Only expenditures incurred after notification of Goa Start-up Policy 2017, being within the validity of this policy and paid for digitally will be considered for reimbursements under this scheme. In case digital payments are not possible then it shall be up to the SPC to admit the expenditure as per its guidelines.

> **Definitions**

**Local start-up:** For the purpose of this scheme “Local start-up” shall mean a start-up in which at least 50% equity/share is held by one or more Goans continuously since the time of inception. 
**Goan:** For the purpose of this scheme the term “Goan” shall mean:
1. Person born in the State of Goa; or,
2. Person having domicile for 10 years or more in the State; or,
3. Spouse of a person covered under any of the conditions mentioned in (1) and (2), through marriages registered in Goa.

scheme_name: Ramanujan Fellowship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The fellowship is open to brilliant Indian scientists and engineers from all over the world who are below the age of 40 years, who want to return to India from abroad.
 1. The applicant should possess a higher degree or equivalent, such as a Ph.D. in Science/Engineering, Master in Engineering or Technology/MD in Medicine, etc., and have adequate professional experience.
 1. These Fellowships are very selective and only those who have a proven/outstanding track record as evident from their research publications and recognitions would be eligible.
 1. In case a fellow is offered a permanent position, he/she will be ineligible for the fellowship.
 1. This fellowship is meant for working in new areas and laboratories in order to expand their research capabilities, other than the one, where the candidate has obtained his/her doctorate degree.
 1. Nominations from the Ph.D. guides of the candidate and nominations from the same institute where they opted for their Ph.D, will not be considered.
 1. The age of the candidate should not be more than 40 years.
 1. The candidate should be working abroad at the time of his nomination for the fellowship.
 1. The Fellowship is only for those scientists who are not holding any permanent/tenure track/contractual position in any Indian Institute/University.

**Note:** In case the selected fellow gets any position such as permanent, tenure track, or contractual position, he/she, will become ineligible for receiving fellowship including a rese

scheme_name: Research Grant For In-Service Faculty Members
eligibility_text_source:
(i) Faculty members regularly appointed and working in the following institutions are eligible to apply under the schemes:
(a) Universities (including constituent and affiliated colleges/institutions) are included under Section 2(f) of the UGC Act, 1956, and have valid accreditation from NAAC.
(b) Deemed to be Universities under Section 3 of the UGC Act 1956 and having valid accreditation from NAAC.
(c) Institutions fully funded by Central or State Governments and empowered to award Degrees.
(d) Institutions of National Importance.
(ii) The age of the applicant should not be greater than 50 years. (Faculty above 50 years of age is not eligible to apply).
(iii) The applicant should have a minimum of 10 years of service left in the University from the date of submission of his / her application.
(iv) The applicant must have successfully supervised a Ph.D. dissertation of 5 full-time candidates
(v) The applicant must have successfully completed at least 2 sponsored research projects funded by national/international government or private agencies.

 - Forensic Science, Police, Criminology Research and Police Administration etc.
 - Culture, Religion, Arts and Heritage etc.
 - Subject related to Law

> **General Conditions:**

 1. In case of Author is more than one, each co-author must fill out the proforma separately.
 1. Under the scheme only those books that are original works of the author in Hindi, are accepted. Translated books are not accepted.
 1. Books awarded previously by any government organization will not become eligible. The author of the book must inform the Department of Official Language immediately if the book is awarded under any other award scheme before the declaration of the awards under the above-mentioned schemes.
 1. Under the scheme, books published from 1st January to 31st December are acceptable.
 1. The book should be an analytical review of the subject it deals with. Books written in the form of a thesis for a PhD, poems, novel, story, play etc., or textbooks will not be eligible under the scheme.
 1. The author/co-author will be responsible for facts and figures given in the boo

eligibility_text_source:

- The child should be 100% disabled.

scheme_name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Education Of Children & Widows Of Ex-Servicemen
eligibility_text_source:
- Applicant must be an ESM/ widow/Orphan dependent.
- Should be of rank Havildar/equivalent and below.
- The scheme is applicable for classes 1 to 12 of school and undergraduate classes of a degree college. This grant is also admissible to widows who wish to pursue 2-year post graduate degree. The ward/widow shall complete the course successfully.
- The applicant should not be drawing education allowance or similar benefit from the State or is employer.

> The scheme is eligible for only first two children. However, in case of twin children, below mentioned condition shall be applicable:-

1. In case the 1st and 2nd children are twins only twins will be eligible as maximum two children are permitted.
1. In case 2nd and 3rd children are twins both will be eligible for education financial assistance alongwith 1st. Thus the ESM can avail for three children subject to fulfilment of above mentioned criteria.

- In case of unfortunate demise of 1st or 2nd child. In case of unfortunate demise of 1st child, 2nd and 3rd children as mentioned in discharge book will be eligible. Similarly in case of demise of 2nd child, the 3rd child will be eligible as mentioned in discharge book. The death certificate will be uploaded as supporting document with online application for scrutiny.

scheme_name: RMEWF- Financial Assistance to Orphan Children of ESM

1. The applicant must be a son below 21 years of age or an unmarried daughter.
1. The applicant must have lost both parents (ESM and spouse).
1. The application must be recommended by the respective Zila Sainik Board (ZSB).

scheme_name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Ex-Servicemen In Penury
eligibility_text_source:

1. Should be of rank Havildar/equivalent from the Navy/AF and below.
1. The ESM/widow should be 65 years of age on 01 Apr of the Financial Year in which applying.
1. After the demise of ESM who was already availing the Penury Grant, his widow is eligible for Penury grant irrespective of her age at the time of the death of ESM. Therefore, in such cases, the condition of a widow attaining the age of 65 years is not applicable.

scheme_name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Vocational Training Of Widows Of ESM

1. The applicant should have relevant spouse details recorded in the service book of the ESM.
1. The applicant should be recommended by the respective Zila Sainik Board (ZSB) and Rajya Sainik Board (RSB).

scheme_name: Silk Tree Plantation
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The beneficiary must belong to a cluster or group.
 1. In mountainous areas, a minimum of 25 farmers is required for a cluster, while in plain areas, at least 50 farmers are mandatory.
 1. The beneficiaries in the group must have land available for the plantation of at least 300 plants.
 1. Silkworm rearing is conducted under departmental supervision. Therefore, clusters must be selected within a 15–20 km radius of departmental silkworm farms. If the distance exceeds this, the selected group must provide one acre of land for a collective rearing center and may conduct silkworm rearing after receiving training.
 1. Priority is given to farmers who already practice sericulture or those who have mulberry saplings available. Mulberry silkworm rearing can be carried out up to an altitude of 4,000 feet, although warmer valley areas are more suitable for this activity.
 1. The department has not specified any particular regions for silkworm rearing; however, most state-run sericulture farms were established in locations where mulberry silk production is feasible.

1. The applicant should be a student.
1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST)/ Scheduled Caste (SC)/ Person with Disability (PH) community.
1. The student should have passed the class-10th Pre-Board/ Pre-Test Exam from the government-recognized school of Tripura with 50% marks.

scheme_name: Safety Audit
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a Micro, Small, or Medium Enterprise (MSME), either newly established or existing.
1. The enterprise must be registered with the appropriate authority (e.g., Udyog Aadhaar / EM Part-II).
1. The safety audit must be conducted by auditors from a recognized ISO-certified agency.
1. The industrial units must also comply with the following conditions:

 - The item of manufacture should not fall in the restrictive list as notified by the State Government from time to time.
 - Investments in new plant, machinery and equipments focussed towards enhancing safety shall only be eligible for subsidy under this scheme.
 - The units should have obtained NOC/CLU from competent authority, if applicable.
 - The units should be in commercial production.
 - The units should be in regular production at the time of disbursement and the assistance shall not be released to the closed unit.

scheme_name: Subsistence Allowance (Sainik Welfare)
eligibility_text_source: - The applicant should be an [Ex-Serviceman](https://sainik.py.gov.in/definition-ex-servicemen) or the Widow of an Ex-Serviceman.- The applicant should be [registered](https://sainik.py.gov.in/registration-procedure) with the Department of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry.

scheme_name: Sparsh Abhiyan: Surgery to Prevent Disability of Poor and Destitute Disabled People of Madhya Pradesh
eligibility_text_source:

- The applicant must be a disabled person.
- The applicant must have a disability as defined in the "Persons with Disabilities (Equal Opportunities, Protection of Rights and Full Participation) Act 1995".
- The applicant’s guardians/guardians' annual income must not exceed ₹96,000/-.
- The applicant must have surgery recommended by a subject specialist doctor.
- The applicant’s surgery must be recommended by a subject specialist surgeon.

scheme_name: Scheme For Arjuna Awards For Outstanding Performance In Sports And Games
eligibility_text_source:

 1. Olympic Games/Asian Games/Commonwealth Games/World Cup/Word Championship disciplines and cricket or any equivalent recognized international Tournament.
 1. Indigenous Games.
 1. Sports for physically challenged.

- The applicant should be a sportsperson with good performance at the international level.
