scheme_name: State Art Exhibition (Student Category)
eligibility_text_source:
1. A participant must be a bona fide student of any recognised art college or institution, studying one or more of the subjects in the exhibition sections. 
1. If an art student submits their entry in the Student Category, they are not eligible to participate in the Artist Category. 
1. Submission must include an official bonafide certificate from the student's institution; entries without it will not be accepted. 
1. Declarations for Originality and Prior Exhibits

 - Each exhibit must be the student’s original work, neither a copy nor an imitation. A declaration to this effect must appear on the back of each entry.
 - If the work has previously won an award or been displayed in prior exhibitions by Kala Academy or any other institution, it will not be accepted, and a declaration to that effect must also be submitted. 

1. Award Winners' Restriction for Next Year
1. A participant who receives an award in a given year is not eligible to receive an award in the same section in the following year

scheme_name: Scheme Of Assistance To Good Borrowers: ‘A’ Category
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be an existing assisted unit classified as ‘A’ Category.
- The applicant must be an existing assisted unit of Medium Scale Industry or Small Scale Industry classified as ‘A’ Category.
- The existing assisted unit must be in operation for more than 3 years.
- The existing assisted unit must be regular in repayment.
- The existing assisted unit must be regular in repayment without availing re-schedulement.
- The applicant can be an existing assisted unit by other institutions or Banks which satisfies the criteria under Good Borrowers, and its fixed assets must be free from encumbrance.
- The applicant can be an existing transport loanee who has settled the loan promptly.

scheme_name: Scheme of Assistance to Good Borrowers: ‘B’ Category
eligibility_text_source:
- Existing assisted units satisfying the criteria under Good Borrowers which have not obtained reschedulement not for more than once and regular in repayment after reschedulement.
- Units assisted by other institutions / banks satisfying the criteria and with the fixed assets in existing units is free from encumbrance.

scheme_name: Scheme for Additional Interest Subsidy over and above RIPS 2024 and Reimbursement of Annual Credit Guarantee Fee
eligibility_text_source:

1. Interest subvention subsidy should have been approved for the applicant under RIPS 2024; and
1. Beneficiary enterprise should fall under any of the following categories:

- One District One Product (ODOP) enterprises in respective districts.
- Enterprises owned by Scheduled Caste (SC)/Scheduled Tribe (ST) or women entrepreneurs.
- Young entrepreneurs in the state in the age group of 18 to 45 years for starting their new enterprise for a maximum loan amount of up to ₹10 crore. 
- Establishment of new MSMEs or expansion/ diversification/ modernization of existing MSMEs in Area Category 3 as defined in RIPS 2024.

1. Family income from all sources should not be more than ₹8,00,000/- per annum during the current financial year. A valid income certificate issued by the state/UT government needs to be enclosed.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Sampoorna Pushti Yojana: Adolescent Girls Nutrition

 1. In the 10 Aspirational districts (Bolangir, Dhenkanal, Gajapati, Kalahandi, Kandhamal, Koraput, Malkangiri, Nabarangpur, Nuapada, Rayagada), girls aged 18-19 years shall be included under the MSPY scheme as the adolescent girls in the 14 -18 years category are enrolled in the Scheme for Adolescent Girls (SAG) of Government of India.

scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME): Assistance in Rent to MSEs
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME): Assistance for Technology Acquisition

scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME):  Assistance for Reimbursement of CGTMSE Fees

- The application must be submitted within within one year from the date of first disbursement of Term loan, or on or before the date of commencement of commercial production, whichever is later.

scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME): Assistance of Capital Investment Subsidy (Manufacturing Sector)

 1. The enterprise must comply with pollution control measures.
 1. Only one type of investment (Forward Integration, Backward Integration, Diversification, or Expansion) will be eligible for subsidy.

scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME): Assistance in Implementation of Information and Communication Technology (ICT)

- Widow.
- Deserted Woman.

 - The institution should have the experience to run coaching for competitive examinations for at least three years. 
 - The institution should have not been declared bankrupt at any point of time.
 - The institution should have not been blacklisted by any debarment or body of the Government at any point of time. 
 - The institution should have the capacity to manage the building/space to run coaching classes from its own resources at Ranchi and Dumka.

> **Eligibility for the Candidates:**

- Should belong to Scheduled Caste (SC) category.
- Should be a resident of Jharkhand.
- Should have the eligible qualifications for the mentioned competitive exams.
- The income ceiling of candidate (income of self and/or income of parents, if dependent on them) should not exceed ₹ 2,50,000/- per annum.

**Note 01:** A maximum of two milch animals per applicant will be eligible for the incentive money under this scheme. However, the recordings of more than two buffaloes/Cows of an applicant may be carried out for collecting data of the top quality Murrah buffaloes/Indigenous cattle (Hariana, Sahiwal, and Belahi). scheme_name: Scheme for Construction of Multipurpose Community Halls in Areas Inhabited by Tribal Community (Sanskruti Bhavan)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must represent one of the following - 

- A Village Panchayat.
- A group of tribals.
- A Non-Governmental Organization (NGO) working for the betterment of tribals in the Village Panchayat area.

2. The applicant must propose the project in areas inhabited by the Tribal Community.
3. The applicant must ensure that the total cost of the project, including the cost of land, must not exceed ₹50,00,000/-.
4. The applicant must ensure that the land is free from all encumbrances.
5. The applicant must ensure that the application is made to the Tribal Welfare Department in the prescribed form.
##### ***The scheme classifies tribal habitations into three classes for sanctioning community halls.***

- For every five community halls sanctioned, the first two community halls must be sanctioned in "Class A areas". "Class A areas" are the tribal areas in the village(s) situated within the Forest areas where the Forest Right Act is implemented.
- For every five community halls sanctioned, the next two community halls must be sanctioned in "Class B areas". "Class B areas" are the tribal areas in the village/hamlet which has the habitation of tribal population for more than 40% of the population in that village/hamlet.
- For every five community halls sanctioned, the next one community hall must be sanctioned in "Class C area". "Class C areas" are the areas in the villages inhabited by the tribal population with density less than 40% of the population of the village.

 1. The nominee should be an Indian citizen between 35-55 years of age working in institutions in India.
 1. The nominee should not be receiving any other fellowships from Government sources during the tenure.

scheme_name: SERB - POWER Research Grants
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicants [Principal Investigator (PI) and Co-Principal Investigator(s) (Co-PI(s))] should be Indian citizens.
 1. The Applicant(s) must hold a regular academic/research position in a recognized academic institution or national laboratory or in any other recognized R&D institution in India.
 1. Applicants from IITs, IISERs, IISc, NITs, Central Universities, and National Labs of Central Government Institutions will be considered under the Level I category, whereas applicants from State Universities/Colleges and Private Academic Institutions should apply under the Level II category.
 1. Faculties recruited through the UGC-Faculty Recharge Program are eligible to apply. INSPIRE Faculty, Ramanujan, and Ramalingaswamy Fellows are also eligible to apply provided they have at least three and half years of tenure remaining at the time of submission of application.
 1. There is a mandatory requirement for female CO-PI from the same institute.
 1. Male CO-PI is not allowed in the POWER Grant Research Scheme.
 1. PhD guide cannot be CO-PI.
 1. PIs who have already got a POWER Grant, are not allowed to apply again in POWER Grant Scheme.
 1. JC Bose fellows and the PIs who have already completed two CRG are not allowed to apply for the POWER Grant Research Scheme.
 1. The number of CO-PIs should not exceed two per project.

**Note 01:** Women Scientists cannot hold POWER Fellowship and POWER Grant at the same time.
**Note 02:** POWER Grant will be regulated through terms of reference

scheme_name: SERB Research Scientist Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The scheme is open only to INSPIRE Faculty and Ramanujan Fellows.
 1. The applicant should have completed or nearing completion of the tenure of INSPIRE Faculty of DST or Ramanujan Fellowship of SERB.
 1. The upper age limit for INSPIRE Faculty/Ramanujan Fellows is 40 years at the time of submission of application.
 1. SRS can be availed only once by a candidate in his/her career.

**Note 01:** The SRS Scheme will be made effective only after the completion of the tenure of the respective INSPIRE Faculty/Ramanujan Fellowship.
**Note 02:** The SRS scheme can be availed only once by a candidate in his/her career.
**Note 03:** The SRS is tenable only in India and can be implemented in any of the recognized academic institutions, national laboratories and other recognized R & D institutions. The host institution should provide necessary administrative and infrastructural support.
**Note 04:** The Faculty/Fellow can either implement the SRS Scheme from the present host institution or opt to choose a different host institution that is suitable to the objectives of the proposed research work.
**Note 05:** The SRSs will not be allowed to work at the same Department/institution where the candidates have earned their PhD/M.S./M.D degree.
**Note 06:** The SRS is purely a temporary and contractual assignment, and is tenable for a period of 2 years, extendable to one more year, subject to performance evaluation. Under any circumstances, the support is not extendable beyond three years.

scheme_name: Self Employment Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility**

1. Applicants must belong to the State Religious Minority Community.
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of the State.
1. The age limit of the applicant is between 18 to 55 years.
1. Family annual income from all sources should not be more than Rs.81,000/- in a rural areas and Rs. 1,03,000 in urban areas.
1. No member of the applicant's family shall be an employee of the State/Central/ Government PSU.
1. Applicants should not have availed of a loan in KMDC earlier.

scheme_name: Self Employment Scheme - Goa
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Individual / Association / Societies / Partnership / Self-help Group (SHG) / Company.
1. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Tribe (ST) Community.
1. The family income of the applicant/beneficiary should not be more than ₹ 1,00,000 per annum.
1. The age of the applicant should not be more than 55 years. 
1. Financial support should only be given to undertake any self-employment activities.
1. The applicant will have to provide two guarantors / Surety who is having a sound financial status. One Guarantor should necessarily be a Government Servant.

**NOTE:** The eligibility criteria can be relaxed in deserving cases by the decision of the BOD.

scheme_name: Self Employment Scheme Gujarat
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
- The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) category.
- The applicant must be unemployed at the time of applying.

scheme_name: Scheme to Encourage Students for Innovative Projects
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a student from an Educational Institute in Goa.
- The applicant must be one of the following - 

 (a) A final year student or a group of students working on a college project.
 (b) A High School or Higher Secondary School student working on a school project participating either in State or National level competitions.

- The applicant must be working on a hardware or software-based Product/Process Innovation.
- The applicant must be registered under the Virtual Innovation Register (VIR) as a New Idea.
- The applicant must not already have received financial support under this scheme.
- The proposed innovative idea/ project in the form of a product/solution must be associated either with Academics, Industry or the Government.
- The proposals should preferably be in the following focus sectors: _Green technology, Clean energy, Industrially utilizable smart materials, Waste to Wealth, Affordable Healthcare, Water & Sewage Management, Renewable Energy sources, Electric Vehicles, Smart Cities, Agri-tech, Meditech, Health care tech, and Digital media, Information Technology Enabled Services (ITES)._

scheme_name: Self-Employment Scheme: Nana Vyavasay Mate Loan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant must belong to the unreserved category.
 1. The applicant's age should be between 18 years and 50 years.
 1. The small business will have to be started within three months of obtaining the loan.
 1. The applicant must be willing to repay the loan in equal monthly installments over 5 years after three months of starting the business.
 1. The annual income limit of the family should be ₹6,00,000/- or less.

**Note:** 

 1. The interest rate on the loan will be 5 percent simple interest per annum and 4 percent for women.
 1. The simple interest will be calculated based on the amount of the loan given per year.
 1. Only 1 person from a family can avail of the scheme benefits.
 1. A maximum of 5 beneficiaries per village will be selected.

scheme_name: Self-Employment Scheme for Scheduled Castes and Tribes
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must belong to a Scheduled Castes and Tribes community.
1. The applicant must be living below the poverty line.
1. The applicant annual income must not exceed ₹15,975/- in rural areas and ₹21,206/- in urban areas.
1. The applicant must not be a defaulter of any bank or financial institution.
1. The applicant must not have availed any subsidy from any government scheme earlier.
1. The applicant must possess a commercial driving license.

scheme_name: Self-Employment Scheme: Transport/ Logistics/ Travels/ Food Court Wyaaj Sahaay
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant must belong to the unreserved category.
 1. The applicant's age should be between 18 years and 50 years.
 1. The applicant must have a valid driving license.
 1. The vehicle purchased under the scheme must be mortgaged (hypothecated) to the corporation.
 1. The applicant must be willing to repay the loan in equal monthly installments over 5 years.
 1. The annual income limit of the family should be ₹6,00,000/- or less.

> **Note**: 

 - The interest rate on the loan will be 5 percent simple interest per annum.
 - The simple interest will be calculated based on the amount of the loan given per year.
 - Only 1 person from a family can avail of the scheme benefits.
 - A maximum of 5 beneficiaries per village will be selected.

scheme_name: Scheme for Enhancement of Technical Competence and Manpower: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Setting Up Anchor Institute
eligibility_text_source:
- The institute must be based in Gujarat.
 - The institution must be an industry-sponsored institute or center of excellence.
 - The institute must have a minimum of 10 years of experience in its sector.
 - The institute must be working in sectors like biotechnology, marine, mining, shipbuilding, aerospace, agribusiness, etc.
 - The institute should have a proven track record of employment generation.
 - The institute must commit to operating for a minimum of 10 years and function on a self-sustaining model after 5 years

scheme_name: Scheme for Enhancement of Technical Competence and Manpower: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Skill Development to MSME Sector
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant unit should belong to the MSME sector.
 - The MSME must sponsor a candidate from its workforce.
 - The candidate must have at least one year of experience in the MSME.
 - The training partner/organization must have at least 10 years of experience.

scheme_name: Scheme for Enhancement of Technical Competence and Manpower: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Setting-up of Specialized Skill Development Centers at Industry
eligibility_text_source:
- Industry Partner must be an industry/association with at least 10 years of experience in skill development.
 - Both entities must be legally registered under Societies Act, Trust Act, or Companies Act.
 - Courses should be aligned with NSDC, NCVT, GCVT, or Sector Skill Council guidelines.
 - A minimum of 25% of course intake must be industry-sponsored.
 - At least 75% of industry-sponsored trainees must be provided employment.
 - The training center must be operational for at least 5 years.

scheme_name: Scheme for Enhancement of Technical Competence and Manpower: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Running Short-term Bridge Courses
eligibility_text_source:
- Host Institute must be an ITIs, Polytechnic, Engineering College, or Government-Affiliated Institution with at least 5 years of experience.
 - Industry Partner must be an industry/association with at least 10 years of experience in skill development.
 - Both entities must be legally registered under Societies Act, Trust Act, or Companies Act.
 - Courses should be aligned with NSDC, NCVT, GCVT, or Sector Skill Council guidelines.
 - A minimum of 25% of course intake must be industry-sponsored.
 - At least 75% of industry-sponsored trainees must be provided employment.
 - The training center must be operational for at least 5 years.

scheme_name: Scheme for Enhancement of Technical Competence and Manpower: Skill Up-gradation Centers (SUCs)
eligibility_text_source:
- Organizations such as Industrial Parks, SEZs, GIDC Estates, Investment Regions, and other registered estates can apply.
 - The applying entity must provide the land/building for the SUC.
 - The training partner must have a minimum of 10 years of experience in skill training.
 - The center must ensure the training of at least 180 candidates per year.
 - Employment placement of trainees must meet the set percentage criteria (60% in the first two years and 75% in the last three years).

scheme_name: Scheme for Enhancement of Technical Competence and Manpower: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Tuition Fees for Enhancement of Skill
eligibility_text_source:
- The training institute must have at least 5 years of experience in skill development or relevant industry.
 - The training program must be industry-responsive and recognized by skill development bodies.
 - The first training batch must commence within 3 months of the sanction order.
 - Minimum 10 trainees per batch must complete the training and certification process.
 - Candidates must not have availed of similar government assistance for the same course.
 - The training duration should not exceed 6 months unless specified by authorities.

scheme_name: Special Education And Vocational Training Through Government Institutions
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Maharashtra State.
1. The applicant should be a Person with Disability (Visually Impaired, Hearing Impaired, Orthopedically Handicapped, etc).
1. The percentage of disability should be 40% and above.
1. For Special Education, the age of the applicant should be between 6 to 18 years.
1. For Vocational Education, the age of the applicant should be 18 years and above.

scheme_name: Scheme for Financial Assistance in Case of Death
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a nominee, spouse, or dependent of the deceased registered worker. 
1. The registered construction workers should not have any dues payable to the Board.
1. Application shall be submitted within 1 year of death.
1. If the registered beneficiary is covered under Group Life Insurance scheme, is taken by the Board, death benefit as envisaged in this scheme will not apply.

scheme_name: Scheme for Funeral Assistance in Case of Death of Registered Construction Worker
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a nominee, spouse, or dependent of the deceased registered worker. 
1. The registered construction workers should not have any dues payable to the Board.

scheme_name: Subsidy for Asset Creation for Intellectual Property: Patent Registration
eligibility_text_source: All new and existing micro, small, and medium manufacturing enterprises located anywhere in Tamil Nadu.

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Cultural Organization for Holding Conference, Seminar, Symposium, Exhibition, Festival, Competition etc.
eligibility_text_source:
1. The institution should have a standing of at least 2 years in the field.
1. Institutions/organizations are required to obtain prior permission/approval of the Government before inviting foreign delegates to seminars and symposium etc.

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Dance and Drama Troupes/Associations
eligibility_text_source:
1. The dance and drama troupes should be presenting new classical or folk dance programs or for staging new dramas, enacted by any cultural body registered in the territory.
1. The troupe/association or body should have a standing at least 2 years.
1. The theme of the dance or the story and dialogue of the drama must be new and original, created by an artist who is a native of the territory by birth or domicile.

scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance for Industrial Infrastructure
eligibility_text_source:

 1. SWD, Sewage and drainage network.

scheme_name: Kishori Balika Yojana (Madhya Pradesh)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
 1. The applicant should be a girl.
 1. The applicant should be aged between 11 and 18 years.
 1. The applicant should be from one of the following categories: General, Other Backward Class (OBC), Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST).

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance to IHMs/FCIs/IITTM/ICI/NCHMCT/PSUs
eligibility_text_source:
• The applicant must be a Society formed by the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory Administration.
• The applicant must be registered under the Societies Registration Act 1860.
• The applicant may be an autonomous society formed by the Central Government, such as the Indian Institute of Tourism & Travel Management (IITTM), National Council for Hotel Management & Catering Technology (NCHMCT), or Indian Culinary Institute (ICI).

##### ***A. Criteria for Setting up a New State Institute of Hotel Management (SIHM)***
• The applicant must ensure the Institute of Hotel Management (IHM) Society is registered under the Societies Registration Act 1860 by the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory Administration.
• The applicant must propose the Institute of Hotel Management be run only by the State Government Society or Union Territory Administration Society.
• The applicant must ensure the State Government/Union Territory Administration concerned has transferred a piece of developed land, free from all encumbrances, necessary for the purpose.
• The applicant must ensure the transferred land measures not less than five acres.
• The applicant must ensure the State Government/Union Territory Administration concerned has committed to providing basic services/utilities like electricity, water supply, and provision of sewerage.
• The applicant must submit land transfer documents in the name of the IHM Society.
• The applicant, if land transfer to th

scheme_name: Scheme for Financial Assistance to Integrated Logistics Facilities: Capital Subsidy
eligibility_text_source:
1. Only new units or projects are eligible for this subsidy.
 1. The subsidy applies to costs related to construction and infrastructure (excluding land cost).
 1. The project must adhere to SOR norms of the government or its undertaking.
 1. The claim must be submitted in a phase-wise manner corresponding to actual expenditures.
 1. Developers must obtain all applicable environmental and statutory clearances.
 1. Basic facilities such as toilets and rest areas for transport vehicle drivers must be provided.
 1. The developer must operate and maintain the logistics facilities for a minimum of five years.

> **Note****:** 

 1. If land is leased or rented, a registered lease/rental deed for a minimum of 10 years between the developer and landowner is mandatory.
 1. Warehouse facilities must not be used for captive purposes by the developer; otherwise, they will be ineligible for assistance.
 1. Logistics facilities cannot be sold or transferred to another entity for at least five years from the date of the sanction letter or the project's commissioning, whichever is later. Facilities must be leased or rented to remain eligible for assistance.

scheme_name: Scheme for Financial Assistance to Integrated Logistics Facilities: Exemption of Electricity Duty
eligibility_text_source:
- The subsidy is applicable only to new units/projects.
 - Developer has to maintain and operate the logistic facilities for at least minimum period of 5 years.

scheme_name: Scheme for Financial Assistance to Integrated Logistics Facilities: Interest Subsidy
eligibility_text_source:
1. The subsidy is applicable only to new units/projects.
 1. The term loan’s first disbursement date must fall within the scheme’s operative period.
 1. Developers must not be declared defaulters as per RBI guidelines.
 1. Repayment against the principal must be timely.

scheme_name: Scheme for Financial Assistance to Integrated Logistics Facilities: Reimbursement of Stamp Duty
eligibility_text_source:
- Only new units/projects are eligible for this reimbursement.
 - Developer is eligible for reimbursement of the Stamp Duty paid on the purchase of required land for the project during the operative period of the scheme.

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance to Literary/Educational Institutions for Organizing Programs/Conferences
eligibility_text_source:
1. A registered literary or educational institution shall be eligible.
 1. Only 2 programs can organize per year by the institution.
 1. The program must be related to literature, education, or cultural discourse.
 1. The institution must use the Gujarati language in all materials (alongside any other language).
 1. The event must clearly acknowledge Gujarat Sahitya Academy through logos and name placement.
 1. The program must involve a minimum 5 speakers.
 1. All payments above ₹5,000/- must be made through banking channels only (RTGS/NEFT/Cheque).

scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance to Logistic Park
eligibility_text_source:
- Industries Association / any enterprise registered under the Societies Act, Partnership Act, Trust Act, Companies Act or Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) constituted for setting up of Logistics Park shall be eligible to develop a Logistic Park under this scheme.
 - The institution must intend to develop a logistic park as per the approved guidelines of the State Level Empowered Committee (SLEC).
 - The institution should possess 100% ownership of the land required for the project.
 - Projects under execution before obtaining registration will not qualify for assistance.
 - Assistance is only available for infrastructure-related Fixed Capital Investment (excluding land cost and vehicles).
 - Projects should be completed within 3 years from the date of approval, with a possible 1-year extension under exceptional circumstances.

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance to New Writers of Gujarati Language and Literature for Publishing Original Works of Creative and Fine Literature
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a first-time writer in the Gujarati language and literature.
 - The manuscript must be an original literary work such as a novel, play, essay, poetry collection, or autobiography.
 - The manuscript must not be edited work or related to non-literary subjects.
 - The applicant must not have received more than five grants from any language academy affiliated with Gujarat Sahitya Academy.
 - The manuscript must be typed, bound, paginated, and submitted in two copies.
 - The applicant must not change the title, content, or format after submission without written permission from the Academy.

Note: Only one application per financial year is allowed per author.

scheme_name: Scheme for Acquisition of Equipment and Others
eligibility_text_source: The applicant must be an organization in the small-scale sector.

scheme_name: Scheme Of Financial Assistance For The Preservation And Development Of Cultural Heritage Of The Himalayas
eligibility_text_source:
1. The voluntary organization should be registered as a society under the Societies Registration Act 1860 or as a public trust under Indian Trust Act, 1882, and shall have been functioning for a period of three years.
 1. Colleges and Universities are also eligible to apply.
 1. The organization should have the capacity to undertake and promote research projects. It should have facilities, resources, and personnel to implement the scheme for which the grant is required.
 1. A college applying for the grant should be affiliated with the University.
 1. Grants from this scheme will be given only to those organizations which are not in receipt of grants from any other source for similar purposes.

**Note 01:** The Colleges and Universities should introduce in their curriculum or research course aspects of studies relating to the preservation of Himalayan art and culture, in case not already done.
**Note 02:** The grants will be ad-hoc and of non-recurring nature.
**Note 03:** Organizations that are doing good work in the field and have resources for meeting matching funds will be given preference.

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Publication of Manuscripts/Research Journals
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant's proposed book or journal for publication must pertain to one or more cultural subjects such as language, literature, philosophy, religion, history, music, dance, drama, and other fine arts.
1. The applicant should be using language Tamil/Malayalam/Telugu/English/French.
1. The applicant's work should be a thesis, dissertation, or original research based on personal study and critical analysis, provided it is not intended for obtaining any degree.
1. Fictional works are not eligible for financial assistance under this scheme.

scheme_name: Silicosis Financial Assistance Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be the legal heir of a deceased person who was engaged in Akik occupation.
- The applicant must be the heir of a self-reliant person who carried on occupation of Akik in Gujarat state.
- The applicant must be the heir of a person who died due to serious disease of silicosis.
- The applicant must be the heir of a worker whose death occurred due to occupational silicosis while engaged in Akik work.
- The applicant must establish the occupational nature of the silicosis disease in relation to Akik work.

scheme_name: Special Financial Assistance Scheme for Meritorious Students of the State for Preparation of Competitive Examinations
eligibility_text_source:
1. The 100 candidates who qualify in the preliminary examination of Group ‘A’ and ‘B’ conducted by the State Public Service Commission.
 1. The candidates must have passed the preliminary examination for Group 'A' or 'B', or the written examination for the Armed Forces (such as NDA, CDS, OTA, Indian Naval Academy, Indian Air Force Academy, etc.) conducted by the Union Public Service Commission.
 1. The candidate must be a native or permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The candidate must have passed Graduate/Postgraduate examinations, or the minimum educational qualification prescribed for the above services, from recognized educational institutions (regular/open) located in the State of Uttarakhand.
 1. The total annual family income of the candidate from all sources should not exceed ₹5 lakh.
 1. The candidate should not have availed the benefit of any other equivalent scheme of the State or Central Government.
 1. A candidate who is engaged in any job/business/profession, or whose spouse is engaged in any job/business/profession, will not be eligible for the benefit.

scheme_name: Scheme for the Award of Scholarship for the Under Graduate Meritorious Students of Various Faculties of Junagadh Agricultural University
eligibility_text_source:
- Applicant must be pursuing an undergraduate program in any faculty of Junagadh Agricultural University.
 - Applicant must have secured at least 45% marks in the Science stream of the HSC examination.

**Note:** The students from College of Agriculture, Junagadh, Mota Bhandariya and Khapat Priority will be given to the girls candidates and if the sufficient number of girls candidates are not available then remaining number of fellowship will be given to economical poor boys candidates on merit basis.

scheme_name: Scheme for Awards to MSMES
eligibility_text_source:
1. Enterprises shall be in continuous production for the previous three years.
 1. Enterprises shall have valid statutory clearances and licenses as may be applicable.

**Note****:** MSME/Start-up will be entitled for only one award in each category during operative period of the scheme in each category.

scheme_name: Scheme to Provide Financial Assistance for Translation of Scholarly Works
eligibility_text_source:
- An individual translator or a translation-handling institution is eligible under this scheme.
 - The translation must be from one Indian language into Gujarati, or from Gujarati into another Indian language.
 - The original author’s or publisher’s written permission for translation must be obtained and attached.
 - The manuscript must be unpublished and originally translated by the applicant.
 - Only one application per financial year is allowed.
 - Applicants who have received financial assistance five times or more under Sahitya Academy schemes are ineligible.

scheme_name: Scheme For Financial Assistance For Veteran Artists
eligibility_text_source:
1. To be eligible for assistance under the Scheme, a person's contribution to art and letters, etc. must be of significance.
 1. Traditional scholars who have made significant contributions in their fields would also be eligible notwithstanding the absence of any published works.
 1. The personal income of the applicant (including the income of the spouse) must not exceed ₹4,000/- per month or an annual income of ₹48,000/-. [This excludes artiste pension assistance amount already getting by a beneficiary from the Government (i.e. concerned State Govt./UT Administration and/or Ministry of Culture)]
 1. The applicant should not be less than 60 (sixty) years of age (This does not apply in the case of a spouse).
 1. The applicant artiste is getting a pension of at least ₹500/- per month from the concerned State Government/UT Administration or is recommended by the Zonal Cultural Centres, Ministry of Culture, for the artists of their Members State where State Pension is not there.
 1. The applicant artists should not be getting financial assistance under other Schemes of the Ministry viz. Repertory Grant etc.

scheme_name: Subsidy For Battery Operated Three-Wheelers
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant must be an individual (such as rickshaw drivers, women entrepreneurs, startups, educated unemployed individuals) or an institution (such as cooperatives, NGOs, educational institutions, local bodies, yatras).
 1. The applicant must purchase a battery-operated three-wheeler from a GEDA-authorized manufacturer or dealer.
 1. The applicant must apply through authorized dealers linked with GEDA.

scheme_name: Subsidy for Battery Operated 2-Wheelers
eligibility_text_source:

 1. The applicant should have opened the Bank Account under “Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana”.

**Note 01:** The payment of a grant to the transgender shall cease from the date of the death of the beneficiary. The amount due up to the corresponding month of death shall be paid to the immediate members of the beneficiary and the passbook shall be surrendered to the Department.

scheme_name: Scheme to Grant Monthly Financial Assistance to the Persons Engaged in Traditional Occupations/Businesses including Motorcycle Pilots
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be of Goan Origin.
- The family income of the applicant from all sources must not exceed ₹1,50,000/- per annum.
- The applicant must not be less than 50 years of age.
- The applicant should be engaged in the traditional occupations/businesses/motor-cycle pilot activities for at least 10 (ten) years before applying for financial assistance under this Scheme.
- The applicant must still be continuing the traditional business/occupation including motorcycle pilot activity.
- The applicant must be declared medically unfit to carry out such activity.

_*Beneficiaries availing financial assistance under Griha Adhar Scheme shall be entitled for availing benefits under this Scheme subject to fulfillment of eligibility conditions laid under this Scheme._
##### ***Criteria Regarding Proof of Activity***

- The applicant having valid registration certificates from the competent authority of the traditional business activities being carried by him may apply under the Scheme.

scheme_name: Sanjay Gandhi Niradhar Grant Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a domicile / permanent resident of the state of Maharashtra.
1. The name of the applicant should be in the list of Below Poverty Line (BPL) families OR the annual income of the family should be less than or equal to ₹ 21,000/-.
1. The applicant should be one of the following:

 a) Destitute Men and Women below 65 Years of Age.
 b) Orphan Children.
 c) All categories of Person with Disability (PwD).
 d) Men and Women Who Are Unable to Sustain Themselves Due to Diseases Like Tuberculosis, Cancer, Aids, Leprosy.
 e) Destitute Widows (including widows of farmers who committed suicide).
 f) Women in the Divorce Process and Divorced but Not Alimony.
 g) Women Who Have Been Tortured and Released From Prostitution.
 h) Transgender.
 i) Devadasi.
 j) Unmarried Women Above 35 Years of Age.
 k) The Wives of Prisoners Serving Sentences in Jail.
 l) Those Suffering From Sickle Cell Disease.

scheme_name: Scheme for Grant of Pension Benefits to Journalists
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be a native of the Union Territory of Puducherry by birth or by continuous residence for not less than ten years.
- The applicant should be 58 years or above in age at the time of application.
- The applicant should have served for a minimum of 20 years in roles such as Full-time Editor, Reporter, Sub-Editor, Press Photographer / Videographer / Correspondent of a Newspaper/Periodical/News Agency/Electronic Media, including Newspapers and Periodicals published in Puducherry and News Agencies/Electronic Media having headquarters outside Puducherry. 
- In the case of Media Persons who are unable to pursue their profession on account of chronic ill-health or are permanently incapacitated from performing their job, the pension shall be sanctioned if they have put in service for at least 10 years in any capacity (as mentioned in Point 4), irrespective of their present age, if they meet all the other eligibility criteria.
- The applicant should not be receiving pension/honoraria from the Central/State Government.
- In the case of the death of the journalist, his/her spouse is eligible for 50%)of the pension.

scheme_name: Surgical Grant For The Prevention Of Disability
eligibility_text_source:
1. The annual income of the applicant/applicant's guardian should not exceed Rs.60,000/-.
1. The applicant should be an Indian citizen.
1. Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttar Pradesh or be a resident of the state for at least 5 years
1. Should not be guilty in any criminal case.

scheme_name: Scheme For Grant Of Rewards To The Children Of Salt Labourers
eligibility_text_source:
1. The children of only those salt workers who have put in a minimum of one year of continuous service in any of the salt works & passing from a recognized Government School are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. A child of a salt worker should be studying in Class VI to XII.
 1. A child of a salt worker to be eligible for a reward should have passed the examination in the first attempt.

**Note 01:** Preference will be given, other things being equal to the SC/ST/BC/Backward area applicants if applicants are both from either of these categories and the general category.
**Note 02:** The awards for girl children be reserved to a minimum of 50% allocation per state. In the absence of female students in a particular Salt Factory/State, the reward could be given to the available eligible male students in each category of other Salt Factory/State.

scheme_name: Scheme for Goa Rajya Vidnyanik Puraskar
eligibility_text_source:
- The scheme is designed for Students, Startups, Innovators, Research Faculty, and Scientists. The award is introduced to be given to Goan Innovators/Scientists.
- The applicant must be a resident of Goa for the last 15 years.
- The project must relate to hardware or software-based product innovation.
- The applicant must be required to provide a Letter of Intent (LOI) in case the proposed idea/project is associated with the Industry or the Government.
- The applicant must be registered under the Virtual Innovation Register (VIR) as a New Idea.
- The applicant can avail of this scheme only once.
- The proposals should preferably be in the following focus sectors: _Green technology, Clean energy, Industrially utilizable smart materials, Waste to Wealth, Affordable Healthcare, Water & Sewage Management, Renewable Energy Sources, Electric Vehicles, Smart Cities, Agri-tech, Meditech, Health Care Tech and Digital media, ITES._

scheme_name: Good Samaritan Scheme
eligibility_text_source:

 - Good Samaritans, who are not willing to disclose their details, would not be awarded under the scheme.

scheme_name: State Government Share Capital Contribution to Co.operative Sugar Factories (Normal Area) (Tribal Area)

scheme_name: State Government SC Scholarship (Class 1 to 10)
eligibility_text_source:

 1. The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
 1. Girl students from Class 1 to 5 and all students (both boys and girls) from Class 6 to 10 are eligible for the benefits under the scheme.
 1. There is no restriction on the annual family income of the students.

**Note 1:** One-time failure in a class is allowed.
**Note 2:** There is no need to reapply for renewal within the same level (Class 1 to 10).

scheme_name: Shilpi Gram Scheme (Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe)
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Sikkim Grant of Scholarship to Students with Disabilities Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student's age should be 5 years and above.

1. The student must be a regular student at a recognized College, University, or School of a recognized Board.
1. The student should pass the examination in one attempt during a particular academic session, If fails the examination during an academic session are not eligible for the scholarship for the same class in the next academic year. 
1. The student must not be receiving any other similar scholarship from other departments or ministries.

**Note 2:** Reapplication is not required for the renewal of the scholarship.

scheme_name: Shelter Home For Women (SHW)

**The applicant should qualify as ANY ONE of the following -**

1. Local Body.
1. Government Body.
1. Body as specified by the Govt. having a Managing Body with powers, duties and responsibilities, the experience of working in the field of women and children welfare, and rendering their services without distinction of caste, religion or language.

eligibility_text_source:

- Criteria: Female Employees; Unit: >= 60%

scheme_name: Special Incentive Assistance to Socially and Educationally Backward Class Trainees for Pre-preparation of Competitive Examination
eligibility_text_source:

1. The applicant must belong to the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC).
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant must have passed Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) or equivalent.
1. The applicant must be preparing for a recognized Class 1, 2, or 3 competitive exam of State or Central Government.
1. The applicant must have paid coaching fees and should have a valid receipt.
1. The annual family income of the applicant must not exceed ₹4,50,000/-.

scheme_name: Supply of Insulated Boxes
eligibility_text_source:
The applicant should be one of the following - 

- A fisherman engaged in selling and marketing fish.
- A fisherman engaged in fishing at sea with a canoe.
- A fisherman engaged in fishing at sea with a mechanized fishing vessel with up to 6-cylinder engines.

scheme_name: Solar Inverter Charger
eligibility_text_source:
1. Any resident family of Haryana with Pariwar Pahichan Patra (PPP) to apply.
 1. All the households who have domestic inverter and not installed solar inverter charger on his/her existing inverter.
 1. Applicants who have not installed Solar Inverter Charger in past 5 years under the Department Scheme.

eligibility_text_source:

- The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹ 75,000/- per annum.

- The applicant must construct a minimum of 10 rooms.

##### ***For Category B Forest Areas***

- The applicant must incur a minimum capital expenditure of ₹3,00,00,000/-.

##### ***For Category C Forest Areas***

- The applicant must construct a minimum of 05 rooms.

1. The applicant should possess a valid passport.
1. The applicant should meet academic and language requirements of the chosen institution.
1. Indian nationals are not eligible under the Study in India Programme.
1. Admissions outside the Study in India portal are not covered.

eligibility_text_source:
**1. A person with any of the following qualifications will be eligible for consideration:-**

 - Professional graduates with minimum qualification as Bachelor’s degree in Agri/ Dairy/ Animal Husbandry/ Veterinary Sciences/ Fisheries/Horticulture/ Textiles/ Handloom / IT duly recommended by the Head of the Department of the UGC / AICTE / ICAR recognized Universities / Institutions as applicable.
 - Professionals (pursuing or completed) MBA Agri Business / MBA Coop / M.Com / MCA / MBA Finance / MBA International Trade/MBA Forestry/MBA Rural Dev / MBA Project Management / Inter ICAI / Inter ICWA qualification duly recommended by the Head of the Department of the UGC / AICTE / ICAR recognized Universities / Institutions as applicable.

2. A person cannot be taken as an intern more than once.
> **Number of Interns and Location:**

• NCDC limits interns to 60 overall, spanning Regional Offices, LINAC, and Head Office annually.
• Each Regional Office can host a maximum of two interns simultaneously, as per NCDC guidelines.
• A maximum of two interns per year is allowed from a specific institution, following NCDC guidelines.

scheme_name: Scheme of Internship for Post-Graduate/Research Students
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India and; 
 - He/She must be pursuing post-graduation/research from a recognized College/University/Research institute in Statistics/ Mathematical Statistics/Operational Research/Economics/Demography or any of the applied fields of Statistics.

**Note:-**

 1. The internship will be for a period of 2 months from the date of joining. 
 1. Each intern/student shall be required to submit report/paper (along with soft copy) at the end of the assignment, including inter-alia, his/her observations and suggestions, if any, regarding any possible improvement in the system.

scheme_name: Student Internship Programme
eligibility_text_source:
> **Minimum Attributes**

 1. The applicant should not be more than 25 years of age.
 1. Candidates pursuing under graduation/ post-graduation / research in any discipline from recognized, reputed institutions, with stellar academic record are eligible to apply.

> **Preferred Attributes**

It includes skills in Information Technology (IT) & Information Communication Technology (ICT) with good communication and interpersonal skills.
> **Other Attributes**

 1. The Student Intern (SI) shall be subject to the laws of secrecy of the country and will sign a Secrecy and Non-Disclosure Agreement before reporting for the internship.
 1. The working hours of the SI shall be same as regular timing of this office. The SI shall not be entitled to any kind of leave.
 1. No extra remuneration shall be allowed for working beyond office hours or on Saturdays/Sundays/Gazetted Holidays.

scheme_name: Summer Internship Programme (PRL)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***Eligibility Criteria for Student:***

- The student should be a citizen of India.
- The student should be pursuing undergraduate/post-graduation from a recognized University/Institution India or Abroad
- The student should have just completed undergraduate/post-graduation from a recognized University/Institution, seeking an internship opportunity (within six months from the date of award of degree) for profile enhancement. 
- The student should possess an aggregate of a minimum of 60% or a CGPA of 6.32 on a scale of 10 at all levels.
- The disciplines from which the applications are encouraged are optics, Laser Physics, Space Physics, Geology, Geophysics, Atmospheric Sciences, Environmental Sciences, Meteorology, Chemistry, Ocean Sciences, and related subjects. - Preference will be given to candidates who are KVPY/DST INSPIRE fellows. - We expect prospective candidates to have reasonably good proficiency in basic physics and mathematics.

> ***Eligibility Criteria for Teacher:***

- The teacher has an academic background in Physics, Applied Physics, Astronomy, Space Physics, Geology, and related fields.
- The teacher should have teaching experience minimum of five years at the graduate/postgraduate level.
- The teacher should have demonstrated interest in research as evidenced by publications or ongoing projects.
- The teacher should have held a PhD degree.

scheme_name: SERB International Research Experience
eligibility_text_source:
1. The scheme is open to Indian nationals only.
 1. Applicant should have a Ph.D. degree in the area of Science and Engineering from recognized institutions in India.
 1. The applicant must hold a permanent position in any Indian research and academic institution.
 1. Applicants having MBBS/MD degrees and Veterinary doctors are also eligible to apply.
 1. Applicants having a Teachers Associateship for Research Excellence (TARE) award are also eligible to apply.
 1. Applicant must submit a letter of support from the overseas host institution along with the application.
 1. The age limit for applying under this scheme shall be 40 years (as of the date of submission of application). Age relaxation of 5 years will be given to applicants belonging to SC/ST/OBC/physically challenged and women categories.

scheme_name: Summer Internship Scheme Of DGFT
eligibility_text_source:

1. The applicant should have secured at least 60% marks in the graduation degree.
1. The applicant should have pursued graduation from one of the following institutions - 

 a) National Schools of Law
 b) Central Universities
 c) AICTE recognized Financial/Economics Institutions
 d) Recognized National Management Institutes
 e) Foreign Universities
 f) Institutions of excellence

scheme_name: Scheme of Internship of Students to DRDO Labs/Estts for Project Training
eligibility_text_source:
1. Students pursuing undergraduate or postgraduate degrees in Engineering or General Sciences are eligible.
 1. Interns will be permitted access only to UNCLASSIFIED areas of DRDO Labs/Establishments.

**Note 1:** The provisions of the Apprentices Act, 1961, do not apply to this scheme.
**Note 2:** DRDO is under no obligation to offer employment to students upon completion of their training.

scheme_name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme- For Incubators
eligibility_text_source:
The eligibility criteria for an incubator to apply in the Startup India Seed Fund scheme are as follows:
1. Incubator must be a legal entity:
 a) A society registered under the Societies Registration Act 1860, or
 b) A Trust registered under the Indian Trusts Act 1882, or
 c) A Private Limited company registered under the Companies Act 1956 or the Companies Act 2013, or
 d) A statutory body created through an Act of the legislature.
2. The incubator should be operational for at least two years from the date of application to the scheme.
3. The incubator must have facilities to seat at least 25 individuals.
4. The incubator must have at least 5 startups undergoing incubation physically on the date of application.
5. The incubator must have a full-time Chief Executive Officer, experienced in business development and entrepreneurship, supported by a capable team responsible for mentoring startups in testing and validating ideas, as well as in finance, legal, and human resources functions.
6. Incubator should not be disbursing seed funds to incubatees using funding from any third-party private entity.
7. The incubator must have been assisted by the Central/State Government(s).
8. In case the incubator has not been assisted by Central or State Government(s):
 a) The incubator must be operational for at least three years.
 b) Must have at least 10 separate startups undergoing incubation in the incubator physically on the date of application.
 c) Must present audited annual reports

scheme_name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme- For Startups
eligibility_text_source:
The eligibility criteria for a startup to apply under the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme shall be as follows:

 1. A startup, recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application.
 1. Startups must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with market fit, viable commercialization, and scope of scaling.
 1. A startup should be using technology in its core product or service, or business model, or distribution model, or methodology to solve the problem being targeted.
 1. Preference would be given to startups creating innovative solutions in sectors such as social impact, waste management, water management, financial inclusion, education, agriculture, food processing, biotechnology, healthcare, energy, mobility, defense, space, railways, oil and gas, textiles, etc.
 1. The startup should not have received more than ₹10 lakhs of monetary support under any other Central or State Government scheme. This does not include prize money from competitions and grand challenges, subsidized working space, founder monthly allowance, access to labs, or access to a prototyping facility.
 1. Shareholding by Indian promoters in the startup should be at least 51% at the time of application to the incubator for the scheme, as per the Companies Act, 2013 and SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018.
 1. A startup applicant can avail seed support in the form of grants and debt/convertible debentures each once as per the guidelines of the scheme.

scheme_name: Scheme for Interest Subvention to Nano Household Enterprises
eligibility_text_source:
A unit/enterprise which satisfies the following conditions is eligible for the scheme:
1. The fixed capital investment is 10 lakh or less.
2. Engaged in manufacturing, services, or job work.
3. In the white and the green category as per pollution control norms.
4. Connected load is 5 HP or less.
> **Obligations of the applicant entrepreneurs:**

1. The applicant shall provide all required details and declare such information as to be true.
2. The applicant shall provide clarifications of further details sought by the recommending/sanctioning authority.
3. The applicant shall allow inspection or verification of any details mentioned in the application including plant and machinery and all other assets if so required by recommending/sanctioning authority.
4. The applicant shall produce originals of any important documents if so required by the Recommending/ sanctioning Authority for verification.

Only two children of the worker's family and dependent mother/father can be included.
