eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be from the Other Backward Class (OBC) category.
1. The applicant should be a domicile of Maharashtra.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 to 50 years.
1. The annual income of the family of the applicant should be - 

- Rural areas: Less than or equal to ₹98,000.
- Urban areas: Less than or equal to ₹1,20,000.

scheme_name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Extension (SMAE)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The applicant must be a farmer.
 1. The farmer must have cultivable land.
 1. The farmer’s name must be duly registered in the relevant land records.

scheme_name: Sub-mission On Agriculture Mechanization
eligibility_text_source:
- All landholding farmers' families, Self Help Groups (SHGs), User Groups, Cooperative Societies, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) and Entrepreneurs
- The farmer must be a native of India.
- Women farmers can also take advantage of this scheme. They will be given priority in this scheme.
- The benefit of this scheme will be given by the central government to those farmers who are financially weak.
- The benefit of this scheme will be given to those farmers who have not taken the benefit of subsidy from any other central scheme earlier.

scheme_name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM) for Custom Hiring Centres / Big Farmers - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a large farmer (owning 5 hectares or more of land), a cooperative group, self-help group, or a custom hiring centre in Uttarakhand.
 1. The farmer or group must have cultivable land located in Uttarakhand.
 1. For an individual large farmer, their name must be recorded in the revenue records. For a group, the names of all group members must be listed in the agricultural land documents.

scheme_name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The applicant must be a farmer engaged in agricultural activities.
 1. SC/ST farmers, small and marginal farmers, and women farmers (of all castes) are eligible for 50% financial assistance under the scheme, while 40% subsidy/financial assistance is provided to other (large) farmers. 
 1. The name of the applicant farmer must be recorded in the revenue records of the cultivable land owned or cultivated by them.

scheme_name: State Marriage Assistance Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to Antodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) or Priority Household (PHH) ration cardholder families. 
1. The bride and bridegroom should be permanent residents of Ladakh
1. The bride should be above 18 years of age.
1. The bridegroom should be above 21 years of age.
1. The marriage should be the first marriage between the bride and the groom.
1. The divyangjan applicants with benchmark disabilities as defined in “The Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 are eligible.

scheme_name: State Marriage Assistance Scheme For Poor Girls
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a female.
1. The applicant must be of marriageable age, i.e. 18 years or above (Legal Age for Marriage in India).
1. The applicant must be from either AAY (Antyodaya Anna Yojana) or PHH (Priority Households) ration card holder families.
1. The applicant must not have availed the financial benefit for the same purpose under any other UT/Centrally Sponsored Scheme, especially Ladli Beti Scheme. She can avail of benefits only once under the scheme.
1. The applicant must have applied at least one month in advance (before the date of marriage) to the concerned District Social Welfare Officer along with proof of marriage, proof of age, copy of AAY or PHH ration card, and Aadhaar card.
1. The minimum qualification of the beneficiary girl has to be 8th Pass or equivalent. However, in the case of school dropouts, the following guidelines shall apply:

a) During the next three years i.e., from the financial year, 2022-2023 to 2024-2025 restructured guidelines shall be implemented irrespective of the education status of the beneficiary girl.
b) From 01-04-2025 onwards beneficiary girl has to compulsorily produce a certificate for having passed 8th Class or equivalent.

scheme_name: Sub-mission on Breed Development of Livestock and Poultry
eligibility_text_source:
**The following are eligible to apply -** 

1. Individuals
1. Self Help Groups (SHGs)
1. Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs)
1. Farmer Cooperatives (FCOs)
1. Joint Liability Groups (JLGs)
1. Section 8 Companies

scheme_name: Sanctioning of M.C.R. Chappals to Senior Citizens
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant's age should be 60 years and above. 
1. The applicant should not have availed the benefit of this scheme earlier.

scheme_name: Haryana State Merit Scholarship Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a citizen of India and a domiciled resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant must have passed the qualifying examination from Punjab, Kurukshetra University, or any other recognized examining body.
1. The applicant must be enrolled in a recognized course in a higher education institution in Haryana or Chandigarh.
1. The applicant must have obtained at least a Second Division in the qualifying examination.
1. For Rural Area Merit Scholarships, the applicant must have secured at least 40% marks in Matric/Higher Secondary (Final)/Pre-University Examinations.
1. The applicant must not be receiving any other merit-based scholarship, except for need-based scholarships from specific welfare boards.

> **Note:**

> **Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:**

1. One Ph.D./M.Phil. scholarship is exclusively tenable at Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi.
1. Rural Area Merit Scholarships are limited to students whose parents permanently reside and earn their livelihood in rural areas.

scheme_name: Garima Greh Shelter Homes For Transgender Persons
eligibility_text_source:
> **The following are the eligibility criteria of Transgender Person as a resident of Garima Greh:**

- Transgenders certified issued through the National Portal for Transgender Persons, and preferably living below the poverty line. Persons having such a certificate from elsewhere may be persuaded to register on the National Portal.
- Transgenders who are abandoned, aged between 18 and 60 years
- Transgenders should not be engaged in sex work and beggary
- Transgenders must be unemployed and not engaged in productive commercial activities

> The person needs to register by filling in the admission form ([Annexure 2](https://transgender.dosje.gov.in/docs/GarimaGrehGuidleines.pdf)) and the personal affidavit as per the prescribed form [Annexure 3](https://transgender.dosje.gov.in/docs/GarimaGrehGuidleines.pdf) provided in the guidelines document.

scheme_name: SMILE - Comprehensive Rehabilitation For Welfare Of Transgender Persons
eligibility_text_source:
- The student should have a Transgender Certificate issued through the National Portal for Transgender person of the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment
 - The scholarships are open to Indian Nationals only.
 - The candidate should not be receiving any other central or state government-funded Pre-Matric/Post-Matric Scholarship. No scholarship will be paid to the students under this scheme from the date they accept another scholarship/stipend. The candidate can, however, accept free lodging or a grant or ad-hoc monetary help from the State Government or any other source for the purchase of books, and equipment or for meeting the expenses on board and lodging in addition to the scholarship amount paid under this scheme

#### Criteria of selection for scholarship:

 - All eligible candidates identifying as Transgender Persons will be considered for scholarship. As the number of scholarships for Transgender persons available in a year is fixed and limited, the selection of applications would be on the basis of total marks achieved in the previous academic year as reflected in the mark sheet.
 - In case of the same merit, preference shall be generated from the ‘Date of Birth’ criteria of the applicant (senior is preferred) and based on the availability of the slots.
 - In the Scholarship, students applying for Post-Matric shall be given preference over Pre-Matric Scholarship.
 - The list of selected candidates would be displayed on the portal at a specified date considering,

scheme_name: SMILE - Comprehensive Rehabilitation For Welfare Of Transgender Persons – Skill Development And Training
eligibility_text_source:
The Transgender persons will be selected as per criteria indicated below:-

- Transgender persons having a valid Transgender certificate & Identity card issued by the National portal for Transgender persons would be automatically eligible to attend the skill development training of their choice on priority.
- The beneficiaries should belong to the Transgender community as notified by the Government of India.
- The Beneficiary shall mandatorily hold a Transgender Certificate issued by the National Portal for Transgender Persons, Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment. [https://transgender.dosje.gov.in/](https://transgender.dosje.gov.in/)- The beneficiaries should not be receiving such benefits from any other Centre/ State scheme.
- There is no income criterion for selection in the skill development programme.
- Transgender persons between the ages of 18-45 years are eligible for undergoing skill development training programmes.
- The skill development training programmes for the target group are free of cost.

#### Note
Candidates Are Eligible To Undergo Training Under Skill Development Training Programme Only Once And Can Participate Only In One Skill Development Training Programme.

scheme_name: State Millet Mission
eligibility_text_source: 1. All farmers from the mountain districts of Uttarakhand are eligible.

scheme_name: Scheme for Margin Money Grant to Nano Units
eligibility_text_source:
1. All new Nano proprietary enterprises in manufacturing/food processing and job works and units engaged in the service sector having any type of value addition, whose project cost includes fixed capital and working capital up to 10 lakhs are eligible for assistance under the scheme.
 1. The unit shall not have availed any grant assistance earlier under any schemes of the Government of India & Government of Kerala or Local Self Government Department.

**Note 01:** Preference is given to special categories such as women, handicapped persons, ex-servicemen, and persons belonging to SC/ST.
**Note 02:** Youth entrepreneurs up to the age of 40 are also given priority under the scheme.
**Note 03:** 30% of the beneficiaries under the scheme shall be women entrepreneurs.
**Note 04:** Industrial units which receive the assistance will be under obligation to remain working continuously for three years from the date of receipt of the grant. However, it is necessary that a unit shall be a working one as of the date of release of the assistance.
> **Obligation of an applicant:**

 1. The applicant shall provide all required details and declare such information as to be true.
 1. The applicant shall provide clarifications of further details sought by the recommending/ sanctioning authority.
 1. The applicant shall allow inspection or verification of any details mentioned in the application including plant and machinery and all other assets if so required by recommending/sanctioning authori

scheme_name: Scheme for Marketing Organisations
eligibility_text_source: Individuals / Partnership Concerns / Private Limited / Public Limited Companies experienced in marketing of Village & Small Industries (VSI) products are eligible.

scheme_name: Scheme to Provide Assistance in Cow Maintenance Expenses to Farmers Practicing Completely Natural Farming Based on Indigenous Cows
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a farmer.
 1. The applicant must be practicing natural farming on at least 1 acre (40 guntha) of land.
 1. The applicant must own a native (indigenous) cow with an identification tag.

> **Note:**

 - Priority will be given to farmers practicing natural agriculture and master trainers who have received training in natural agriculture from certified trainers.
 - Assistance to one beneficiary per account (as per sample number 8-A).

scheme_name: Scheme for Management and Purchase of Bulls Provided for Breeding of State Gir/ Kankarej Breed
eligibility_text_source:
1. A Gram Panchayat or a Gaushala registered must be registered under the Charity Act.
1. The Gram Panchayat or a Gaushala must have a minimum of 100 breedable cows.

**Note:** If there is not sufficient number of breeding cows in a single village, then the breeding cows of the neighboring villages should be covered and a guarantee should be given.

scheme_name: State Mountaineering Reward Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a native of Gujarat.
- The applicant should have climbed a height of 7,000 meters once or a height of more than 6,000 meters twice.
- The climbing achievement of the applicant must be confirmed by the Director of the Indian Mountaineering Foundation (IMF).
- The applicant should not be involved in any criminal activity.

****This award can also be given posthumously, for which the legal heir of the awardee will have to apply.***

scheme_name: Shaadi Mubarak Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The unmarried girl should belong to the minority community.
2. The unmarried girl should be a resident of Telangana state.
3. The unmarried girl should have completed 18 years of age at the time of marriage.
4. The wedding of the minority community girl should be on or after October 2, 2014.
5. The combined income of the Parents shall not exceed ₹2,00,000/- per annum.

scheme_name: Sewing Machine Scheme- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a woman worker employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
 1. The woman worker should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
 1. The monthly income of the woman worker should not exceed ₹18,000/-.
 1. The service period of the woman worker is fixed for 2 years.
 1. The woman worker will receive the benefits after every five years in the whole service period.

scheme_name: Solar Home System (Manohar Jyoti)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should possess a valid Pariwar Pahichan Patra (PPP).

scheme_name: Sewing Machine Scheme (HBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a woman and engaged in building or construction work.
 1. The applicant should be registered under the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The registered woman worker must have a minimum of one year’s regular membership.
 1. The applicant should have an active membership of the Board.
 1. This benefit is admissible once in a lifetime.
 1. The application frequency is limited to once.

scheme_name: State Merit Scholarship Manipur
eligibility_text_source:
1. Scholarship of Rs. 6000/- per annum is provided to the first 300 students who passed the class X examination conducted by the Board of Secondary Education Manipur for a period of 2 years.
1. Scholarship of Rs. 12000/- per annum is provided to the first 125 students of Science, 125 students of Social Sciences, and 50 students of Commerce who passed the class XII examination conducted by the Council of Higher Secondary Education, Manipur for a period of 3 years.
1. Students in full-time employment are not eligible for the scholarship.
1. Students should not receive any other scholarship/stipend for the same course of study for which they are receiving the said merit scholarship.
1. Candidates who have passed the qualifying examination in a year prior to the year of the award of the scholarship are not eligible for consideration.
1. The scholarship is renewable from year to year within the same stage of education.
1. Good conduct and regularity in attendance are required for the continuance of the scholarship. The scholarship will be canceled if a scholar gives up the chosen course of study before its completion or changes the course of study without the approval of the State Government. The decision of the State Government will be final and binding in all such matters.
1. The scholarship may be renewed for a different course of study if the scholar has shifted to another course instead of continuing with the course for which the scholarship was originally granted.
1. The max

scheme_name: Seed Multiplication Scheme of Paddy, Millets, Pulses, Oilseeds and Cotton
eligibility_text_source:
- The beneficiary should be a Farmer.
- The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
- The beneficiary should produce and supply seeds for the Department of Agriculture on a contract basis.
- The seed Farms should be registered by the Seed Producer viz., the Assistant Director of Agriculture of the respective Blocks.
- Field Run Produce has to satisfy the standards of physical/genetic purity, moisture levels and germination percentage as prescribed in the Certification standards.

scheme_name: Sub mission on Seeds and Planting Material
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must belong to one of the following categories: all categories of farmers, State Agricultural Universities (SAUs), Farmers' Producer Organisations (FPOs), Co-operative enterprises, or Government enterprises.
1. The applicant must attach land records along with the application form.
1. The applicant must have their own bank account.

scheme_name: State Medical Scholarship (Scholarship Scheme for MBBS & Allied Courses, Nursing and Paramedical Students)
eligibility_text_source:
A student should fulfill the following criteria to be eligible for this Scholarship:

 1. The applicant/student should be an inhabitant of Nagaland.
 1. Students selected through NEET/NSEE/State Departmental Examination only and undergoing medical, nursing and paramedical courses shall be eligible to apply.
 1. Students failing to complete their curriculum within the specified duration of the course (repeaters) shall not be entitled to an additional stipend.

scheme_name: Scholarship/Merit Scholarship Schools SC/ST/OBC/Min (Studying in College/Technical/ Professional Institutions)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
- The applicant should belong to the SC/ST/OBC/Minority category.
- The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized school, college, or institution in India.
- The applicant should be a student.
- The applicant should have secured a 60% or above marks in the previous examination.
- If the applicant is from OBC/Minority Community, his/her annual family income should not exceed ₹2,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Scholarship/Merit scholarship to OBC/MIN/SC/ST students from Class I to XII
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be from Scheduled Caste (SC)/Scheduled Tribe (ST)/Other Backward Classes (OBC)/Minority (including Jain) community

- Students from the Scheduled Caste (SC)/Scheduled Tribe (ST)/Minority category should be studying in Class 1st to 12th.
- Students from the Other Backward Classes (OBC) category should be studying in Class 6th to 12th.
- The applicant should be studying in a Government/Aided/Recognised school affiliated to the Directorate of Education/Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan/National Open School/Schools affiliated to NDMC/Delhi Cantonment Board, East Delhi Municipal Corporation, North Delhi Municipal Corporation, and South Delhi Municipal Corporation.
- For students from the OBC/Minority category, the income of the family should be less than or equal to ₹2,00,000/-.
- The applicant can avail of only one scholarship, either under the State Plan Scheme or Centrally Sponsored Scholarship Scheme, whichever is more beneficial.

scheme_name: State Merit Scholarship to Under Graduate Girls Students
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a girl.
1. The applicant should be a student.
1. The applicant should have passed 10+2 with at least 60% marks.
1. The applicant should be pursuing an undergraduate general degree course in a Government College in Haryana.
1. The applicant should not hold any other scholarship or stipend.
1. The applicant may accept a grant for books, equipment, boarding, or lodging from the State Government or other sources.
1. There is no income limit for availing of this scholarship.

**Preference/Priority**
If multiple students secure the same marks, priority will be given based on:

- Marks are calculated up to three decimal places.
- If still tied, the younger applicant will get priority.

scheme_name: Shramyogi Home Town Scheme- Gujarat Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The worker/labour should have been working continuously with the company in Gujarat for one year and his/her labour welfare fund should have been deposited in the board to avail the benefits under the scheme.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will be provided once within a block of two years. Currently, the block years are set as 2022-23 and 2023-24.
 1. If both the husband/wife are employed, only one of the beneficiaries will get the benefit of this scheme.
 1. A Shram Yogi shall travel only after obtaining prior approval by applying prior to travel.
 1. Within one month of the completion of the tour, necessary proofs along with in-principle approval have to be submitted by the worker/labour.

scheme_name: Sanction of Motorized Three Wheeler to Orthopedically Challenged
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be between 18 to 45 years of age.
1. The applicant should have a disability of 70% or above.
1. The applicant's family annual income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should be a bonafede student pursuing graduation or higher courses regularly.
1. The applicant should be self-employed (including in agriculture and allied activities) or wage/salary employed for a minimum period of one year, and must have passed at least the 10th class.
1. The applicant should obtain a valid driving licence for driving an adapted vehicle (previously called an invalid carriage) as per the Motor Vehicle Act within two months from the date of selection.
1. The applicant is eligible for sanction only once in a lifetime.

scheme_name: National Fellowship for Students with Disabilities
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a person with disability (PWD) as defined under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, with at least 40% disability.
1. The applicant must have a valid disability certificate issued by the competent authority.
1. The applicant must have secured admission to an M. Phil. or Ph.D. program in a university/institution recognized by the University Grants Commission (UGC).
1. The applicant must have a good academic record.

scheme_name: Sukhatme National Award in Statistics
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be an Indian National without distinction of race, occupation, position and gender.
1. The applicant should be at least 45 years of age as on 31st December of the financial year in which the award is announced, and is eligible to apply for the said award.
1. The applicant should not be an official involved in the selection process of the awardees.
1. The applicant’s family members should also not be part of the selection process of the awardees.

scheme_name: Shree Nanaji Deshmukh Awas Yojana (GBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The construction workers who are registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
 1. A house is allotted jointly to a registered construction worker husband or wife and either of the husband or wife is a registered construction worker will be eligible for the benefit of this scheme.
 1. No land/property or building should be in the name of the registered construction worker husband or wife or any of their family members.
 1. The benefit of this scheme is given once per family to the registered construction worker.

**Note 01:** The construction worker should be registered in this scheme for at least 2 years.
**Note 02:** Application for this scheme has to be made after one year of house allotment.

scheme_name: Sanitary Napkin Distribution - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source: 1. All girls aged between 10 to 19 years residing in Uttarakhand are eligible under this scheme.

scheme_name: Supplementary Nutrition Programme- Bihar
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a resident of Bihar.
 1. All children aged 6 months to 6 years, as well as pregnant women and lactating mothers enrolled in Anganwadi centers across the state, are eligible to receive benefits under the programme.

scheme_name: "Sugarcane Nursery Premium" Sub-Scheme of the "Crop Production Technology" Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a resident/native of Puducherry.
- The applicant should be a Farmer.

scheme_name: Sanitary Napkin Scheme
eligibility_text_source: All adolescent girls and women are eligible to purchase sanitary napkins at subsidized rates from Anganwadi centres.

scheme_name: Sanitary Napkin Scheme (UKBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a registered construction worker with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
 1. The benefits of the scheme will be provided to the daughters of registered construction workers and to women construction workers registered with the Board.

scheme_name: Sakhi Niwas - Working Women's Hostel
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
- The applicant must be a woman pursuing higher education or professional training, or a working woman.

scheme_name: Scheme for the Welfare of Schedule Caste Families in Fisheries Sector: Subsidy on 2nd Year to 5th Year Lease Money
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant should have a valid Family ID Card (Parivar Pehchan Patra).
1. The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Caste category.
1. The applicant's age should be 18 years or above.
1. Subsidy will be provided on lease money from 2nd to 5th year of lease.
1. The maximum area eligible for subsidy is 4 hectares per applicant.
1. The subsidy is applicable only on Panchayati ponds and government water sheds.

scheme_name: Scheme of Awarding Porkizhi to the Indigent Kalaimamani Awardee Artists
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The applicant should have received the Kalaimamani Award.
1. The applicant should be living under indigent circumstances. 
1. The age of the applicant should be 58 years or above.
1. The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹12,000/-.

scheme_name: Intensive Fisheries Development Programme - Subsidy on Commercial Four-Wheeler Vehicles
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant should possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant should be at least 18 years of age.

scheme_name: Scheme of Conferring ‘Kalaichemmal’ Awards
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be an eminent artist. 
- The applicant should be a domicile of Tamil Nadu or should be a resident of the state for at least five years.
- The eminent artist should have outstanding records of achievement in the field of Contemporary or Traditional art.
- The eminent artist should have continuous involvement in their fields.
- The eminent artist should have participated in art Exhibitions conducted on behalf of the Government or in the major exhibitions of Art conducted by others.
- The eminent artist should receive awards at the National-level or State-level art Exhibitions.

scheme_name: Scheme of Conferring ‘Kalaimamani’ Award
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Artist.
1. The artist should be popular and famous.
1. The artist should have professionally rendered yeomen services for the enrichment of the traditional arts.
1. The artist's performance should have had lasting contribution, popularity, and merit in the art disciplines of Tamil Literature, Music, Bharathanatyam, Drama, Film, Folk arts, Painting, Sculptures, Vigadam, etc.
1. An artist who is popular in various art disciplines can be conferred the Kalaimamani Award for one discipline only. 
1. If more than one artist is jointly engaged in artistic pursuit in any of the art disciplines, the award will be given to them jointly.

**NOTE 1:** This award is conferred regardless of caste, religion, race and age.
**NOTE 2:** Individual works of art or a single achievement will not be considered for conferring this award.

scheme_name: Scheme of Extending Financial Assistance to Folk Artists, Troupes for the Purchase of Musical Instruments, Costumes
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Folk Artist/Troupe.
- The applicant should be continuously performing in the programs.
- The applicant should have performed in the programs for more than three years.

scheme_name: Scheme of Extending Financial Assistance to New Drama Productions
eligibility_text_source:
- The dramas should reflect the assistance of Tamil art forms, culture, civilization, tradition, society and history. 
- The drama should not be staged in advance. 
- The drama should not show caste, religious, or political animosity.
- No additional dialogues or scenes should be added to the drama scripts without prior approval of the Manram. 
- The drama scripts should be selected by the Manram.

scheme_name: Scheme of Extending Family Maintenance Grant to the Families of the Deceased Famous Artists
eligibility_text_source:
1. The deceased should have been a famous artist in the art disciplines of music/ drama/ dance/ film/ folk arts.
1. The benefit of the scheme will be given only on the death of the famous artist.
1. The applicant should be the legal heir of the deceased.

scheme_name: Scheme of Extending Grants for the Publication of Rare Books on Arts
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Author/Writer.
1. The applicant should lack sufficient financial resources to cover the publishing expenses of the rare books.
1. The book should have been considered rare for the development of arts
1. The submitted book should not have been published before.

scheme_name: Scheme of Encouragement to Young Artists
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The applicant should be a domicile of Tamil Nadu.
1. In the case of music artist, the age should be above 16 years and within 30 years.
1. In the case of Bharatanatyam artist, the age should be over 15 years and within 30 years. 
1. The orchestra artists such as ghatam, ganjira, muharshingh, konnakkol are also eligible to apply. 
1. The artist should participate in any one of the following performances: Vocal, Bharatanatyam, Kathakalakshepam, Veena, Violin (solo) Nadaswaram, Flute, Jalatharangam, Gottuvadhyam, Clarinet, Guitar, Mandolin, Saxophone.

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance to Indigent Artists
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The applicant should be in indigent circumstances.
1. The age of the applicant should be 58 years or above.
1. The applicant should have professionally served for the enrichment of one or more art disciplines such as music, dance, drama, film folk arts, etc.
1. The annual income of the applicant or their legal spouse should not exceed ₹12,000/-.

scheme_name: Supply of Fodder Minikit
eligibility_text_source:
1. All farmers belonging to the General, Scheduled Caste (SC), and Scheduled Tribe (ST) categories are eligible.
 1. The applicant must own agricultural land.

scheme_name: Scheme of Giving Awards to Eminent Artists - Kalai Ilamani Award
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The applicant's age should be 18 years and below.

scheme_name: Scheme of Giving Awards to Eminent Artists - Kalai Mudhumani Award
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The applicant's age should be 66 years and above.

scheme_name: Scheme of Giving Awards to Eminent Artists - Kalai Nanmani Award
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 51 to 65 years.

scheme_name: Scheme of Giving Awards to Eminent Artists - Kalai Sudarmani Award
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 36 to 50 years.

scheme_name: Scheme of Giving Awards to Eminent Artists - Kalai Valarmani Award
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an artist.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 19 to 35 years.

scheme_name: Scheme of Grant-In-Aid for Conduct of Art Exhibitions: Group Shows
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a group of artists.

1. The artists should be natives of Tamil Nadu by continuous residence for not less than 5 years.
1. The artists should have continuous involvement in their field of painting and sculpture.

eligibility_text_source:
**General Eligibility**

- The applicant must be a tea manufacturing unit manufacturing Crush, Tear, Curl Tea, Orthodox Tea, or Speciality tea including Green tea, Oolong Tea, White tea, Yellow tea and Purple tea.
- The applicant must be located entirely in Assam.
- The applicant must not have any part of the plantation or manufacturing facility located outside the State of Assam.

**Plant & Machinery Subsidy Specific Eligibility**

- The applicant must be manufacturing Orthodox tea, Green tea, White tea, Oolong tea, Singhpo tea, Purple Tea, Yellow tea or other Speciality tea (not manufactured through Crush, Tear, Curl process).
- The applicant must have started production on or after 01-04-2020 and before 31-03-2028.
- The applicant must be either an entirely new unit or an existing unit which has expanded its capacity by creating additional infrastructure for production of Orthodox and Speciality Tea.
- The applicant must use all new machinery (no subsidy available on second hand machinery or parts of a machine).
