scheme_name: Self Employment Scheme For Rehabilitation Of Manual Scavengers
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be engaged or employed as a Manual Scavenger by an individual or a local authority or an agency or a contractor (as identified during the survey conducted by State Governments & allotted MS-ID/Receipt No. and their dependents).
 1. Sanitation Workers and their dependents are eligible only for assistance for sanitation-related self-employment projects.

scheme_name: State Rice Mission Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
1. The applicant should be a bonafide farmer engaged in agriculture.

scheme_name: Silicosis Rehabilitation Pension- Haryana Silicosis Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. Only workers who are confirmed to be suffering from Silicosis by the Silicosis Diagnosis Board, Haryana, will be eligible for the assistance.
 1. The Silicosis-affected worker should be categorized as either category A, B, or C according to the ILO Classification.
 1. The worker should have been registered with the Haryana Silicosis Board.
 1. The worker should have been employed in a factory or at a construction site.

scheme_name: State Reward to the Recipient of National Award
eligibility_text_source:
a) The applicant should be a Goan artist who has received any of the following National Awards:

1. Sahitya Akademi Award. 
1. Sangeet Natak Akademi Award. 
1. Lalit Kala Akademi Award. 
1. Padma Award for the contribution to the field of art & culture (for literature, music, dance, theatre, fine art, folk art etc.) 
1. Bhartiya Dnyanpeeth Award. 
1. Saraswati Sanman. 
1. Sahitya Akademi Jivan Gaurav Puraskar (Lifetime Achievement Award). 
1. Sangeet Natak Academy Fellowship. 
1. Any other National or International Award specially recognized by the Government of Goa for the purpose of this scheme from time to time. 

b) Those living artists who have already received such awards stated above shall also be eligible for this scheme.

**NOTE:** Under this scheme, an artist shall be eligible to receive the benefit only once, although he gets more than one award from amongst above mentioned awards.

scheme_name: Scheme for Relief and Rehabilitation of Women Acid Victims
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an acid attack victim girl/woman.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant should be a victim of an acid attack occurring on or after 02.05.2011.
1. The applicant should have suffered an acid attack within Haryana.
1. The applicant should not have been fully cured or completed all medical procedures, as continuing treatment is covered under the scheme.

> **RTS Time Limit:**

> This scheme's RTS (Right to Service) time limit is 30 days.

scheme_name: Sarothi Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility** 

1. All innovative startup industrial activities and the new individual applicant will be eligible to get the benefit under the Sarothi scheme.
1. New Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) units in Assam are eligible for these benefits.
1. Priority will be given to an applicant if he or she undergoes any skill development training.
1. If the applicant is a defaulter of any Bank or Financial Institution, he or she will not be eligible for benefits under the Sarothi scheme.

scheme_name: Salary Reimbursement Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. All the Start-ups certified by the Start-up Promotion Cell (SPC) having a valid start-up certificate number are eligible to apply for this scheme.
1. The employees should be hired for a period of at least 12 months.
1. For availing the benefit under clause 3.1, the employee/s whose salary is being claimed for reimbursement must be an IT professional/s. 
1. The bank accounts of employees should be linked to Aadhaar.
1. The bank accounts of the Directors of the company should be linked to Aadhaar.
1. Only expenditures incurred after notification of Goa Start-up Policy 2017, being within the validity of this policy and paid for digitally will be considered for reimbursements under this scheme. In case digital payments are not possible then it shall be up to the decision of SPC as per its due diligence to admit the expenditure.

scheme_name: Sheep Rearing Scheme (HSFDC)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste Category.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
 1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹1,80,000/- in rural areas and urban areas.
 1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed the BPL limit.
 1. The applicant's name should be included in the BPL Survey List.

**Note:** The eligibility of the applicant is verified by the field staff before sponsoring the same to the bank for sanction.

scheme_name: State Research Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicable for Scheduled Tribe and Indigenous students of Nagaland who are pursuing Ph.D/D.Litt. course from recognized Universities within India.
1. Scholars who have availed this Scholarship three times are no longer eligible.
1. Applicant must not be a beneficiary of any other Fellowship/Scholarship scheme.
1. Applicant must not be an employee of any government/semi-government establishment.

Note: To apply as a Fresh candidate, the student must be enrolled in the first year of the course. Renewal is applicable only to beneficiaries of this Scheme from the previous year.

scheme_name: Sant Ravidaas Shiksha Protsaahan Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The construction worker must be registered with the Board and be actively updated.
1. The construction worker must have completed at least one year (365 days) of membership with the Board after registration.
1. The age of the child should be as follows according to the class on July 1 of each year:

 - Class 1 and 2: Maximum age 6 to 8 years
 - Class 3 to 5: Maximum age 8 to 11 years
 - Class 6 to 8: Maximum age 11 to 14 years
 - Class 9 to 12: Maximum age 14 to 18 years
 - Graduate and postgraduate: Maximum age 18 to 25 years
 - Graduate (MBBS) and postgraduate degree/diploma in medicine from any government educational institution: Maximum age 25 to 35 years
 - Research in any subject: Maximum age 25 to 35 years
 - Study at Indian Institute of Management (IIM), Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), National Institute of Technology (NIT), National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT), and National Law University: Maximum age 25 to 35 years

1. Aadhaar certification of the construction worker's children is required for the benefits of the scheme.
1. The child must be enrolled in an educational institution that is recognized by a government-established education board/university.
1. The benefits will be payable to a maximum of two children of registered construction workers.
1. Registered construction workers' sons/daughters will be eligible to receive a subsidy for purchasing a bicycle to go to school after passing class 9, 10, 11, or 12 and entering the next class.

scheme_name: Sanskrit Shodh Chhaatravrtti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a student registered for a Ph.D. in Sanskrit at any university in Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must belong to the General, Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, and Economically Weaker Section. 
1. A total of 20 research scholars are eligible for the scholarship, including 10 from the General category, 5 from Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes, and 5 from Economically Weaker Section (EWS) certificate holders.
1. Selection will be based on the highest marks obtained in the NET examination.

scheme_name: Scheme for Reimbursement of Testing and Certification Charges for Start-ups and Telecom MSEs
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Start-up registered with the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Government of India.
1. The applicant should be a Micro or Small Enterprise registered under the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME).
1. The applicant should be incorporated within Indian territory.
1. The applicant’s annual revenue should not exceed ₹5,00,00,000/- in any financial year since incorporation.
1. The applicant (Start-up) should not have raised funds exceeding ₹10,00,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Scheme for Relief to the Un-registered Workers
eligibility_text_source:
> **Eligibility for Accidental Death of Unregistered Worker:**

1. The applicant should be dependent on the deceased unregistered worker. 
1. The worker must be unregistered with the Andhra Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The worker must have died in an accident at a construction site or related project during the course of employment.

> **Eligibility for Permanent Disability:** 

1. The worker should have a permanent disability due to accidents at construction sites.
1. The worker should have a disability of 50% or above.

> **Eligibility for Permanent Partial Disability:** 

1. The worker should have a permanent partial**** disability due to accidents at construction sites.1. The worker should have a disability of 50% or below.

scheme_name: Sneha Sparsha
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary should be a child below 12 years of age.
1. The beneficiary should be a resident of the state of Assam.
1. The beneficiary should be affected by some serious ailment(s).
1. The annual income of the family of the beneficiary should be less than ₹ 5,00,000, while priority shall be given to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.

scheme_name: Mission Saksham Anganwadi & Poshan 2.0, DNH & DD
states: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a child below 6 years of age.
1. The applicant should be a Pregnant Woman.
1. The applicant should be a Lactating Mother.
1. The applicant should be an Adolescent Girl aged 14-18 years.

scheme_name: Santwana Scheme - Karnataka
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to the religious minority category of the Karnataka state.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of the state.
1. The applicant's age should be between 18 to 55 years.
1. The family income from all sources should not exceed more than ₹8,00,000/-
1. The applicant's family members should not be employees in the Central or State Government. 
1. The applicant or his/her family should not have availed a loan facility from Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation (EXCEPT ARIVU EDUCATION LOAN SCHEME ) in the last five years.

scheme_name: Snehapoorvam Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Kerala State.
 1. The child who has lost either father or mother or both.
 1. The applicant should belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
 1. The students from the first standard to degree classes are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. For children belonging to Above Poverty Line (APL) category, annual income should be below ₹ 20,000 in rural areas (Local body/Grama Panchayat) and ₹22,375 in urban areas (Corporation/ Municipality).

**Note:** Candidate children below 5 years of age can be directly eligible.

scheme_name: Shikshashree Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The students should belong to Scheduled Caste.
 1. Students of classes V-VIII are eligible for the scheme.
 1. The annual family income of the student should not exceed ₹ 2.5 lakhs.
 1. Only day scholars belonging to West Bengal can apply for this scholarship.
 1. The applicant should be a regular full-time student reading in a Government School/Government-aided School and Govt. recognized schools in West Bengal.
 1. The award once made will continue subject to subsequent application of the student up to class VIII and good conduct and attendance and otherwise being eligible.
 1. The applicant should not get any other scholarship or stipend in any form for the same period. In case it is detected that the incumbent has drawn any amount of similar character from any other source this scholarship has to be refunded.
 1. The applicant should be a holder of a bank account in a nationalized bank with a core banking facility. 
 1. The Scholarships for studying in any class will be available for only one year. If a student has to repeat a class, he/she would not get a scholarship for that class for a second year. However, if he or she is promoted to the next class subsequently he/she will enjoy the benefit provided the incumbent applicant is otherwise eligible.

scheme_name: Sponsorship Scheme (90% Centrally Funded)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The child/beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. Children up to 18 years of age belonging to families with an annual income of up to ₹72,000/- in rural areas and ₹96,000/- in urban areas.
 1. Children who have lost both parents/guardians.
 1. Children covered under the PM CARES scheme, children belonging to families that have lost their primary breadwinner.
 1. Children in conflict with the law (CCL).
 1. Child labor victims, child trafficking victims, children affected by child marriage, POCSO victims, children registered on the Bal Swaraj Portal, as well as other children in need of care and protection.

scheme_name: Sagar Samman Award- Recognition of Outstanding Maritime Training Institutes
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a Directorate General of Shipping (DGS) approved maritime training institute.
1. The applicant should have participated in the Comprehensive Inspection Programme (CIP).
1. The applicant should have demonstrated consistent and high performance in training quality and outcomes.
1. The applicant should fall under one of the following categories:

- Pre-sea training institutes for Officer Cadets (Nautical & Engineering);
- Pre-sea training institutes for Rating Trainees;
- Post-sea training institutes conducting Competency Courses.

scheme_name: Sagar Samman Award- Sagar Samman Award for Excellence
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian national or PIO.
1. The applicant should have consistent high-level contributions.
1. The applicant should contribute in leadership, innovation, or HR development.
1. The applicant should have measurable impact in the maritime sector.

scheme_name: Sagar Samman Award- Sagar Samman Award for Gallantry
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian seafarer serving on any vessel.
1. The applicant should have performed an act of exceptional bravery in adverse maritime conditions.
1. The applicant’s act should involve saving lives, property, or environment beyond normal duty.

scheme_name: Sagar Samman Award- Sagar Samman Varuna Award
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian citizen, Person of Indian Origin (PIO), or Overseas Citizen of India (OCI).
1. The applicant should have at least 5 years of outstanding contributions.
1. The applicant should have contributed significantly in at least 2 major maritime areas.
1. The applicant should demonstrate leadership, innovation, and institutional impact.
1. The applicant should have impeccable integrity and reputation.
1. The applicant should be nominated by at least 2 entities or peers; self-nomination requires 2 recommendations.
1. The applicant should not have any reputational or ethical issues.
1. Government serving officers are not eligible.

scheme_name: scholarship scheme - Uttarakhand Agricultural Production & Marketing Board, Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a student.
1. The applicant must be the son or daughter of a small or marginal farmer, agricultural laborer, or mandi worker, or their dependent.
1. The applicant’s total annual family income must not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Sant Sohirobanath Ambiye Dnyanvruddhi Shishyavrutti- Bursary Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be pursuing a diploma, graduation, or post-graduation in a recognized institution located in Goa.
1. The applicant's institution should be affiliated with Goa University or recognized by the Directorate of Higher Education, Directorate of Technical Education, or any authority approved by the Government of India or Government of Goa.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in a full-time course.
1. The applicant's course duration should not exceed 5 years.
1. The applicant pursuing a diploma course should have completed standard 10th in Goa.1. The applicant pursuing a graduation course should have completed Standard 10th and Standard 12th or a diploma in Goa.1. The applicant pursuing a post-graduation course should have completed Standard 10th, Standard 12th or a diploma, and graduation in Goa.1. The total annual family income (parents/guardian combined) must be less than ₹5,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should not be receiving similar financial assistance, scholarship, or benefit under any other government scheme.

> **Note:**

- Relaxation in the qualifying education requirement is applicable for students graduating from premier institutions such as the Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs), National Institutes of Technology (NITs), Indian Institutes of Management (IIMs), and similar institutions, as per scheme norms.

scheme_name: Saksham Scheme for Backward Classes
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
 1. The applicant should be between 18 and 35 years old.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Backward Class Category.
 1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/- in both rural and urban areas.
 1. The applicant should have professional and academic training.

scheme_name: Scheme for Small Business Owners of Scheduled Castes to Buy a Place of Business/Shop
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
 1. There is no income limit.
 1. Loan assistance is given to the beneficiary only for the purpose of shop or business at one place.
 1. The subsidy will be paid after three months of shop opening.
 1. The benefits of this scheme will be given first priority to educated unemployed, unemployed mill workers, persons with technical & professional experience and self-employed persons with qualifications.
 1. Loans/assistance are also provided for shops/business premises allotted on long-term rent by the State Government or State Government owned Boards/Corporations, Urban Development Authorities, Local Self Government Bodies, or Gram/Nagar Panchayats. However, the shop/business premises will have to be mortgaged to the government till the loan amount for the allotted shop/business premises is repaid.
 1. If the applicant constructs on his own land, he will have to submit proof that the title of his land is cleared, and the land is' uncultivated '.
 1. Applicants desirous of availing the benefit of this scheme have to provide security of a maximum of two local persons having immovable property equal to the full amount of the loan.

**Note 01:** The place of business/shop purchased with the loan assistance obtained under this scheme should be in accordance with the land rules and construction rules and if any defect is found in this regard, the amount of loan/assistance

scheme_name: Samajik Sewa Bhatta Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Sikkim.
 1. The applicant should be a traditional faith healer such as Dhami, Jhakri, Bijjuwa, Bhungthing, Mangpa, Phedengba, Paw, Baidang, Janapa, Yeba, Yema, Sambas, or Pheriwala.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme on Establishment of 25 R.I.R. Bird’s Unit, 100 Broiler Bird’s Unit and Stipend for Poultry Farming Training for Scheduled Caste People
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be residing in Gujarat.
 1. The applicant must be a Scheduled Caste individual.
 1. The applicant must be an adult (18 years or above).
 1. The applicant must have completed a 6-day poultry farming training.

> **Note:** Females and beneficiaries Below Poverty Line (BPL) will be given priority.

scheme_name: State Scholarship for Class 6th to 10th (Denotified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic )
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a student.
1. The applicant should be from a Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Community.
1. The applicant should be studying in Class 6th to 10th.

_*The applicant should be eligible for the scholarship even if they have failed one year._
_*The applicant should not need to reapply for renewal if they continue in the same category of classes (e.g., 6th to 8th or 9th to 10th)._

scheme_name: State Senior Citizens Home
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Mizoram.
1. The applicant's age should be 60 years or older.
1. The applicant should have no supporter/relative.

scheme_name: Scholarship To Scheduled Castes Students Studying In Government ITI’s
eligibility_text_source:
1. The Scholarship will be given to all Scheduled Castes students who are studying in Government ITI's situated in Haryana State.
 1. The students should belong to the Scheduled Castes of Haryana.
 1. The student should be a resident of Haryana State.

**Note:** The students taking benefits of this scheme will not be eligible to get benefits under any other State/Central Scheme.

scheme_name: Stipend to Scheduled Caste Trainees in Technical Training Institutions
eligibility_text_source:
1. The candidates/trainees who are undergoing training in the Government Technical Training Institutions of the Union Territory of Puducherry are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. The candidate should belong to the Scheduled Caste community.
 1. There is no income limit to apply under the scheme.

scheme_name: Satfera Samuhlagna (DCW) Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a newly married female.
1. The applicant should belong to a Socially and Educationally Backward Class (SEBC) or Economically Backward Class.
1. The applicant’s annual individual income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Scholarship To Son And Daughter Of Differently Abled Persons
eligibility_text_source:
1. Children of any Differently Abled persons holding the identity card issued by the welfare board for the Differently Abled. 
1. This scholarship is applicable for students studying from Standard 11th to the Postgraduate level, which includes ITI/Polytechnic diploma courses and other higher education programs.

scheme_name: Supply of Sarees and Dhoties to Differently Abled Persons
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
- The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹ 75,000/- per annum.
- The applicant should have a disability of 40% or above.
- The applicant should be 18 years or above in age.
- The applicant should have registered their name in the local anganwadi centre.
- The applicant should be a recipient of Financial Assistance from the Directorate of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry.

scheme_name: Scholarships to Students with Disabilities Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. All students of Punjab having 40 % and above disability (In any disability contained in the schedule of RPwD Act 2016) certificate.
 1. The monthly income of family/guardians should not exceed ₹5,000/- per month.
 1. The candidate should have secured at least 40% marks in the previous annual examination.
 1. The scholarship will be provided to each student with disability from 1st class onwards.

scheme_name: Sona Sobaran Dhoti Saree Distribution Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant should have a valid Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) or Priority Household (PHH) card under NFSA, 2013.
1. Green Card holders under the Jharkhand State Food Security Scheme (JSFSS) are eligible.
1. The applicant must possess or apply for an Aadhaar number.

scheme_name: Establishment Of Small Scale Dairy Unit (SSDU)
states: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of the Union Territory of Daman and Diu and Dadra and Nagar Haveli.
1. The applicant should establish a small-scale dairy unit with 10 milch cows.
1. The applicant should procure indigenous Gir breed cows only.
1. The applicant should avail a bank loan for implementation of the project.
1. The applicant should utilise the subsidy strictly for approved components.
1. The applicant should maintain records as per the General Financial Rules, 1963.

> **Preference / Reservation / Relaxation:** The applicant belonging to the Scheduled Caste or the Scheduled Tribe category is eligible for a higher subsidy rate under the back-ended subsidy and insurance components.

scheme_name: Saksham Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a student.
 1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The applicant must have at least 40% disability.
 1. The applicant’s annual family income must not exceed ₹8,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme For Establishment Of 100 Broiler Birds' Farm For Schedule Caste
eligibility_text_source:
- The benefits of this scheme will only be given to the adult of Schedule Caste people of the society of Gujarat State.
- The beneficiary will have to provide income proof and a caste certificate along with the application.
- Females and beneficiaries below the poverty line will be given priority.
- The beneficiary should have taken poultry-keeping training.
- The beneficiary should have either his/her own land or will have to take land for two years on a lease base.
- The beneficiary will have to prepare a poultry shed having a capacity of 100 Sq. feet.
- The beneficiary will have to bring 100 low-input broiler birds, poultry feed, and water & feed utensils.
- As and when the Government officer visit the farm the beneficiary will have to show income and expenditure statement.
- In normal cases, the unit will have to maintain for two years.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme On Establishment Of - 25 Kadaknath/R.I.R. Birds Unit To Scheduled Tribes
eligibility_text_source:
1. The benefits of this scheme will only be given to the adult of Scheduled Tribe people of the society of Gujarat State.
1. The beneficiary will have to provide income proof and a caste certificate along with application.
1. Females and beneficiaries below poverty line will be given priority.
1. The beneficiary should have taken poultry-keeping training.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme On Establishment Of 25 R.I.R. Birds' Unit For Weaker Section
eligibility_text_source:
1. The benefits of this scheme will only be given to the adult of the economically weaker section people of the society of Gujarat state.
1. The beneficiary will have to provide income proof along with the application.
1. Females and beneficiaries below the poverty line will be given priority.
1. The beneficiary should have taken poultry-keeping training.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme On Establishment Of 40 R.I.R. Birds Unit For Divyang People
eligibility_text_source:
1. The benefits of this scheme will only be given to the adult of Divyang people of the society of Gujarat State.
1. The beneficiary will have to provide income proof along with the application.
1. Females and beneficiaries below poverty line will be given priority.
1. The beneficiary should have taken poultry-keeping training.
1. The beneficiary will have to manage to keep the birds safe during night hours.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme on Establishment of 25 R.I.R. Bird’s unit 40 R.I.R. Bird’s Unit and Stipend for Poultry Farming Training
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be from Gujarat.
 - The applicant must belong to the Economically Weaker Section(EWS).
 - The applicant must have taken 6 days of poultry-rearing training.

**Note:**

 - Divyang people of Gujarat state are also eligible to apply.
 - Priority is given to females and Below Poverty Line (BPL) applicants.

scheme_name: Stipends to Students of Economically Backward Class for Technical and Vocational Courses after SSC
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should belong to Economically Weaker Section category.
1. The applicant should be a student who has passed the Secondary School Certificate (SSC) examination.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized professional or Government Industrial Training Institute (ITI) course.
1. The applicant’s individual and family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/- per annum.

scheme_name: Scholarship for Children of Ex-Servicemen for Vocational and Technical Education
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should be an ex-serviceman of the rank of Havildar or below, or his widow/dependent.
1. The applicant should be pursuing a vocational or technical education course included in the [Prime Minister Scholarship Scheme](https://www.ksb.gov.in/writereaddata/DownLoad/List_of_Authorised_professional_degree_courses_under_PMSS.pdf) (PMSS). 1. The applicant should not be receiving benefits under the Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme or any other scholarship scheme.
1. This scholarship is not admissible in cases of Battle Casualty/Disability and Physical Casualty (attributable cases), as in such cases, scholarships are already provided by the Army Headquarters.
1. The applicant must have passed the previous academic year successfully; failure to pass results in the discontinuation of the scholarship.
1. The scholarship will not be payable to more than two children of an ex-serviceman.

**Notes:**

- Once selected, the scholarship will continue for the remaining duration of the course, subject to annual renewal.
- In case of failure in any year, the scholarship will be cancelled for the entire course.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme For Establishment Of Goat Unit (10+1) For Scheduled Caste People
eligibility_text_source:
1. This scheme can be applicable only to the adult beneficiaries of the Scheduled Caste people.
 1. The benefit has been provided to only one person per family.
 1. The Beneficiary has to give his/her photograph with the goat unit.
 1. The beneficiary should purchase 1 to 2 year old female goats and a 2 to 3 years old buck.
 1. The beneficiary has to contribute 50% of the unit cost of the scheme for food, water, fodder, residence, and concentrates expenses.
 1. In general circumstances, the unit should be run for 3 years.

**Note 01:** The beneficiary has to purchase a goat's unit within 90 days of pre-approval of the online application. If the beneficiary fails to establish the goat unit within the time limit or proves disabled then the approval order given to the beneficiary will be canceled and the approval order will be given to the beneficiary who is next on the waiting list.
**Note 02:** Beneficiary has to note the record of milk production and has to give register and information during the visit of the department technical officer.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme For Establishment Of Goat Unit (10+1) For General Category People
eligibility_text_source:
1. This scheme can be applicable only to the adult beneficiaries of the general category people of Gujarat State.
2. The benefit has provided to only one person per family.
3. The Beneficiary has to give his/her photograph with the goats unit.
4. In general circumstances, the unit should be run for 3 years.
**Note 01:** The beneficiary has to purchase goats unit within 90 days of pre-approval of the online application. If the beneficiary fails to establish the goat unit within the time limit or proves disabled then the approval order given to the beneficiary will be cancelled and the approval order will be given to the beneficiary who is next in the waiting list.
**Note 02:** The Beneficiary has to note the record of milk production and has to give a register and information during the visit of the department technical officer.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme For Establishment Of Goat Unit (10+1) For Scheduled Tribe Women
eligibility_text_source:
1. Applicant should be a Scheduled Tribe (ST) woman.
 1. The benefit has provided to only one person per family.
 1. Priority will be given if the applicant is:

 - A Scheduled Tribe widow, or
 - An abandoned woman.

> **Note 01:** The beneficiary has to purchase goats unit within 90 days of pre-approval of the online application. If the beneficiary fails to establish the goat unit within the time limit or proves disabled then the approval order given to the beneficiary will be cancelled and the approval order will be given to the beneficiary who is next in the waiting list.

> **Note 02:** The beneficiary has to note the record of milk production and has to give the register and information during the visit of the department technical officer.

scheme_name: Shreshtam Scheme For Ensuring Participation Of PwDs In Arts & Sports
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native and resident of Kerala State.
 1. The participants who have showcased their talents in arts and sporting activities in State/ National level championships will be eligible to apply.
 1. The applicant should be a Person with Disability (PwD) having 40% or more disability.
 1. The annual family income of the applicant should be below ₹1,00,000/-.
 1. The applicant should be undergoing training from State/National Academies/Centers.

scheme_name: Scholarship Scheme for Girl Children of Workers of Jute Mills/MSME JDP Units
eligibility_text_source:
- Scholarship to the girl children on Passing out the Examination in 2021(2021-22), 2022 (2022-23), 2023 (2023-24), 2024 (2024-25), 2025 (2025-26) - Total 5 Years (2021-26) 15th Finance Commission period.
- The scheme will be applicable to the girl children of the workers of Jute Mills and MSME - JDP units engaged in the production of jute products.

scheme_name: Vishesh Yogyajan Chhatravriti Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should be a student. 
1. The applicant should have at least 40% disability. 
1. The applicant should be a regular student in a government or recognized educational institution.
1. The applicant’s family annual income should not exceed ₹2,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should have passed the previous academic year.
1. The applicant should not be a recipient of any other state government or NGO/trust-provided scholarship or allowance.

scheme_name: Intensive Fisheries Development Programme - Subsidy on Solar System for fish/shrimp culture
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should have a Parivar Pehchaan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant's age should be a minimum of 18 years.
1. The applicant should not be a government or semi-government employee.
1. A minimum of 2.5-acre area is required for fish/shrimp culture. 

> **Note:** The benefit/subsidy will be provided only once to a beneficiary on a single unit.

scheme_name: Shaktidoot Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must have participated in games recognized by the School Games Federation of India or included in the Commonwealth Games, Asian Games, Afro-Asian Games, or Olympics, as determined by a committee appointed by the Government.
 1. The applicant must below 20 years of age.
 1. The applicant must have won state-level competitions based on their sporting achievements.
 1. The applicant must have achieved success in junior or sub-junior competitions of the open category organized by a recognized All India Federation, prioritized based on their accomplishments.
 1. The applicant must not use banned substances or have any impairment that disqualifies them from participation, as such individuals will be immediately dropped from the scheme.

> **Note:**

 1. The applicant’s sports achievements over the previous two years will be considered for selection under the scheme.
 1. The above criteria for eligibility will be generally taken into account but the committee may decide to grant such players an exceptional status under this scheme in view of the exceptional achievements of any athlete, in order to expand his talent at the international level and to fully develop his abilities.

scheme_name: Stipend Scheme for Grandchildren of Freedom Fighters
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a grandchild (third generation) of a freedom fighter.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in a government or government-aided college/university in Haryana.
1. The applicant should be pursuing a non-professional course.
1. The applicant should have passed the previous examination based on which admission was granted.
1. The applicant should maintain satisfactory attendance and conduct.
1. The applicant should not have failed the previous year’s university examination.

**Reinstatement Criteria:**
If a student drops out but re-joins classes and maintains at least 50% attendance for three months, the previous and current quarter’s stipend is released.

scheme_name: Swayam Saksham Scheme (Gujarat Gopalak Vikas Nigam)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should belong to the Rabari and Bharwad castes from the socially and educationally backward classes.
1. The applicant's age should be between 18 and 35 years.
1. The applicant's annual income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Prani Sampad Samruddhi Yojana: Small Scale Goat / Sheep Farming (10+1 Capacity)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Odisha.
1. The applicant must be interested in small, marginal farmers and landless labourers.
1. The applicant must be willing to establish a new Goat/Sheep Unit (10+1 capacity) in individual mode.
1. The applicant must have an Aadhaar linked at the time of submission of the application.
1. The applicant must have a suitable proposed site for the establishment of the goat/sheep unit, verified by the Block Level Inspection Team (BLIT).
1. The applicant proposal must be for a new project only (as verified by BLIT at the time of initial verification).
1. The applicant must have, or be willing to arrange, the remaining cost (after subsidy) through a bank loan or self-finance.
> **Preference / Priority / Reservation:**

- Priority is given to SC, ST, Women, Transgender, and Differently-Abled categories, who are eligible for 60% subsidy.
- General (Male) category applicants are eligible for 50% subsidy.
- The total target is 1,875 units for SC, ST, and General (including Women, Transgender, and Differently-Abled) beneficiaries.
- In case of non-availability of beneficiaries from a particular reserved category (SC or ST), the target may be interchanged among the reserved categories. If no beneficiary is available from any reserved category, the target may be allotted to the General category.
- District-wise targets are specified in [Annexure-VI](https://fard.odisha.gov.in/sites/default/files/2025-12/Small%20scale%20goat%20sheep%20farming%2010%

scheme_name: Spectacles Scheme- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a worker employed in industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
2. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
3. The monthly salary of the worker should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
4. The service period of the worker is fixed for one year.
5. The worker will get the benefit after every five years in whole service period.
6. After doctor's prescription, application will have to be submitted within three months from the date of bill for purchasing spectacles.
7. The benefit of this scheme will be available only once for each beneficiary.

scheme_name: Simon Sing Horo Special Post-Matric Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam.
- The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community.
- The applicant must have passed one of the following examinations - 

 (a) High School Leaving Certificate (HSLC) examinations from the Board of Secondary Education (SEBA) or an equivalent educational board recognized by the Government of Assam during the current year.
 (b) The applicant must have passed the Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate (HSSLC) examinations in any stream from the Assam Higher Secondary Education Council (AHSEC) or an equivalent educational board recognized by the Government of Assam during the current year.

- The applicant's family income must not exceed ₹5,00,000/- per annum.

****The applicant applying for this incentive scheme shall also be eligible for the normal Post-Matric Scholarship, Financial Assistance for Higher Studies, and Financial Assistance for Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM)/General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM)/Technical Courses, if eligible, in the same year.***

scheme_name: Scheme For Subsidy On Interest For Establishment Of 1 To 20 Milch Animal Farm
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Gujarat State.
 1. Only one member of the family should be the beneficiary of the scheme.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will be available to all the farmers of Gujarat State.
 1. The applicant will be eligible for assistance only if he has obtained a loan for setting up the unit in the current financial year 2023-24 through a nationalized bank or a financial institution approved by the Reserve Bank of India.

**Note 01:** The land should be owned by a member of the family or a beneficiary of the scheme.
**Note 02:** The grant made under this Act may not be used for any other purpose and shall be paid annually.
**Note 03:** The scheme shall provide for the establishment of a unit of livestock (cows and buffaloes) by obtaining a certificate from the Animal Husbandry Authority.

scheme_name: Scheme For Subsidy On Interest For Establishment Of 1 To 20 Milch Animal Farm For Scheduled Caste
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Gujarat State.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category only.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will be available to the small and poor farmers of Gujarat State.
 1. The applicant will be eligible for assistance only if he has obtained a loan for setting up the unit in the current financial year 2023-24 through a nationalized bank or a financial institution approved by the Reserve Bank of India.

**Note 01:** The land should be owned by a member of the family or a beneficiary of the scheme.
**Note 02:** The grant made under this Act may not be used for any other purpose and shall be paid annually.
**Note 03:** The scheme shall provide for the establishment of a unit of livestock (cows and buffaloes) by obtaining a certificate from the Animal Husbandry Authority.

scheme_name: Scheme For Subsidy On Interest For Establishment Of 1 To 20 Milch Animal Farm For Scheduled Tribe
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Gujarat State.
 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category only.
 1. The benefit of this scheme will be available to the small and poor farmers of Gujarat State.
 1. The applicant will be eligible for assistance only if he/she has obtained a loan for setting up the unit in the current financial year 2023-24 through a nationalized bank or a financial institution approved by the Reserve Bank of India.

**Note 01:** The land should be owned by a member of the family or a beneficiary of the scheme.
**Note 02:** The grant made under this Act may not be used for any other purpose and shall be paid annually.
**Note 03:** The scheme shall provide for the establishment of a unit of livestock (cows and buffaloes) by obtaining a certificate from the Animal Husbandry Authority.

scheme_name: Special Scheme for Increasing the Storage Capacity of Perishable Products
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a farmer.
 - The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.

scheme_name: Sports Scholarship Jharkhand
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a Scheduled Tribe student and a resident of Jharkhand. 
1. The applicant must be studying in a middle school, high school, or college.
1. The applicant must actively participate in state or national-level sports competitions. 
1. The athletes achieving national-level recognition are eligible.
1. The applicant belongs to families below the poverty line are eligible.

scheme_name: Sastraposhini Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. Only government high schools in the State with sufficient facilities (three rooms to establish Physics, Chemistry, and Biology laboratories), and qualified faculty in science are eligible.
 1. The schools applying for the scheme shall have the following basic requirements:

 - Sufficient space to establish Sastraposhini laboratories (separate rooms for physics, chemistry, and biology laboratories).
 - Qualified teachers in three subject areas (physics, chemistry, and biology).
 - Sufficient number of students in the high school section.
 - Extend of administrative support from the Headmaster/Principal/Management for the smooth implementation of the scheme.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme on Establishment of 25 R.I.R./Kadaknath & 1000 Broiler Bird’s Unit and Stipend for Poultry Farming Training for Scheduled Tribes People of the State
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be from Gujarat.
 - The applicant must be an adult Scheduled Tribe (ST) individual.
 - The applicant must have completed 6 days of poultry rearing training for the 25 R.I.R./Kadaknath bird unit and stipend scheme.
 - The applicant must have completed any poultry rearing training for the 1000 broiler birds unit scheme.

**Note:**

 - Priority is given to females and Below Poverty Line (BPL) applicants.

scheme_name: Scheme For Soft Loan To The Grant-in-aid/ Non-grant In Aid Institutions For Maintenance And Upgradation Of Infrastructure Facilities
eligibility_text_source:
All the aided/non-aided institutions of higher learning, including institutions providing professional courses, shall be eligible to apply for the loan subject to the following conditions:

1. The institution should be situated in Goa.
1. The society which runs the institution should have been registered under the Societies Registration Act.
1. The institution should have been in operation for at least 10 years, preceding the date of application of the loan. 
1. The institution should have a minimum strength of 300 students on campus.
1. The average passing percentage should not be below 60% in the last three years out of five years immediately preceding the date of application for the loan.
1. The institution seeking the loan should meet ANY ONE of the following two requirements:

 a) The institutions concerned should be affiliated with Goa University.
 b) The institutions should have been recognized by AICTE/MCI/ACI/DCI or any other statutory body of the Government of India/Government of Goa.

> **Preference/ Priority**

1. A special preference shall be given to the institutions from less developed Taluka’s such as Canacona, Pernem, Sattari, Sanquelim, Quepem, Bicholim and Sanguem.
1. Since the financial facilities under the scheme at any point in time are limited, priority would be given to the older institutions over the newer ones.

scheme_name: Small Scale Lending Scheme (Microfinance)
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must belong to the Rabari and Bharwad castes among the socially and educationally backward classes.
- The applicant must be aged between 21 to 45 years.
- The applicant must have an annual income of less than or equal to ₹3,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Stipends to Students of Minority Caste for Technical and Vocational Courses after SSC
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should belong to Minority Caste.
1. The applicant should be a student who has passed the Secondary School Certificate (SSC) examination.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized professional or Government Industrial Training Institute (ITI) course.
1. The applicant’s individual and family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/- per annum.

scheme_name: Swavalambi/Sanchari Marata Malige
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be leather artisans.
1. The applicant should belongs to sub-castes Arundathiyar, Chammadiya, Chamar, Chambar, Chamagar, Madar, Madig, Madiga, Mini Madig, Jambavalu, Haralayya, Machigar, Mochigar, Mochi, muchi, Telugu Mochi, kamaati mocha, Rohidas, Dhor, kakkayya, Kankayya, Samagara, Samagar, Aadhi Anddra, Aadhi Dravida, Aadhi Karnataka among scheduled castes.
1. The age of the applicant should be 18 years.
1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹30,000/- in rural areas and ₹25,000/- in urban areas.
1. The applicant or their family members must not have received benefits from other schemes, except training programs.
1. The applicant's family members should not be employed in government or semi-government organizations.

scheme_name: Sanskrit Scholarship
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a student.
1. The students studying Sanskrit at various levels—Post Graduate (M.A. Classic), Acharya, Graduate (B.A. Classic), Shastri, Uttar Madhyama, Purva Madhyama, and Prathama—in any government or aided non-government college.
1. The annual family income of the applicant's parents or guardians should not exceed ₹54,000/-.
1. The applicant's Academic Qualifications should be one of the following:

- Post Graduate: 50% marks in degree examination.
- Acharya: 50% marks in Shastri or equivalent examination.
- Graduate: 50% marks in 10+2 or equivalent examination.
- Shastri: 45% marks in Purva Madhyama.
- Uttar Madhyama: 45% marks in Prathma or equivalent.
- Purva Madhyama: On the basis of merit in the 5th examination.

scheme_name: Snehayanam Scheme For Mothers Of Persons With Special Needs
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native and resident of Kerala State.
 1. Widowed/legally divorced/ wives abandoned by husbands are eligible to apply under this scheme.
 1. The mothers of PwDs covered under the National Trust Act (NTA) will be eligible to apply under this scheme.
 1. The applicants should belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) category and do not have a source of steady income.
 1. The beneficiaries must possess a valid 3-wheeler license.
 1. The age limit of the applicant has to be 55 years or below.

**Note 01:** The tax and insurance for the vehicles will have to be borne by the beneficiary.
**Note 02:** The vehicle must be registered solely in the name of the beneficiary and the vehicle ownership cannot be transferred or sold.

scheme_name: Stipends to Students of NT/DNT Caste for Technical and Vocational Courses after SSC
eligibility_text_source:

1. The applicant should be a student who has passed the Secondary School Certificate (SSC) examination.
1. The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized professional or Government Industrial Training Institute (ITI) course.
1. The applicant’s individual and family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/- per annum.

scheme_name: Scheme To Support Orphan Child/Children Of Widows Belonging To Scheduled Tribe Community
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) Community.

> ***For Children of Widows (Applicant is the Widow)***

- The applicant must be a Dayanand Social Security Scheme (DSSS) beneficiary.
- The applicant must have minor children.

> ***For Orphans (Applicant is the Guardian)***

- The applicant must be the guardian of an orphan.
- The orphan must be under the care of a guardian, which includes grandparents, near relatives, or a protective home/dharmasala.
- The orphan must be up to the age of eighteen years.

scheme_name: State Scholarship of Class 1st to 5th (Scheduled Caste)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.
1. The student should be enrolled in Class 1 to 5 in a recognized educational institution.
1. The student should be a regular student of the institution.

scheme_name: State Scholarship of Class 6th to 10th (Scheduled Caste)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh
1. The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.
1. The student should be enrolled in classes 6th to 10th in a recognized educational institution..
1. The student should apply for the scholarship before the 31st of October.

scheme_name: Stipend Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

 - The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
 - The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
 - The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

 - The applicant should be a registered construction worker Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab or the child of a registered construction worker.
 - The applicant should be enrolled in an approved institute or university.
 - The applicant should be studying/ pursuing course in any of the following:

 1. Primary Education: 1st class to 5th class
 1. Middle School: 6th class to 8th class
 1. High School: 9th and 10th class
 1. Senior Secondary: 11th and 12th class
 1. College Students: All types of graduation/Post-Graduation, including technical or professional studies
 1. Vocational Training: ITI/Polytechnic
 1. Professional Courses: Medical/Engineering (All types of Medic

scheme_name: Shagun Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
eligibility_text_source:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

 - The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
 - The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
 - The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
 - The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
 - The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
 - The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

 - It should be the first marriage of the daughter/ self.
 - The scheme applies only to the marriage of up to two daughters.
 - The applicant must submit an application within 3 months after the marriage date.

scheme_name: Stipend Scheme For Poultry Farming Training For Divyang People
eligibility_text_source:
1. The benefits of this scheme will only be given to the adult of Divyang people of the society of Gujarat State.
1. The beneficiary will have to provide an income proof certificate.
1. Females and beneficiaries Below Poverty Line will be given priority.

scheme_name: Stipend Scheme For Poultry Farming Training For Schedule Caste
eligibility_text_source:
1. The benefits of this scheme will only be given to the adult of Schedule Caste people of the society of Gujarat State.
1. Beneficiary will have to provide income proof and caste certificate.
1. Females and beneficiaries Below Poverty Line will be given beneficiary priority.

scheme_name: Stipend Scheme For Poultry Farming Training For Weaker Section
eligibility_text_source:
1. The benefits of this scheme will only be given to the adult of economically weaker sections people of the society of Gujarat State.
 1. The beneficiary will have to provide income proof along with the application.
 1. Females and beneficiaries Below Poverty Line will be given priority.

scheme_name: Swasraya Scheme For Parents/Mothers Of Physically Handicapped/Mentally Retarded Persons
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native and resident of Kerala State.
 1. The applicant should be from Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
 1. The parents (mothers) with children having 70% or more disability / mental retardation are eligible to apply under the scheme.
 1. Single/Unwed mothers/Wives abandoned by husbands/Legally divorced women/Separated women are eligible to apply under this scheme.
 1. Close relatives (women- as mentioned in the above category) who act as caretakers of severely disabled/mentally challenged patients can also apply.

**Note 01:** Mothers of severely disabled/mentally challenged bedridden patients will be given priority.
**Note 02:** Beneficiaries of the “Aswasakiranam Scheme” can also apply under the scheme.

scheme_name: Social Security Pension Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Chhattisgarh.
 1. The applicant should be either a person with a disability or a dwarf person.
 1. Children with disabilities (CwDs) aged 6-17 years are eligible, the CwDs in the age group of 6-14 should be school-going.
 1. Persons with disabilities who are 18 years or older and have a disability of 40% or above are eligible for the scheme.
 1. The applicant should not be receiving pension benefits from any other central or state government pension scheme.
 1. The applicant's name should be included in the list of families living the Below Poverty Line.

**Note:** Disabled children aged 6-14 years who are not attending school are not eligible.

scheme_name: Silicosis (Bimari) Se Pidit Shramiko Ke Liye Aarthik Evam Punarvas Sahayata Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. Workers registered with the Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board.
 1. Workers who claim to be working in the organized sector but cannot prove it, or construction workers who are not eligible for registration with the Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, will also get the benefits.
 1. Competent doctors must confirm the Silicosis disease to the worker.

scheme_name: Sugarcane Seed Replacement Programme / Production Enhancement Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a farmer cultivating sugarcane.
1. The applicant must be a permanent member of a cooperative sugarcane development society.
1. The applicant must practice advanced sugarcane farming.
1. The applicant’s name must be registered in the land records of the state.

scheme_name: Shrama Shakthi Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The age limit of the applicant should be between 18 and 55 years.
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of the State.
1. Applicants must belong to State Religious Minority Community.
1. Family income from all sources should be within Rs. 3.50 lakh per annum.
1. An applicant or his/her family members should not have availed benefits under any other scheme (Excluding the Arivu Scheme) of the KMDCL in the last 5 years.

scheme_name: Salary Subsidy Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. All the new and existing units must have their operations in Goa.
2. The applicant must be one of the below:

- Proprietorship Firm
- Private Limited Company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013 (or equivalent)
- Public Limited Company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013 (or equivalent)
- Registered Partnership Firm incorporated under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 (or equivalent)
- Limited Liability Partnership incorporated under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008 (or equivalent)

3. The bank accounts of the unit and/or its partner(s)/director(s) should be linked to Aadhaar.
4. The bank accounts of Freshers should be linked to Aadhaar.
5. The subsidy is valid for Freshers who have been employed for a minimum period of one year.
6. For the purpose of implementation of this incentive, individuals who have worked for more than two years before pursuing post-graduation are not considered freshers. 
7. In instances where the individual has less than two years of experience prior to the Post graduation, the individual is considered as a fresher, however, the time duration for the individual is considered as a fresher is calculated on a prorate basis.

scheme_name: Sant Surdas Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Gujarat.
 1. The applicant should be 0 to 17 years age group.
 1. Percentage of disability of 80 or more or those who cannot move even with help of artificial limbs independently.
 1. The applicant should be registered in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) list and BPL score is up to 20.
 1. The benefit of this scheme is eligible to the disabled person of Gujarat living under the Below Poverty Line (disabled person included in the BPL from the Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India for the rural area and the Ministry of Housing & Urban Poverty Alleviation, Government of India for the urban area) are eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
 1. The applicant should be possessing disability card issued by state government.

**Note:** The amount of assistance will be stopped when the applicant/beneficiary reached age of 18 years.

scheme_name: Special Scholarship to Students Belonging to the Most Backward Castes among Scheduled Castes (Vandaniya Sant Shri Vasiyadada Scholarship)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student should belong to the most backward castes among the scheduled castes of Gujarat.
 1. The student should be studying in Standard I to X.
 1. There is no income limit.
 1. The most backward castes eligible under the scheme in Gujarat state include Valmiki, Hadi, Nadia, Senva, Turi, Garo, Harijan-Bava, and Vanker-Sadhu, who are granted special scholarships.

scheme_name: Scholarship/Stipend to SC Students for ITI/Professional Courses (Swami Vivekanand Scholarship)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste of Gujarat.
 1. The student should be studying in professional course in a Government or Private Industrial Training Institute (ITI).
 1. The annual family income of the student should be up to ₹6,00,000/- (For Rural & Urban Areas).

scheme_name: Special Scholarship to SC Students in Selected Schools
eligibility_text_source:
1. The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste Category of Gujarat.
 1. The student should be studying in the selected/designated school.
 1. The family income of the student should not be more than 10 times of the income determined for the poverty line. (For Rural Area: ₹6,00,000/- & for Urban Area: ₹6,00,000/).
 1. Students entering Class 8th or 11th will be eligible for assistance only once every year.
 1. Assistance is also available for Class 6 at Balachadi School, Jamnagar, and Sainik School, Kherwa.

**Note:** The Scheduled Caste (SC) students who are studying in famous private selected schools like Doon School, Dehradun; Sofiya School, Abu; Mayo School, Ajmer; Sainik School, Balachadi; Mahila Sainik School, Kherva; Dist. Mehsana, etc. are eligible to apply under the scheme.

scheme_name: Samriddhi – Scheme for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Students for Setting Start-Ups
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be an All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) approved institute or an All India Council for Technical Education approved University Department.
- The applicant (Institute/University Department) must have been in existence for a period of at least 10 years.
- The applicant (Institution) must possess a good incubation center.
- The incubation center's activities must have been happening for at least 5 years.

scheme_name: Swayam Saksham Yojana (GBCDC)

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme for Self-help Groups of Minorities
eligibility_text_source:

1. Members of Self-Help Groups should not be receiving support from the corporation.

1. Self-Help Groups should maintain adequate bookkeeping records.

scheme_name: Swatantrata Sainik Samman Pension Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
For the purpose of granting a pension under the scheme, a freedom fighter is:
> **(a) A person who had suffered a minimum imprisonment of six months in the mainland jails before Independence.**

1. However, ex-INA personnel will be eligible for pension if the imprisonment/detention suffered by them was outside India. 
1. The minimum period of actual imprisonment for pension eligibility has been reduced to three months, in the case of women and SC/ST freedom fighters from 01.08.1980.

**Explanation**
1. Detention under the orders of the competent authority will be considered imprisonment. 
2. A period of normal remission of upto one month will be treated as part of actual imprisonment. 
3. In the case of a trial ending in conviction, the trial period will be counted towards actual imprisonment suffered. 
4. A broken period of imprisonment will be totalled up for computing the qualifying period. 

**Eligible Dependents**

1. For the purpose of granting Samman pension, the family includes (if the freedom fighter is not alive) mother, father, widower/widow if he/she has not since remarried, and unmarried daughters.
1. Not more than one eligible dependent can be granted a pension and in the event of availability of more than one dependent the sequence of eligibility will be widow/widower, unmarried daughters, mother and father.

> **(b) A person who remained underground for more than six months provided he was:** 

1. a proclaimed offender; or 
2. one on whom an award for arrest/h

eligibility_text_source:

eligibility_text_source:

 1. Minimum 60% marks in the Madhyamik Pariksha exam.

 - **Diploma Courses:**

 1. Minimum 60% marks in the qualifying exam (excluding optional subjects).

 1. Eligible students include those enrolled in the first year after passing the Madhyamik Pariksha or its equivalent, and those enrolled in the second year through lateral entry (after passing HS or equivalent).

 1. Minimum 60% marks in aggregate in the HS exam conducted by WBCHSE/Madrasha Siksha Parishad.
 1. Separate merit lists are prepared for Arts, Commerce, and Science streams.

 - **Postgraduate (PG):**

 1. Minimum 53% marks in the Honours subject at the graduation level.

 1. Kanyashree scholarship recipients (married/unmarried) pursuing PG in Science, Arts, and Commerce with a minimum 45% in their undergraduate degree are eligible. They don't need to submit income proof.

 1. Full-time M.Phil. or Ph.D. research scholars registered after April 1, 2017, are eligible (up to a maximum of 2 years for M.Phil. and 4 years for Ph.D.).

 1. Acquisition of multiple machines of similar nature will not be entertained under the scheme. Only one machine will be eligible for the assistance under the scheme in such cases.
 1. The improvement in performance due to technology acquisition can be demonstrated as follows:

 - An increase of at least 10% in Production Rate (Number of units produced/Total time taken); or
 - A decrease of at least 5% in Cycle Time (Net Production Time/ Number of Units Produced); or
 - A decrease of at least 2.5% in Rejection Rate (Number of units rejected/ Total number of units produced); or

scheme_name: The Goa State Incentives to Encourage Investments Scheme
eligibility_text_source:

a) The applicant must be a unit that is going into production on or after 01-10-2017.
b) The applicant must be a unit that has gone into production on or after 01-10-2007 but not availed of any of the benefit under the old schemes specified under Schedule B.
c) The applicant must be a unit that has gone into production on or after 01-10-2007 but not completely exhausted benefits of the old schemes to be eligible for continuing benefits to the extent specified in this scheme.
d) The applicant must be a unit that has started production after 01-10-2007 and has availed benefit under old schemes mentioned under Schedule B, and has carried out substantial expansion during the validity period of this scheme.

- _The applicant may receive additional benefits if they are a woman entrepreneur._
- _The applicant may receive additional benefits if they are a Scheduled Caste (SC) entrepreneur._

scheme_name: Tripura Lynching/Mob Violence Victim Compensation Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The victim should be a resident of Tripura.
 1. The victim or his dependents who have suffered loss or injury as a result of lynching/mob violence and who require rehabilitation are eligible for the grant of compensation.
 1. Victim shall be eligible for the grant of compensation if ordered by the Court.
 1. Victim shall be eligible for the grant of compensation if:

 - The offender is not traced or identified, but the victim is identified and the victim has to incur a lot of expenses on physical and mental rehabilitation.
 - The victim or claimant must report the crime to the Officer-in-Charge of the local Police Station under whose jurisdiction the offense was committed or to the Judicial Magistrate having jurisdiction before claiming compensation;
 - The victim or claimant (in the case of the death of the victim) cooperates with the Police and prosecution during the investigation and trial of the case. Turning hostile, refusing to depose, or turning up during trial shall be considered to be non-cooperation.
 - The death or permanent incapacitation of the victim was not the result of the victim's wrongdoing or substantial provocation.
 - The victim suffers substantial loss of income as a result of the crime or is unable to take care of expenses of medical treatment for injury caused on account of the crime.
 - Due to the death of the victim as a result of the crime, dependents may claim compensation if the family becomes destitute and there is no earning member in the fa

scheme_name: The Meghalaya Victim Compensation Scheme, 2022
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant may apply for a grant of compensation under sub-section (4) of Section 357-A of the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) if the offender is not traced or identified.
1. The applicant should require financial support for physical and mental rehabilitation resulting from the crime.
1. The applicant should report the crime to the officer-in-charge of the local police station or to the Magistrate having jurisdiction.
1. The applicant may also be eligible if the police have taken suo motu cognizance of the offense committed under their jurisdiction.
1. The applicant must fully cooperate with the police and prosecution authorities.
1. The applicant must cooperate from the stage of investigation until the conclusion of the trial.
1. The applicant must not turn hostile, refuse to depose, or fail to appear during the trial. Such actions will be treated as non-cooperation.

scheme_name: Tiatr Script Writing Competition
eligibility_text_source:
##### ***Basic Requirements***

- The applicant must write scripts on themes as specified by Tiatr Academy of Goa.
- The applicant must ensure the script has not been published or performed earlier.
- The applicant must write original scripts only.

##### ***Script Format Requirements***

- The applicant must write scripts in traditional tiatr format.
- The applicant must include minimum 6 to 7 podd'de in the script.
- The applicant must include at least 4 Cantos in the script.
- The applicant must write scripts in Konkani language using Roman script.
- The applicant must ensure comedy is inter-related to the main plot (however, comedy may be absent in religious tiatr).

##### ***Content Guidelines***

- The applicant must ensure scripts do not contain adverse remarks against any Governments/Individuals/Institutions.
- The applicant must ensure scripts do not contain content against caste, creed and religion that may hurt sentiments.

scheme_name: Unemployment Allowance Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
Subject to the provisions of this Scheme, an educated unemployed applicant shall be eligible for the grant of Unemployment Allowance, who fulfills the following criteria: 

 1. She/he should be unemployed (should not be employed in Govt. sector nor in PSU or in private sector nor self employed) and should be a Bonafide Himachali. 
 1. She/he should have passed minimum 10+2 from any Board/University/Institution recognized by H.P. Govt. 
 1. She/he should be registered with any Employment Exchange in Himachal Pradesh as on the date of application since 1 year. 
 1. Her/his annual family income for the Financial Year immediately preceding date of application, from all sources including that of spouse should be less than ₹2 Lakh.
 1. She/he should be above 20 years and below 35 years of age, as on the date of application. 
 1. She/he should not be self-employed. 
 1. She/he should not be a dismissed Govt. employee. 
 1. She/he should not be convicted of any offence resulting in imprisonment for a period of 48 hours or more. 
 1. She/he should not be a regular student pursuing any course. 
 1. **She/he should not be availing Skill Development Allowance.**

scheme_name: Unemployment Allowance
eligibility_text_source:
**Conditions of qualification**

 1. The applicant should be a native of Chhattisgarh.
 1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 years to 35 years as on 01 April of the year in which the application is made.
 1. The applicant must have passed the minimum Higher Secondary (12th) examination from a recognized board.

 1. The applicant should not have any source of income of his own and the annual income of the applicant's family from all sources should not exceed Rs.2,50,000/-.

**Conditions of disqualification**

 1. If more than one member of a family fulfills the conditions of eligibility for unemployment allowance, only one member of the family will be approved for unemployment allowance. In such a situation, unemployment allowance will be approved to that member whose age is more. In case of equality of age, the member who got registered first in the employment office will be eligible for unemployment allowance. In case both the age and date of employment registration are same, the member who has higher educational qualification will be eligible.

scheme_name: Union Home Minister’s Medal for Excellence in Police Training
eligibility_text_source:

- The officer must have completed a minimum of two years of service in the training institute continuously as on the 1st of April of the year of consideration.
- The total length of service in training institutes (same or more number of institutes) must not be less than three years.
- No Judicial Proceedings must have been in pendency against the recommendee at the time of considering his/her name for the Medal.
- No Departmental Enquiry must have been in pendency against the recommendee at the time of considering his/her name for the Medal.
- The period of punishment must not be in currency against the recommendee at the time of considering his/her name for the Medal.
- The recommendee must not have been given any major penalty in the last five years.
- The recommendee must not have been given any minor penalty in the last five years.
- The recommendee must not have been awarded the Home Minister’s Medal for Excellence in Training on previous occasions.
- The Annual Confidential Report grading of the nominee should be ‘Outstanding’ or ‘Very Good’ in three years out of the last five years.
- The Annual Confidential Report grading of the nominee should be ‘Good’ in the remaining two years.
- There should not be any adverse entry in the last five years.

scheme_name: Uttarakhand Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Policy - 2023
eligibility_text_source:
1. Micro, small, and medium manufacturing enterprises in Uttarakhand, excluding those in the restricted categories under the policy, will be eligible.
 1. The benefits of the policy will be applicable only if there is a minimum of 25% additional capital investment in the existing enterprise along with at least a 25% increase in capacity.
 1. Under the Uttarakhand Enterprise Single Window Facilitation and Licensing Act, 2012, enterprises must obtain in-principle approval from the District/State Empowered Committee before commencing commercial production by applying through the CAF (Common Application Form) via the single-window portal.
 1. Financial incentives will be provided based on the category of the enterprise (Micro, Small, Medium) and the classification of the district/area (Category A, B, C, D).
 1. For new units or substantial expansion of existing enterprises, commercial production must commence during the policy’s period of operation, starting from 01.08.2023.

The applicant should obtain in-principle approval (CAF) through the Single Window Portal under the Uttarakhand Udhyam Ekul Khidki Sugamata aur Anugyapan Adhiniyam, 2012 (as amended), prior to establishing a new unit or expanding an existing one. This is necessary for the grants mentioned under this scheme.
1. The applicant's unit should provide a minimum of 70% permanent local employment.
1. The applicant's tourism unit should be mandatorily registered with the State Goods and Services Tax Department.
1. Units that commence commercial operations under this scheme within 180 days from the date of the notification will need to submit documentation and records for capital investment assistance in a comprehensive and clear manner.
1. The right to interpret the rules/provisions of this scheme, or to add, remove, or modify new tourism activities, is v

scheme_name: Uttar Pradesh Mukhyamantri Bal Seva Yojana (Genearal)
eligibility_text_source:
**Non-Institutional Children:**

1. Children under 18 who have lost both parents or one parent due to non-Covid reasons.
1. Adolescents 18-23 who lost both or one parent due to Covid or any reason, and are pursuing higher education after completing 12th grade.
1. Children of divorced/abandoned mothers.
1. Children whose parents/guardian are imprisoned.
1. Children rescued from child labor, begging, or prostitution.
1. Children of families involved in begging/prostitution.
1. An assistance will be provided to maximum two children of the family.
1. The annual income of the family of all the categories of children should be less than ₹3,00,000/- but the condition of income limit will not apply to those where both mother and father have died.

**Institutional Children:**

1. Under the age group of 0-18, previously lived in children's homes and reintegrated back into families or residing in child care institutions.
1. Support: Receives financial assistance and family/aftercare services for readjustment.

scheme_name: Uttar Pradesh Old Age Pension Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
**Eligibility** 

1. Age should be between 60 to 150.
1. Applicant should be below poverty line or his/her annual income should be upto for Rural Areas Rs. 46,080/- and Urban Areas Rs. 56,460/-.

scheme_name: Uttam Pashu Puraskar Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
