2. The beneficiary/livestock owner of Cattle/Buffaloes giving 15 liters or more milk per day is eligible to apply under the scheme.

scheme_name: Udyogini Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a woman.
1. The family income of the applicant should be less than ₹ 1,50,000/- for women belonging to general and special categories. No limit on family income for widowed or disabled women.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 to 55 years for all categories.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Karnataka.
1. The applicant should not have defaulted on any past loan with any financial institution.

**Reservation / Preference / Priority**

1. Preference must be given to the extremely poor, destitute, widow, and physically challenged. 
1. In addition, preference may be given to candidates who have undergone prior skill development training or vocational training conducted by any KSWDC or any other department. 
1. About 10% of the allocated target has been earmarked to benefit applicants of members of World Bank-assisted Swashakthi or Stree Shakthi groups.

scheme_name: Uttarakhand Shaheed Kosh Anudan
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
 1. The applicant should be a widow or dependent of a soldier or paramilitary force personnel martyred in a war, border conflict, or internal security operation.

> **Distribution Criteria:**

(a) In case the martyr was married:

 - Widow (Veer Nari) – 60% of the grant amount
 - Parents – 40% of the grant amount

(b) If the parents are not alive, then the full prescribed amount shall be given to the widow (Veer Nari) and where the widow (Veer Nari) is not alive then 40% of the prescribed amount shall be given to the parents and 60% shall be distributed equally among all the dependent children.
(c) In a situation where the parents and the widow (Veer Nari) are not alive, the prescribed amount shall be divided equally among all the dependent children.
(d) In a situation where the widow (Veer Nari) is not alive and has no children, the entire prescribed amount will be given to the parents.(e) In a situation where the martyr is unmarried or widower, the prescribed amount will be given to the parents.

scheme_name: University Scholarship (Ph.D.) - Uttarakhand

scheme_name: Uchch Shiksha Puraskar Yojana

 1. For post-graduation level applicants, the student should have passed graduation/post-graduation with at least second division.
 1. It is mandatory to study Urdu as a subject to receive the scholarship.
 1. The scholarship is applicable only for those who are regularly enrolled in recognized colleges/universities.

scheme_name: Uttarakhand State Science & Technology Congress (USSTC)

scheme_name: Uttarakhand Tourism and Travel Business Registration Rules
eligibility_text_source:
The following units are eligible for registration under the Rules:

 - **Residential Units:** Hotels, motels, resorts, guest houses, dharamshalas, ashrams, etc. - **Travel Trade-related Units:** Travel agents, domestic tour operators, excursion agents. - **Catering-related Units:** Restaurants, beer bars, fast food centres, food plazas, food courts, etc. - **Entertainment-related Units:** Amusement/theme parks, golf courses, ropeways, sound and light shows, angling units, etc. - A**dventure Tourism-related Units:** Adventure tour operators, kayaking, canoeing, water sports, river rafting, wall climbing, mountaineering, rock climbing, paragliding, etc. - **Other Units:** Handicraft/souvenir shops, yoga and meditation centres/sadhana kutirs, tourist information centres, and nature/adventure/wildlife photography units, etc.

scheme_name: Destitute Widow Pension Scheme - Uttarakhand
eligibility_text_source:

 1. The widow must be at least 18 years of age.
 1. The widow’s family monthly income should not exceed ₹4,000/-, or she must possess a valid BPL (Below Poverty Line) certificate.
 1. The widow must not be receiving any other pension benefits.

scheme_name: Udita Yojana
eligibility_text_source: - All adolescent girls and women aged 18 to 49 will be beneficiaries.

scheme_name: Unemployed Youth Employment Generation Programme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Tamil Nadu.
2. The applicant should be a minimum of 18 years of age; and
 (a) **for the General category:** maximum of 35 years of age.
 (b) **for Special category (SC/ ST/MBC/ BC/ Minorities/ Ex-Servicemen/ Differently abled/ Transgender):** maximum of 45 years of age.

4. The family income of the applicant should not exceed ₹5 lakhs per annum.

7. Project cost should not be more than ₹15 lakhs for manufacturing, and ₹5 lakhs for Trading / Service projects.

 1. The students pursuing courses from Govt. approved institutions are eligible to apply under this scheme.

scheme_name: Vidyajyothi Scheme- Financial Aid For Uniforms And Study Materials To PH Students
eligibility_text_source:

1. The applicant should be a student.
1. The applicant should be studying in Government / Aided Institution in Kerala.

1. The applicant’s annual family income is not applicable under this scheme.

**Note:** The preference will be given to the students of Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.

scheme_name: Vayomadhuram Scheme For Providing Glucometer To Senior Citizens
eligibility_text_source:

**Note:** If there are a large number of applicants, priority will be given to older persons.

scheme_name: Varnam Scheme For Transgender Students Pursuing Distance Education Courses

scheme_name: Vivah Sahayata Yojana
eligibility_text_source:

1. The bride should be at least 18 years of age.

scheme_name: Vidhya Sadhana Yojana Gujarat
eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Vivah Sahayata Yojana - Madhya Pradesh
eligibility_text_source:
1. The industrial establishment/institution must be covered under the Madhya Pradesh Labour Welfare Fund Act 1982 and the Board must be receiving regular contributions from the establishment.
1. The application form in the prescribed format must be submitted either 15 days before the wedding date or 60 days after the wedding date.
1. Bride and groom age should be 18 and 21 respectively.
1. The applicant must have been working in the establishment for at least one year.
1. If the applicant has availed the benefit of marriage assistance under any other scheme, he/she will be ineligible for this scheme.

scheme_name: Vivah Sahayata Yojana (Labor Welfare Board Madhya Pradesh)
eligibility_text_source:
**For Registration**

1. The applicant should be working in an industrial establishment covered under the Madhya Pradesh Labour Welfare Fund Act 1982.
1. The applicant should be employed in the establishment for at least one year.
1. The applicant should be from an establishment that regularly contributes to the Board.
1. The applicant should be submitting the application form 15 days before or within 60 days after the wedding.
1. The bride should be at least 18 years old, and the groom should be at least 21 years old.
1. The applicant should not have availed marriage assistance under any other scheme.

**For Application**

1. The applicant should be a daughter of a worker/employee in a covered institution.
1. The applicant daughter should be above 18 years of age.
1. The worker or institution should be covered under the Madhya Pradesh Labor Welfare Fund Act, 1982.

scheme_name: Videsh Siksha Yojana (MPBOCWWB)
eligibility_text_source:

 1. If the student is receiving benefits from any other source, he/she will not be eligible for this scheme.

scheme_name: Veda Vyasa Scheme for Vedic Education
eligibility_text_source:

eligibility_text_source:

scheme_name: Vidhyadeep Yojana
eligibility_text_source:

- The application must be submitted within ninety (90) days from the date of death or accident.

scheme_name: Widow B.Ed Scheme-Rajasthan
eligibility_text_source:

eligibility_text_source:
- The Scheme shall be generally applicable to all eligible tourism projects/units/expansion projects of existing units set up on or after the effective date (i.e. 22nd February, 2021) of this incentive scheme.

 Or,

scheme_name: Kanyashree Prakalpa
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
1. The applicant should be enrolled and regularly attending an educational institution.
1. The applicant should be unmarried.
1. The applicant should be from a family where the total income is not more than ₹1,20,000/- per year.
1. The applicant for the Annual Scholarship (K1) should be between the ages of 13 and 18 years.
1. The applicant for the One-Time Grant (K2) should be between the ages of 18 and 19 years.
1. The applicant should have a bank account opened in their own name, and the name on the application form must match the name on the bank account.
1. The educational institution that the applicant is studying in must be registered in West Bengal and recognized by the government.

> **Educational Institution Type Eligibility:****** The applicant will be eligible for Kanyashree's Annual Scholarship or the One-Time Grant if they are studying in any of the following:

- In Class VIII or above in a school, madrassah, or equivalent open school course.
- In a college or an equivalent open university course.
- A Vocational Training Centre, Technical Training Centre, or Industrial Training Centre.
- Girls enrolled in Sports Institutes may apply for the One-Time Grant; however, they cannot apply for the Annual Scholarship.

> **Relaxations:** Girls who are specially-abled (with a disability of 40% or more), or are living in a Home registered under the Juvenile Justice Act, 2000, or have lost both parents, may apply for Kan

scheme_name: West Bengal Migrant Workers’ Welfare Scheme: Accidental Disability
eligibility_text_source:
> **For registration as a Migrant Worker:**

 - The applicant must be a resident of West Bengal.
 - The applicant’s age should be between 18 and 60 years.
 - The applicant must be ordinarily a worker who is employed/ likely to be employed in any State/Union Territories other than the state of West Bengal/ Outside India.

> **For the application of Welfare Scheme:**

- The applicant should be a migrant worker.
- The applicant should be registered with the West Bengal Migrant Workers’ Welfare Board, Labour Dept, Govt of West Bengal.
- The migrant worker should have a disability of 40%.
- The disability should have been caused due to an accident during work.
- The disability should be certified by a Government Hospital.

scheme_name: West Bengal Migrant Workers’ Welfare Scheme: Repatriation of Dead Body
eligibility_text_source:
> **For registration as a Migrant Worker:**

- The applicant must be a resident of West Bengal.
- The applicant’s age should be between 18-60 years.
- The applicant must be ordinarily a worker who is employed/ likely to be employed in any State/Union Territories other than the state of West Bengal/ Outside India.

> **For the application of Welfare Scheme:**

- The beneficiary should be a nominee i.e., a person nominated by the registered Migrant Worker from his/ her family.
- The migrant worker should have lost his/her life due to death during the course of employment outside the State of West Bengal.

scheme_name: West Bengal Migrant Workers’ Welfare Scheme: Cremation
eligibility_text_source:
> **For registration as a Migrant Worker:**

 - The applicant must be a resident of West Bengal.
 - The applicant’s age should be between 18-60 years.
 - The applicant must be ordinarily a worker who is employed/ likely to be employed in any State/Union Territories other than the state of West Bengal/ Outside India.

> **For the application of Welfare Scheme:**

 - The beneficiary should be a nominee i.e., a person nominated by the registered Migrant Worker from his/ her family.
 - The migrant worker should have lost his/ her life due to death during the course of employment at his/ her work place or the place he/ she had to stay for the purpose of the job.

scheme_name: West Bengal Migrant Workers’ Welfare Scheme: Death Assistance
eligibility_text_source:
> **For registration as a Migrant Worker:**

- The applicant must be a resident of West Bengal.
- The applicant’s age should be between 18-60 years.
- The applicant must be ordinarily a worker who is employed/ likely to be employed in any State/Union Territories other than the state of West Bengal/ Outside India.

> **For the application of Welfare Scheme:**

- The beneficiary should be a nominee i.e., a person nominated by the registered Migrant Worker from his/ her family.
- The migrant worker should have lost his/her life due to natural death/accidental death.

scheme_name: Rupashree Prakalpa
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a woman who proposes to be married.
1. The applicant should have attained the age of 18 years.
1. The applicant should be unmarried on the date of submitting her application.
1. The proposed marriage should be her first marriage.
1. The applicant should have been born in West Bengal, OR she should have been a resident of West Bengal for the last 5 years, OR her parents should be permanent residents of West Bengal.
1. The applicant's family income should not be more than ₹1,50,000/- per annum.
1. Her prospective groom should have attained the age of 21 years.
1. The applicant should have an active bank account for which she is the sole account-holder. The bank account should be in a bank that has an IFSC (Indian Financial System Code) and a MICR (Magnetic Ink Character Recognition) code and transacts e-payments through NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer).

scheme_name: Sabooj Sathi
eligibility_text_source:
The SABOOJ SATHI Scheme is a government initiative launched by the West Bengal government to provide bicycles to students studying in government-run and government-aided schools from class IX to XII. 

***The eligibility criteria for the SABOOJ SATHI Scheme are as follows:***

1. **Age Limit:** The student must be between 13 to 18 years of age.1. **Schooling:** The student must be studying in a government-run or government-aided school in West Bengal.1. **Residency:** The student must be a permanent resident of West Bengal.1. **Family Income:** The family income of the student should not exceed Rs. 2 lakh per annum.1. **Distance:** The student should live at least 2 km away from their school.1. **Attendance:** The student must have at least 60% attendance in the last academic year.1. **Ownership:** The student should not already own a bicycle.1. **Class:** The student must be studying in class IX to XII.

_It is important to note that the eligibility criteria may vary depending on the specific guidelines of the scheme in each state or region. Additionally, the application process and required documents may differ depending on the government's instructions._

scheme_name: West Bengal Student Credit Card Scheme

1. Indian citizen & resident of West Bengal for 10 years (self-declaration).
1. Applicant should not be aged more than 40 years at application time.
1. Applicant should enrolled in higher education programs within or outside the country in schools, madrasahs, colleges, universities, IITs, IIMs, IISc, IIESTs, ISIs, NLUs, AIIMSs, NITs, XLRI, BITS, SPA, NID, IIFTs, ICFAI Business School, etc. or studying in coaching institutes for various competitive exams such as Engineering, Medical, Law, IAS, IPS, WBCS, SSC, etc.

**Note:** Students upload progress reports after each semester/year.

scheme_name: WISE Post-Doctoral Fellowship
eligibility_text_source:
> **Employment:**

Candidates in regular employment are not eligible for WISE-PDF. However, women scientists who are temporarily employed in research or academics may apply for the scheme but they have to leave their earlier assignment if the WISE-PDF project is approved. The position of research staff in any project, Post-Doctoral Fellow, Research Associate, etc. will be considered a temporary assignment.
> **Age:**

The minimum age to apply is 27 years and the maximum is 60 years. Women scientists, less than 27 years of age are NOT eligible.
> **Educational Qualification:**

Women Scientists having a Ph.D. in Basic/ Applied Science or an equivalent degree like MD, MS, MDS, etc. are eligible for WISE-PDF.
> **Break in Career:**

Break in career is not mandatory to apply for WISE-PDF, however, women who have a break are also encouraged to apply for this programme.

scheme_name: WISE Fellowship for Ph.D.
eligibility_text_source:
Unemployed women who want to pursue a Ph.D. can apply for a fellowship under WISE-PhD. The programme is open for both candidates; those who have not yet registered as well as those who have already registered for a Ph.D., but not availing of a fellowship from any sources. However, candidates who have completed two (2) years duration in Ph.D. as of the date of application are NOT eligible. The following are the primary eligibility criteria:
> **Qualification:**

The applicant should have an M. Sc./ M. Tech. or equivalent degree like B. Tech., M.Phil., M. Pharm. etc. in basic and applied sciences from a recognized University/Institute.
> **Age:**

The minimum age to apply for the programme is 27 years and the maximum is 45 years (A relaxation of 3 years in the upper age limit is allowed for SC/ ST/ PH candidates). The cut-off date for age calculation would be the date of submission of the WISE-PhD application.
> **Employment:** 

Women in regular employment are not eligible for WISE-PhD.

eligibility_text_source:

 - Candidates in regular employment are not eligible for WISE-SCOPE.

**Note 01:** The women scientists who are temporarily employed in research or academics may apply to this program but they have to leave earlier assignments, if, selected for the WISE-SCOPE program.
**Note 02:** The position of the applicant in any project, as Post-Doctoral Fellow, Research Associate, etc. will be considered a temporary assignment.
> **Age:**

 - The minimum age to apply is 27 years and the maximum is 60 years as of the date of submission.

> **Educational Qualification:**

 - Women Scientists who have completed a Ph.D. in Basic or Applied Sciences or have equivalent degrees like MD/MS/MDS are eligible for the WISE-SCOPE fellowship.

scheme_name: "Fishing Ban Period Assistance" under "Welfare and Relief for Fishermen During Lean Seasons and Natural Calamities Scheme"
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be an ordinarily resident of the Union territory of Puducherry.
- The applicant should be a fisherman.
- The applicant should belong to a group designated to be "Below Poverty Line".
- *Beneficiary shall also be considered based on the decisions taken periodically by the Governing Body of the Pondicherry Fishermen Welfare and Distress Relief Society.
- The applicant should not be ordinarily residing in any foreign nation during the natural calamity, lean season, or ban on the fishing period for which relief assistance is issued.
- The beneficiary or any other members of the ration card should not be in regular service in the Government/Quasi Government/Government undertakings/Retired Government Pensioner.

*** The benefit under this scheme shall not be denied only on the ground that the head of the family or family member is a recipient of Old Age Pension (OAP);**
*** The benefit under this scheme shall not be restricted on the ground that the fisherman is in possession of mechanized or motorized or traditional fishing boats or is not in possession of the said fishing boats.**

scheme_name: "Immediate Death Relief Assistance" under "Welfare and Relief for Fishermen During Lean Seasons and Natural Calamities Scheme"
eligibility_text_source:

- The deceased fisherman should have been a resident of the Union territory of Puducherry.
- The deceased fisherman must have lost his/her life while fishing. 
- The deceased fisherman must have been in the age group of 18-60 years.
- The deceased fisherman must not have been a beneficiary of the old age pension scheme. 
- The deceased fisherman should have enrolled as a member of the Fishermen/Fisherwomen Co-operative Society.

- The beneficiary or any other members of the ration card should not be in regular service in the Government/Quasi Government/Government undertakings/Retired Government Pensioner.

*** The benefit under this scheme shall not be denied only on the ground that the head of the family or family member is a recipient of Old Age Pension (OAP);**
*** The benefit under this scheme shall not be restricted on the ground that the fisherman has mechanized or motorized or traditional fishing boats or does not have the said fishing boats.**

scheme_name: Whale Shark Conservation Programme
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be a fisherman, particularly from the Kharva and Koli communities residing in the coastal talukas of Veraval, Sutrapada, and Mangrol in Junagadh district.
- The applicant's fishing net must incur damage or require cutting during an operation to rescue a trapped Whale Shark.
- The applicant, or the rescue team involved, must ensure the rescue operation is thoroughly documented through either videography or photography.
- The applicant, under the self-documentation scheme, must photograph the trapped Whale Shark, release it on the spot, and then submit these photographs along with incident details to the Forests and Environment Department after returning to the sea coast.

1. The applicant should set up their own business trade, and small industry to earn their livelihood.
1. The applicant's annual income does not exceed ₹35,000/-. 
1. Physically handicapped individuals are also eligible to apply under this scheme.

scheme_name: Welfare Services for Children
eligibility_text_source:
The following individual types of applicants are eligible under this scheme:

- Orphans and abandoned children
- Street children
- Children affected by HIV/AIDS
- Child labourers
- Juvenile delinquents

scheme_name: Welfare Services for Disabled Persons
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Individuals (Beneficiaries):**

- The applicant must be a person with disability having at least 40% disability, duly certified by a competent medical authority.
- The applicant must belong to any category such as locomotor, visual, hearing, mental, or multiple disabilities.
- The applicant must require support for rehabilitation, medical treatment, education, or livelihood.

> **For Organizations:**

- The organization must be a registered voluntary organization/NGO/institution.
- The organization must have experience in implementing disability welfare programmes.
- The organization must have adequate infrastructure and trained personnel to provide services.
- The organization must ensure that services are provided without discrimination.

scheme_name: Welfare Services for Women
eligibility_text_source:
The following individual types of applicants are eligible under this scheme:

- Widows without family or financial support
- Women facing domestic violence
- Trafficked or rescued women/girls
- Women affected by natural disasters
- Women with HIV/AIDS
- Economically backward women
- Women labourers, including domestic workers

scheme_name: Welfare Services for Other Socially Disabled Persons (Senior Citizens & Destitute)
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Individuals (Beneficiaries):**

- The applicant must be a senior citizen aged 65 years or above.
- The applicant must belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
- The applicant must be a destitute person without any regular means of livelihood.
- The applicant must not have family members or relatives to provide financial or social support.

> **For Organizations:**

- The applicant must be a registered voluntary organization/NGO/institution.
- The applicant must be engaged in providing welfare services for senior citizens or destitute persons.
- The applicant must have adequate infrastructure such as old age homes, day care centres, or care facilities.
- The applicant must have experience in implementing elderly care or social welfare programmes.
- The applicant must ensure that services are provided without discrimination on any grounds.

scheme_name: Union Territory of Lakshadweep Pension Scheme for Widows
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of the Union Territory of Lakshadweep.
1. The applicant should be a widow.
1. The applicant should not have remarried.
1. The applicant should have only nominal income, not exceeding ₹1,500/- per month (₹18,000/- per annum).
1. The applicant should not be a beneficiary of any other similar pension scheme provided by the government.

scheme_name: Working Women Hostel
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant should be a woman.
 - The applicant should be working individual.

scheme_name: World War II Pension
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
 1. The applicant should be either an ex-serviceman who served during World War II (1st September 1939 to 15th August 1945) or his widow.
 1. The applicant should not be receiving any pension or financial assistance from any other source.

scheme_name: Yatra Anudan
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand. 
1. The applicant must be a researcher or scientist.

scheme_name: Young Achievers Awards
eligibility_text_source:
- The applicant must be not more than 35 years of age.
- The applicant must have contributed in the field of tiatr for at least 10 years.
- The applicant must have performed in at least 15 tiatrs.
- The applicant's contribution should be considered in areas such as acting, script writing, directing, singing, lyricist work, set designing, etc.

***Relaxation***
The age limit may be relaxed if the health of the artiste is not well.

***Selection Criteria***

- The applicant's contribution as an actor, script writer, director, singer, lyricist, set designer, etc.
- The applicant should have received or won any awards in his/her field.
- The applicant should have done any innovation on the tiatr stage.
- The applicant, if a singer/musician, should have songs recorded on Compact Discs, Digital Versatile Discs, All India Radio, Doordarshan, online, etc.
- The applicant should have acted/directed/sung/composed music/composed lyrics/designed sets/provided background music/designed light effects for dramas in other languages or in films.
- The applicant, if script writer or lyricist, should have published any books of his/her scripts/lyrics.
- The applicant, if a writer/director, should have written/directed at least 7 tiatrs.
- The applicant's contribution in acting/singing/providing live music/stage setting/background music, providing lights, as a makeup artiste should be at least for 10 years and should have performed in at least 15 tiatrs.

scheme_name: Youth Art Training Fellowship Scheme
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be interested in training in one art discipline such as singing, instrumental music, dance, theatre, painting, or crafts.
1. The applicant should be between 18 and 35 years of age.

scheme_name: Yashwantrao Chavan Mukta Vasahat Yojana for VJNTs
eligibility_text_source:
1. The family of the applicant should be residing in Maharashtra.
1. The family of the applicant should belong to the original VJNT (Vimukta Jati and Nomadic Tribes) category.
1. The family of the applicant should not be having their own house.
1. The family of the applicant should be residing in a tent house or hut.
1. The family of the applicant should be landless.
1. The family of the applicant should be residing in one place for at least six months and, for the remaining months, should be wandering from village to village for livelihood.
1. The annual income of the family of the applicant should be less than ₹1,00,000/-.
1. The family of the applicant should not have availed of the benefits of any other housing scheme such as Gharkul etc. 
1. The benefit of this scheme will be given to one member only of the eligible family.

_*Priority shall be given to families living in tents, with no earning hand widows, divorcees, handicapped and flood-affected persons._

scheme_name: Youth and Eco Club
eligibility_text_source: 1. All primary, upper primary, and secondary government schools in Uttarakhand are eligible.

scheme_name: Youth Engagement through Empowered Youth Organisations (YESS)
eligibility_text_source:
**For Youth Organisations**

1. The organisation should be a registered society, trust, charitable organisation, Section 8 company, or not-for-profit entity under the Societies Registration Act of 1860 for at least 3 months.
1. The organisation should maintain financial accounts with a clear framework for effective and transparent management.
1. The organisation should have primary beneficiaries under the age of 35 and be open to all citizens of the state without distinction of religion, race, caste, or language.
1. The organisation should have at least 50% of registered members under the age of 35 as of the application date.
1. The organisation should not be blacklisted or barred by any government or private entity and should have no pending or ongoing litigation related to misconduct, misappropriation of funds, or sexual harassment.

**For Individuals**

1. The applicant should be a citizen of Meghalaya and under the age of 35 as of the application date.
1. The applicant should have a bank account in their name (single or joint account).
1. The applicant should be able to provide proof of selection for the competition or event for which the grant is being requested.

scheme_name: Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme for Electronics and IT: Young Faculty Research Fellowships
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an Indian citizen.
 1. The applicant must have obtained PhD degree from a recognized University with first class (in terms of grades, etc.) in all preceding levels and a good academic record throughout.
 1. The applicant must have a regular position in the institute and should be engaged in research.
 1. The upper age limit for the fellowship is 40 years on the date of submission of application.
 1. YFRF under Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme can be availed only once by a candidate in his/her career.
 1. The applicant availing any other fellowship is not eligible to apply.
 1. Proposals for allotment of YFRF are to be submitted through eligible institutions only.
 1. Each institution in the proposal may submit up to three nominations for the award for each round of call for applications.

***Discontinuation of the Fellowship:***

 1. The performance of YFRF would be reviewed periodically as per the discretion of PhD Cell, DIC; MeitY.
 1. A detailed performance assessment/review of award would be conducted annually and decision on continuation of YFRF would be taken accordingly.
 1. If any fellow wishes to discontinue the fellowship, the institution should inform Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme immediately. One month's notice is to be given to DIC through the institution before the date of discontinuation.
 1. The implementing institution should not incur any expenditure from the date of termination of the project or the date of resignation of the fellow. The institu

scheme_name: Youth Hostel Scheme
eligibility_text_source: 1. There is no age restriction but if applicant is below 18 then he/she must be accompanied by a parent or guardian when staying in a youth hostel.

scheme_name: Young Interns Program
eligibility_text_source:
> **General**

1. The age of the applicant should be between 21 years and 30 years.
1. The applicant should have excellent communication/presentation/intra–personal skills/soft skills.
1. The applicant should have good Knowledge of English as well as Hindi language (Reading, Speaking, Writing and Understanding)
1. The applicant should have well-versed ICT skills and knowledge of MS Office and other similar programs on alternative platforms.
1. It is mandatory for all the interns to have their own Laptop and data card/internet facility.

> **Education Specific**

1. The applicant should have a post-graduate qualification with a minimum of 60% marks or a CGPA of 6 on a scale of 10 in any stream from a recognized university.
1. The graduate applicant of Engineering/Medical/Law/CA/CS Streams from a recognized University with a minimum 60% mark or a CGPA of 6 on a scale of 10 shall also be eligible for this internship. 

> **Reservation / Preference / Priority**

1. The applicant with working experience in the field of his/her choice of department shall be given priority.

**NOTE 1:** Any other additional educational and/or technical qualifications and experience shall be as per the special requirements submitted by the concerned department/district collector/divisional HQ, if any.
**NOTE 2:** In case of any dispute, the decision of the Secretary-in-charge of, Statistics Department will be binding and final.

scheme_name: Young Investigators Programme in Biotechnology
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should possess a Ph.D. in any branch of Life Sciences.
2. The applicant should possess three years of post-doctoral experience in Biotechnology.
3. The applicant should be less than 40 years of age.
**Note:** Five years of relaxation in age would be available to scientists belonging to SC/ST/OBC, Women, and Physically Handicapped categories.

scheme_name: Yuvak Mangal Dal and Mahila Mangal Dal Swavalamban Yojana
eligibility_text_source: 1. The applicant must be a recognized Yuvak Mangal Dal or Mahila Mangal Dal in Uttarakhand, formally affiliated with the Department of Youth Welfare and PRD, Government of Uttarakhand.

scheme_name: Yatra Pass in DTC non AC Buses for the Construction Workers
eligibility_text_source:
> **For Registration**

- The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
- The applicant should be a Building/Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have served the Board for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For Application**

- The applicant should be above 5 years of age.

scheme_name: Yuva Srujan Puraskar (Navsarjan Chetana Puraskar)
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be an individual artist.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 25 and 40 years. Only in exceptional cases, the minimum & maximum age could be relaxed by upto 5 years.
1. The applicant should have given extraordinary contributions in the field of Art and culture at large in his/ her respective field of art in specific.
1. The applicant should have contributed to his/her respective field of art in Goa for a minimum 5 years out of his/her career as a 
1. practising artist.
1. No award will be presented posthumously under this scheme. However, during the selection process if the death of any artist occurs after the recommendation of his name then the Task Force Committee may consider his name for the award, in such circumstances, the award will be presented posthumously.

scheme_name: Yuvasree Prakalpa
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
1. The applicant must be unemployed.
1. The applicant should be enrolled with the Employment Bank as a ‘job seeker’.
1. The applicant should be class 8th pass and above.
1. The applicant should be between 18-45 years of age on the 1st day of April of the year in which he/she is considered under the scheme.
1. The applicant had not to availed financial assistance /loan under any state/central government sponsored Self-Employment scheme.
1. Only one member of a family is eligible to receive assistance under the scheme.

scheme_name: Dr. Y.V. Singh Award for Best Thesis in Vegetable Science
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant must be a student of Govind Ballabh Pant University of Agriculture and Technology, Pantnagar.
 1. The applicant must have submitted a thesis in the field of Vegetable Science at the university.
 1. The award is granted for a period of one year only.

scheme_name: Yoga Wellness Center
eligibility_text_source:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The beneficiary can belong to any age group.
1. The beneficiary must be registered at an identified Government Ayurvedic Centre or Hospital.

scheme_name: Zero Budget Natural Farming
eligibility_text_source:
1. Interested farmers should be selected for the scheme who have a special interest in natural farming. Every farmer must have a minimum of one-acre land ownership or family account holder.
1. Interested farmers may be from the same village, neighboring villages, or different villages as far as possible. 
1. Interested farmers will be selected by the Regional Assistant Agriculture Officer / Agriculture Supervisor.
1. Horticulture crops and animal husbandry should also be included in natural farming.
1. For the selection of farmers, priority should be given to SC, ST, BPL, small and marginal farmers as much as possible. Youths up to 35 years should be included for natural farming so that their capacity can increase.
1. Necessary material like drum, bucket, jug etc. will be given on grant to the concerned farmer for setting up the input unit for natural farming. 
1. Farmers selected under Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana should not be included in this scheme.

scheme_name: Zupda Vijlikaran Yojana
eligibility_text_source:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant should belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) or low-income family.
1. The applicant's family annual income should not exceed ₹1,50,000/-.
